For `jig render`, create a standalone Environment with filters registered but no template directory — load the template file directly by path via `render_str()` or equivalent\\n- [x] 2.5: Unit tests for variable validation — every VarType, required missing, default fallback, enum rejection, array item mismatch, merge precedence\\n- [x] 2.6: Unit tests + insta snapshot tests for all 13 filters and template rendering (conditionals, loops, comments, raw blocks, undefined vars, syntax errors)\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- All 13 filters produce correct output per AC-3.4 through AC-3.14\\n- `jig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\"class_name\\\": \\\"BookingService\\\"}'` renders to stdout\\n- Type mismatch exits 4 with expected vs actual type (AC-2.7)\\n- Missing required variable exits 4 with variable name and hint (AC-2.5)\\n- Merge precedence: inline --vars wins over --vars-stdin wins over --vars-file wins over defaults (AC-2.4)\\n- Undefined template variable exits 2 with \\\"did you mean?\\\" hint (AC-3.17)\\n- Template syntax error exits 2 with file path and line number (AC-3.18)\\n- Same inputs produce byte-identical output across runs (AC-N1.1)\\n- `jig render template.j2 --vars '...' --to output.txt` writes to file instead of stdout (AC-7.4)\\n- Multiple validation errors accumulated and reported together (AC-2.11)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/variables.rs` (full implementation)\\n- `src/filters.rs`\\n- `src/renderer.rs`\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- heck crate for case conversions\\n- minijinja crate for template rendering\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 3: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-4, FR-6, FR-7 (complete), NFR-2 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-6\\n\\nFile creation with templated output paths, directory auto-creation, skip_if_exists, and dual-stream output (JSON stdout / human stderr). After this phase, `jig run` works for create-only recipes.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 3.1: `src/operations/mod.rs` — ExecutionContext struct (base_dir, dry_run, force, virtual_files for dry-run state); OpResult enum (Success with action/path/lines/location, Skip, Error); operation dispatch (create only initially). In dry-run mode, create ops populate virtual_files instead of writing to disk.\\n- [x] 3.2: `src/operations/create.rs` — render `to` path as template; create parent directories; write rendered content; skip_if_exists logic; --force override; --base-dir path resolution\\n- [x] 3.3: `src/output.rs` — OutputMode enum (Json/Human/Quiet); TTY auto-detection; JSON serialization of operations array + files_written/files_skipped; track per-path write/skip status for files_written and files_skipped arrays; colored human output to stderr; --verbose includes rendered content\\n- [x] 3.4: Wire `run` subcommand in main.rs with --dry-run, --json, --quiet, --force, --base-dir, --verbose; render all template contents AND templated path fields (`to`, `inject`, `skip_if`) into a Vec before executing any operation — rendering failures abort before any file is touched; execute operations in declaration order; fail-fast on operation errors — stop execution on first failure\\n- [x] 3.5: Unit tests for create operation — happy path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, templated paths, directory creation, file-already-exists error\\n- [x] 3.6: Unit tests for output formatting — JSON schema matches SPEC, human output, quiet mode, verbose mode, dry-run\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `jig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'` creates files at templated paths (AC-4.1, AC-4.2)\\n- Parent directories created automatically (AC-4.3)\\n- skip_if_exists: true skips existing files with action:\\\"skip\\\" (AC-4.4)\\n- Default (skip_if_exists: false) errors on existing file without --force (AC-4.5)\\n- --force overwrites regardless (AC-4.6)\\n- --base-dir changes output root (AC-4.7)\\n- --dry-run produces output but writes nothing (AC-6.8)\\n- JSON output when piped, human output when TTY (AC-6.1, AC-6.2)\\n- --json forces JSON (AC-6.3), --quiet suppresses non-errors (AC-6.4)\\n- Operations execute in declaration order (AC-N6.1)\\n- Second run with skip_if_exists: true reports all skips (AC-N2.1)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs`\\n- `src/operations/create.rs`\\n- `src/output.rs`\\n- `src/main.rs` (run subcommand)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- owo-colors crate for terminal coloring\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 4: Inject Operation\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-5, NFR-2 (complete), NFR-6\\n\\nContent injection into existing files via regex anchoring. All injection modes: after, before, prepend, append, with at:first/last match selection and skip_if idempotency. This completes the v0.1 operation set.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 4.1: `src/operations/inject.rs` — read target file (or from virtual_files if target created in same dry-run); skip_if string search (skip_if string search must check virtual_files content when target was created in same dry-run); regex anchor matching (after/before); at:first/at:last match selection; prepend/append modes; render inject path as template; write modified content; inject ops update virtual_files with post-injection content for subsequent operations\\n- [x] 4.2: Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\n- [x] 4.3: Unit tests for every injection mode — after (first match), after (last match), before, prepend, append, skip_if, missing target file error, regex no-match error, templated inject path\\n- [x] 4.4: Integration test: recipe with create + inject in same run (create file, then inject into it — tests ordered execution AC-N6.2)\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- after: content on line after first match (AC-5.1)\\n- before: content on line before first match (AC-5.2)\\n- prepend: content at start of file (AC-5.3)\\n- append: content at end of file (AC-5.4)\\n- at:last uses last match (AC-5.5), at:first (default) uses first (AC-5.6)\\n- skip_if: skips when string found in file, reports action:\\\"skip\\\" (AC-5.7)\\n- Regex no-match exits 3 with pattern, file path, hint (AC-5.8)\\n- Missing target file exits 3 (AC-5.9)\\n- Inject path renders as template (AC-5.11)\\n- Create-then-inject in same recipe works (AC-N6.2)\\n- Second run with skip_if shows all skips — no duplicate content (AC-N2.2)\\n- Inject success reports action:\\\"inject\\\" with path, location, line count (AC-5.10)\\n- at field ignored when prepend/append specified; after/before without regex exits 1 (AC-5.12, AC-5.13)\\n- Invalid regex pattern in after/before exits 1 (AC-5.14)\\n- Multiple inject modes (after+before etc.) specified exits 1 (AC-5.15)\\n- --force has no effect on inject operations (AC-5.16)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/inject.rs`\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- regex crate\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 5: Integration Test Framework\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\n\\nFixture-based integration test harness that makes adding new test cases a matter of adding a directory. Snapshot tests for all output formats. This phase validates the entire v0.1 pipeline end-to-end.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 5.1: Test harness in `tests/integration.rs` — fixture discovery; copy existing/ to temp dir; run jig as subprocess; diff output against expected/; assert JSON output against expected_output.json; assert exit code against expected_exit_code\\n- [x] 5.2: Fixtures for create operations — simple create, templated path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, directory creation\\n- [x] 5.3: Fixtures for inject operations — after/before/prepend/append, at:first/at:last, skip_if\\n- [x] 5.4: Fixtures for error cases — missing vars, bad type, missing template, missing target file, regex no-match, malformed YAML, file exists without force\\n- [x] 5.5: Fixtures for combined operations — create + inject in one recipe, multi-file recipe, idempotency (run twice)\\n- [x] 5.6: insta snapshot tests for JSON output format, human output format, error message format\\n- [x] 5.7: Determinism test — run same recipe twice, assert byte-identical output (AC-N1.1)\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `cargo test` runs all unit + integration + snapshot tests green\\n- Every operation mode has at least one fixture\\n- Every error exit code (1-4) has at least one fixture\\n- Adding a new test case requires only a new directory, no code changes. Integration fixtures are auto-discovered from directories (no code changes to add tests). Spec-level unit tests are named functions in `#[cfg(test)]` modules (e.g., `spec::fr1::ac_1_1`). The two layers serve different purposes.\\n- Idempotency fixture: second run produces all skips, no file changes\\n- Binary has no dynamic dependencies beyond system libc (verified via `otool -L` on macOS, `ldd` on Linux)\\n- Every `TEST-*` ID in SPEC.md has a corresponding test function named `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}` or `spec::nfr{N}::ac_n{N}_{M}`\\n- Error fixtures assert that error JSON contains `what`, `where`, `why`, and `hint` fields (at least one fixture per exit code)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `tests/integration.rs`\\n- `tests/fixtures/` (directory tree)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- insta crate for snapshot testing\\n- assert_cmd or similar for subprocess testing (or raw std::process::Command)\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\n- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\n- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\n\\n## Key Decisions\\n\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\n| Operations as enum, not trait objects | `FileOp` enum with match dispatch | Only 2 variants in v0.1 (create, inject). Trait abstraction adds complexity for no benefit until user-defined operations exist, which isn't planned. (ARCHITECTURE D-4) |\\n| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages (ARCHITECTURE D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\n| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable (ARCHITECTURE D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\n| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism (ARCHITECTURE D-5, I-1) |\\n| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate, good Display/Error trait impls |\\n| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\n| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\n| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling. Acceptable dependency for correctness. |\\n| \\\"did you mean?\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) against declared variable names | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Hand-rolling edit distance serves I-10 (graceful degradation) — the error tells the caller exactly what to fix. |\\n| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves, even for ones minijinja provides (replace, indent, join) | We control exact behavior. No surprises from upstream minijinja changes. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\n| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. Templates reference variables not created files. (D-1) |\\n| Extra variables pass through | Don't error or warn on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness here breaks real usage. |\\n| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later operations may depend on earlier ones (e.g., inject targets a prior create). (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\n\\n## Risks / Open Questions\\n\\nAll resolved — see Resolved Questions below.\\n\\n## Resolved Questions\\n\\n- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\n- **\\\"did you mean?\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names. minijinja doesn't provide this. Serves I-10.\\n- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. We own the behavior, upstream changes don't affect us. Serves I-1.\\n- **TTY detection**: `std::io::IsTerminal` (Rust 1.70+) works correctly. No external crate needed.\\n- **stdin variable reading**: Documented as single-purpose per invocation. Non-interactive design (I-3) makes this a non-issue.\\n- **Large file handling for inject**: Reads entire file into memory. Acceptable for source files. Documented as known limitation.\\n- **pluralizer irregular plurals**: Works for common irregulars (person→people, child→children). Acceptable coverage.\\n- **minijinja filter override**: Overriding built-in filters works. All 13 registered as custom filters.\\n\\n## Completion Summary\\n\\n- **Completed**: 2026-04-04\\n- **Tests**: 191 total (177 unit + 2 CLI integration + 12 fixture integration)\\n- **Review**: Dual-agent review cycle (Claude + Codex) came back clean — 0 Critical, 0 Major findings\\n- **All 5 phases delivered**: validate, vars, render, run (create + inject), full integration test suite\\n- **CLI commands working**: `jig validate`, `jig vars`, `jig render`, `jig run`\\n\\n## Execution Order\\n\\n```\\nPhase 1 ──► Phase 2 ──► Phase 3 ──► Phase 4 ──► Phase 5\\nskeleton     vars+render  create+output inject     integration\\n                                                    tests\\n```\\n\\nEach phase produces a working, testable artifact. No phase depends on \\\"wiring up later.\\\" The CLI grows incrementally:\\n- After Phase 1: `jig validate`, `jig vars`\\n- After Phase 2: + `jig render`\\n- After Phase 3: + `jig run` (create only)\\n- After Phase 4: + `jig run` (create + inject) — **v0.1 complete**\\n- After Phase 5: Full test coverage validates the release\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:58:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_29\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,320p' CLAUDE.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# CLAUDE.md\\n\\nThis file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.\\n\\n## Project Status\\n\\n**v0.3 workflows complete.** All four file operations (create, inject, replace, patch) plus multi-recipe workflows with conditional steps, variable mapping, and error handling. 343 tests passing. Next up: libraries (v0.4).\\n\\n## What jig Is\\n\\njig is a template rendering CLI purpose-built for LLM code generation workflows. It takes a recipe (YAML) + variables (JSON) and produces deterministic file operations (create, inject, patch, replace). It is designed to be called by LLMs, not humans at a terminal.\\n\\nThe full specification lives in `jig.md`. Read it before making architectural decisions.\\n\\n## Language and Build\\n\\nRust.\\n\\n```bash\\ncargo build              # build\\ncargo test               # run all tests\\ncargo test <test_name>   # run a single test\\ncargo run -- <args>      # run the CLI\\ncargo clippy             # lint\\n```\\n\\n### Planned Dependencies\\n\\n| Crate | Purpose |\\n|-------|---------|\\n| `minijinja` | Jinja2 template rendering |\\n| `serde` + `serde_yaml` + `serde_json` | Recipe and variable parsing |\\n| `regex` | Injection/replace pattern matching |\\n| `clap` | CLI argument parsing |\\n| `heck` | Case conversion filters (snake_case, camelCase, etc.) |\\n| `owo-colors` | Terminal coloring |\\n| `insta` | Snapshot testing |\\n\\n## Architecture\\n\\nPlanned source layout:\\n\\n```\\nsrc/\\n  main.rs              # CLI entry point (clap)\\n  recipe.rs            # Recipe YAML parsing and validation\\n  variables.rs         # Variable declaration, type checking, JSON merging\\n  renderer.rs          # Jinja2 rendering via minijinja\\n  operations/\\n    mod.rs             # Operation trait and dispatch\\n    create.rs          # Create new files\\n    inject.rs          # Inject into existing files (line-level)\\n    patch.rs           # Patch existing files (scope-aware anchoring)\\n    replace.rs         # Replace regions in existing files\\n  scope/\\n    mod.rs             # Scope detection dispatch\\n    indent.rs          # Indentation-based (Python, YAML)\\n    delimiter.rs       # Delimiter-based (braces, brackets, parens)\\n    position.rs        # Semantic positions (after_last_field, etc.)\\n  workflow.rs          # Multi-recipe orchestration, conditional steps\\n  library/\\n    mod.rs             # Library manifest parsing\\n    install.rs         # Add/remove/update libraries\\n    discover.rs        # Recipe and workflow discovery\\n    conventions.rs     # Convention mapping and overrides\\n  filters.rs           # Custom Jinja2 filters (snakecase, pluralize, etc.)\\n  output.rs            # Human-readable and JSON output formatting\\n  error.rs             # Structured error types with exit codes\\n```\\n\\n## Core Concepts\\n\\n- **Recipe**: YAML file declaring variables and file operations. Lives alongside its templates.\\n- **Operations**: `create` (new file), `inject` (insert at anchor in existing file), `patch` (scope-aware insert using anchor/scope/position), `replace` (swap region between markers).\\n- **Anchor system**: Pattern match + scope (class_body, function_body, braces, etc.) + position (after_last_field, before_close, etc.) for structural code insertion without a full parser.\\n- **Scope detection**: Indentation-based for Python/YAML, delimiter-based for C-family. Lightweight heuristics, not AST.\\n- **Workflows**: Multi-recipe chains with conditional steps (`when`), variable mapping, and error handling modes.\\n- **Libraries**: Versioned recipe collections for a framework (e.g., jig-django). Installed globally or per-project, with convention overrides.\\n\\n## CLI Commands\\n\\n```\\njig run <recipe> --vars '<json>'    # Execute a recipe\\njig render <template> --vars '...'  # Render a single template\\njig validate <recipe>               # Check recipe is well-formed\\njig vars <recipe>                   # Show expected variables (JSON)\\njig workflow <name> --vars '...'    # Run a multi-recipe workflow\\njig scan <recipe> <path>            # Reverse-extract variables from existing code\\njig check <recipe> <path>           # Conformance verification\\njig library add|remove|update|list  # Manage recipe libraries\\n```\\n\\n## Exit Codes\\n\\n| Code | Meaning |\\n|------|---------|\\n| 0 | Success |\\n| 1 | Recipe validation error |\\n| 2 | Template rendering error |\\n| 3 | File operation error |\\n| 4 | Variable validation error |\\n\\n## Design Principles\\n\\n1. JSON in, files out. Non-interactive by default.\\n2. Deterministic: same recipe + same variables + same files = same output.\\n3. Idempotent: every operation safe to re-run (`skip_if`, `skip_if_exists`).\\n4. Transparent failures: errors include what/where/why + rendered content so the LLM can fall back to manual editing.\\n5. JSON to stdout, human-readable to stderr. Auto-detect TTY vs piped.\\n6. Templates live with the consumer, not in a central directory.\\n\\n## Testing Strategy\\n\\n- **Unit tests**: Recipe parsing, variable validation, template rendering, each operation type.\\n- **Integration tests**: Fixture directories with `recipe.yaml`, `vars.json`, `templates/`, `existing/`, and `expected/`. Test runner copies existing/ to temp dir, runs jig, diffs against expected/.\\n- **Snapshot tests**: `insta` crate for template rendering output.\\n- **Agent evals** (`eval/`): End-to-end tests where real LLM agents invoke jig against fixture codebases, scored on assertion pass rate, jig usage, and efficiency vs. baseline.\\n\\n## Slash Commands\\n\\n- `/review` - Adversarial code review (channels Linus, Hickey, Cantrill, Katz, Klabnik)\\n- `/plan-review <path>` - Adversarial review of planning/design docs for contradictions and gaps\\n- `/spacex [path]` - Apply The Algorithm to simplify code (Question, Delete, Simplify, Accelerate, Automate)\\n- `/clean-copy` - Rewrite branch with clean, narrative commit history\\n\\n## Roadmap Reference\\n\\nThe spec defines a phased roadmap (v0.1 through v1.0+). When implementing, follow the milestone order in `jig.md` — MVP core engine first, then patches, workflows, libraries, distribution, and advanced features (scan, infer, check).\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/replace-patch/reviews/code-review-codex-20260404-165354.md:80:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_40\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh = run_fixture(&fixture, work_dir);\\n   581\\t    let json = normalize_json(&stdout, work_dir);\\n   582\\t    let pretty = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap();\\n   583\\t    insta::assert_snapshot!(\\\"json_output_combined\\\", pretty);\\n   584\\t}\\n   585\\t\\n   586\\t// ── Static binary check ──────────────────────────────────────────────\\n   587\\t\\n   588\\t/// AC-N3.1: Binary has no dynamic dependencies beyond system libc.\\n   589\\t#[test]\\n   590\\tfn binary_no_extra_dynamic_deps() {\\n   591\\t    let binary = jig_bin();\\n   592\\t\\n   593\\t    if cfg!(target_os = \\\"macos\\\") {\\n   594\\t        let output = Command::new(\\\"otool\\\")\\n   595\\t            .args([\\\"-L\\\", &binary.display().to_string()])\\n   596\\t            .output()\\n   597\\t            .expect(\\\"otool failed\\\");\\n   598\\t        let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);\\n   599\\t        // On macOS, only system libraries should appear.\\n   600\\t        for line in stdout.lines().skip(1) {\\n   601\\t            let line = line.trim();\\n   602\\t            if line.is_empty() {\\n   603\\t                continue;\\n   604\\t            }\\n   605\\t            assert!(\\n   606\\t                line.contains(\\\"/usr/lib/\\\") || line.contains(\\\"/System/\\\"),\\n   607\\t                \\\"unexpected dynamic dependency: {}\\\",\\n   608\\t                line\\n   609\\t            );\\n   610\\t        }\\n   611\\t    } else if cfg!(target_os = \\\"linux\\\") {\\n   612\\t        let output = Command::new(\\\"ldd\\\")\\n   613\\t            .arg(binary.display().to_string())\\n   614\\t            .output()\\n   615\\t            .expect(\\\"ldd failed\\\");\\n   616\\t        let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);\\n   617\\t        for line in stdout.lines() {\\n   618\\t            let line = line.trim();\\n   619\\t            if line.is_empty() || line.contains(\\\"statically linked\\\") {\\n   620\\t                continue;\\n   621\\t            }\\n   622\\t            // Allow libc, libpthread, libdl, libm, libgcc, ld-linux\\n   623\\t            let allowed = [\\n   624\\t                \\\"libc.\\\", \\\"libpthread.\\\", \\\"libdl.\\\", \\\"libm.\\\", \\\"libgcc_s.\\\",\\n   625\\t                \\\"ld-linux\\\", \\\"linux-vdso\\\", \\\"librt.\\\",\\n   626\\t            ];\\n   627\\t            assert!(\\n   628\\t                allowed.iter().any(|a| line.contains(a)),\\n   629\\t                \\\"unexpected dynamic dependency: {}\\\",\\n   630\\t                line\\n   631\\t            );\\n   632\\t        }\\n   633\\t    }\\n   634\\t}\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/workflows/exec/consolidation-20260404-201852.md:45:- M4: `cmd_run` and `run_recipe` are divergent copies — consolidate before libraries\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:76:**Depends on:** v0.3 (workflows reference library recipes)\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:84:- `jig-library.yaml` format: name, version, description, framework, language\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:90:- `jig library add <url|path>` — install from git repo or local directory\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:91:- `jig library remove <name>` — uninstall\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:92:- `jig library update <name>` — pull latest\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:93:- `jig library list` — show installed libraries\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:94:- `jig library recipes <name>` — list all recipes in a library\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:95:- `jig library info <name>/<recipe>` — show recipe details\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:96:- `jig library workflows <name>` — list workflows\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:99:- Global: `~/.jig/libraries/`\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:100:- Project-local: `.jig/libraries/` (takes precedence)\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:103:- `.jigrc.yaml` per-project convention remapping\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:107:- `.jig/overrides/<library>/<recipe>/templates/` — replace a single template without forking\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:108:- `.jig/extensions/<library>/<recipe>/` — add new recipes namespaced under a library\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:110:**What this unlocks:** Libraries make jig useful beyond a single project. They're also a prerequisite for the MCP server (which needs `jig_library_recipes` to work), the Claude Code plugin (which wraps a library), and meaningful agent evals (which need a library like jig-django to test against).\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:113:- Does `jig library add` from git clone the whole repo or just the jig-library.yaml + recipe dirs?\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:115:- How does `jig run` resolve a library recipe path (e.g., `jig run django/model/add-field`)? Is it `jig run --library django model/add-field` or path-based?\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:116:- Does `jig workflow` accept library-qualified workflow names directly?\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:123:**Depends on:** v0.4 (distribution should include library install capability)\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:156:**Depends on:** v0.4 (libraries), MCP server (agent track)\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:168:*jig-django as reference library + plugin:*\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:169:- First community library: recipes for model, service, view, schema, admin, test, factory\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:172:- Dual-publish: installable as `jig library add` AND as a Claude Code plugin\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:177:  jig-library.yaml              # library manifest\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:200:**Depends on:** v0.4 (needs library recipes to scan/check against)\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:218:- Directory-level checking across all library concerns\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:254:**Depends on:** v0.4 (libraries), v0.3 (workflows)\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:260:- Cross-library workflows: steps reference different libraries (`library: django`, `library: vue`)\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:262:- Type mapping definitions in library manifests (`openapi_to_django`, `openapi_to_typescript`)\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:280:Also: comprehensive documentation site, homebrew-core publication, 3+ community libraries.\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:291:**Can start after:** v0.2 (core engine exists), but full value requires v0.4 (libraries)\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:314:| `jig_library_recipes` | `jig library recipes <library> --json` |\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:323:- Phase 2 (after v0.4): add `jig_library_recipes` — agents can discover library recipes\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:368:**Can start after:** v0.3 (needs workflows for meaningful scenarios), full value at v0.4+ (needs libraries)\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:411:| Eval-1 | After v0.3 | Harness skeleton + 3-5 basic scenarios (single-recipe, no library). Tests: can agents call `jig run` and `jig workflow` correctly? Baseline comparison. |\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:412:| Eval-2 | After v0.4 | Add library scenarios (10-15 total). Tests: recipe discovery, variable extraction from existing code, workflow invocation. Full scoring. |\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:439:### `.jigrc.yaml` Configuration\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:443:Project-wide defaults: `base_dir`, `vars_file`, custom shell filters. Convention overrides for libraries land in the same file.\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:448:| Convention overrides (`libraries.django.conventions`) | v0.4 (libraries) |\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:468:v0.1 ✅ ─► v0.2 ✅ ─► v0.3 workflows ─► v0.4 libraries ─► v0.5 distribution\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:484:v0.9 polyglot ◄── v0.4 libraries + v0.3 workflows\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:489:- **MCP server** needs v0.4 (libraries) for `jig_library_recipes` tool, but Phase 1 (`jig_run`, `jig_vars`, `jig_workflow`) can ship with v0.3\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:490:- **Agent evals** need v0.3 (workflows) minimum, v0.4 (libraries) for meaningful scenarios\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:491:- **Plugin (v0.6)** needs MCP server + v0.4 libraries + first library (jig-django)\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:492:- **Scan/Check (v0.7)** needs v0.4 libraries to know what to scan against\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:494:- **Distribution (v0.5)** is technically independent but should ship after v0.4 so the distributed binary includes library support\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:505:| v0.4 libraries | PLAN + SPEC needed. Most complex remaining spec work — manifest format, install logic, convention overrides, extensions. | After v0.3 done |\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:507:| v0.6 plugin | PLAN + SPEC needed. Depends on MCP + libraries existing first. | After v0.4 + MCP done |\ndocs/ROADMAP.md:523:| Library registry | line 1995 | Searchable index like crates.io for jig libraries add/remove/update\ndocs/ARCHITECTURE.md:574:- `src/library/discover.rs` — recipe listing\ndocs/ARCHITECTURE.md:575:- `src/library/conventions.rs` — convention mapping and 356\\t        exit_codes_seen.insert(exit_code);\\n   357\\t\\n   358\\t        if is_workflow_fixture(fixture) {\\n   359\\t            // Workflow error fixtures: JSON output has steps array with error details,\\n   360\\t            // or pre-execution errors go to stderr.\\n   361\\t            if !stdout.trim().is_empty() {\\n   362\\t                let json: serde_json::Value = step.get(\\\"error\\\") {\\n   368\\t                            assert!(err[\\\"what\\\"].is_string(), \\\"fixture '{}': error missing 'what'\\\", name);\\n   369\\t 2 || exit_code == 4) && !stderr.is_empty() {\\n   378\\t                assert!(\\n   379\\t                    stderr.contains(\\\"where:\\\") || stderr.contains(\\\"why:\\\"),\\n   380\\t    14\\\\t// ── OnError enum ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n    15\\\\t\\\\n    16\\\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\\\\n    17\\\\tpub enum OnError {\\\\n    18\\\\t    Stop,\\\\n    19\\\\t    Continue,\\\\n    20\\\\t    Report,\\\\n    21\\\\t}\\\\n    22\\\\t\\\\n    23\\\\t#[allow(clippy::derivable_impls)]\\\\n    24\\\\timpl Default for OnError {\\\\n    25\\\\t    fn default() -> Self {\\\\n    26\\\\t        OnError::Stop\\\\n    27\\\\t    }\\\\n    28\\\\t}\\\\n    29\\\\t\\\\n    30\\\\timpl std::fmt::Display for OnError {\\\\n    31\\\\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\\\\n    32\\\\t        match self {\\\\n    33\\\\t            OnError::Stop => write!(f, \\\\\\\"stop\\\\\\\"),\\\\n    34\\\\t            OnError::Continue => write!(f, \\\\\\\"continue\\\\\\\"),\\\\n    35\\\\t            OnError::Report => write!(f, \\\\\\\"report\\\\\\\"),\\\\n    36\\\\t        }\\\\n    37\\\\t    }\\\\n    38\\\\t}\\\\n    39\\\\t\\\\n    40\\\\timpl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for OnError {\\\\n    41\\\\t    fn deserialize<D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<Self, D::Error>\\\\n    42\\\\t {\\\\n    47\\\\t            \\\\\\\"stop\\\\\\\" => Ok(OnError::Stop),\\\\n    48\\\\t            \\\\\\\"continue\\\\\\\" => Ok(OnError::Continue),\\\\n    49\\\\t            \\\\\\\"report\\\\\\\" => Ok(OnError::Report),\\\\n    50\\\\t            _ => Err(serde::de::Error::custom(format!(\\\\n    51\\\\t                \\\\\\\"invalid on_error value '{}': allowed values are stop, continue, report\\\\\\\",\\\\n    52\\\\t                s\\\\n    53\\\\t            ))),\\\\n    54\\\\t        }\\\\n    55\\\\t    }\\\\n    56\\\\t}\\\\n    57\\\\t\\\\n    58\\\\t// ── Workflow types ───────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n    59\\\\t\\\\n    60\\\\t#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\\\\n    61\\\\tstruct RawWorkflow {\\\\n    62\\\\t    name: Option<String>,\\\\n 181\\\\t\\\\n   182\\\\t    match (has_steps, has_files) {\\\\n   183\\\\t        (true, true) => Err(JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\\\\n   184\\\\t            what: \\\\\\\"ambiguous keys\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   187\\\\t            hint: \\\\\\\"a workflow uses 'steps', a recipe uses 'files' — remove one\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   188\\\\t        })),\\\\n   189\\\\t        (true, false) => Ok(FileType::Workflow),\\\\n   190\\\\t        (false, true) => Ok(FileType::Recipe),\\\\n   191\\\\t        (false, false) => Err(JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\\\\n })),\\\\n   197\\\\t    }\\\\n   198\\\\t}\\\\n   199\\\\t\\\\n   200\\\\t// ── Workflow loading ─────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   201\\\\t\\\\n   202\\\\tpub fn load_workflow(path: typed struct.\\\\n   236\\\\t    let raw: RawWorkflow = serde_yaml::from_str(&content).map_err(|e| {\\\\n   237\\\\t        JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\\\\n   238\\\\t 19\\\\t#[derive(Parser)]\\\\n    20\\\\t#[command(name = \\\\\\\"jig\\\\\\\", version, about = \\\\\\\"Template rendering CLI for LLM code generation workflows\\\\\\\")]\\\\n    21\\\\tstruct Cli {\\\\n    22\\\\t    /// Inline variables as JSON string\\\\n    23\\\\t    #[arg(long, global = true)]\\\\n    24\\\\t    vars: Option<String>,\\\\n    25\\\\t\\\\n    26\\\\t    /// Variables from a global = true)]\\\\n    32\\\\t    vars_stdin: bool,\\\\n    33\\\\t\\\\n    34\\\\t    /// Preview without writing files\\\\n    35\\\\t    #[arg(long, global = true)]\\\\n    36\\\\t    dry_run: stderr output\\\\n    43\\\\t    #[arg(long, global = true)]\\\\n    44\\\\t    quiet: bool,\\\\n    45\\\\t\\\\n    46\\\\t    /// Overwrite existing files\\\\n    47\\\\t    #[arg(long, global = true)]\\\\n    48\\\\t    force: bool,\\\\n    49\\\\t\\\\n    50\\\\t    /// Resolve output paths from this directory\\\\n    51\\\\t    #[arg(long, global = true)]\\\\n    52\\\\t    base_dir: Option<PathBuf>,\\\\n    53\\\\t\\\\n    54\\\\t    /// Include rendered content in output\\\\n    55\\\\t    #[arg(long, global = true)]\\\\n    56\\\\t    verbose: bool,\\\\n    57\\\\t\\\\n    58\\\\t    #[command(subcommand)]\\\\n    59\\\\t    command: Commands,\\\\n    60\\\\t}\\\\n    61\\\\t\\\\n    62\\\\t#[derive(Subcommand)]\\\\n    63\\\\tenum Commands {\\\\n    64\\\\t    /// Parse a recipe and report whether it is valid\\\\n    65\\\\t    Validate {\\\\n    66\\\\t        /// Path to the recipe YAML file\\\\n    67\\\\t        recipe: PathBuf,\\\\n    68\\\\t    },\\\\n operations in a recipe\\\\n    83\\\\t    Run {\\\\n    84\\\\t        /// Path to the recipe YAML file\\\\n    85\\\\t        recipe: PathBuf,\\\\n    86\\\\t    },\\\\n    87\\\\t    /// Execute a multi-recipe workflow\\\\n    88\\\\t    Workflow {\\\\n    89\\\\t        /// Path to the workflow YAML file\\\\n    90\\\\t        path: PathBuf,\\\\n    91\\\\t    },\\\\n    92\\\\t}\\\\n    93\\\\t\\\\n    94\\\\tfn main() {\\\\n    95\\\\t    let cli = Cli::parse();\\\\n    96\\\\t    let result = run(cli);\\\\n    97\\\\t    match result {\\\\n    98\\\\t        Ok(code) => process::exit(code),\\\\n    99\\\\t        Err(e) => {\\\\n   100\\\\t            eprintln!(\\\\\\\"{e}\\\\\\\");\\\\n   101\\\\t            process::exit(e.exit_code());\\\\n   102\\\\t recipe } => cmd_validate(&recipe, cli.json),\\\\n   109\\\\t        Commands::Vars { recipe } => cmd_vars(&recipe),\\\\n   110\\\\t        Commands::Render { template, to } => {\\\\n   111\\\\t            cmd_render(&template, to.as_deref(), cli.vars.as_deref(), cli.vars_file.as_deref(), cli.vars_stdin)\\\\n   112\\\\t        }\\\\n   113\\\\t        Commands::Run { recipe } => {\\\\n   114\\\\t            cmd_run(\\\\n   115\\\\t                &recipe,\\\\n   116\\\\t                cli.vars.as_deref(),\\\\n   117\\\\t                cli.vars_file.as_deref(),\\\\n   118\\\\t                cli.vars_stdin,\\\\n   119\\\\t                cli.dry_run,\\\\n   120\\\\t                cli.json,\\\\n   121\\\\t                cli.quiet,\\\\n   122\\\\t                cli.force,\\\\n   123\\\\t                cli.base_dir.as_deref(),\\\\n   124\\\\t                cli.verbose,\\\\n   125\\\\t            )\\\\n   126\\\\t        }\\\\n   127\\\\t        Commands::Workflow { path } => {\\\\n   128\\\\t            cmd_workflow(\\\\n   129\\\\t                &path,\\\\n   130\\\\t                cli.vars.as_deref(),\\\\n   131\\\\t                cli.vars_file.as_deref(),\\\\n   132\\\\t   137\\\\t                cli.base_dir.as_deref(),\\\\n   138\\\\t                cli.verbose,\\\\n   139\\\\t            )\\\\n   140\\\\t        }\\\\n   141\\\\t    }\\\\n   142\\\\t}\\\\n   143\\\\t\\\\n   144\\\\tfn cmd_validate(path: &std::path::Path, json: bool) -> Result<i32, JigError> {\\\\n   145\\\\t    match workflow::detect_file_type(path)? {\\\\n   146\\\\t        workflow::FileType::Recipe => {\\\\n   147\\\\t            let recipe = Recipe::load(path)?;\\\\n   148\\\\t            if json {\\\\n   149\\\\t                let output = build_validate_json(&recipe);\\\\n   150\\\\t                println!(\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&output).unwrap());\\\\n   151\\\\t            } else {\\\\n   152\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\\\\"Recipe is valid: {}\\\\\\\", path.display());\\\\n   153\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\\\\"  Variables: {}\\\\\\\", recipe.variables.len());\\\\n   154\\\\t                if !recipe.variables.is_empty() {\\\\n   155\\\\t                    for name in recipe.variables.keys() {\\\\n   156\\\\t                        eprintln!(\\\\\\\"    - {name}\\\\\\\");\\\\n   157\\\\t                    }\\\\n   158\\\\t                }\\\\n   159\\\\t                let op_types = summarize_op_types(&recipe);\\\\n   160\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\\\\"  Operations: {} ({})\\\\\\\", recipe.files.len(), op_types);\\\\n   161\\\\t            }\\\\n   162\\\\t        }\\\\n   163\\\\t        workflow::FileType::Workflow => {\\\\n   164\\\\t            let validation = workflow::validate_workflow(path)?;\\\\n   165\\\\t            if json {\\\\n   166\\\\t                let output = output::build_workflow_validate_json(&validation);\\\\n   167\\\\t path.display());\\\\n   170\\\\t                if let Some(ref name) = validation.name {\\\\n   171\\\\t                    eprintln!(\\\\\\\"  Name: {name}\\\\\\\");\\\\n   172\\\\t                }\\\\n   173\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\\\\"  Variables: {}\\\\\\\", validation.variables.len());\\\\n   174\\\\t                if !validation.variables.is_empty() {\\\\n   175\\\\t                    for name in validation.variables.keys() {\\\\n   176\\\\t                        eprintln!(\\\\\\\"    - {name}\\\\\\\");\\\\n   177\\\\t                    }\\\\n   178\\\\t                }\\\\n   179\\\\t                let unconditional = validation.steps.iter().filter(|s| !s.conditional).count();\\\\n   180\\\\t                let conditional = validation.steps.iter().filter(|s| s.conditional).count();\\\\n   181\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\n   182\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"  Steps: {} ({} unconditional, {} conditional)\\\\\\\",\\\\n   183\\\\t                    validation.steps.len(),\\\\n   184\\\\t                    unconditional,\\\\n   185\\\\t                    conditional\\\\n   186\\\\t                );\\\\n   187\\\\t                for (i, step) in validation.steps.iter().enumerate() {\\\\n   188\\\\t                    let cond = if step.conditional { \\\\\\\" (conditional)\\\\\\\" } else { \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\" };\\\\n   189\\\\t                    let status = if step.valid { \\\\\\\"valid\\\\\\\" } else { \\\\\\\"INVALID\\\\\\\" };\\\\n   190\\\\t                    eprintln!(\\\\\\\"    {}. {} — {}{}\\\\\\\", i + 1, step.recipe, status, cond);\\\\n   191\\\\t                }\\\\n   192\\\\t            }\\\\n   193\\\\t        }\\\\n   194\\\\t    }\\\\n   195\\\\t\\\\n   196\\\\t    Ok(0)\\\\n   197\\\\t}\\\\n   198\\\\t\\\\n   199\\\\tfn cmd_vars(path: &std::path::Path) -> Result<i32, JigError> {\\\\n   200\\\\t    let vars = match workflow::detect_file_type(path)? {\\\\n   201\\\\t        workflow::FileType::Recipe => {\\\\n   202\\\\t            let recipe = Recipe::load(path)?;\\\\n   203\\\\t            variables::vars_json(&recipe.variables)\\\\n   204\\\\t        }\\\\n   205\\\\t        workflow::FileType::Workflow => {\\\\n   206\\\\t            let wf = workflow::load_workflow(path)?;\\\\n   207\\\\t            variables::vars_json(&wf.variables)\\\\n   208\\\\t        }\\\\n   209\\\\t    };\\\\n   210\\\\t    println!(\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&vars).unwrap());\\\\n   211\\\\t    Ok(0)\\\\n   212\\\\t}\\\\n   213\\\\t\\\\n   214\\\\tfn cmd_render(\\\\n   215\\\\t    template_path: &std::path::Path,\\\\n   216\\\\t    to: Option<&std::path::Path>,\\\\n   217\\\\t    inline_vars: Option<&str>,\\\\n   218\\\\t    vars_file: Option<&std::path::Path>,\\\\n   219\\\\t    vars_stdin: bool,\\\\n   220\\\\t) -> Result<i32, JigError> {\\\\n   221\\\\t    // Read the template file\\\\n   222\\\\t    let source = std::fs::read_to_string(template_path).map_err(|e| {\\\\n   223\\\\t        JigError::TemplateRendering(crate::error::StructuredError {\\\\n   224\\\\t            what: format!(\\\\\\\"cannot read template file '{}'\\\\\\\", template_path.display()),\\\\n   225\\\\t            where_: template_path.display().to_string(),\\\\n   226\\\\t            why: e.to_string(),\\\\n   227\\\\t            hint: \\\\\\\"check the template file path\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   228\\\\t        })\\\\n   229\\\\t    })?;\\\\n   230\\\\t\\\\n   231\\\\t    // Collect variables from all sources (no recipe context → no type validation)\\\\n   232\\\\t    let vars = variables::collect_vars(inline_vars, vars_file, vars_stdin)?;\\\\n   233\\\\t\\\\n   234\\\\t    // Create standalone environment and render\\\\n   235\\\\t    let env = renderer::create_standalone_env();\\\\n   236\\\\t    let source_name = template_path.display().to_string();\\\\n   237\\\\t    let output = renderer::render_string(&env, &source, &vars, &source_name)?;\\\\n   238\\\\t\\\\n   239\\\\t    // Write to file or stdout\\\\n   240\\\\t    match to {\\\\n   241\\\\t        Some(path) => {\\\\n   242\\\\t            if let Some(parent) = path.parent().filter(|p| !p.as_os_str().is_empty()) {\\\\n   243\\\\t                std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).map_err(|e| {\\\\n   244\\\\t                    JigError::FileOperation(crate::error::StructuredError {\\\\n   245\\\\t                        what: format!(\\\\\\\"cannot create output directory '{}'\\\\\\\", parent.display()),\\\\n   246\\\\t                        where_: path.display().to_string(),\\\\n   247\\\\t                        why: e.to_string(),\\\\n   248\\\\t                        hint: \\\\\\\"check directory permissions\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   249\\\\t                    })\\\\n   250\\\\t                })?;\\\\n   251\\\\t            }\\\\n   252\\\\t            std::fs::write(path, &output).map_err(|e| {\\\\n   253\\\\t                JigError::FileOperation(crate::error::StructuredError {\\\\n   254\\\\t                    what: format!(\\\\\\\"cannot write output file '{}'\\\\\\\", path.display()),\\\\n   255\\\\t                    where_: path.display().to_string(),\\\\n   256\\\\t                    why: e.to_string(),\\\\n   257\\\\t                    hint: \\\\\\\"check file permissions\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   258\\\\t                })\\\\n   259\\\\t            })?;\\\\n   260\\\\t        }\\\\n   261\\\\t        None => {\\\\n   262\\\\t            print!(\\\\\\\"{output}\\\\\\\");\\\\n   263\\\\t        }\\\\n   264\\\\t    }\\\\n   265\\\\t\\\\n   266\\\\t    Ok(0)\\\\n   267\\\\t}\\\\n   268\\\\t\\\\n   269\\\\t#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\\\\n   270\\\\tfn cmd_run(\\\\n   271\\\\t    recipe_path: &std::path::Path,\\\\n   272\\\\t    inline_vars: Option<&str>,\\\\n   273\\\\t    vars_file: Option<&std::path::Path>,\\\\n   274\\\\t    vars_stdin: bool,\\\\n   275\\\\t    dry_run: bool,\\\\n   276\\\\t    force_json: bool,\\\\n   277\\\\t    quiet: bool,\\\\n   278\\\\t    force: bool,\\\\n   279\\\\t    base_dir: Option<&std::path::Path>,\\\\n   280\\\\t    verbose: bool,\\\\n   281\\\\t) -> Result<i32, JigError> {\\\\n   282\\\\t    let mode = output::detect_mode(force_json);\\\\n   283\\\\t\\\\n   284\\\\t    // Helper: handle pre-operation errors respecting --json and --quiet.\\\\n   285\\\\t    // In JSON/piped mode, emit a JSON error envelope to stdout.\\\\n   286\\\\t    // In human mode with --quiet, suppress stderr.\\\\n   287\\\\t    // Returns Ok(exit_code) so main() doesn't double-print.\\\\n   288\\\\t    let handle_early_error = |e: JigError| -> Result<i32, JigError> {\\\\n   289\\\\t        let code = e.exit_code();\\\\n   290\\\\t        match mode {\\\\n   291\\\\t            output::OutputMode::Json => {\\\\n   292\\\\t                let se = e.structured_error().clone();\\\\n   293\\\\t                let json = serde_json::json!({\\\\n   294\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"dry_run\\\\\\\": dry_run,\\\\n   295\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"operations\\\\\\\": [{\\\\n   296\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\",\\\\n   297\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n   298\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"what\\\\\\\": se.what,\\\\n   299\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"where\\\\\\\": se.where_,\\\\n   300\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"why\\\\\\\": se.why,\\\\n   301\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"hint\\\\\\\": se.hint,\\\\n   302\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"rendered_content\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n   303\\\\t                    }],\\\\n   304\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"files_written\\\\\\\": [],\\\\n   305\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"files_skipped\\\\\\\": [],\\\\n   306\\\\t                });\\\\n   307\\\\t                println!(\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap());\\\\n   308\\\\t                Ok(code)\\\\n   309\\\\t            }\\\\n   310\\\\t            output::OutputMode::Human => {\\\\n   311\\\\t                if !quiet {\\\\n   312\\\\t                    eprintln!(\\\\\\\"{e}\\\\\\\");\\\\n   313\\\\t                }\\\\n   314\\\\t                Ok(code)\\\\n   315\\\\t            }\\\\n   316\\\\t        }\\\\n   317\\\\t    };\\\\n   318\\\\t\\\\n   319\\\\t    // 1. Load and validate recipe (exit 1 on failure).\\\\n   320\\\\t    let recipe = match Recipe::load(recipe_path) {\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n workflow_vars.clone();\\\\n   366\\\\t\\\\n   367\\\\t    // Apply vars_map (simultaneous rename).\\\\n   368\\\\t    if let Some(ref vm) = step.vars_map {\\\\n   369\\\\t        let obj = vars.as_object_mut().unwrap();\\\\n   370\\\\t\\\\n   371\\\\t        // 1. Snapshot all source values.\\\\n   372\\\\t        let mut snapshots: Vec<(String, String, Option<Value>)> = Vec::new();\\\\n   373\\\\t        for (source, target) in vm {\\\\n   374\\\\t            let val = obj.get(source).cloned();\\\\n   375\\\\t            snapshots.push((source.clone(), target.clone(), val));\\\\n   376\\\\t        }\\\\n   377\\\\t\\\\n   378\\\\t        // 2. Remove all source keys.\\\\n   379\\\\t        for (source, _, _) in &snapshots {\\\\n   380\\\\t            obj.remove(source);\\\\n   381\\\\t        }\\\\n   382\\\\t\\\\n   383\\\\t        // 3. Insert all target keys (with values from snapshots).\\\\n   384\\\\t        for (_, target, val) in snapshots {\\\\n   385\\\\t            if let Some(v) = val {\\\\n   386\\\\t                obj.insert(target, v);\\\\n   387\\\\t            }\\\\n   388\\\\t            // AC-4.7: nonexistent source → silently ignored\\\\n   389\\\\t        }\\\\n   390\\\\t    }\\\\n   391\\\\t\\\\n   392\\\\t    // Apply vars overrides.\\\\n   393\\\\t    if let Some(ref overrides) = step.vars {\\\\n   394\\\\t        let obj = vars.as_object_mut().unwrap();\\\\n   395\\\\t        for (key, val) in overrides {\\\\n   396\\\\t            obj.insert(key.clone(), val.clone());\\\\n   397\\\\t        }\\\\n   398\\\\t    }\\\\n   399\\\\t\\\\n   400\\\\t    Ok(vars)\\\\n   401\\\\t}\\\\n trimmed.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\\\\\\\"false\\\\\\\") || trimmed == \\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\" {\\\\n   412\\\\t        Ok(false)\\\\n   413\\\\t    } else {\\\\n   414\\\\t        Ok(true)\\\\n   415\\\\t    }\\\\n   416\\\\t}\\\\n   417\\\\t\\\\n   418\\\\t// ── Workflow execution ───────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   419\\\\t\\\\n   420\\\\tpub fn execute_workflow(\\\\n   421\\\\t provided variables in the given context.\\\\n   549\\\\t/// Returns Ok(results) on success, or Err((error, partial_results)) on failure.\\\\n   550\\\\t#[allow(clippy::result_large_err)]\\\\n   551\\\\tpub fn run_recipe(\\\\n   552\\\\t    recipe: &Recipe,\\\\n   553\\\\t    vars: &Value,\\\\n   554\\\\t    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\n   555\\\\t    verbose: bool,\\\\n   556\\\\t) -> 570\\\\t            FileOp::Create { to, .. } => {\\\\n   571\\\\t                renderer::render_path_template(&env, to, vars, &format!(\\\\\\\"files[{}].to\\\\\\\", i))\\\\n   572\\\\t            }\\\\n   573\\\\t            FileOp::Inject { inject, .. } => {\\\\n   574\\\\t                renderer::render_path_template(&env, inject, vars, &format!(\\\\\\\"files[{}].inject\\\\\\\", i))\\\\n   575\\\\t            }\\\\n   576\\\\t            FileOp::Replace { replace, .. } => {\\\\n   577\\\\t                renderer::render_path_template(&env, replace, vars, renderer::render_path_template(&env, patch, vars, &format!(\\\\\\\"files[{}].patch\\\\\\\", i))\\\\n   581\\\\t            }\\\\n   582\\\\t        }\\\\n   583\\\\t        .map_err(|e| (e, vec![]))?;\\\\n   584\\\\t\\\\n   585\\\\t        let rendered_skip_if = match file_op {\\\\n   586\\\\t            FileOp::Inject { skip_if: Some(expr), .. }\\\\n   587\\\\t            | FileOp::Patch { skip_if: Some(expr), .. } => {\\\\n   588\\\\t                Some(\\\\n   589\\\\t                    renderer::render_path_template(&env, expr, vars, &format!(\\\\\\\"files[{}].skip_if\\\\\\\", i))\\\\n   590\\\\t                        .map_err(|e| (e, vec![]))?,\\\\n   591\\\\t                )\\\\n   592\\\\t            }\\\\n   593\\\\t operations in order, fail-fast.\\\\n   605\\\\t    let mut results = Vec::with_capacity(prepared_ops.len());\\\\n   606\\\\t    for prepared in &prepared_ops {\\\\n   607\\\\t        let result = operations::execute_operation(prepared, ctx, verbose);\\\\n   608\\\\t        let is_err = result.is_error();\\\\n   609\\\\t        results.push(result);\\\\n   610\\\\t        if is_err {\\\\n   611\\\\t            // Extract the error from the last result.\\\\n   612\\\\t            if let Some(jig_err) = operations::op_error_to_jig_error(results.last().unwrap()) {\\\\n   613\\\\t file_op.template(), &vars) {\\\\n   345\\\\t            Ok(c) => c,\\\\n   346\\\\t            Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\\\\n   347\\\\t        };\\\\n   348\\\\t\\\\n   349\\\\t handle_early_error(e),\\\\n   361\\\\t                }\\\\n   362\\\\t            }\\\\n   363\\\\t            recipe::FileOp::Replace { replace, .. } => {\\\\n   364\\\\t                match renderer::render_path_template(&env, replace, &vars, &format!(\\\\\\\"files[{}].replace\\\\\\\", i)) {\\\\n   365\\\\t                    Ok(p) => p,\\\\n   366\\\\t                    Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\\\\n   367\\\\t                }\\\\n   368\\\\t            }\\\\n   369\\\\t            recipe::FileOp::Patch { patch, .. } => {\\\\n   370\\\\t                match renderer::render_path_template(&env, patch, &vars, &format!(\\\\\\\"files[{}].patch\\\\\\\", i)) {\\\\n   371\\\\t                    Ok(p) => p,\\\\n   372\\\\t                    Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\\\\n   373\\\\t                }\\\\n   374\\\\t            }\\\\n   375\\\\t        };\\\\n   376\\\\t\\\\n   377\\\\t        // Render skip_if for inject and patch operations.\\\\n   378\\\\t        let rendered_skip_if = match file_op {\\\\n   379\\\\t            recipe::FileOp::Inject { skip_if: Some(skip_if_expr), .. }\\\\n   380\\\\t            | recipe::FileOp::Patch { skip_if: Some(skip_if_expr), .. } => {\\\\n   381\\\\t                match renderer::render_path_template(\\\\n   382\\\\t                    &env, skip_if_expr, &vars, &format!(\\\\\\\"files[{}].skip_if\\\\\\\", i),\\\\n   383\\\\t                ) {\\\\n   384\\\\t                    Ok(s) => Some(s),\\\\n   385\\\\t                    Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\\\\n   386\\\\t                }\\\\n   387\\\\t            }\\\\n   388\\\\t            _ => None,\\\\n   389\\\\t        };\\\\n   390\\\\t\\\\n   391\\\\t        prepared_ops.push(operations::PreparedOp {\\\\n   392\\\\t            file_op: file_op.clone(),\\\\n   393\\\\t            rendered_content,\\\\n   394\\\\t            rendered_path,\\\\n   395\\\\t            rendered_skip_if,\\\\n   396\\\\t        });\\\\n   397\\\\t    }\\\\n   398\\\\t\\\\n   399\\\\t    // 4. Validate base_dir exists (AC-4.10). Done after rendering to respect\\\\n   400\\\\t    // pipeline stage ordering: recipe(1) → variables(4) → rendering(2) → file ops(3) (AC-N5.2).\\\\n   401\\\\t    let resolved_base = match base_dir {\\\\n   402\\\\t        Some(bd) => {\\\\n   403\\\\t            if !bd.is_dir() {\\\\n   404\\\\t                return handle_early_error(JigError::FileOperation(crate::error::StructuredError {\\\\n   405\\\\t                    what: format!(\\\\\\\"base directory does not exist: '{}'\\\\\\\", bd.display()),\\\\n   406\\\\t                    where_: bd.display().to_string(),\\\\n   407\\\\t                    why: \\\\\\\"the specified --base-dir path is not an existing directory\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   408\\\\t                    hint: \\\\\\\"create the directory first, or omit --base-dir to use the current directory\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   409\\\\t                }));\\\\n   410\\\\t            }\\\\n   411\\\\t            bd.to_path_buf()\\\\n   412\\\\t        }\\\\n   413\\\\t        None => std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from(\\\\\\\".\\\\\\\")),\\\\n   414\\\\t    };\\\\n   415\\\\t\\\\n   416\\\\t    // 5. Execute operations in declaration order, fail-fast on error.\\\\n   417\\\\t    let mut ctx = operations::ExecutionContext::new(resolved_base, dry_run, force);\\\\n   418\\\\t    let mut results: Vec<operations::OpResult> = Vec::with_capacity(prepared_ops.len());\\\\n   419\\\\t\\\\n   420\\\\t    for prepared in &prepared_ops {\\\\n   421\\\\t        let result = operations::execute_operation(prepared, &mut ctx, verbose);\\\\n   422\\\\t        let is_err = result.is_error();\\\\n   423\\\\t        results.push(result);\\\\n   424\\\\t        if is_err {\\\\n   425\\\\t            break; // Fail-fast: stop on first error (AC-6.10).\\\\n   426\\\\t        }\\\\n   427\\\\t    }\\\\n   428\\\\t\\\\n   429\\\\t    // 6. Format and emit output.\\\\n   430\\\\t    let has_error = results.iter().any(|r| r.is_error());\\\\n   431\\\\t\\\\n   432\\\\t    match mode {\\\\n   433\\\\t        output::OutputMode::Json => {\\\\n   434\\\\t            let json = output::format_json(&results, dry_run, verbose);\\\\n   435\\\\t            println!(\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap());\\\\n   436\\\\t        }\\\\n   437\\\\t        output::OutputMode::Human => {\\\\n   438\\\\t            if !quiet {\\\\n   439\\\\t                output::format_human(&results, dry_run, verbose);\\\\n   440\\\\t            }\\\\n   441\\\\t        }\\\\n   442\\\\t    }\\\\n   443\\\\t\\\\n   444\\\\t    // 7. Return appropriate exit code.\\\\n   445\\\\t    // Error details are already in the JSON/human output above, so return Ok\\\\n   446\\\\t    // with the exit code to avoid duplicate stderr output from main().\\\\n   447\\\\t    if has_error {\\\\n   448\\\\t        if let Some(last) = results.last()\\\\n   449\\\\t            && let Some(jig_err) = operations::op_error_to_jig_error(last) {\\\\n   450\\\\t                return Ok(jig_err.exit_code());\\\\n   451\\\\t            }\\\\n   452\\\\t    }\\\\n   453\\\\t\\\\n   454\\\\t    Ok(0)\\\\n   455\\\\t}\\\\n   456\\\\t\\\\n   457\\\\t#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\\\\n   458\\\\tfn cmd_workflow(\\\\n   459\\\\t    workflow_path: &std::path::Path,\\\\n   460\\\\t    inline_vars: Option<&str>,\\\\n   461\\\\t    vars_file: Option<&std::path::Path>,\\\\n   462\\\\t    vars_stdin: bool,\\\\n   463\\\\t    dry_run: bool,\\\\n   464\\\\t    force_json: bool,\\\\n   465\\\\t    quiet: bool,\\\\n   466\\\\t    force: bool,\\\\n   467\\\\t    base_dir: Option<&std::path::Path>,\\\\n   468\\\\t    verbose: bool,\\\\n   469\\\\t) -> Result<i32, JigError> {\\\\n   470\\\\t    let mode = output::detect_mode(force_json);\\\\n   471\\\\t\\\\n   472\\\\t    // Helper for early errors.\\\\n   473\\\\t    let handle_early_error = |e: JigError| -> Result<i32, JigError> {\\\\n   474\\\\t        let code = e.exit_code();\\\\n   475\\\\t        match mode {\\\\n   476\\\\t            output::OutputMode::Json => {\\\\n   477\\\\t                let se = e.structured_error().clone();\\\\n   478\\\\t                let json = serde_json::json!({\\\\n   479\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"dry_run\\\\\\\": dry_run,\\\\n   480\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"workflow\\\\\\\": serde_json::Value::Null,\\\\n   481\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"on_error\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"stop\\\\\\\",\\\\n   482\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\",\\\\n   483\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"steps\\\\\\\": [{\\\\n   484\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"recipe\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n   485\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\",\\\\n   486\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\": {\\\\n   487\\\\t                            \\\\\\\"what\\\\\\\": se.what,\\\\n   488\\\\t                            \\\\\\\"where\\\\\\\": se.where_,\\\\n   489\\\\t                            \\\\\\\"why\\\\\\\": se.why,\\\\n   490\\\\t                            \\\\\\\"hint\\\\\\\": se.hint,\\\\n   491\\\\t                        },\\\\n   492\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"operations\\\\\\\": [],\\\\n   493\\\\t                    }],\\\\n   494\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"files_written\\\\\\\": [],\\\\n   495\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"files_skipped\\\\\\\": [],\\\\n   496\\\\t                });\\\\n   497\\\\t                println!(\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap());\\\\n   498\\\\t                Ok(code)\\\\n   499\\\\t            }\\\\n   500\\\\t            output::OutputMode::Human => {\\\\n   501\\\\t                if !quiet {\\\\n   502\\\\t                    eprintln!(\\\\\\\"{e}\\\\\\\");\\\\n   503\\\\t                }\\\\n   504\\\\t                Ok(code)\\\\n   505\\\\t            }\\\\n   506\\\\t        }\\\\n   507\\\\t    };\\\\n   508\\\\t\\\\n   509\\\\t    // Guard: check if file is a recipe instead of a workflow.\\\\n   510\\\\t    match workflow::detect_file_type(workflow_path) {\\\\n   511\\\\t        Ok(workflow::FileType::Recipe) => {\\\\n   512\\\\t            return handle_early_error(JigError::RecipeValidation(\\\\n   513\\\\t                crate::error::StructuredError {\\\\n   514\\\\t                    what: \\\\\\\"expected a workflow file, got a recipe file\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   515\\\\t                    where_: workflow_path.display().to_string(),\\\\n   516\\\\t                    why: \\\\\\\"file has 'files' (recipe) instead of 'steps' (workflow)\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   517\\\\t                    hint: \\\\\\\"use 'jig run' to execute recipes, or provide a workflow file with 'steps'\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   518\\\\t                },\\\\n   519\\\\t            ));\\\\n   520\\\\t        }\\\\n   521\\\\t        Ok(workflow::FileType::Workflow) => {} // proceed\\\\n   522\\\\t        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\\\\n   523\\\\t    }\\\\n   524\\\\t\\\\n   525\\\\t    // Load workflow.\\\\n   526\\\\t    let wf = match workflow::load_workflow(workflow_path) {\\\\n   527\\\\t        Ok(w) => w,\\\\n   528\\\\t        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\\\\n   529\\\\t    };\\\\n   530\\\\t\\\\n   531\\\\t    // Collect and validate workflow-level variables.\\\\n   532\\\\t    let provided = match variables::collect_vars(inline_vars, vars_file, vars_stdin) {\\\\n   533\\\\t        Ok(v) => v,\\\\n   534\\\\t        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\\\\n   535\\\\t    };\\\\n   536\\\\t    let vars = match variables::validate_variables(&wf.variables, &provided) {\\\\n   537\\\\t        Ok(v) => v,\\\\n   538\\\\t        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\\\\n   539\\\\t    };\\\\n   540\\\\t\\\\n   541\\\\t    // Validate base_dir exists.\\\\n   542\\\\t    let resolved_base = match base_dir {\\\\n   543\\\\t        Some(bd) => {\\\\n   544\\\\t            if !bd.is_dir() {\\\\n   545\\\\t                return handle_early_error(JigError::FileOperation(crate::error::StructuredError {\\\\n   546\\\\t                    what: format!(\\\\\\\"base directory does not exist: '{}'\\\\\\\", bd.display()),\\\\n   547\\\\t                    where_: bd.display().to_string(),\\\\n   548\\\\t                    why: \\\\\\\"the specified --base-dir path is not an existing directory\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   549\\\\t                    hint: \\\\\\\"create the directory first, or omit --base-dir to use the current directory\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   550\\\\t                }));\\\\n   551\\\\t            }\\\\n   552\\\\t            bd.to_path_buf()\\\\n   553\\\\t        }\\\\n   554\\\\t        None => std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from(\\\\\\\".\\\\\\\")),\\\\n   555\\\\t    };\\\\n   556\\\\t\\\\n   557\\\\t    // Execute workflow.\\\\n   558\\\\t    let mut ctx = operations::ExecutionContext::new(resolved_base, dry_run, force);\\\\n   559\\\\t    let result = workflow::execute_workflow(&wf, vars, &mut ctx, verbose);\\\\n   560\\\\t\\\\n   561\\\\t    // Determine exit code based on step results and their effective on_error modes.\\\\n   562\\\\t    let exit_code = compute_workflow_exit_code(&wf, &result);\\\\n   563\\\\t\\\\n   564\\\\t    // Format output.\\\\n   565\\\\t    match mode {\\\\n   566\\\\t        output::OutputMode::Json => {\\\\n   567\\\\t            let json = output::format_workflow_json(&result, dry_run, verbose, exit_code);\\\\n   568\\\\t            println!(\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap());\\\\n   569\\\\t        }\\\\n   570\\\\t        output::OutputMode::Human => {\\\\n   571\\\\t            if !quiet {\\\\n   572\\\\t                output::format_workflow_human(&result, dry_run, verbose);\\\\n   573\\\\t            }\\\\n   574\\\\t        }\\\\n   575\\\\t    }\\\\n   576\\\\t\\\\n   577\\\\t    Ok(exit_code)\\\\n   578\\\\t}\\\\n   579\\\\t\\\\n   580\\\\t/// Compute the exit code for a completed workflow based on step results\\\\n   581\\\\t/// and their effective on_error modes.\\\\n   582\\\\tfn compute_workflow_exit_code(\\\\n   583\\\\t    wf: &workflow::Workflow,\\\\n   584\\\\t    result: &workflow::WorkflowResult,\\\\n   585\\\\t) -> i32 {\\\\n   586\\\\t    let mut has_report_error = false;\\\\n   587\\\\t\\\\n   588\\\\t    for (i, step_result) in result.steps.iter().enumerate() {\\\\n   589\\\\t        if let workflow::StepResult::Error { error, .. } = step_result {\\\\n   590\\\\t            let effective_mode = wf\\\\n   591\\\\t                .steps\\\\n   592\\\\t                .get(i)\\\\n   593\\\\t                .and_then(|s| s.on_error)\\\\n   594\\\\t                .unwrap_or(wf.on_error);\\\\n   595\\\\t\\\\n   596\\\\t            match effective_mode {\\\\n   597\\\\t                workflow::OnError::Stop => {\\\\n   598\\\\t                    // This step halted execution — return its error code.\\\\n   599\\\\t                    return error.exit_code();\\\\n   600\\\\t                }\\\\n   601\\\\t                workflow::OnError::Continue => {\\\\n   602\\\\t                    // Tolerated — no effect on exit code.\\\\n   603\\\\t                }\\\\n   604\\\\t                workflow::OnError::Report => {\\\\n   605\\\\t                    has_report_error = true;\\\\n   606\\\\t                }\\\\n   607\\\\t            }\\\\n   608\\\\t        }\\\\n   609\\\\t    }\\\\n   610\\\\t\\\\n   611\\\\t    if has_report_error {\\\\n   612\\\\t        3\\\\n   613\\\\t    } else {\\\\n   614\\\\t        0\\\\n   615\\\\t    }\\\\n   616\\\\t}\\\\n   617\\\\t\\\\n   618\\\\tfn build_validate_json(recipe: &Recipe) -> serde_json::Value {\\\\n   619\\\\t    let vars = variables::vars_json(&recipe.variables);\\\\n   620\\\\t\\\\n   621\\\\t    let ops: Vec<serde_json::Value> = recipe.files.iter().map(|op| {\\\\n   622\\\\t        let mut m = serde_json::Map::new();\\\\n   623\\\\t        m.insert(\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\".into(), serde_json::Value::String(op.op_type_str().into()));\\\\n   624\\\\t        match op {\\\\n   625\\\\t            recipe::FileOp::Create { to, .. } => {\\\\n   626\\\\t                m.insert(\\\\\\\"to\\\\\\\".into(), serde_json::Value::String(to.clone()));\\\\n   627\\\\t            }\\\\n   628\\\\t            recipe::FileOp::Inject { inject, .. } => {\\\\n   629\\\\t                m.insert(\\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\".into(), serde_json::Value::String(inject.clone()));\\\\n   630\\\\t            }\\\\n   631\\\\t            recipe::FileOp::Replace { replace, .. } => {\\\\n   632\\\\t                m.insert(\\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\".into(), serde_json::Value::String(replace.clone()));\\\\n   633\\\\t            }\\\\n   634\\\\t            recipe::FileOp::Patch { patch, .. } => {\\\\n   635\\\\t                m.insert(\\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\".into(), serde_json::Value::String(patch.clone()));\\\\n   636\\\\t            }\\\\n   637\\\\t        }\\\\n   638\\\\t        serde_json::Value::Object(m)\\\\n   639\\\\t    }).collect();\\\\n   640\\\\t\\\\n   641\\\\t    serde_json::json!({\\\\n   642\\\\t        \\\\\\\"valid\\\\\\\": true,\\\\n   643\\\\t        \\\\\\\"name\\\\\\\": recipe.name,\\\\n   644\\\\t        \\\\\\\"description\\\\\\\": recipe.description,\\\\n   645\\\\t        \\\\\\\"variables\\\\\\\": vars,\\\\n   646\\\\t 0usize;\\\\n   652\\\\t    let mut inject_count = 0usize;\\\\n   653\\\\t    let mut replace_count = 0usize;\\\\n   654\\\\t    let mut patch_count = 0usize;\\\\n   655\\\\t    for op in &recipe.files {\\\\n   656\\\\t        match op {\\\\n   657\\\\t            recipe::FileOp::Create { .. } => create_count += 1,\\\\n   658\\\\t            recipe::FileOp::Inject { .. } => inject_count += 1,\\\\n   659\\\\t            recipe::FileOp::Replace { .. } => replace_count += 1,\\\\n   660\\\\t            recipe::FileOp::Patch { .. } => patch_count += 1,\\\\n   661\\\\t        }\\\\n   662\\\\t 667\\\\t    if inject_count > 0 {\\\\n   668\\\\t        parts.push(format!(\\\\\\\"{inject_count} inject\\\\\\\"));\\\\n   669\\\\t    }\\\\n   670\\\\t    if replace_count > 0 {\\\\n   671\\\\t patch\\\\\\\"));\\\\n   675\\\\t    }\\\\n   676\\\\t    if parts.is_empty() {\\\\n   677\\\\t        \\\\\\\"none\\\\\\\".to_string()\\\\n   678\\\\t    } else {\\\\n   679\\\\t        parts.join(\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\")\\\\n   680\\\\t    }\\\\n   681\\\\t}\\\\n   682\\\\t\\\\n   683\\\\t#[cfg(test)]\\\\n   684\\\\tmod tests {\\\\n   685\\\\t    use super::*;\\\\n   686\\\\t    use
