'1,360p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"     1\\\\tuse std::io::IsTerminal;\\\\n     2\\\\t\\\\n     3\\\\tuse indexmap::IndexSet;\\\\n     4\\\\tuse owo_colors::OwoColorize;\\\\n     5\\\\tuse serde_json::Value;\\\\n     6\\\\t\\\\n     7\\\\tuse crate::operations::OpResult;\\\\n     8\\\\tuse crate::variables;\\\\n     9\\\\tuse crate::workflow;\\\\n    10\\\\t\\\\n    11\\\\t// ── Output mode ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n    12\\\\t\\\\n    13\\\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\\\\n    14\\\\tpub enum OutputMode {\\\\n    15\\\\t    /// JSON to stdout (piped or --json).\\\\n    16\\\\t    Json,\\\\n    17\\\\t    /// Human-readable colored text to stderr.\\\\n    18\\\\t    Human,\\\\n    19\\\\t}\\\\n    20\\\\t\\\\n    21\\\\t/// Determine output mode from flags and TTY detection.\\\\n    22\\\\t/// --json forces JSON. Otherwise: piped → JSON, TTY → Human.\\\\n    23\\\\tpub fn detect_mode(force_json: bool) -> OutputMode {\\\\n    24\\\\t    if force_json || !std::io::stdout().is_terminal() {\\\\n    25\\\\t        OutputMode::Json\\\\n    26\\\\t    } else {\\\\n    27\\\\t        OutputMode::Human\\\\n    28\\\\t    }\\\\n    29\\\\t}\\\\n    30\\\\t\\\\n    31\\\\t// ── JSON output ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n    32\\\\t\\\\n    33\\\\t/// Build the full JSON output object for `jig run`.\\\\n    34\\\\tpub fn format_json(results: &[OpResult], dry_run: bool, verbose: bool) -> Value {\\\\n    35\\\\t    let operations: Vec<Value> = results.iter().map(|r| op_result_to_json(r, verbose)).collect();\\\\n    36\\\\t    let (files_written, files_skipped) = compute_file_summaries(results);\\\\n    37\\\\t\\\\n    38\\\\t    serde_json::json!({\\\\n    39\\\\t        \\\\\\\"dry_run\\\\\\\": dry_run,\\\\n    40\\\\t        \\\\\\\"operations\\\\\\\": operations,\\\\n    41\\\\t        \\\\\\\"files_written\\\\\\\": files_written.into_iter().collect::<Vec<_>>(),\\\\n    42\\\\t        \\\\\\\"files_skipped\\\\\\\": files_skipped.into_iter().collect::<Vec<_>>(),\\\\n    43\\\\t    })\\\\n    44\\\\t}\\\\n    45\\\\t\\\\n    46\\\\tpub(crate) fn op_result_to_json(result: &OpResult, verbose: bool) -> Value {\\\\n    47\\\\t    match result {\\\\n    48\\\\t        OpResult::Success { action, path, lines, location, rendered_content, scope_diagnostics } => {\\\\n    49\\\\t            let mut obj = serde_json::json!({\\\\n    50\\\\t                \\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": action,\\\\n    51\\\\t                \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\": path.display().to_string(),\\\\n    52\\\\t                \\\\\\\"lines\\\\\\\": lines,\\\\n    53\\\\t            });\\\\n    54\\\\t            if let Some(loc) = location {\\\\n    55\\\\t                obj[\\\\\\\"location\\\\\\\"] = Value::String(loc.clone());\\\\n    56\\\\t            }\\\\n    57\\\\t            if verbose\\\\n    58\\\\t                && let Some(content) = rendered_content {\\\\n    59\\\\t                    obj[\\\\\\\"rendered_content\\\\\\\"] = Value::String(content.clone());\\\\n    60\\\\t                }\\\\n    61\\\\t            if verbose\\\\n    62\\\\t                && let Some(diag) = scope_diagnostics {\\\\n    63\\\\t                    let mut diag_obj = serde_json::json!({\\\\n    64\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"anchor_line\\\\\\\": diag.anchor_line,\\\\n    65\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"scope_start\\\\\\\": diag.scope_start,\\\\n    66\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"scope_end\\\\\\\": diag.scope_end,\\\\n    67\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"insertion_line\\\\\\\": diag.insertion_line,\\\\n    68\\\\t                    });\\\\n    69\\\\t                    if let Some(fl) = diag.find_match_line {\\\\n    70\\\\t                        diag_obj[\\\\\\\"find_match_line\\\\\\\"] = Value::Number(fl.into());\\\\n    71\\\\t                    }\\\\n    72\\\\t                    if let Some((ref from, ref to)) = diag.position_fallback {\\\\n    73\\\\t                        diag_obj[\\\\\\\"position_fallback\\\\\\\"] = serde_json::json!({\\\\n    74\\\\t                            \\\\\\\"from\\\\\\\": from,\\\\n    75\\\\t                            \\\\\\\"to\\\\\\\": to,\\\\n    76\\\\t                        });\\\\n    77\\\\t                    }\\\\n    78\\\\t                    obj[\\\\\\\"scope_diagnostics\\\\\\\"] = diag_obj;\\\\n    79\\\\t                }\\\\n    80\\\\t            obj\\\\n    81\\\\t        }\\\\n    82\\\\t        OpResult::Skip { path, reason, rendered_content } => {\\\\n    83\\\\t            let mut obj = serde_json::json!({\\\\n    84\\\\t                \\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\",\\\\n    85\\\\t                \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\": path.display().to_string(),\\\\n    86\\\\t                \\\\\\\"reason\\\\\\\": reason,\\\\n    87\\\\t            });\\\\n    88\\\\t            if verbose\\\\n    89\\\\t                && let Some(content) = rendered_content {\\\\n    90\\\\t                    obj[\\\\\\\"rendered_content\\\\\\\"] = Value::String(content.clone());\\\\n    91\\\\t                }\\\\n    92\\\\t            obj\\\\n    93\\\\t        }\\\\n    94\\\\t        OpResult::Error { path, error, rendered_content } => {\\\\n    95\\\\t            serde_json::json!({\\\\n    96\\\\t                \\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\",\\\\n    97\\\\t                \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\": path.display().to_string(),\\\\n    98\\\\t                \\\\\\\"what\\\\\\\": error.what,\\\\n    99\\\\t                \\\\\\\"where\\\\\\\": error.where_,\\\\n   100\\\\t                \\\\\\\"why\\\\\\\": error.why,\\\\n   101\\\\t                \\\\\\\"hint\\\\\\\": error.hint,\\\\n   102\\\\t                \\\\\\\"rendered_content\\\\\\\": rendered_content,\\\\n   103\\\\t            })\\\\n   104\\\\t        }\\\\n   105\\\\t    }\\\\n   106\\\\t}\\\\n   107\\\\t\\\\n   108\\\\t/// Compute files_written and files_skipped arrays.\\\\n   109\\\\t/// A path appears in files_written if any operation wrote to it.\\\\n   110\\\\t/// A path appears in files_skipped only if ALL operations targeting it were skipped.\\\\n   111\\\\t/// Paths appear in order of first encounter.\\\\n   112\\\\tpub(crate) fn compute_file_summaries(results: &[OpResult]) -> (IndexSet<String>, IndexSet<String>) {\\\\n   113\\\\t    // Track encounter order, writes, and errors to compute summaries.\\\\n   114\\\\t    let mut encounter_order: IndexSet<String> = IndexSet::new();\\\\n   115\\\\t    let mut was_written: std::collections::HashSet<String> = std::collections::HashSet::new();\\\\n   116\\\\t    let mut had_error: std::collections::HashSet<String> = std::collections::HashSet::new();\\\\n   117\\\\t\\\\n   118\\\\t    for result in results {\\\\n   119\\\\t        let path_str = result.path().display().to_string();\\\\n   120\\\\t        encounter_order.insert(path_str.clone());\\\\n   121\\\\t\\\\n   122\\\\t        match result {\\\\n   123\\\\t            OpResult::Success { .. } => {\\\\n   124\\\\t                was_written.insert(path_str);\\\\n   125\\\\t            }\\\\n   126\\\\t            OpResult::Error { .. } => {\\\\n   127\\\\t                had_error.insert(path_str);\\\\n   128\\\\t            }\\\\n   129\\\\t            OpResult::Skip { .. } => {}\\\\n   130\\\\t        }\\\\n   131\\\\t    }\\\\n   132\\\\t\\\\n   133\\\\t    let mut written: IndexSet<String> = IndexSet::new();\\\\n   134\\\\t    let mut skipped: IndexSet<String> = IndexSet::new();\\\\n   135\\\\t\\\\n   136\\\\t    for path in &encounter_order {\\\\n   137\\\\t        if was_written.contains(path) {\\\\n   138\\\\t            written.insert(path.clone());\\\\n   139\\\\t        } else if !had_error.contains(path) {\\\\n   140\\\\t            // Only count as skipped if ALL operations were skips (no writes, no errors).\\\\n   141\\\\t            skipped.insert(path.clone());\\\\n   142\\\\t        }\\\\n   143\\\\t    }\\\\n   144\\\\t\\\\n   145\\\\t    (written, skipped)\\\\n   146\\\\t}\\\\n   147\\\\t\\\\n   148\\\\t/// Same as compute_file_summaries but takes references (for workflow aggregation).\\\\n   149\\\\tfn compute_file_summaries_from_refs(results: &[&OpResult]) -> (IndexSet<String>, IndexSet<String>) {\\\\n   150\\\\t    let mut encounter_order: IndexSet<String> = IndexSet::new();\\\\n   151\\\\t    let mut was_written: std::collections::HashSet<String> = std::collections::HashSet::new();\\\\n   152\\\\t    let mut had_error: std::collections::HashSet<String> = std::collections::HashSet::new();\\\\n   153\\\\t\\\\n   154\\\\t    for result in results {\\\\n   155\\\\t        let path_str = result.path().display().to_string();\\\\n   156\\\\t        encounter_order.insert(path_str.clone());\\\\n   157\\\\t\\\\n   158\\\\t        match result {\\\\n   159\\\\t            OpResult::Success { .. } => {\\\\n   160\\\\t                was_written.insert(path_str);\\\\n   161\\\\t            }\\\\n   162\\\\t            OpResult::Error { .. } => {\\\\n   163\\\\t                had_error.insert(path_str);\\\\n   164\\\\t            }\\\\n   165\\\\t            OpResult::Skip { .. } => {}\\\\n   166\\\\t        }\\\\n   167\\\\t    }\\\\n   168\\\\t\\\\n   169\\\\t    let mut written: IndexSet<String> = IndexSet::new();\\\\n   170\\\\t    let mut skipped: IndexSet<String> = IndexSet::new();\\\\n   171\\\\t\\\\n   172\\\\t    for path in &encounter_order {\\\\n   173\\\\t        if was_written.contains(path) {\\\\n   174\\\\t            written.insert(path.clone());\\\\n   175\\\\t        } else if !had_error.contains(path) {\\\\n   176\\\\t            skipped.insert(path.clone());\\\\n   177\\\\t        }\\\\n   178\\\\t    }\\\\n   179\\\\t\\\\n   180\\\\t    (written, skipped)\\\\n   181\\\\t}\\\\n   182\\\\t\\\\n   183\\\\t// ─�� Human output ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   184\\\\t\\\\n   185\\\\t/// Write human-readable output to stderr.\\\\n   186\\\\tpub fn format_human(results: &[OpResult], dry_run: bool, verbose: bool) {\\\\n   187\\\\t    if dry_run {\\\\n   188\\\\t        eprintln!(\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"(dry run)\\\\\\\".dimmed());\\\\n   189\\\\t    }\\\\n   190\\\\t\\\\n   191\\\\t    for result in results {\\\\n   192\\\\t        match result {\\\\n   193\\\\t            OpResult::Success { action, path, lines, location, rendered_content, scope_diagnostics } => {\\\\n   194\\\\t                let action_str = action.to_string();\\\\n   195\\\\t                eprint!(\\\\\\\"  {} {}\\\\\\\", action_str.green(), path.display());\\\\n   196\\\\t                if let Some(loc) = location {\\\\n   197\\\\t                    eprint!(\\\\\\\" ({})\\\\\\\", loc);\\\\n   198\\\\t                }\\\\n   199\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\\\\" ({} lines)\\\\\\\", lines);\\\\n   200\\\\t                if verbose {\\\\n   201\\\\t                    if let Some(content) = rendered_content {\\\\n   202\\\\t                        for line in content.lines() {\\\\n   203\\\\t                            eprintln!(\\\\\\\"    {}\\\\\\\", line.dimmed());\\\\n   204\\\\t                        }\\\\n   205\\\\t                    }\\\\n   206\\\\t                    if let Some(diag) = scope_diagnostics {\\\\n   207\\\\t                        eprintln!(\\\\\\\"    {} anchor={} scope={}-{} insert={}{}\\\\\\\",\\\\n   208\\\\t                            \\\\\\\"diagnostics:\\\\\\\".dimmed(),\\\\n   209\\\\t                            diag.anchor_line, diag.scope_start, diag.scope_end,\\\\n   210\\\\t                            diag.insertion_line,\\\\n   211\\\\t                            diag.find_match_line.map(|l| format!(\\\\\\\" find={}\\\\\\\", l)).unwrap_or_default(),\\\\n   212\\\\t                        );\\\\n   213\\\\t                    }\\\\n   214\\\\t                }\\\\n   215\\\\t            }\\\\n   216\\\\t            OpResult::Skip { path, reason, rendered_content } => {\\\\n   217\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\\\\"  {} {} — {}\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\".yellow(), path.display(), reason);\\\\n   218\\\\t                if verbose\\\\n   219\\\\t                    && let Some(content) = rendered_content {\\\\n   220\\\\t                        for line in content.lines() {\\\\n   221\\\\t                            eprintln!(\\\\\\\"    {}\\\\\\\", line.dimmed());\\\\n   222\\\\t                        }\\\\n   223\\\\t                    }\\\\n   224\\\\t            }\\\\n   225\\\\t            OpResult::Error { path, error, rendered_content } => {\\\\n   226\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\\\\"  {} {}\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\".red(), path.display());\\\\n   227\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\\\\"    what: {}\\\\\\\", error.what);\\\\n   228\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\\\\"    where: {}\\\\\\\", error.where_);\\\\n   229\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\\\\"    why: {}\\\\\\\", error.why);\\\\n   230\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\\\\"    hint: {}\\\\\\\", error.hint);\\\\n   231\\\\t                if verbose || !rendered_content.is_empty() {\\\\n   232\\\\t                    eprintln!(\\\\\\\"    rendered content ({} lines):\\\\\\\", rendered_content.lines().count());\\\\n   233\\\\t                    for line in rendered_content.lines() {\\\\n   234\\\\t                        eprintln!(\\\\\\\"      {}\\\\\\\", line.dimmed());\\\\n   235\\\\t                    }\\\\n   236\\\\t                }\\\\n   237\\\\t            }\\\\n   238\\\\t        }\\\\n   239\\\\t    }\\\\n   240\\\\t\\\\n   241\\\\t    let (written, skipped) = compute_file_summaries(results);\\\\n   242\\\\t    if !written.is_empty() || !skipped.is_empty() {\\\\n   243\\\\t        eprintln!();\\\\n   244\\\\t        if !written.is_empty() {\\\\n   245\\\\t            eprintln!(\\\\n   246\\\\t                \\\\\\\"{}: {}\\\\\\\",\\\\n   247\\\\t                if dry_run { \\\\\\\"would write\\\\\\\" } else { \\\\\\\"wrote\\\\\\\" },\\\\n   248\\\\t                written.len()\\\\n   249\\\\t            );\\\\n   250\\\\t        }\\\\n   251\\\\t        if !skipped.is_empty() {\\\\n   252\\\\t            eprintln!(\\\\\\\"skipped: {}\\\\\\\", skipped.len());\\\\n   253\\\\t        }\\\\n   254\\\\t    }\\\\n   255\\\\t}\\\\n   256\\\\t\\\\n   257\\\\t// ── Workflow validation JSON ──────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   258\\\\t\\\\n   259\\\\tpub fn build_workflow_validate_json(validation: &workflow::WorkflowValidation) -> Value {\\\\n   260\\\\t    let vars = variables::vars_json(&validation.variables);\\\\n   261\\\\t\\\\n   262\\\\t    let steps: Vec<Value> = validation\\\\n   263\\\\t        .steps\\\\n   264\\\\t        .iter()\\\\n   265\\\\t step.valid,\\\\n   269\\\\t                \\\\\\\"conditional\\\\\\\": step.conditional,\\\\n   270\\\\t            });\\\\n   271\\\\t            if let Some(ref when) = step.when {\\\\n   272\\\\t obj[\\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\"] = Value::String(err.clone());\\\\n   276\\\\t            }\\\\n   277\\\\t            obj\\\\n   278\\\\t        })\\\\n   279\\\\t        .collect();\\\\n   280\\\\t\\\\n   281\\\\t    serde_json::json!({\\\\n   282\\\\t        \\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"workflow\\\\\\\",\\\\n   283\\\\t        \\\\\\\"valid\\\\\\\": true,\\\\n   284\\\\t        \\\\\\\"name\\\\\\\": validation.name,\\\\n   285\\\\t        \\\\\\\"description\\\\\\\": validation.description,\\\\n   286\\\\t        \\\\\\\"variables\\\\\\\": vars,\\\\n   287\\\\t        \\\\\\\"steps\\\\\\\": steps,\\\\n   288\\\\t    })\\\\n   289\\\\t}\\\\n   290\\\\t\\\\n   291\\\\t// ── Workflow execution JSON ──────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   292\\\\t\\\\n   293\\\\tpub fn format_workflow_json(\\\\n   294\\\\t    result: &workflow::WorkflowResult,\\\\n   295\\\\t    dry_run: bool,\\\\n   296\\\\t    verbose: bool,\\\\n   297\\\\t    exit_code: i32,\\\\n   298\\\\t) -> Value {\\\\n   299\\\\t    let has_errors = result.steps.iter().any(|s| s.is_error());\\\\n   300\\\\t    let status = if !has_errors || exit_code == 0 {\\\\n   301\\\\t 305\\\\t        \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\"\\\\n   306\\\\t    };\\\\n   307\\\\t\\\\n   308\\\\t    let mut all_ops: Vec<&OpResult> = Vec::new();\\\\n   309\\\\t    let steps: Vec<Value> = result\\\\n   310\\\\t        .steps\\\\n   311\\\\t        .iter()\\\\n   312\\\\t        .map(|step| match step {\\\\n   313\\\\t            workflow::StepResult::Success { recipe, operations } => {\\\\n   314\\\\t                let ops_json: Vec<Value> = operations\\\\n   315\\\\t                    .iter()\\\\n   316\\\\t                    .map(|r| op_result_to_json(r, verbose))\\\\n   317\\\\t                    .collect();\\\\n   318\\\\t                let (written, skipped) = compute_file_summaries(operations);\\\\n   319\\\\t                all_ops.extend(operations.iter());\\\\n   320\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"operations\\\\\\\": ops_json,\\\\n   324\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"files_written\\\\\\\": written.into_iter().collect::<Vec<_>>(),\\\\n   325\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"files_skipped\\\\\\\": skipped.into_iter().collect::<Vec<_>>(),\\\\n   326\\\\t                })\\\\n   327\\\\t            }\\\\n   328\\\\t            workflow::StepResult::Skipped { recipe, reason } => {\\\\n   329\\\\t                serde_json::json!({\\\\n   330\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"recipe\\\\\\\": recipe,\\\\n   331\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"skipped\\\\\\\",\\\\n   332\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"reason\\\\\\\": reason,\\\\n   333\\\\t                })\\\\n   334\\\\t            }\\\\n   335\\\\t            workflow::StepResult::Error {\\\\n   336\\\\t                recipe,\\\\n   337\\\\t                error,\\\\n   338\\\\t                operations,\\\\n   339\\\\t                rendered_content,\\\\n   340\\\\t            } => {\\\\n   341\\\\t                let ops_json: Vec<Value> = operations\\\\n   342\\\\t                    .iter()\\\\n   343\\\\t error.structured_error();\\\\n   347\\\\t                let mut obj = serde_json::json!({\\\\n   348\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"recipe\\\\\\\": recipe,\\\\n   349\\\\t rendered_content {\\\\n   359\\\\t                    obj[\\\\\\\"rendered_content\\\\\\\"] = Value::String(content.clone());\\\\n   360\\\\t                }\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n    19\\t{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_8\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"nl -ba src/output.rs | sed -n '360,620p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n '360,620p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"   360\\\\t                }\\\\n   361\\\\t                obj\\\\n   362\\\\t            }\\\\n   363\\\\t        })\\\\n   364\\\\t result: &workflow::WorkflowResult,\\\\n   384\\\\t    dry_run: bool,\\\\n   385\\\\t    verbose: bool,\\\\n   386\\\\t) {\\\\n   387\\\\t    if dry_run {\\\\n   388\\\\t        eprintln!(\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"(dry run)\\\\\\\".dimmed());\\\\n   389\\\\t    }\\\\n   390\\\\t\\\\n   391\\\\t    let total = result.steps.len();\\\\n   392\\\\t    let mut succeeded = 0usize;\\\\n   393\\\\t    let mut skipped = 0usize;\\\\n   394\\\\t    let mut failed = 0usize;\\\\n   395\\\\t\\\\n   396\\\\t    for (i, step) in result.steps.iter().enumerate() {\\\\n   397\\\\t {}/{}: {}\\\\\\\",\\\\n   401\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"Step\\\\\\\".bold(),\\\\n   402\\\\t                    i + 1,\\\\n   403\\\\t                    total,\\\\n   404\\\\t {}\\\\\\\",\\\\n   412\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"Step\\\\\\\".bold(),\\\\n   413\\\\t                    i + 1,\\\\n   414\\\\t                    total,\\\\n   415\\\\t operations,\\\\n   425\\\\t                rendered_content: _,\\\\n   426\\\\t            } => {\\\\n   427\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\n   428\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n{} {}/{}: {} {}\\\\\\\",\\\\n   429\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"Step\\\\\\\".bold(),\\\\n   430\\\\t                    i + 1,\\\\n   431\\\\t                    total,\\\\n   432\\\\t   format_human(operations, false, verbose);\\\\n   437\\\\t                }\\\\n   438\\\\t                let se = error.structured_error();\\\\n   439\\\\t                eprintln!(\\\\\\\" 451\\\\t    );\\\\n   452\\\\t}\\\\n   453\\\\t\\\\n   454\\\\t// ── Tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   455\\\\t\\\\n   456\\\\t#[cfg(test)]\\\\n   457\\\\tmod tests {\\\\n   458\\\\t    use std::path::PathBuf;\\\\n   459\\\\t\\\\n   460\\\\t    use super::*;\\\\n   461\\\\t    use crate::error::StructuredError;\\\\n   462\\\\t\\\\n   463\\\\t    fn success_result(path: &str, lines: usize) -> OpResult {\\\\n   464\\\\t        OpResult::Success {\\\\n   465\\\\t            action: \\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\",\\\\n   466\\\\t            path: PathBuf::from(path),\\\\n   467\\\\t            lines,\\\\n   468\\\\t            location: None,\\\\n   469\\\\t            rendered_content: Some(\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\".into()),\\\\n   470\\\\t            scope_diagnostics: None,\\\\n   471\\\\t        }\\\\n   472\\\\t    }\\\\n   473\\\\t\\\\n   474\\\\t    fn skip_result(path: &str, reason: &str) -> OpResult {\\\\n   475\\\\t          why: \\\\\\\"conflict\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   489\\\\t                hint: \\\\\\\"use --force\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   490\\\\t            },\\\\n   491\\\\t            rendered_content: \\\\\\\"rendered\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   492\\\\t        }\\\\n   493\\\\t    }\\\\n   494\\\\t\\\\n   495\\\\t    // ── AC-6.3: --json forces JSON output ──\\\\n   496\\\\t\\\\n   497\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   498\\\\t    fn ac_6_3_force_json() {\\\\n   499\\\\t        let mode = detect_mode(true);\\\\n   500\\\\t        assert_eq!(mode, OutputMode::Json);\\\\n   501\\\\t    }\\\\n   502\\\\t\\\\n   503\\\\t    // ── AC-6.5: JSON includes operations array with correct fields ──\\\\n   504\\\\t\\\\n   505\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   506\\\\t    fn ac_6_5_json_operations_array() {\\\\n   507\\\\t        let results = vec![\\\\n   508\\\\t            success_result(\\\\\\\"src/main.rs\\\\\\\", 10),\\\\n   509\\\\t            skip_result(\\\\\\\"src/lib.rs\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"skip_if_exists: true\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   510\\\\t        ];\\\\n   511\\\\t        let json = format_json(&results, false, false);\\\\n   512\\\\t        let ops = json[\\\\\\\"operations\\\\\\\"].as_array().unwrap();\\\\n   513\\\\t        assert_eq!(ops.len(), 2);\\\\n   514\\\\t        assert_eq!(ops[0][\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\"], \\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\");\\\\n   515\\\\t        assert_eq!(ops[0][\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\"], \\\\\\\"src/main.rs\\\\\\\");\\\\n   516\\\\t        assert_eq!(ops[0][\\\\\\\"lines\\\\\\\"], 10);\\\\n   517\\\\t        assert_eq!(ops[1][\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\"], \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\");\\\\n   518\\\\t        assert_eq!(ops[1][\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\"], \\\\\\\"src/lib.rs\\\\\\\");\\\\n   519\\\\t        assert!(ops[1][\\\\\\\"reason\\\\\\\"].as_str().unwrap().contains(\\\\\\\"skip_if_exists\\\\\\\"));\\\\n   520\\\\t    }\\\\n   521\\\\t\\\\n   522\\\\t    // ── AC-6.5: Error operation has all structured error fields ──\\\\n   523\\\\t\\\\n   524\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   525\\\\t    fn ac_6_5_json_error_fields() {\\\\n   526\\\\t        let results = vec![error_result(\\\\\\\"conflict.rs\\\\\\\")];\\\\n   527\\\\t        let json = format_json(&results, false, false);\\\\n   528\\\\t        let op = &json[\\\\\\\"operations\\\\\\\"][0];\\\\n   529\\\\t        assert_eq!(op[\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\"], \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\");\\\\n   530\\\\t        assert!(op[\\\\\\\"what\\\\\\\"].is_string());\\\\n   531\\\\t 534\\\\t        assert!(op[\\\\\\\"rendered_content\\\\\\\"].is_string());\\\\n   535\\\\t    }\\\\n   536\\\\t\\\\n   537\\\\t    // ── AC-6.6: files_written and files_skipped arrays ──\\\\n   538\\\\t\\\\n   539\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   540\\\\t    fn ac_6_6_file_summaries() {\\\\n   541\\\\t        let results = vec![\\\\n   542\\\\t            success_result(\\\\\\\"a.rs\\\\\\\", 5),\\\\n 27\\t{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_12\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"nl -ba tests/integration.rs | sed -n '1,360p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"     1\\\\t//! Fixture-based integration tests for jig CLI.\\\\n     2\\\\t//!\\\\n     3\\\\t//! Each subdirectory under `tests/fixtures/` is a test case. Adding a new test\\\\n     4\\\\t//! case requires only adding a directory — no code changes.\\\\n     5\\\\t//!\\\\n     6\\\\t//! Fixture layout:\\\\n     7\\\\t//!   recipe.yaml          — recipe to run\\\\n     8\\\\t//!   vars.json            — variables (JSON)\\\\n     9\\\\t//!   templates/           — template files (referenced by recipe)\\\\n    10\\\\t//!   existing/            — pre-existing files, copied to temp dir before run\\\\n    11\\\\t//!   expected/            — expected files after run (diffed against actual)\\\\n    12\\\\t//!   expected_output.json — (optional) expected JSON output structure\\\\n    13\\\\t//!   expected_exit_code   — (optional) expected exit code (default: 0)\\\\n    14\\\\t//!   force                — (optional) if present, pass --force\\\\n    15\\\\t\\\\n    16\\\\tuse std::fs;\\\\n    17\\\\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\\\\n    18\\\\tuse std::process::Command;\\\\n    19\\\\t\\\\n    20\\\\t// ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n    21\\\\t\\\\n    22\\\\tfn jig_bin() -> PathBuf {\\\\n    23\\\\t    PathBuf::from(env!(\\\\\\\"CARGO_BIN_EXE_jig\\\\\\\"))\\\\n    24\\\\t}\\\\n    25\\\\t\\\\n    26\\\\tfn fixtures_dir() -> PathBuf {\\\\n    27\\\\t    PathBuf::from(env!(\\\\\\\"CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR\\\\\\\")).join(\\\\\\\"tests/fixtures\\\\\\\")\\\\n    28\\\\t}\\\\n    29\\\\t\\\\n    30\\\\t/// Discover all fixture directories (immediate children of tests/fixtures/).\\\\n    31\\\\t/// Supports both recipe fixtures (recipe.yaml) and workflow fixtures (workflow.yaml).\\\\n    32\\\\tfn discover_fixtures() -> Vec<PathBuf> {\\\\n    33\\\\t    let dir = fixtures_dir();\\\\n    34\\\\t    let mut fixtures: Vec<PathBuf> = fs::read_dir(&dir)\\\\n    35\\\\t        .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!(\\\\\\\"cannot read fixtures dir {}: {}\\\\\\\", dir.display(), e))\\\\n    36\\\\t        .filter_map(|entry| {\\\\n    37\\\\t            let entry = entry.ok()?;\\\\n    38\\\\t            let path = entry.path();\\\\n    39\\\\t            if path.is_dir()\\\\n    40\\\\t                && (path.join(\\\\\\\"recipe.yaml\\\\\\\").exists() || path.join(\\\\\\\"workflow.yaml\\\\\\\").exists())\\\\n    41\\\\t            {\\\\n    42\\\\t                Some(path)\\\\n    43\\\\t            } else {\\\\n    44\\\\t                None\\\\n    45\\\\t            }\\\\n    46\\\\t        })\\\\n    47\\\\t        .collect();\\\\n    48\\\\t    fixtures.sort();\\\\n    49\\\\t    fixtures\\\\n    50\\\\t}\\\\n    51\\\\t\\\\n    52\\\\t/// Determine if a fixture is a workflow fixture (has workflow.yaml).\\\\n    53\\\\tfn is_workflow_fixture(fixture: &Path) -> bool {\\\\n    54\\\\t    fixture.join(\\\\\\\"workflow.yaml\\\\\\\").exists()\\\\n    55\\\\t}\\\\n    56\\\\t\\\\n    57\\\\t/// Read expected exit code from fixture (default 0).\\\\n    58\\\\tfn expected_exit_code(fixture: &Path) -> i32 {\\\\n    59\\\\t    let path = fixture.join(\\\\\\\"expected_exit_code\\\\\\\");\\\\n    60\\\\t    if path.exists() {\\\\n    61\\\\t        fs::read_to_string(&path)\\\\n    62\\\\t            .unwrap()\\\\n    63\\\\t            .trim()\\\\n    64\\\\t            .parse()\\\\n    65\\\\t            .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!(\\\\\\\"bad expected_exit_code in {}: {}\\\\\\\", fixture.display(), e))\\\\n    66\\\\t    } else {\\\\n    67\\\\t        0\\\\n    68\\\\t    }\\\\n    69\\\\t}\\\\n    70\\\\t\\\\n    71\\\\t/// Run jig on a fixture, returning (exit_code, stdout, stderr).\\\\n    72\\\\t/// Automatically detects recipe vs workflow fixtures.\\\\n    73\\\\tfn run_fixture(fixture: &Path, work_dir: &Path) -> (i32, String, String) {\\\\n    74\\\\t    let vars_json = fs::read_to_string(fixture.join(\\\\\\\"vars.json\\\\\\\")).unwrap_or_else(|_| \\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\".into());\\\\n    75\\\\t\\\\n    76\\\\t    let mut cmd = Command::new(jig_bin());\\\\n    77\\\\t\\\\n    78\\\\t    if is_workflow_fixture(fixture) {\\\\n    79\\\\t        let workflow = fixture.join(\\\\\\\"workflow.yaml\\\\\\\");\\\\n    80\\\\t        cmd.args([\\\\\\\"workflow\\\\\\\", &workflow.display().to_string()]);\\\\n    81\\\\t    } else {\\\\n    82\\\\t        let recipe = fixture.join(\\\\\\\"recipe.yaml\\\\\\\");\\\\n    83\\\\t        cmd.args([\\\\\\\"run\\\\\\\", &recipe.display().to_string()]);\\\\n    84\\\\t    }\\\\n    85\\\\t\\\\n    86\\\\t    cmd.args([\\\\\\\"--vars\\\\\\\", &vars_json])\\\\n    87\\\\t        .args([\\\\\\\"--base-dir\\\\\\\", &work_dir.display().to_string()])\\\\n    88\\\\t        .arg(\\\\\\\"--json\\\\\\\");\\\\n    89\\\\t\\\\n    90\\\\t    if fixture.join(\\\\\\\"force\\\\\\\").exists() {\\\\n    91\\\\t        cmd.arg(\\\\\\\"--force\\\\\\\");\\\\n    92\\\\t    }\\\\n    93\\\\t\\\\n    94\\\\t    if fixture.join(\\\\\\\"dry_run\\\\\\\").exists() {\\\\n    95\\\\t        cmd.arg(\\\\\\\"--dry-run\\\\\\\");\\\\n    96\\\\t    }\\\\n    97\\\\t\\\\n    98\\\\t    let output = cmd.output().expect(\\\\\\\"failed to run jig\\\\\\\");\\\\n    99\\\\t\\\\n   100\\\\t    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);\\\\n   101\\\\t    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string();\\\\n   102\\\\t    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string();\\\\n   103\\\\t    (code, stdout, stderr)\\\\n   104\\\\t}\\\\n   105\\\\t\\\\n   106\\\\t/// Copy existing/ files into work_dir.\\\\n   107\\\\tfn setup_existing(fixture: &Path, work_dir: &Path) {\\\\n   108\\\\t    let existing = fixture.join(\\\\\\\"existing\\\\\\\");\\\\n   109\\\\t    if existing.is_dir() {\\\\n   110\\\\t        copy_dir_recursive(&existing, work_dir);\\\\n   111\\\\t    }\\\\n   112\\\\t}\\\\n   113\\\\t\\\\n   114\\\\t/// Recursively copy src/ contents into dst/.\\\\n   115\\\\tfn copy_dir_recursive(src: &Path, dst: &Path) {\\\\n   116\\\\t    for entry in fs::read_dir(src).unwrap() {\\\\n   117\\\\t        let entry = entry.unwrap();\\\\n   118\\\\t        let src_path = entry.path();\\\\n   119\\\\t        let dst_path = dst.join(entry.file_name());\\\\n   120\\\\t        if src_path.is_dir() {\\\\n   121\\\\t            fs::create_dir_all(&dst_path).unwrap();\\\\n   122\\\\t            copy_dir_recursive(&src_path, &dst_path);\\\\n   123\\\\t        } else {\\\\n   124\\\\t            if let Some(parent) = dst_path.parent() {\\\\n   125\\\\t                fs::create_dir_all(parent).unwrap();\\\\n   126\\\\t            }\\\\n   127\\\\t            fs::copy(&src_path, &dst_path).unwrap();\\\\n   128\\\\t        }\\\\n   129\\\\t    }\\\\n   130\\\\t}\\\\n   131\\\\t\\\\n   132\\\\t/// Compare work_dir contents against expected/ directory.\\\\n   133\\\\t/// Returns a list of (relative_path, diff_description) for mismatches.\\\\n   134\\\\tfn diff_expected(fixture: &Path, work_dir: &Path) -> Vec<(String, String)> {\\\\n   135\\\\t    let expected = fixture.join(\\\\\\\"expected\\\\\\\");\\\\n   136\\\\t    if !expected.is_dir() {\\\\n   137\\\\t        return vec![];\\\\n   138\\\\t    }\\\\n   139\\\\t\\\\n   140\\\\t    let mut diffs = vec![];\\\\n   141\\\\t    diff_dir_recursive(&expected, work_dir, &expected, &mut diffs);\\\\n   142\\\\t    diffs\\\\n   143\\\\t}\\\\n   144\\\\t\\\\n   145\\\\tfn diff_dir_recursive(\\\\n   146\\\\t    expected_root: &Path,\\\\n   147\\\\t    work_dir: &Path,\\\\n   148\\\\t    current: &Path,\\\\n   149\\\\t    diffs: &mut Vec<(String, String)>,\\\\n   150\\\\t) {\\\\n   151\\\\t    for entry in fs::read_dir(current).unwrap() {\\\\n   152\\\\t        let entry = entry.unwrap();\\\\n   153\\\\t        let path = entry.path();\\\\n   154\\\\t        let rel = path.strip_prefix(expected_root).unwrap();\\\\n   155\\\\t\\\\n   156\\\\t        if path.is_dir() {\\\\n   157\\\\t            diff_dir_recursive(expected_root, work_dir, &path, diffs);\\\\n   158\\\\t        } else {\\\\n   159\\\\t            let actual_path = work_dir.join(rel);\\\\n   160\\\\t            if !actual_path.exists() {\\\\n   161\\\\t                diffs.push((rel.display().to_string(), \\\\\\\"file missing\\\\\\\".into()));\\\\n   162\\\\t                continue;\\\\n   163\\\\t            }\\\\n   164\\\\t            let expected_bytes = fs::read(&path).unwrap();\\\\n   165\\\\t            let actual_bytes = fs::read(&actual_path).unwrap();\\\\n   166\\\\t            if expected_bytes != actual_bytes {\\\\n   167\\\\t                let expected_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&expected_bytes);\\\\n   168\\\\t                let actual_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&actual_bytes);\\\\n   169\\\\t                diffs.push((\\\\n   170\\\\t                    rel.display().to_string(),\\\\n   171\\\\t                    format!(\\\\n   172\\\\t                        \\\\\\\"content mismatch:\\\\\\\\n--- expected\\\\\\\\n+++ actual\\\\\\\\n-{}\\\\\\\\n+{}\\\\\\\",\\\\n   173\\\\t                        expected_str.replace('\\\\\\\\n', \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n-\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   174\\\\t                        actual_str.replace('\\\\\\\\n', \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n+\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   175\\\\t                    ),\\\\n   176\\\\t                ));\\\\n   177\\\\t            }\\\\n   178\\\\t        }\\\\n   179\\\\t    }\\\\n   180\\\\t}\\\\n   181\\\\t\\\\n   182\\\\t/// Normalize JSON output: replace absolute paths with relative paths\\\\n   183\\\\t/// (strip the work_dir prefix).\\\\n   184\\\\tfn normalize_json(json_str: &str, work_dir: &Path) -> serde_json::Value {\\\\n   185\\\\t    let work_dir_str = work_dir.display().to_string();\\\\n   186\\\\t    let normalized = json_str.replace(&format!(\\\\\\\"{}/\\\\\\\", work_dir_str), \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\");\\\\n   187\\\\t    serde_json::from_str(&normalized).unwrap_or_else(|e| {\\\\n   188\\\\t        panic!(\\\\\\\"failed failed\\\\\\\");\\\\n   644\\\\t        let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);\\\\n   645\\\\t        for line in stdout.lines() {\\\\n   646\\\\t            let line = 228\\\\t    replace: Option<String>,\\\\n   229\\\\t    patch: Option<String>,\\\\n   230\\\\t    #[serde(default)]\\\\n   231\\\\t    skip_if_exists: bool,\\\\n   232\\\\t    after: Option<String>,\\\\n   233\\\\t    before: Option<String>,\\\\n   234\\\\t    #[serde(default)]\\\\n   235\\\\t    prepend: bool,\\\\n   236\\\\t    #[serde(default)]\\\\n   237\\\\t    append: bool,\\\\n   238\\\\t    #[serde(default)]\\\\n   239\\\\t    at: Option<String>,\\\\n   240\\\\t    skip_if: Option<String>,\\\\n   241\\\\t    between: Option<RawBetween>,\\\\n   242\\\\t messages.\\\\n   246\\\\t    #[serde(flatten)]\\\\n   247\\\\t    extra: std::collections::HashMap<String, serde_yaml::Value>,\\\\n   248\\\\t}\\\\n   249\\\\t\\\\n   250\\\\t// ── Parsing + validation ────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   251\\\\t\\\\n   252\\\\timpl Recipe {\\\\n   253\\\\t    /// Load and validate a recipe from a YAML file.\\\\n   254\\\\t    pub fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, JigError> {\\\\n   255\\\\t        let path = path\\\\n   256\\\\t            .canonicalize()\\\\n   257\\\\t            .map_err(|_| recipe_err(\\\\n   258\\\\t                \\\\\\\"recipe file not found\\\\\\\",\\\\n   259\\\\t                &path.display().to_string(),\\\\n   260\\\\t                \\\\\\\"the file does not exist at the specified path\\\\\\\",\\\\n   261\\\\t                \\\\\\\"check the file path and try again\\\\\\\",\\\\n   262\\\\t            ))?;\\\\n   263\\\\t\\\\n   264\\\\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&path)\\\\n   265\\\\t            .map_err(|e| recipe_err(\\\\n   266\\\\t                \\\\\\\"cannot read recipe file\\\\\\\",\\\\n   267\\\\t                &path.display().to_string(),\\\\n   268\\\\t                &e.to_string(),\\\\n   269\\\\t                \\\\\\\"check file permissions\\\\\\\",\\\\n   270\\\\t            ))?;\\\\n   271\\\\t\\\\n   272\\\\t        let recipe_dir = path.parent().unwrap_or(Path::new(\\\\\\\".\\\\\\\")).to_path_buf();\\\\n   273\\\\t        Self::parse(&content, recipe_dir, &path)\\\\n   274\\\\t    }\\\\n   275\\\\t\\\\n   276\\\\t    /// Parse recipe YAML content with a known recipe directory and source path.\\\\n   277\\\\t    pub fn parse(yaml: &str, recipe_dir: PathBuf, source_path: &Path) -> Result<Self, JigError> {\\\\n   278\\\\t        let raw: RawRecipe = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml)\\\\n   279\\\\t            .map_err(|e| recipe_err(\\\\n   280\\\\t                \\\\\\\"malformed recipe YAML\\\\\\\",\\\\n   281\\\\t                &source_path.display().to_string(),\\\\n   282\\\\t                &e.to_string(),\\\\n   283\\\\t                \\\\\\\"check YAML syntax — ensure proper indentation and field names\\\\\\\",\\\\n   284\\\\t            ))?;\\\\n   285\\\\t\\\\n   286\\\\t        let mut files = Vec::with_capacity(raw.files.len());\\\\n   287\\\\t        for (i, raw_op) in raw.files.into_iter().enumerate() {\\\\n   288\\\\t            let op = convert_file_op(raw_op, i, source_path)?;\\\\n   289\\\\t            files.push(op);\\\\n   290\\\\t        }\\\\n   291\\\\t\\\\n   292\\\\t        let recipe = Recipe {\\\\n   293\\\\t            name: raw.name,\\\\n   294\\\\t            description: raw.description,\\\\n   295\\\\t            variables: raw.variables,\\\\n   296\\\\t            files,\\\\n   297\\\\t            recipe_dir: recipe_dir.clone(),\\\\n   298\\\\t        };\\\\n   299\\\\t\\\\n   300\\\\t        // Validate template files exist.\\\\n   301\\\\t        recipe.validate_templates()?;\\\\n   302\\\\t\\\\n   303\\\\t        Ok(recipe)\\\\n   304\\\\t    }\\\\n   305\\\\t\\\\n   306\\\\t    fn validate_templates(&self) -> Result<(), JigError> {\\\\n   307\\\\t        for (i, op) in self.files.iter().enumerate() {\\\\n   308\\\\t            let tmpl = op.template();\\\\n   309\\\\t            let resolved = self.recipe_dir.join(tmpl);\\\\n   310\\\\t            if !resolved.is_file() {\\\\n   311\\\\t                return Err(recipe_err(\\\\n   312\\\\t                    &format!(\\\\\\\"template file not found: '{tmpl}'\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   313\\\\t                    &format!(\\\\\\\"files[{i}].template in recipe\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   314\\\\t                    &format!(\\\\\\\"looked for '{}' but it does not exist\\\\\\\", resolved.display()),\\\\n   315\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"ensure the template file exists relative to the recipe file location\\\\\\\",\\\\n   316\\\\t                ));\\\\n   317\\\\t            }\\\\n   318\\\\t        }\\\\n   319\\\\t        Ok(())\\\\n   320\\\\t    }\\\\n   321\\\\t\\\\n   322\\\\t    /// Resolve a template path relative to the recipe directory.\\\\n   323\\\\t    #[allow(dead_code)] // Used in later phases\\\\n   324\\\\t    pub fn resolve_template(&self, template: &str) -> PathBuf {\\\\n   325\\\\t        self.recipe_dir.join(template)\\\\n   326\\\\t    }\\\\n   327\\\\t}\\\\n   328\\\\t\\\\n   329\\\\t/// Convert a raw file op into a typed FileOp, validating structure.\\\\n   330\\\\tfn convert_file_op(raw: RawFileOp, index: usize, source: &Path) -> Result<FileOp, JigError> {\\\\n   331\\\\t    let loc = format!(\\\\\\\"files[{index}] in {}\\\\\\\", source.display());\\\\n   332\\\\t\\\\n   333\\\\t    // Template is required for all operations.\\\\n   334\\\\t    let template = raw.template.ok_or_else(|| recipe_err(\\\\n   335\\\\t        \\\\\\\"missing required field 'template'\\\\\\\",\\\\n   336\\\\t        &loc,\\\\n   337\\\\t        \\\\\\\"every file operation must specify a template\\\\\\\",\\\\n   338\\\\t        \\\\\\\"add a 'template' field pointing to the Jinja2 template file\\\\\\\",\\\\n   339\\\\t    ))?;\\\\n   340\\\\t\\\\n   341\\\\t    // Reject unknown fields with a helpful message.\\\\n   342\\\\t    if !raw.extra.is_empty() {\\\\n   343\\\\t        let unknown: Vec<&String> = raw.extra.keys().collect();\\\\n   344\\\\t        return Err(recipe_err(\\\\n   345\\\\t            &format!(\\\\\\\"unknown field(s): {}\\\\\\\", unknown.iter().map(|k| format!(\\\\\\\"'{k}'\\\\\\\")).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\")),\\\\n   346\\\\t            &loc,\\\\n   347\\\\t            \\\\\\\"these fields are not recognized in a file operation\\\\\\\",\\\\n   348\\\\t            \\\\\\\"remove the unknown fields — valid fields are: template, to, inject, replace, patch, skip_if_exists, after, before, prepend, append, at, skip_if, between, pattern, fallback, anchor\\\\\\\",\\\\n   349\\\\t        ));\\\\n   350\\\\t    }\\\\n   351\\\\t\\\\n   352\\\\t    // Count how many operation-type fields are present.\\\\n   353\\\\t    let has_to = raw.to.is_some();\\\\n   354\\\\t    let has_inject = raw.inject.is_some();\\\\n   355\\\\t    let has_replace = raw.replace.is_some();\\\\n   356\\\\t    let has_patch = raw.patch.is_some();\\\\n   357\\\\t    let type_count = [has_to, has_inject, has_replace, has_patch]\\\\n   358\\\\t        .iter()\\\\n   359\\\\t        .filter(|&&b| b)\\\\n   360\\\\t        .count();\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n '1,320p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n '1,320p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"     1\\\\tuse minijinja::{Environment, UndefinedBehavior};\\\\n     2\\\\tuse serde_json::Value;\\\\n     3\\\\t\\\\n     4\\\\tuse crate::error::{JigError, StructuredError};\\\\n     5\\\\tuse crate::filters;\\\\n     6\\\\tuse crate::recipe::Recipe;\\\\n     7\\\\t\\\\n     8\\\\t/// Create a minijinja Environment configured for a recipe context.\\\\n     9\\\\t/// Loads all templates from the recipe directory and registers filters.\\\\n    10\\\\t#[allow(dead_code)] // Used in later phases (jig run)\\\\n    11\\\\tpub fn create_recipe_env(recipe: &Recipe) -> Result<Environment<'static>, JigError> {\\\\n    12\\\\t    let mut env = Environment::new();\\\\n    13\\\\t    env.set_undefined_behavior(UndefinedBehavior::Strict);\\\\n    14\\\\t    filters::register_all(&mut env);\\\\n    15\\\\t\\\\n    16\\\\t    for (i, op) in recipe.files.iter().enumerate() {\\\\n    17\\\\t        let tmpl_path = recipe.recipe_dir.join(op.template());\\\\n    18\\\\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&tmpl_path).map_err(|e| {\\\\n    19\\\\t            JigError::TemplateRendering(StructuredError {\\\\n    20\\\\t                what: format!(\\\\\\\"cannot read template file '{}'\\\\\\\", op.template()),\\\\n    21\\\\t                where_: format!(\\\\\\\"files[{}].template\\\\\\\", i),\\\\n    22\\\\t                why: e.to_string(),\\\\n    23\\\\t                hint: \\\\\\\"ensure the template file is readable\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    24\\\\t            })\\\\n    25\\\\t        })?;\\\\n    26\\\\t        env.add_template_owned(op.template().to_string(), content)\\\\n    27\\\\t            .map_err(|e| template_syntax_error(op.template(), &e))?;\\\\n    28\\\\t    }\\\\n    29\\\\t\\\\n    30\\\\t    Ok(env)\\\\n    31\\\\t}\\\\n    32\\\\t\\\\n    33\\\\t/// Create a standalone minijinja Environment (for `jig render`).\\\\n    34\\\\t/// No templates pre-loaded; use `render_string` for one-off rendering.\\\\n    35\\\\tpub fn create_standalone_env() -> Environment<'static> {\\\\n    36\\\\t    let mut env = Environment::new();\\\\n    37\\\\t    env.set_undefined_behavior(UndefinedBehavior::Strict);\\\\n    38\\\\t    filters::register_all(&mut env);\\\\n    39\\\\t    env\\\\n    40\\\\t}\\\\n    41\\\\t\\\\n    42\\\\t/// Render a named template that's already loaded in the environment.\\\\n    43\\\\t#[allow(dead_code)] // Used in later phases (jig run)\\\\n    44\\\\tpub fn render_template(\\\\n    45\\\\t    env: &Environment,\\\\n    46\\\\t    template_name: &str,\\\\n    47\\\\t    vars: &Value,\\\\n    48\\\\t) -> Result<String, JigError> {\\\\n    49\\\\t    let tmpl = env.get_template(template_name).map_err(|e| {\\\\n    50\\\\t        JigError::TemplateRendering(StructuredError {\\\\n    51\\\\t            what: format!(\\\\\\\"template '{}' not found in environment\\\\\\\", template_name),\\\\n    52\\\\t            where_: template_name.to_string(),\\\\n    53\\\\t            why: e.to_string(),\\\\n    54\\\\t            hint: \\\\\\\"ensure the template is loaded before rendering\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    55\\\\t        })\\\\n    56\\\\t    })?;\\\\n    57\\\\t\\\\n    58\\\\t    let source = tmpl.source();\\\\n    59\\\\t    tmpl.render(vars)\\\\n    60\\\\t        .map_err(|e| convert_render_error(&e, template_name, source, vars))\\\\n    61\\\\t}\\\\n    62\\\\t\\\\n    63\\\\t/// Render a template string directly (for `jig render` standalone mode).\\\\n    64\\\\tpub fn render_string(\\\\n    65\\\\t    env: &Environment,\\\\n    66\\\\t    source: &str,\\\\n    67\\\\t    vars: &Value,\\\\n    68\\\\t    source_path: &str,\\\\n    69\\\\t) -> Result<String, JigError> {\\\\n    70\\\\t    env.render_str(source, vars)\\\\n    71\\\\t        .map_err(|e| convert_render_error(&e, source_path, source, vars))\\\\n    72\\\\t}\\\\n    73\\\\t\\\\n    74\\\\t/// Render a template string and return a Jinja2 template expression result.\\\\n    75\\\\t/// Used for rendering path templates like `to` and `inject` fields.\\\\n    76\\\\t#[allow(dead_code)] // Used in later phases (jig run)\\\\n    77\\\\tpub fn render_path_template(\\\\n    78\\\\t    env: &Environment,\\\\n    79\\\\t    template_expr: &str,\\\\n    80\\\\t    vars: &Value,\\\\n    81\\\\t    context_desc: &str,\\\\n    82\\\\t) -> Result<String, JigError> {\\\\n    83\\\\t    env.render_str(template_expr, vars)\\\\n    84\\\\t        .map_err(|e| convert_render_error(&e, context_desc, template_expr, vars))\\\\n    85\\\\t}\\\\n    86\\\\t\\\\n    87\\\\t// ── Error conversion ───────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n    88\\\\t\\\\n    89\\\\tfn convert_render_error(\\\\n    90\\\\t    err: &minijinja::Error,\\\\n    91\\\\t    source_path: &str,\\\\n    92\\\\t    template_source: &str,\\\\n    93\\\\t    vars: &Value,\\\\n    94\\\\t) -> JigError {\\\\n    95\\\\t    let kind = err.kind();\\\\n    96\\\\t    let line_info = err.line().map(|l| format!(\\\\\\\" (line {})\\\\\\\", l)).unwrap_or_default();\\\\n    97\\\\t\\\\n    98\\\\t    match kind {\\\\n    99\\\\t        minijinja::ErrorKind::UndefinedError => {\\\\n   100\\\\t            // minijinja doesn't include the variable name in the error.\\\\n   101\\\\t            // Extract it from the template source by finding references not in vars.\\\\n   102\\\\t            let var_name = find_undefined_variable(template_source, err.line(), vars);\\\\n   103\\\\t            let hint = did_you_mean_hint(&var_name, vars);\\\\n   104\\\\t\\\\n   105\\\\t            JigError::TemplateRendering(StructuredError {\\\\n   106\\\\t                what: format!(\\\\\\\"undefined variable '{}'\\\\\\\", var_name),\\\\n   107\\\\t                where_: format!(\\\\\\\"{}{}\\\\\\\", source_path, line_info),\\\\n   108\\\\t                why: format!(\\\\\\\"variable '{}' is not defined in the provided context\\\\\\\", var_name),\\\\n   109\\\\t                hint,\\\\n   110\\\\t            })\\\\n   111\\\\t        }\\\\n   112\\\\t        minijinja::ErrorKind::SyntaxError => {\\\\n   113\\\\t            let detail = err.detail().unwrap_or(\\\\\\\"syntax error\\\\\\\");\\\\n   114\\\\t            JigError::TemplateRendering(StructuredError {\\\\n   115\\\\t                what: \\\\\\\"template syntax error\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   116\\\\t                where_: format!(\\\\\\\"{}{}\\\\\\\", source_path, line_info),\\\\n   117\\\\t                why: detail.to_string(),\\\\n   118\\\\t                hint: \\\\\\\"check Jinja2 syntax — ensure all blocks are properly closed\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   119\\\\t            })\\\\n   120\\\\t        }\\\\n   121\\\\t        _ => {\\\\n   122\\\\t            let detail = err.detail().unwrap_or(\\\\\\\"rendering error\\\\\\\");\\\\n   123\\\\t            JigError::TemplateRendering(StructuredError {\\\\n   124\\\\t                what: \\\\\\\"template rendering error\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   125\\\\t                where_: format!(\\\\\\\"{}{}\\\\\\\", source_path, line_info),\\\\n   126\\\\t                why: detail.to_string(),\\\\n   127\\\\t                hint: \\\\\\\"check template syntax and variable references\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   128\\\\t            })\\\\n   129\\\\t        }\\\\n   130\\\\t    }\\\\n   131\\\\t}\\\\n   132\\\\t\\\\n   133\\\\t#[allow(dead_code)] // Used in recipe env creation\\\\n   134\\\\tfn template_syntax_error(template_name: &str, err: &minijinja::Error) -> JigError {\\\\n   135\\\\t    let line_info = err.line().map(|l| format!(\\\\\\\" (line {})\\\\\\\", l)).unwrap_or_default();\\\\n   136\\\\t    let detail = err.detail().unwrap_or(\\\\\\\"syntax error\\\\\\\");\\\\n   137\\\\t\\\\n   138\\\\t    JigError::TemplateRendering(StructuredError {\\\\n   139\\\\t        what: \\\\\\\"template syntax error\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   140\\\\t        where_: format!(\\\\\\\"{}{}\\\\\\\", template_name, line_info),\\\\n   141\\\\t        why: detail.to_string(),\\\\n   142\\\\t        hint: \\\\\\\"check Jinja2 syntax — ensure all blocks are properly closed\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   143\\\\t    })\\\\n   144\\\\t}\\\\n   145\\\\t\\\\n   146\\\\t/// Find the undefined variable in a template by scanning for references\\\\n   147\\\\t/// not present in the variable context.\\\\n   148\\\\t///\\\\n   149\\\\t/// Limitation: this is a best-effort heuristic using regex. It extracts only the\\\\n   150\\\\t/// first identifier from expressions like `{{ user.name }}` (yields \\\\\\\"user\\\\\\\", not\\\\n   151\\\\t/// \\\\\\\"user.name\\\\\\\"). Falls back to \\\\\\\"unknown\\\\\\\" if no match is found. minijinja does not\\\\n   152\\\\t/// expose the undefined variable name directly, so this is the best we can do for v0.1.\\\\n   153\\\\tfn find_undefined_variable(template_source: &str, error_line: Option<usize>, vars: &Value) -> String {\\\\n   154\\\\t    let var_keys: Vec<&str> = match vars.as_object() {\\\\n   155\\\\t        Some(obj) => obj.keys().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect(),\\\\n   156\\\\t        None => vec![],\\\\n   157\\\\t    };\\\\n   158\\\\t\\\\n   159\\\\t    // Extract variable references from the template.\\\\n   160\\\\t    // Matches {{ name }}, {{ name | filter }}, {{ name.attr }}, etc.\\\\n   161\\\\t    let re = regex::Regex::new(r\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\{\\\\\\\\{[\\\\\\\\s-]*([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)\\\\\\\").unwrap();\\\\n   162\\\\t    // Also check {% if/for/elif blocks\\\\n   163\\\\t    let block_re = regex::Regex::new(r\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\{%[\\\\\\\\s-]*(?:if|elif|for\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s+in|set\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*=)\\\\\\\\s+([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)\\\\\\\").unwrap();\\\\n   164\\\\t\\\\n   165\\\\t    let lines: Vec<&str> = template_source.lines().collect();\\\\n   166\\\\t\\\\n   167\\\\t    // If we have a line number, check that line first\\\\n   168\\\\t    if let Some(line_num) = error_line.filter(|&l| l > 0 && l <= lines.len()) {\\\\n   169\\\\t        let line = lines[line_num - 1];\\\\n   170\\\\t        for cap in re.captures_iter(line).chain(block_re.captures_iter(line)) {\\\\n   171\\\\t            let name = &cap[1];\\\\n   172\\\\t            if !var_keys.contains(&name) {\\\\n   173\\\\t                return name.to_string();\\\\n   174\\\\t            }\\\\n   175\\\\t        }\\\\n   176\\\\t    }\\\\n   177\\\\t\\\\n   178\\\\t    // Fall back to scanning the entire template\\\\n   179\\\\t    for cap in re.captures_iter(template_source).chain(block_re.captures_iter(template_source)) {\\\\n   180\\\\t        let name = &cap[1];\\\\n   181\\\\t        if !var_keys.contains(&name) {\\\\n   182\\\\t            return name.to_string();\\\\n   183\\\\t        }\\\\n   184\\\\t    }\\\\n   185\\\\t\\\\n   186\\\\t    \\\\\\\"unknown\\\\\\\".to_string()\\\\n   187\\\\t}\\\\n   188\\\\t\\\\n   189\\\\t/// Generate a \\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\" hint using Levenshtein distance.\\\\n   190\\\\t/// Looks at all keys in the provided variable context.\\\\n   191\\\\tfn did_you_mean_hint(var_name: &str, vars: &Value) -> String {\\\\n   192\\\\t    let keys: Vec<&str> = match vars.as_object() {\\\\n   193\\\\t        Some(obj) => obj.keys().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect(),\\\\n   194\\\\t        None => return format!(\\\\\\\"check variable name '{var_name}'\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   195\\\\t    };\\\\n   196\\\\t\\\\n   197\\\\t    if keys.is_empty() {\\\\n   198\\\\t        return format!(\\\\\\\"check variable name '{}' — no variables were provided\\\\\\\", var_name);\\\\n   199\\\\t    }\\\\n   200\\\\t\\\\n   201\\\\t    let mut best_match: Option<(&str, usize)> = None;\\\\n   202\\\\t    for key in &keys {\\\\n   203\\\\t        let dist = strsim::levenshtein(var_name, key);\\\\n   204\\\\t        if dist <= 3 && (best_match.is_none() || dist < best_match.unwrap().1) {\\\\n   205\\\\t            best_match = Some((key, dist));\\\\n   206\\\\t        }\\\\n   207\\\\t    }\\\\n   208\\\\t\\\\n   209\\\\t    match best_match {\\\\n   210\\\\t        Some((suggestion, _)) => format!(\\\\\\\"did you mean '{suggestion}'?\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   211\\\\t        None => format!(\\\\\\\"check variable name '{}' — available variables: {}\\\\\\\", var_name, keys.join(\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\")),\\\\n   212\\\\t    }\\\\n   213\\\\t}\\\\n   214\\\\t\\\\n   215\\\\t// ── Tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   216\\\\t\\\\n   217\\\\t#[cfg(test)]\\\\n   218\\\\tmod tests {\\\\n   219\\\\t    use super::*;\\\\n   220\\\\t    use serde_json::json;\\\\n   221\\\\t\\\\n   222\\\\t    fn render(template: &str, vars: &Value) -> Result<String, JigError> {\\\\n   223\\\\t        let env = create_standalone_env();\\\\n   224\\\\t        render_string(&env, template, vars, \\\\\\\"test.j2\\\\\\\")\\\\n   225\\\\t    }\\\\n   226\\\\t\\\\n   227\\\\t    // ── AC-3.1: Variable substitution ──────────────────────────\\\\n   228\\\\t\\\\n   229\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   230\\\\t    fn ac_3_1_variable_substitution() {\\\\n   231\\\\t        let vars = json!({\\\\\\\"class_name\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"module\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"bookings\\\\\\\"});\\\\n   232\\\\t        let result = render(\\\\\\\"class {{ class_name }} in {{ module }}\\\\\\\", &vars).unwrap();\\\\n   233\\\\t        assert_eq!(result, \\\\\\\"class BookingService in bookings\\\\\\\");\\\\n   234\\\\t    }\\\\n   235\\\\t\\\\n   236\\\\t    // ── AC-3.2: Conditional blocks ─────────────────────────────\\\\n   237\\\\t\\\\n   238\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   239\\\\t    fn ac_3_2_conditionals() {\\\\n   240\\\\t        let vars = json!({\\\\\\\"auth\\\\\\\": true});\\\\n   241\\\\t        let tmpl = \\\\\\\"{% if auth %}secured{% else %}open{% endif %}\\\\\\\";\\\\n   242\\\\t        assert_eq!(render(tmpl, &vars).unwrap(), \\\\\\\"secured\\\\\\\");\\\\n   243\\\\t\\\\n   244\\\\t        let vars = json!({\\\\\\\"auth\\\\\\\": false});\\\\n   245\\\\t        assert_eq!(render(tmpl, &vars).unwrap(), \\\\\\\"open\\\\\\\");\\\\n   246\\\\t    }\\\\n   247\\\\t\\\\n   248\\\\t    // ── AC-3.3: For loops ──────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   249\\\\t\\\\n   250\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   251\\\\t    fn ac_3_3_for_loops() {\\\\n   252\\\\t        let vars = json!({\\\\\\\"items\\\\\\\": [\\\\\\\"alpha\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"beta\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"gamma\\\\\\\"]});\\\\n   253\\\\t        let tmpl = \\\\\\\"{% for item in items %}{{ item }}\\\\\\\\n{% endfor %}\\\\\\\";\\\\n   254\\\\t        let result = render(tmpl, &vars).unwrap();\\\\n   255\\\\t        assert_eq!(result, \\\\\\\"alpha\\\\\\\\nbeta\\\\\\\\ngamma\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\");\\\\n   256\\\\t    }\\\\n   257\\\\t\\\\n   258\\\\t    // ── AC-3.15: Comments stripped ─────────────────────────────\\\\n   259\\\\t\\\\n   260\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   261\\\\t    fn ac_3_15_comments_stripped() {\\\\n   262\\\\t        let vars = json!({\\\\\\\"x\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\"});\\\\n   263\\\\t        let result = render(\\\\\\\"{# this is a comment #}{{ x }}\\\\\\\", &vars).unwrap();\\\\n   264\\\\t        assert_eq!(result, \\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\");\\\\n   265\\\\t    }\\\\n   266\\\\t\\\\n   267\\\\t    // ── AC-3.16: Raw blocks ────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   268\\\\t\\\\n   269\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   270\\\\t    fn ac_3_16_raw_blocks() {\\\\n   271\\\\t        let vars = json!({});\\\\n   272\\\\t        let result = render(\\\\\\\"{% raw %}{{ not_rendered }}{% endraw %}\\\\\\\", &vars).unwrap();\\\\n   273\\\\t        assert_eq!(result, \\\\\\\"{{ not_rendered }}\\\\\\\");\\\\n   274\\\\t    }\\\\n   275\\\\t\\\\n   276\\\\t    // ── AC-3.17: Undefined variable with did-you-mean ──────────\\\\n   277\\\\t\\\\n   278\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   279\\\\t    fn ac_3_17_undefined_variable_did_you_mean() {\\\\n   280\\\\t        let vars = json!({\\\\\\\"class_name\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"Foo\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"module_name\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"bar\\\\\\\"});\\\\n   281\\\\t        let err = render(\\\\\\\"{{ clss_name }}\\\\\\\", &vars).unwrap_err();\\\\n   282\\\\t        assert_eq!(err.exit_code(), 2);\\\\n   283\\\\t        let se = err.structured_error();\\\\n   284\\\\t        assert!(se.what.contains(\\\\\\\"undefined variable\\\\\\\"), \\\\\\\"what was: {}\\\\\\\", se.what);\\\\n   285\\\\t        assert!(se.what.contains(\\\\\\\"clss_name\\\\\\\"), \\\\\\\"what was: {}\\\\\\\", se.what);\\\\n   286\\\\t        assert!(se.hint.contains(\\\\\\\"did you mean\\\\\\\"), \\\\\\\"hint was: {}\\\\\\\", se.hint);\\\\n   287\\\\t        assert!(se.hint.contains(\\\\\\\"class_name\\\\\\\"), \\\\\\\"hint was: {}\\\\\\\", se.hint);\\\\n   288\\\\t    }\\\\n   289\\\\t\\\\n   290\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   291\\\\t    fn ac_3_17_undefined_variable_no_close_match() {\\\\n   292\\\\t        let vars = json!({\\\\\\\"foo\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"bar\\\\\\\"});\\\\n   293\\\\t        let err = render(\\\\\\\"{{ completely_different }}\\\\\\\", &vars).unwrap_err();\\\\n   294\\\\t        assert_eq!(err.exit_code(), 2);\\\\n   295\\\\t        let se = err.structured_error();\\\\n   296\\\\t        assert!(se.what.contains(\\\\\\\"undefined variable\\\\\\\"));\\\\n   297\\\\t        assert!(se.hint.contains(\\\\\\\"foo\\\\\\\"), \\\\\\\"hint was: {}\\\\\\\", se.hint);\\\\n   298\\\\t    }\\\\n   299\\\\t\\\\n   300\\\\t    // ── AC-3.18: Syntax error with file + line ─────────────────\\\\n   301\\\\t\\\\n   302\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   303\\\\t    fn ac_3_18_syntax_error() {\\\\n   304\\\\t        let vars = json!({});\\\\n   305\\\\t        let err = render(\\\\\\\"{% if unclosed %}\\\\\\\", &vars).unwrap_err();\\\\n   306\\\\t        assert_eq!(err.exit_code(), 2);\\\\n   307\\\\t        let se = err.structured_error();\\\\n   308\\\\t        assert!(se.what.contains(\\\\\\\"syntax error\\\\\\\"));\\\\n   309\\\\t        assert!(se.where_.contains(\\\\\\\"test.j2\\\\\\\"));\\\\n   310\\\\t    }\\\\n   311\\\\t\\\\n   312\\\\t    // ── AC-N1.1: Deterministic output ──────────────────────────\\\\n   313\\\\t\\\\n   314\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n   315\\\\t    fn ac_n1_1_deterministic() {\\\\n   316\\\\t        let vars = json!({\\\\\\\"name\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"Foo\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"items\\\\\\\": [\\\\\\\"a\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"b\\\\\\\"]});\\\\n   317\\\\t        let tmpl = \\\\\\\"{{ name | snakecase }}\\\\\\\\n{% for x in items %}{{ x }}\\\\\\\\n{% endfor %}\\\\\\\";\\\\n   318\\\\t        let r1 = render(tmpl, &vars).unwrap();\\\\n   319\\\\t        let r2 = render(tmpl, &vars).unwrap();\\\\n   320\\\\t        assert_eq!(r1, r2, \\\\\\\"output must be byte-identical across runs\\\\\\\");\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n    40\\t{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_19\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"nl -ba src/variables.rs | sed -n '1,340p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n    41\\t{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_19\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"nl -ba src/variables.rs | sed -n '1,340p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"     1\\\\tuse std::io::Read;\\\\n     2\\\\tuse std::path::Path;\\\\n     3\\\\t\\\\n     4\\\\tuse indexmap::IndexMap;\\\\n     5\\\\tuse serde_json::Value;\\\\n     6\\\\t\\\\n     7\\\\tuse crate::error::{JigError, StructuredError};\\\\n     8\\\\tuse crate::recipe::{VarType, VariableDecl};\\\\n     9\\\\t\\\\n    10\\\\t// ── Public API: vars output ────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n    11\\\\t\\\\n    12\\\\t/// Build the vars JSON output for `jig vars`.\\\\n    13\\\\tpub fn vars_json(variables: &IndexMap<String, VariableDecl>) -> Value {\\\\n    14\\\\t    let mut map = serde_json::Map::new();\\\\n    15\\\\t    for (name, decl) in variables {\\\\n    16\\\\t        let mut entry = serde_json::Map::new();\\\\n    17\\\\t        entry.insert(\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\".into(), Value::String(decl.var_type.to_string()));\\\\n    18\\\\t        entry.insert(\\\\\\\"required\\\\\\\".into(), Value::Bool(decl.required));\\\\n    19\\\\t        if let Some(ref default) = decl.default {\\\\n    20\\\\t            entry.insert(\\\\\\\"default\\\\\\\".into(), default.clone());\\\\n    21\\\\t        }\\\\n    22\\\\t        if let Some(ref desc) = decl.description {\\\\n    23\\\\t            entry.insert(\\\\\\\"description\\\\\\\".into(), Value::String(desc.clone()));\\\\n    24\\\\t        }\\\\n    25\\\\t        if let Some(ref values) = decl.values {\\\\n    26\\\\t            entry.insert(\\\\n    27\\\\t                \\\\\\\"values\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    28\\\\t                Value::Array(values.iter().map(|v| Value::String(v.clone())).collect()),\\\\n    29\\\\t            );\\\\n    30\\\\t        }\\\\n    31\\\\t        if let Some(ref items) = decl.items {\\\\n    32\\\\t            entry.insert(\\\\\\\"items\\\\\\\".into(), Value::String(items.to_string()));\\\\n    33\\\\t        }\\\\n    34\\\\t        map.insert(name.clone(), Value::Object(entry));\\\\n    35\\\\t    }\\\\n    36\\\\t    Value::Object(map)\\\\n    37\\\\t}\\\\n    38\\\\t\\\\n    39\\\\t// ── Public API: variable collection ────────────────────────────────\\\\n    40\\\\t\\\\n    41\\\\t/// Parse inline JSON from --vars.\\\\n    42\\\\tpub fn parse_vars_inline(json_str: &str) -> Result<Value, JigError> {\\\\n    43\\\\t    serde_json::from_str(json_str).map_err(|e| {\\\\n    44\\\\t        JigError::VariableValidation(vec![StructuredError {\\\\n    45\\\\t            what: \\\\\\\"invalid JSON in --vars\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    46\\\\t            where_: \\\\\\\"--vars argument\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    47\\\\t            why: e.to_string(),\\\\n    48\\\\t            hint: \\\\\\\"check JSON syntax — ensure keys are quoted and values are valid\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    49\\\\t        }])\\\\n    50\\\\t    })\\\\n    51\\\\t}\\\\n    52\\\\t\\\\n    53\\\\t/// Parse JSON from a file (--vars-file).\\\\n    54\\\\tpub fn parse_vars_file(path: &Path) -> Result<Value, JigError> {\\\\n    55\\\\t    let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| {\\\\n    56\\\\t        let (what, why) = if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound {\\\\n    57\\\\t            (\\\\n    58\\\\t                \\\\\\\"vars file not found\\\\\\\".to_string(),\\\\n    59\\\\t                \\\\\\\"the file does not exist at the specified path\\\\\\\".to_string(),\\\\n    60\\\\t            )\\\\n    61\\\\t        } else {\\\\n    62\\\\t            (\\\\n    63\\\\t                \\\\\\\"cannot read vars file\\\\\\\".to_string(),\\\\n    64\\\\t                e.to_string(),\\\\n    65\\\\t            )\\\\n    66\\\\t        };\\\\n    67\\\\t        JigError::VariableValidation(vec![StructuredError {\\\\n    68\\\\t            what,\\\\n    69\\\\t            where_: path.display().to_string(),\\\\n    70\\\\t            why,\\\\n    71\\\\t            hint: \\\\\\\"check the --vars-file path and try again\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    72\\\\t        }])\\\\n    73\\\\t    })?;\\\\n    74\\\\t    serde_json::from_str(&content).map_err(|e| {\\\\n    75\\\\t        JigError::VariableValidation(vec![StructuredError {\\\\n    76\\\\t            what: \\\\\\\"invalid JSON in vars file\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    77\\\\t            where_: path.display().to_string(),\\\\n    78\\\\t            why: e.to_string(),\\\\n    79\\\\t            hint: \\\\\\\"check JSON syntax in the vars file\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    80\\\\t        }])\\\\n    81\\\\t    })\\\\n    82\\\\t}\\\\n    83\\\\t\\\\n    84\\\\t/// Parse JSON from stdin (--vars-stdin).\\\\n    85\\\\tpub fn parse_vars_stdin() -> Result<Value, JigError> {\\\\n    86\\\\t    let mut buf = String::new();\\\\n    87\\\\t    std::io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut buf).map_err(|e| {\\\\n    88\\\\t        JigError::VariableValidation(vec![StructuredError {\\\\n    89\\\\t            what: \\\\\\\"failed to read stdin\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    90\\\\t            where_: \\\\\\\"stdin\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    91\\\\t            why: e.to_string(),\\\\n    92\\\\t            hint: \\\\\\\"ensure stdin contains valid JSON\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    93\\\\t        }])\\\\n    94\\\\t    })?;\\\\n    95\\\\t    serde_json::from_str(&buf).map_err(|e| {\\\\n    96\\\\t        JigError::VariableValidation(vec![StructuredError {\\\\n    97\\\\t            what: \\\\\\\"invalid JSON from stdin\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    98\\\\t            where_: \\\\\\\"stdin (--vars-stdin)\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    99\\\\t            why: e.to_string(),\\\\n   100\\\\t            hint: \\\\\\\"check JSON syntax in stdin input\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   101\\\\t        }])\\\\n   102\\\\t    })\\\\n   103\\\\t}\\\\n   104\\\\t\\\\n   105\\\\t/// Collect variables from all sources and merge with precedence:\\\\n   106\\\\t/// defaults < vars-file < vars-stdin < inline --vars.\\\\n   107\\\\t/// Returns the merged JSON object (no type validation yet).\\\\n   108\\\\tpub fn collect_vars(\\\\n   109\\\\t    inline: Option<&str>,\\\\n   110\\\\t    file: Option<&Path>,\\\\n   111\\\\t    stdin: bool,\\\\n   112\\\\t) -> Result<Value, JigError> {\\\\n   113\\\\t    let file_val = match file {\\\\n   114\\\\t        Some(path) => Some(parse_vars_file(path)?),\\\\n   115\\\\t        None => None,\\\\n   116\\\\t    };\\\\n   117\\\\t    let stdin_val = if stdin {\\\\n   118\\\\t        Some(parse_vars_stdin()?)\\\\n   119\\\\t    } else {\\\\n   120\\\\t        None\\\\n   121\\\\t    };\\\\n   122\\\\t    let inline_val = match inline {\\\\n   123\\\\t        Some(s) => Some(parse_vars_inline(s)?),\\\\n   124\\\\t        None => None,\\\\n   125\\\\t    };\\\\n   126\\\\t\\\\n   127\\\\t    let mut merged = Value::Object(serde_json::Map::new());\\\\n   128\\\\t    if let Some(f) = file_val {\\\\n   129\\\\t        merge_json(&mut merged, &f);\\\\n   130\\\\t    }\\\\n   131\\\\t    if let Some(s) = stdin_val {\\\\n   132\\\\t        merge_json(&mut merged, &s);\\\\n   133\\\\t    }\\\\n   134\\\\t    if let Some(i) = inline_val {\\\\n   135\\\\t        merge_json(&mut merged, &i);\\\\n   136\\\\t    }\\\\n   137\\\\t    Ok(merged)\\\\n   138\\\\t}\\\\n   139\\\\t\\\\n   140\\\\t// ── Public API: validation ─────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   141\\\\t\\\\n   142\\\\t/// Validate and merge variables against recipe declarations.\\\\n   143\\\\t/// Applies defaults, then merges provided values, then type-checks.\\\\n   144\\\\t/// Accumulates all validation errors before returning (AC-2.11).\\\\n   145\\\\t/// Extra keys not in declarations pass through without error (AC-2.12).\\\\n   146\\\\t#[allow(dead_code)] // Used in later phases (jig run) and tests\\\\n   147\\\\tpub fn validate_variables(\\\\n   148\\\\t    decls: &IndexMap<String, VariableDecl>,\\\\n   149\\\\t    provided: &Value,\\\\n   150\\\\t) -> Result<Value, JigError> {\\\\n   151\\\\t    let provided_obj = provided.as_object().cloned().unwrap_or_default();\\\\n   152\\\\t\\\\n   153\\\\t    // Start with defaults, then overlay provided values.\\\\n   154\\\\t    let mut merged = serde_json::Map::new();\\\\n   155\\\\t    for (name, decl) in decls {\\\\n   156\\\\t        if let Some(ref default) = decl.default {\\\\n   157\\\\t            merged.insert(name.clone(), default.clone());\\\\n   158\\\\t        }\\\\n   159\\\\t    }\\\\n   160\\\\t    for (key, val) in &provided_obj {\\\\n   161\\\\t        merged.insert(key.clone(), val.clone());\\\\n   162\\\\t    }\\\\n   163\\\\t\\\\n   164\\\\t    let mut errors = Vec::new();\\\\n   165\\\\t\\\\n   166\\\\t    for (name, decl) in decls {\\\\n   167\\\\t        match merged.get(name) {\\\\n   168\\\\t            None if decl.required => {\\\\n   169\\\\t                errors.push(StructuredError {\\\\n   170\\\\t                    what: format!(\\\\\\\"missing required variable '{name}'\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   171\\\\t                    where_: format!(\\\\\\\"variable '{name}'\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   172\\\\t                    why: format!(\\\\\\\"variable '{name}' is declared as required but no value was provided\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   173\\\\t                    hint: format!(\\\\\\\"add '{name}' to --vars, e.g. --vars '{{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":...}}'\\\\\\\", name),\\\\n   174\\\\t                });\\\\n   175\\\\t            }\\\\n   176\\\\t            None => {\\\\n   177\\\\t                // Not required, no default — just absent. That's fine.\\\\n   178\\\\t            }\\\\n   179\\\\t            Some(val) => {\\\\n   180\\\\t                check_type(name, decl, val, &mut errors);\\\\n   181\\\\t            }\\\\n   182\\\\t        }\\\\n   183\\\\t    }\\\\n   184\\\\t\\\\n   185\\\\t    if !errors.is_empty() {\\\\n   186\\\\t        return Err(JigError::VariableValidation(errors));\\\\n   187\\\\t    }\\\\n   188\\\\t\\\\n   189\\\\t    Ok(Value::Object(merged))\\\\n   190\\\\t}\\\\n   191\\\\t\\\\n   192\\\\t// ── Internal helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   193\\\\t\\\\n   194\\\\tfn merge_json(base: &mut Value, overlay: &Value) {\\\\n   195\\\\t    if let (Some(base_obj), Some(overlay_obj)) = (base.as_object_mut(), overlay.as_object()) {\\\\n   196\\\\t        for (key, val) in overlay_obj {\\\\n   197\\\\t            base_obj.insert(key.clone(), val.clone());\\\\n   198\\\\t        }\\\\n   199\\\\t    }\\\\n   200\\\\t}\\\\n   201\\\\t\\\\n   202\\\\tfn check_type(name: &str, decl: &VariableDecl, val: &Value, errors: &mut Vec<StructuredError>) {\\\\n   203\\\\t    let actual_type = json_type_name(val);\\\\n   204\\\\t\\\\n   205\\\\t    match decl.var_type {\\\\n   206\\\\t        VarType::String => {\\\\n   207\\\\t            if !val.is_string() {\\\\n   208\\\\t                errors.push(type_mismatch_error(name, \\\\\\\"string\\\\\\\", actual_type, val));\\\\n   209\\\\t            }\\\\n   210\\\\t        }\\\\n   211\\\\t        VarType::Number => {\\\\n   212\\\\t            if !val.is_number() {\\\\n   213\\\\t                errors.push(type_mismatch_error(name, \\\\\\\"number\\\\\\\", actual_type, val));\\\\n   214\\\\t            }\\\\n   215\\\\t        }\\\\n   216\\\\t        VarType::Boolean => {\\\\n   217\\\\t            if !val.is_boolean() {\\\\n   218\\\\t                errors.push(type_mismatch_error(name, \\\\\\\"boolean\\\\\\\", actual_type, val));\\\\n   219\\\\t            }\\\\n   220\\\\t        }\\\\n   221\\\\t        VarType::Array => {\\\\n   222\\\\t            if !val.is_array() {\\\\n   223\\\\t                errors.push(type_mismatch_error(name, \\\\\\\"array\\\\\\\", actual_type, val));\\\\n   224\\\\t            } else if let Some(ref item_type) = decl.items {\\\\n   225\\\\t                check_array_items(name, item_type, val.as_array().unwrap(), errors);\\\\n   226\\\\t            }\\\\n   227\\\\t        }\\\\n   228\\\\t        VarType::Object => {\\\\n   229\\\\t            if !val.is_object() {\\\\n   230\\\\t                errors.push(type_mismatch_error(name, \\\\\\\"object\\\\\\\", actual_type, val));\\\\n   231\\\\t            }\\\\n   232\\\\t        }\\\\n   233\\\\t        VarType::Enum => {\\\\n   234\\\\t            #[allow(clippy::collapsible_if)]\\\\n   235\\\\t            if let Some(s) = val.as_str() {\\\\n   236\\\\t                if let Some(ref allowed) = decl.values {\\\\n   237\\\\t                    if !allowed.contains(&s.to_string()) {\\\\n   238\\\\t                        errors.push(StructuredError {\\\\n   239\\\\t                            what: format!(\\\\\\\"invalid enum value for '{name}'\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   240\\\\t                            where_: format!(\\\\\\\"variable '{name}'\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   241\\\\t                            why: format!(\\\\n   242\\\\t                                \\\\\\\"value '{}' is not in the allowed set: [{}]\\\\\\\",\\\\n   243\\\\t                                s,\\\\n   244\\\\t                                allowed.join(\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\")\\\\n   245\\\\t                            ),\\\\n   246\\\\t                            hint: format!(\\\\\\\"use one of: {}\\\\\\\", allowed.join(\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\")),\\\\n   247\\\\t                        });\\\\n   248\\\\t                    }\\\\n   249\\\\t                }\\\\n   250\\\\t            } else {\\\\n   251\\\\t                errors.push(type_mismatch_error(name, \\\\\\\"enum (string)\\\\\\\", actual_type, val));\\\\n   252\\\\t            }\\\\n   253\\\\t        }\\\\n   254\\\\t    }\\\\n   255\\\\t}\\\\n   256\\\\t\\\\n   257\\\\tfn check_array_items(\\\\n   258\\\\t    name: &str,\\\\n   259\\\\t    item_type: &VarType,\\\\n   260\\\\t    items: &[Value],\\\\n   261\\\\t    errors: &mut Vec<StructuredError>,\\\\n   262\\\\t) {\\\\n   263\\\\t    for (i, item) in items.iter().enumerate() {\\\\n   264\\\\t        let ok = match item_type {\\\\n   265\\\\t            VarType::String => item.is_string(),\\\\n   266\\\\t            VarType::Number => item.is_number(),\\\\n   267\\\\t            VarType::Boolean => item.is_boolean(),\\\\n   268\\\\t            VarType::Array => item.is_array(),\\\\n   269\\\\t            VarType::Object => item.is_object(),\\\\n   270\\\\t            VarType::Enum => item.is_string(), // enum items are strings\\\\n   271\\\\t        };\\\\n   272\\\\t        if !ok {\\\\n   273\\\\t            errors.push(StructuredError {\\\\n   274\\\\t                what: format!(\\\\\\\"array item type mismatch in '{name}'\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   275\\\\t                where_: format!(\\\\\\\"variable '{name}[{i}]'\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   276\\\\t                why: format!(\\\\n   277\\\\t                    \\\\\\\"expected item type {}, got {}\\\\\\\",\\\\n   278\\\\t                    item_type,\\\\n   279\\\\t                    json_type_name(item)\\\\n   280\\\\t                ),\\\\n   281\\\\t                hint: format!(\\\\\\\"ensure all elements of '{name}' are of type {item_type}\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   282\\\\t            });\\\\n   283\\\\t        }\\\\n   284\\\\t    }\\\\n   285\\\\t}\\\\n   286\\\\t\\\\n   287\\\\tfn type_mismatch_error(name: &str, expected: &str, actual: &str, val: &Value) -> StructuredError {\\\\n   288\\\\t    let val_str = val.to_string();\\\\n   289\\\\t    let truncated = if val_str.len() > 80 {\\\\n   290\\\\t        format!(\\\\\\\"{}...\\\\\\\", &val_str[..77])\\\\n   291\\\\t    } else {\\\\n   292\\\\t        val_str\\\\n   293\\\\t    };\\\\n   294\\\\t    StructuredError {\\\\n   295\\\\t        what: format!(\\\\\\\"type mismatch for variable '{name}'\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   296\\\\t        where_: format!(\\\\\\\"variable '{name}'\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   297\\\\t        why: format!(\\\\\\\"expected {expected}, got {actual}: {truncated}\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   298\\\\t        hint: format!(\\\\\\\"provide a {expected} value for '{name}'\\\\\\\"),\\\\n   299\\\\t    }\\\\n   300\\\\t}\\\\n   301\\\\t\\\\n   302\\\\tfn json_type_name(val: &Value) -> &'static str {\\\\n   303\\\\t    match val {\\\\n   304\\\\t        Value::Null => \\\\\\\"null\\\\\\\",\\\\n   305\\\\t        Value::Bool(_) => \\\\\\\"boolean\\\\\\\",\\\\n   306\\\\t        Value::Number(_) => \\\\\\\"number\\\\\\\",\\\\n   307\\\\t        Value::String(_) => \\\\\\\"string\\\\\\\",\\\\n   308\\\\t        Value::Array(_) => \\\\\\\"array\\\\\\\",\\\\n   309\\\\t        Value::Object(_) => \\\\\\\"object\\\\\\\",\\\\n   310\\\\t    }\\\\n   311\\\\t}\\\\n   312\\\\t\\\\n   313\\\\t// ──  5\\\\t    pub what: String,\\\\n     6\\\\t    #[serde(rename = \\\\\\\"where\\\\\\\")]\\\\n     7\\\\t    pub where_: String,\\\\n     8\\\\t    pub why: String,\\\\n     9\\\\t    pub hint: String,\\\\n    10\\\\t}\\\\n    11\\\\t\\\\n    12\\\\timpl fmt::Display for StructuredError {\\\\n    13\\\\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\\\n    14\\\\t self.why, self.hint\\\\n    18\\\\t        )\\\\n    19\\\\t    }\\\\n    20\\\\t}\\\\n    21\\\\t\\\\n    22\\\\t#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\\\\n    23\\\\t#[allow(dead_code)] // Variants used incrementally across phases\\\\n    24\\\\tpub enum JigError {\\\\n    25\\\\t    #[error(\\\\\\\"{0}\\\\\\\")]\\\\n    26\\\\t    RecipeValidation(StructuredError),\\\\n    27\\\\t    #[error(\\\\\\\"{0}\\\\\\\")]\\\\n    28\\\\t    TemplateRendering(StructuredError),\\\\n    29\\\\t    #[error(\\\\\\\"{0}\\\\\\\")]\\\\n    30\\\\t    FileOperation(StructuredError),\\\\n    31\\\\t format_variable_errors(errors: &[StructuredError]) -> String {\\\\n    36\\\\t    errors\\\\n    37\\\\t        .iter()\\\\n    38\\\\t        .map(|e| e.to_string())\\\\n    39\\\\t // Used in tests and later phases\\\\n    54\\\\t    pub fn structured_error(&self) -> &StructuredError {\\\\n    55\\\\t        match self {\\\\n    56\\\\t
