structured_errors(&self) -> &[StructuredError] {\\\\n    65\\\\t        match self {\\\\n    66\\\\t            JigError::RecipeValidation(e)\\\\n    67\\\\t            | JigError::TemplateRendering(e)\\\\n    68\\\\t            | JigError::FileOperation(e) => std::slice::from_ref(e),\\\\n    69\\\\t            JigError::VariableValidation(errors) => errors,\\\\n    70\\\\t        }\\\\n    71\\\\t    }\\\\n    72\\\\t}\\\\n    73\\\\t\\\\n    74\\\\t#[cfg(test)]\\\\n    75\\\\tmod tests {\\\\n    76\\\\t    use super::*;\\\\n    77\\\\t\\\\n    78\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n    79\\\\t    fn exit_code_mapping() {\\\\n    80\\\\t        let err = StructuredError {\\\\n    81\\\\t            what: \\\\\\\"test\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    82\\\\t            where_: \\\\\\\"test\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    83\\\\t            why: \\\\\\\"test\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    84\\\\t            hint: \\\\\\\"test\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    85\\\\t        };\\\\n    86\\\\t        assert_eq!(JigError::RecipeValidation(err.clone()).exit_code(), 1);\\\\n    87\\\\t        assert_eq!(JigError::TemplateRendering(err.clone()).exit_code(), 2);\\\\n    88\\\\t        assert_eq!(JigError::FileOperation(err.clone()).exit_code(), 3);\\\\n    89\\\\t        assert_eq!(JigError::VariableValidation(vec![err]).exit_code(), 4);\\\\n    90\\\\t    }\\\\n    91\\\\t\\\\n    92\\\\t    #[test]\\\\n    93\\\\t    fn structured_error_has_all_fields() {\\\\n    94\\\\t        let err = StructuredError {\\\\n    95\\\\t            what: \\\\\\\"file not found\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    96\\\\t            where_: \\\\\\\"recipe.yaml\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    97\\\\t            why: \\\\\\\"the file does not exist at the specified path\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n 6\\\\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\\\\n     7\\\\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\\\\n     8\\\\t\\\\n     9\\\\tuse crate::error::{JigError, StructuredError};\\\\n    10\\\\tuse crate::recipe::FileOp;\\\\n    11\\\\t\\\\n    12\\\\t// ── Execution context ─────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n    13\\\\t\\\\n    14\\\\t/// Shared state for operation execution.\\\\n    15\\\\tpub struct ExecutionContext {\\\\n    16\\\\t    /// Base directory for resolving output paths (default: cwd).\\\\n    17\\\\t    pub base_dir: PathBuf,\\\\n    18\\\\t    /// If true, produce output but write nothing to disk.\\\\n    19\\\\t    pub dry_run: bool,\\\\n    20\\\\t    /// If true, overwrite existing files on create.\\\\n    21\\\\t    pub force: bool,\\\\n    22\\\\t    /// Virtual file state for dry-run mode: path -> content.\\\\n    23\\\\t    /// Create ops populate this instead of writing to disk.\\\\n    24\\\\t    /// Inject ops read from here if the target was created in the same run.\\\\n    25\\\\t    pub virtual_files: HashMap<PathBuf, String>,\\\\n    26\\\\t}\\\\n    27\\\\t\\\\n    28\\\\timpl ExecutionContext {\\\\n    29\\\\t    pub fn new(base_dir: PathBuf, dry_run: bool, force: bool) -> Self {\\\\n    30\\\\t        Self {\\\\n    31\\\\t            base_dir,\\\\n    32\\\\t            dry_run,\\\\n    33\\\\t            force,\\\\n    34\\\\t            virtual_files: HashMap::new(),\\\\n    35\\\\t        }\\\\n    36\\\\t    }\\\\n    37\\\\t\\\\n    38\\\\t    /// Resolve an output path relative to the base directory.\\\\n    39\\\\t    /// Normalizes the path and rejects traversal outside base_dir.\\\\n    40\\\\t    pub fn resolve_path(&self, relative: &str) -> PathBuf {\\\\n    41\\\\t        let joined = self.base_dir.join(relative);\\\\n    42\\\\t        // Normalize by resolving . and .. components.\\\\n    43\\\\t        let mut normalized = PathBuf::new();\\\\n    44\\\\t        for component in joined.components() {\\\\n    45\\\\t            match component {\\\\n    46\\\\t                std::path::Component::ParentDir => { normalized.pop(); }\\\\n    47\\\\t                std::path::Component::CurDir => {}\\\\n    48\\\\t                c => normalized.push(c),\\\\n    49\\\\t            }\\\\n    50\\\\t        }\\\\n    51\\\\t        // If the normalized path escapes base_dir, clamp to base_dir.\\\\n    52\\\\t        if !normalized.starts_with(&self.base_dir) {\\\\n    53\\\\t            return self.base_dir.join(\\\\n    54\\\\t                std::path::Path::new(relative).file_name().unwrap_or_default()\\\\n    55\\\\t            );\\\\n    56\\\\t        }\\\\n    57\\\\t        normalized\\\\n    58\\\\t    }\\\\n    59\\\\t}\\\\n    60\\\\t\\\\n    61\\\\t// ── Operation result ──────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n    62\\\\t\\\\n    63\\\\t/// Diagnostic details for patch operations (included in verbose JSON output).\\\\n    64\\\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\\\\n    65\\\\tpub struct ScopeDiagnostics {\\\\n    66\\\\t    pub anchor_line: usize,\\\\n    67\\\\t    pub scope_start: usize,\\\\n    68\\\\t    pub scope_end: usize,\\\\n    69\\\\t    pub insertion_line: usize,\\\\n    70\\\\t    pub find_match_line: Option<usize>,\\\\n    71\\\\t    pub position_fallback: Option<(String, String)>,\\\\n    72\\\\t}\\\\n    73\\\\t\\\\n    74\\\\t/// Result of a single file operation.\\\\n    75\\\\t#[derive(Debug)]\\\\n    76\\\\tpub enum OpResult {\\\\n    77\\\\t    Success {\\\\n    78\\\\t        action: &'static str,\\\\n    79\\\\t        path: PathBuf,\\\\n    80\\\\t        lines: usize,\\\\n    81\\\\t        location: Option<String>,\\\\n    82\\\\t        rendered_content: Option<String>,\\\\n    83\\\\t        scope_diagnostics: Option<ScopeDiagnostics>,\\\\n    84\\\\t    },\\\\n    85\\\\t    Skip {\\\\n    86\\\\t        path: PathBuf,\\\\n    87\\\\t        reason: String,\\\\n    88\\\\t        rendered_content: Option<String>,\\\\n    89\\\\t    },\\\\n    90\\\\t    Error {\\\\n    91\\\\t        path: PathBuf,\\\\n    92\\\\t        error: StructuredError,\\\\n    93\\\\t        rendered_content: String,\\\\n    94\\\\t    },\\\\n    95\\\\t}\\\\n    96\\\\t\\\\n    97\\\\timpl OpResult {\\\\n    98\\\\t    pub fn path(&self) -> &Path {\\\\n    99\\\\t        match self {\\\\n   100\\\\t            OpResult::Success { path, .. }\\\\n   101\\\\t            | OpResult::Skip { path, .. }\\\\n   102\\\\t            | OpResult::Error { path, .. } => path,\\\\n   103\\\\t        }\\\\n   104\\\\t    }\\\\n   105\\\\t\\\\n   106\\\\t    pub fn is_error(&self) -> bool {\\\\n   107\\\\t        matches!(self, OpResult::Error { .. })\\\\n   108\\\\t    }\\\\n   109\\\\t\\\\n   110\\\\t    pub fn is_write(&self) -> bool {\\\\n   111\\\\t        matches!(self, OpResult::Success { .. })\\\\n   112\\\\t    }\\\\n   113\\\\t}\\\\n   114\\\\t\\\\n   115\\\\t// ── Dispatch ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   116\\\\t\\\\n   117\\\\t/// Pre-rendered operation ready for execution.\\\\n   118\\\\tpub struct PreparedOp {\\\\n   119\\\\t    pub file_op: FileOp,\\\\n   120\\\\t    pub rendered_content: String,\\\\n   121\\\\t    pub rendered_path: String,\\\\n   122\\\\t    /// For inject: rendered skip_if string (if any).\\\\n   123\\\\t    pub rendered_skip_if: Option<String>,\\\\n   124\\\\t}\\\\n   125\\\\t\\\\n   126\\\\t/// Execute a single prepared operation.\\\\n   127\\\\tpub fn execute_operation(\\\\n   128\\\\t    prepared: &PreparedOp,\\\\n   129\\\\t    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\n   130\\\\t    verbose: bool,\\\\n   131\\\\t) -> OpResult {\\\\n   132\\\\t    match &prepared.file_op {\\\\n   133\\\\t        FileOp::Create { skip_if_exists, .. } => {\\\\n   134\\\\t            create::execute(\\\\n   135\\\\t                &prepared.rendered_path,\\\\n   136\\\\t                &prepared.rendered_content,\\\\n   137\\\\t                *skip_if_exists,\\\\n   138\\\\t                ctx,\\\\n   139\\\\t                verbose,\\\\n   140\\\\t            )\\\\n   141\\\\t        }\\\\n   142\\\\t        FileOp::Inject { mode, .. } => {\\\\n   143\\\\t            inject::execute(\\\\n   144\\\\t                &prepared.rendered_path,\\\\n   145\\\\t                &prepared.rendered_content,\\\\n   146\\\\t                prepared.rendered_skip_if.as_deref(),\\\\n   147\\\\t                mode,\\\\n   148\\\\t                ctx,\\\\n   149\\\\t                verbose,\\\\n   150\\\\t            )\\\\n   151\\\\t        }\\\\n   152\\\\t        FileOp::Replace { spec, fallback, .. } => {\\\\n   153\\\\t            replace::execute(\\\\n   154\\\\t                &prepared.rendered_path,\\\\n   155\\\\t                &prepared.rendered_content,\\\\n   156\\\\t                spec,\\\\n   157\\\\t                fallback,\\\\n   158\\\\t                ctx,\\\\n   159\\\\t                verbose,\\\\n   160\\\\t            )\\\\n   161\\\\t        }\\\\n   162\\\\t        FileOp::Patch { anchor, .. } => {\\\\n   163\\\\t            patch::execute(\\\\n   164\\\\t                &prepared.rendered_path,\\\\n   165\\\\t                &prepared.rendered_content,\\\\n   166\\\\t                prepared.rendered_skip_if.as_deref(),\\\\n   167\\\\t                anchor,\\\\n   168\\\\t                ctx,\\\\n   169\\\\t                verbose,\\\\n   170\\\\t            )\\\\n   171\\\\t        }\\\\n   172\\\\t    }\\\\n   173\\\\t}\\\\n   174\\\\t\\\\n   175\\\\t/// Convert an OpResult::Error into a JigError for fail-fast propagation.\\\\n   176\\\\tpub fn op_error_to_jig_error(result: &OpResult) -> Option<JigError> {\\\\n   177\\\\t    match result {\\\\n   178\\\\t        OpResult::Error { error, .. } => Some(JigError::FileOperation(error.clone())),\\\\n   179\\\\t        _ => None,\\\\n   180\\\\t    }\\\\n   181\\\\t}\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n -n '1,340p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"     1\\\\tuse regex::Regex;\\\\n     2\\\\t\\\\n     3\\\\tuse crate::error::StructuredError;\\\\n     4\\\\tuse crate::recipe::{InjectMode, MatchPosition};\\\\n     5\\\\t\\\\n     6\\\\tuse super::{ExecutionContext, OpResult};\\\\n     7\\\\t\\\\n     8\\\\t/// Execute an inject operation: insert rendered content into an existing file.\\\\n     9\\\\t///\\\\n    10\\\\t/// - `rendered_path` is the already-rendered target file path.\\\\n    11\\\\t/// - `rendered_content` is the template output to inject.\\\\n    12\\\\t/// - `rendered_skip_if` is the rendered skip_if string (if any).\\\\n    13\\\\t/// - `mode` determines where to inject (after/before regex, prepend, append).\\\\n    14\\\\t/// - Reads from virtual_files if target was created in same run, else from disk.\\\\n    15\\\\t/// - Updates virtual_files with post-injection content for subsequent operations.\\\\n    16\\\\tpub fn execute(\\\\n    17\\\\t    rendered_path: &str,\\\\n    18\\\\t    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n    19\\\\t    rendered_skip_if: Option<&str>,\\\\n    20\\\\t    mode: &InjectMode,\\\\n    21\\\\t    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\n    22\\\\t    verbose: bool,\\\\n    23\\\\t) -> OpResult {\\\\n    24\\\\t    let target = ctx.resolve_path(rendered_path);\\\\n    25\\\\t    let injected_lines = rendered_content.lines().count();\\\\n    26\\\\t    let content_for_verbose = if verbose {\\\\n    27\\\\t        Some(rendered_content.to_string())\\\\n    28\\\\t    } else {\\\\n    29\\\\t        None\\\\n    30\\\\t    };\\\\n    31\\\\t\\\\n    32\\\\t    // Read target file content: prefer virtual_files, then disk.\\\\n    33\\\\t    let file_content = if let Some(content) = ctx.virtual_files.get(&target) {\\\\n    34\\\\t        content.clone()\\\\n    35\\\\t    } else {\\\\n    36\\\\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(&target) {\\\\n    37\\\\t            Ok(content) => content,\\\\n    38\\\\t            Err(_) => {\\\\n    39\\\\t                return OpResult::Error {\\\\n    40\\\\t                    path: target.clone(),\\\\n    41\\\\t                    error: StructuredError {\\\\n    42\\\\t                        what: format!(\\\\\\\"target file not found: '{}'\\\\\\\", target.display()),\\\\n    43\\\\t                        where_: target.display().to_string(),\\\\n    44\\\\t                        why: \\\\\\\"the file does not exist and was not created by a prior operation in this run\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    45\\\\t                        hint: \\\\\\\"ensure the target file exists, or add a create operation before this inject\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    46\\\\t                    },\\\\n    47\\\\t                    rendered_content: rendered_content.to_string(),\\\\n    48\\\\t                };\\\\n    49\\\\t            }\\\\n    50\\\\t        }\\\\n    51\\\\t    };\\\\n    52\\\\t\\\\n    53\\\\t    // Check skip_if: if the rendered string is found in the file, skip.\\\\n    54\\\\t    if let Some(skip_str) = rendered_skip_if\\\\n    55\\\\t        && file_content.contains(skip_str)\\\\n    56\\\\t    {\\\\n    57\\\\t        return OpResult::Skip {\\\\n    58\\\\t            path: target,\\\\n    59\\\\t            reason: format!(\\\\\\\"skip_if matched: {skip_str}\\\\\\\"),\\\\n    60\\\\t            rendered_content: content_for_verbose,\\\\n    61\\\\t        };\\\\n    62\\\\t    }\\\\n    63\\\\t\\\\n    64\\\\t    // Perform injection based on mode.\\\\n    65\\\\t    let (new_content, location) = match mode {\\\\n    66\\\\t        InjectMode::After { pattern, at } => {\\\\n    67\\\\t            match inject_after_before(&file_content, rendered_content, pattern, at, true, &target) {\\\\n    68\\\\t                Ok(v) => v,\\\\n    69\\\\t                Err(op_result) => return op_result,\\\\n    70\\\\t            }\\\\n    71\\\\t        }\\\\n    72\\\\t        InjectMode::Before { pattern, at } => {\\\\n    73\\\\t            match inject_after_before(&file_content, rendered_content, pattern, at, false, &target) {\\\\n    74\\\\t                Ok(v) => v,\\\\n    75\\\\t                Err(op_result) => return op_result,\\\\n    76\\\\t            }\\\\n    77\\\\t        }\\\\n    78\\\\t        InjectMode::Prepend => {\\\\n    79\\\\t            let mut result = String::with_capacity(rendered_content.len() + file_content.len() + 1);\\\\n    80\\\\t            result.push_str(rendered_content);\\\\n    81\\\\t            if !rendered_content.ends_with('\\\\\\\\n') && !file_content.is_empty() {\\\\n    82\\\\t                result.push('\\\\\\\\n');\\\\n    83\\\\t            }\\\\n    84\\\\t            result.push_str(&file_content);\\\\n    85\\\\t            (result, \\\\\\\"prepend\\\\\\\".to_string())\\\\n    86\\\\t        }\\\\n    87\\\\t        InjectMode::Append => {\\\\n    88\\\\t            let mut result = String::with_capacity(file_content.len() + rendered_content.len() + 1);\\\\n    89\\\\t            result.push_str(&file_content);\\\\n    90\\\\t            if !file_content.ends_with('\\\\\\\\n') && !file_content.is_empty() {\\\\n    91\\\\t                result.push('\\\\\\\\n');\\\\n    92\\\\t            }\\\\n    93\\\\t            result.push_str(rendered_content);\\\\n    94\\\\t            (result, \\\\\\\"append\\\\\\\".to_string())\\\\n    95\\\\t        }\\\\n    96\\\\t    };\\\\n    97\\\\t\\\\n    98\\\\t    // Write the modified content.\\\\n    99\\\\t    if ctx.dry_run {\\\\n   100\\\\t        ctx.virtual_files.insert(target.clone(), new_content);\\\\n   101\\\\t    } else {\\\\n   102\\\\t        if let Err(e) = std::fs::write(&target, &new_content) {\\\\n   103\\\\t            return OpResult::Error {\\\\n   104\\\\t                path: target.clone(),\\\\n   105\\\\t                error: StructuredError {\\\\n   106\\\\t                    what: format!(\\\\\\\"cannot write file '{}'\\\\\\\", target.display()),\\\\n   107\\\\t                    where_: target.display().to_string(),\\\\n   108\\\\t                    why: e.to_string(),\\\\n   109\\\\t                    hint: \\\\\\\"check file permissions\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   110\\\\t                },\\\\n   111\\\\t                rendered_content: rendered_content.to_string(),\\\\n   112\\\\t            };\\\\n   113\\\\t        }\\\\n   114\\\\t        // Also update virtual_files so subsequent ops in this run see the new content.\\\\n   115\\\\t        ctx.virtual_files.insert(target.clone(), new_content);\\\\n   116\\\\t    }\\\\n   117\\\\t\\\\n   118\\\\t    OpResult::Success {\\\\n   119\\\\t        action: \\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\",\\\\n   120\\\\t        path: target,\\\\n   121\\\\t        lines: injected_lines,\\\\n   122\\\\t        location: Some(location),\\\\n   123\\\\t        rendered_content: content_for_verbose,\\\\n   124\\\\t        scope_diagnostics: None,\\\\n   125\\\\t    }\\\\n   126\\\\t}\\\\n   127\\\\t\\\\n   128\\\\t/// Inject content after or before a regex match.\\\\n   129\\\\t/// Returns (new_content, location_description) or an OpResult::Error.\\\\n   130\\\\t#[allow(clippy::result_large_err)]\\\\n   131\\\\tfn inject_after_before(\\\\n   132\\\\t    file_content: &str,\\\\n   133\\\\t    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n   134\\\\t    pattern: &str,\\\\n   135\\\\t    at: &MatchPosition,\\\\n   136\\\\t    is_after: bool,\\\\n   137\\\\t    target: &std::path::Path,\\\\n   138\\\\t) -> Result<(String, String), OpResult> {\\\\n   139\\\\t    let re = Regex::new(pattern).expect(\\\\\\\"regex was validated at parse time\\\\\\\");\\\\n   140\\\\t\\\\n   141\\\\t    let lines: Vec<&str> = file_content.lines().collect();\\\\n   142\\\\t    let matching_indices: Vec<usize> = lines\\\\n   143\\\\t        .iter()\\\\n   144\\\\t        .enumerate()\\\\n   145\\\\t        .filter(|(_, line)| re.is_match(line))\\\\n   146\\\\t        .map(|(i, _)| i)\\\\n   147\\\\t        .collect();\\\\n   148\\\\t\\\\n   149\\\\t    if matching_indices.is_empty() {\\\\n   150\\\\t        return Err(OpResult::Error {\\\\n   151\\\\t            path: target.to_path_buf(),\\\\n   152\\\\t            error: StructuredError {\\\\n   153\\\\t                what: format!(\\\\\\\"regex pattern matched no lines in '{}'\\\\\\\", target.display()),\\\\n   154\\\\t                where_: target.display().to_string(),\\\\n   155\\\\t                why: format!(\\\\\\\"pattern '{}' did not match any line in the file\\\\\\\", pattern),\\\\n   156\\\\t                hint: \\\\\\\"check the regex pattern against the file contents\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   157\\\\t            },\\\\n   158\\\\t            rendered_content: rendered_content.to_string(),\\\\n   159\\\\t        });\\\\n   160\\\\t    }\\\\n   161\\\\t\\\\n   162\\\\t    let match_idx = match at {\\\\n   163\\\\t        MatchPosition::First => matching_indices[0],\\\\n   164\\\\t        MatchPosition::Last => *matching_indices.last().unwrap(),\\\\n   165\\\\t    };\\\\n   166\\\\t\\\\n   167\\\\t    // Build new content by inserting at the right position.\\\\n   168\\\\t    let direction = if is_after { \\\\\\\"after\\\\\\\" } else { \\\\\\\"before\\\\\\\" };\\\\n   169\\\\t    let at_str = match at {\\\\n   170\\\\t        MatchPosition::First => \\\\\\\"first\\\\\\\",\\\\n   171\\\\t        MatchPosition::Last => \\\\\\\"last\\\\\\\",\\\\n   172\\\\t    };\\\\n   173\\\\t    // Line numbers are 1-based for human display.\\\\n   174\\\\t    let location = format!(\\\\\\\"{direction}:{pattern} ({at_str} match, line {})\\\\\\\", match_idx + 1);\\\\n   175\\\\t\\\\n   176\\\\t    let mut result = String::new();\\\\n   177\\\\t    let has_trailing_newline = file_content.ends_with('\\\\\\\\n');\\\\n   178\\\\t\\\\n   179\\\\t    if is_after {\\\\n   180\\\\t        // Insert after match_idx line.\\\\n   181\\\\t        for (i, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\\\\n   182\\\\t            result.push_str(line);\\\\n   183\\\\t            result.push('\\\\\\\\n');\\\\n   184\\\\t            if i == match_idx {\\\\n   185\\\\t                result.push_str(rendered_content);\\\\n   186\\\\t                if !rendered_content.ends_with('\\\\\\\\n') {\\\\n   187\\\\t                    result.push('\\\\\\\\n');\\\\n   188\\\\t                }\\\\n   189\\\\t            }\\\\n   190\\\\t        }\\\\n   191\\\\t    } else {\\\\n   192\\\\t        // Insert before match_idx line.\\\\n   193\\\\t        for (i, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\\\\n   194\\\\t            if i == match_idx {\\\\n   195\\\\t                result.push_str(rendered_content);\\\\n   196\\\\t                if !rendered_content.ends_with('\\\\\\\\n') {\\\\n   197\\\\t                    result.push('\\\\\\\\n');\\\\n   198\\\\t                }\\\\n   199\\\\t            }\\\\n   200\\\\t            result.push_str(line);\\\\n   201\\\\t            result.push('\\\\\\\\n');\\\\n   202\\\\t        }\\\\n   203\\\\t    }\\\\n   204\\\\t\\\\n   205\\\\t    // Preserve original trailing newline behavior.\\\\n   206\\\\t    if !has_trailing_newline && result.ends_with('\\\\\\\\n') {\\\\n   207\\\\t        result.pop();\\\\n   208\\\\t    }\\\\n   209\\\\t\\\\n   210\\\\t    Ok((result, location))\\\\n   211\\\\t}\\\\n   212\\\\t\\\\n   213\\\\t// ── Tests -n '1,260p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"     1\\\\tuse crate::error::StructuredError;\\\\n     2\\\\t\\\\n     3\\\\tuse super::{ExecutionContext, OpResult};\\\\n     4\\\\t\\\\n     5\\\\t/// Execute a create operation: write rendered content to a file.\\\\n     6\\\\t///\\\\n     7\\\\t/// - Renders `to` path already resolved by caller.\\\\n     8\\\\t/// - Creates parent directories as needed.\\\\n     9\\\\t/// - Respects skip_if_exists, --force, --dry-run, --base-dir.\\\\n    10\\\\tpub fn execute(\\\\n    11\\\\t    rendered_path: &str,\\\\n    12\\\\t    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n    13\\\\t    skip_if_exists: bool,\\\\n    14\\\\t    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\n    15\\\\t    verbose: bool,\\\\n    16\\\\t) -> OpResult {\\\\n    17\\\\t    let target = ctx.resolve_path(rendered_path);\\\\n    18\\\\t    let lines = rendered_content.lines().count();\\\\n    19\\\\t    let content_for_verbose = if verbose {\\\\n    20\\\\t        Some(rendered_content.to_string())\\\\n    21\\\\t    } else {\\\\n    22\\\\t        None\\\\n    23\\\\t    };\\\\n    24\\\\t\\\\n    25\\\\t    // Check if file already exists (on disk or in virtual state).\\\\n    26\\\\t    let exists = target.exists() || ctx.virtual_files.contains_key(&target);\\\\n    27\\\\t\\\\n    28\\\\t    if exists && !ctx.force {\\\\n    29\\\\t        if skip_if_exists {\\\\n    30\\\\t            return OpResult::Skip {\\\\n    31\\\\t                path: target,\\\\n    32\\\\t                reason: \\\\\\\"file already exists (skip_if_exists: true)\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    33\\\\t                rendered_content: content_for_verbose,\\\\n    34\\\\t            };\\\\n    35\\\\t        } else {\\\\n    36\\\\t            return OpResult::Error {\\\\n    37\\\\t                path: target.clone(),\\\\n    38\\\\t                error: StructuredError {\\\\n    39\\\\t                    what: format!(\\\\\\\"file already exists: '{}'\\\\\\\", target.display()),\\\\n    40\\\\t                    where_: target.display().to_string(),\\\\n    41\\\\t                    why: \\\\\\\"skip_if_exists is false and --force was not specified\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    42\\\\t                    hint: \\\\\\\"use --force to overwrite, or set skip_if_exists: true in the recipe\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    43\\\\t                },\\\\n    44\\\\t                rendered_content: rendered_content.to_string(),\\\\n    45\\\\t            };\\\\n    46\\\\t        }\\\\n    47\\\\t    }\\\\n    48\\\\t\\\\n    49\\\\t    if ctx.dry_run {\\\\n    50\\\\t        // Record in virtual file state but don't write to disk.\\\\n    51\\\\t        ctx.virtual_files.insert(target.clone(), rendered_content.to_string());\\\\n    52\\\\t        return OpResult::Success {\\\\n    53\\\\t            action: \\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\",\\\\n    54\\\\t            path: target,\\\\n    55\\\\t            lines,\\\\n    56\\\\t 61\\\\t\\\\n    62\\\\t    // Create parent directories.\\\\n    63\\\\t    if let Some(parent) = target.parent().filter(|p| !p.as_os_str().is_empty())\\\\n    64\\\\t        && let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(parent) {\\\\n    65\\\\t            let parent_display = parent.display().to_string();\\\\n    66\\\\t            return OpResult::Error {\\\\n    67\\\\t                path: target,\\\\n    68\\\\t                error: StructuredError {\\\\n    69\\\\t                    what: format!(\\\\\\\"cannot create parent directory '{}'\\\\\\\", parent_display),\\\\n    70\\\\t 73\\\\t                },\\\\n    74\\\\t                rendered_content: rendered_content.to_string(),\\\\n    75\\\\t            };\\\\n    76\\\\t        }\\\\n    77\\\\t\\\\n    78\\\\t    // Write the file.\\\\n    79\\\\t    if let Err(e) = std::fs::write(&target, rendered_content) {\\\\n    80\\\\t        return OpResult::Error {\\\\n    81\\\\t            path: target.clone(),\\\\n action: \\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\",\\\\n    94\\\\t        path: target,\\\\n    95\\\\t        lines,\\\\n    96\\\\t        location: None,\\\\n    97\\\\t        rendered_content: 23\\\\t\\\\n    24\\\\t    // Read target file: prefer virtual_files, then disk.\\\\n    25\\\\t    let file_content = if let Some(content) = ctx.virtual_files.get(&target) {\\\\n    26\\\\t {\\\\n    31\\\\t                return OpResult::Error {\\\\n    32\\\\t                    path: target.clone(),\\\\n    33\\\\t                    error: StructuredError {\\\\n    34\\\\t MatchResult::Found { start_idx, end_idx, mode } => {\\\\n    55\\\\t            // Build new content by replacing the matched region.\\\\n    56\\\\t            let (new_content, location) = match mode {\\\\n    57\\\\t                MatchMode::Between => {\\\\n    58\\\\t                    // Exclusive: preserve marker lines, replace content between.\\\\n    59\\\\t                    let mut result_lines: Vec<&str> = Vec::new();\\\\n    60\\\\t                    for (i, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\\\\n    61\\\\t                        if i <= start_idx || i >= end_idx {\\\\n end_idx + 1, replaced_count,\\\\n    76\\\\t                    );\\\\n    77\\\\t                    (new, loc)\\\\n    78\\\\t                }\\\\n    79\\\\t                MatchMode::Pattern => {\\\\n    80\\\\t                    // Inclusive: replace matched lines entirely.\\\\n    81\\\\t                    let mut result_lines: Vec<&str> = Vec::new();\\\\n    82\\\\t result\\\\n   149\\\\t}\\\\n   150\\\\t\\\\n   151\\\\tenum MatchMode {\\\\n   152\\\\t    Between,\\\\n   153\\\\t    Pattern,\\\\n   154\\\\t}\\\\n   155\\\\t\\\\n   156\\\\tenum MatchResult {\\\\n match_between(lines: &[&str], start_pattern: &str, end_pattern: &str, target: &std::path::Path) -> MatchResult {\\\\n   163\\\\t    let start_re = Regex::new(start_pattern).expect(\\\\\\\"regex start_idx, end_idx, mode: MatchMode::Between }\\\\n   189\\\\t}\\\\n   190\\\\t\\\\n   191\\\\tfn match_pattern(lines: &[&str], pattern: &str) -> MatchResult {\\\\n   192\\\\t    let re = Regex::new(pattern).expect(\\\\\\\"regex validated at parse time\\\\\\\");\\\\n   193\\\\t\\\\n   194\\\\t    // Find first contiguous block of matching lines.\\\\n   195\\\\t    let first = match lines.iter().position(|line| re.is_match(line)) {\\\\n   196\\\\t        Some(idx) => idx,\\\\n   197\\\\t        None => return MatchResult::NoMatch,\\\\n   198\\\\t    };\\\\n   199\\\\t\\\\n   200\\\\t    let mut last = first;\\\\n   201\\\\t    for i in (first + 1)..lines.len() {\\\\n   202\\\\t        if re.is_match(lines[i]) {\\\\n   203\\\\t            last = i;\\\\n   204\\\\t        } else {\\\\n   205\\\\t            break;\\\\n   206\\\\t        }\\\\n   207\\\\t    }\\\\n   208\\\\t\\\\n   209\\\\t    MatchResult::Found { start_idx: first, end_idx: last, mode: MatchMode::Pattern }\\\\n   210\\\\t}\\\\n   211\\\\t\\\\n   212\\\\tfn apply_fallback(\\\\n   213\\\\t    file_content: &str,\\\\n   214\\\\t    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n   215\\\\t    fallback: &Fallback,\\\\n fallback {\\\\n   221\\\\t        Fallback::Append => {\\\\n   222\\\\t            let mut new_content = file_content.to_string();\\\\n   223\\\\t            if !new_content.ends_with('\\\\\\\\n') && !new_content.is_empty() {\\\\n   224\\\\t                new_content.push('\\\\\\\\n');\\\\n   225\\\\t            }\\\\n   226\\\\t            new_content.push_str(rendered_content);\\\\n   227\\\\t rendered_content.lines().count(),\\\\n   234\\\\t                location: Some(\\\\\\\"fallback:append\\\\\\\".to_string()),\\\\n   235\\\\t                rendered_content: content_for_verbose,\\\\n   236\\\\t                scope_diagnostics: None,\\\\n   237\\\\t            }\\\\n   238\\\\t        }\\\\n   239\\\\t        Fallback::Prepend => {\\\\n   240\\\\t            let mut new_content = content_for_verbose,\\\\n   255\\\\t                scope_diagnostics: None,\\\\n   256\\\\t            }\\\\n   257\\\\t        }\\\\n   258\\\\t        Fallback::Skip => {\\\\n   259\\\\t target.display()),\\\\n   270\\\\t                    where_: target.display().to_string(),\\\\n   271\\\\t                    why: \\\\\\\"the match pattern did not find any matching region in the file\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   272\\\\t                    hint: \\\\\\\"check the pattern/between markers against the file contents, or set fallback: skip/append/prepend\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n   273\\\\t 279\\\\t\\\\n   280\\\\tfn write_back(\\\\n   281\\\\t    target: &std::path::Path,\\\\n   282\\\\t    new_content: &str,\\\\n   283\\\\t    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n   284\\\\t    ctx: &mut
