ScopeType};\\\\n     5\\\\tuse crate::scope;\\\\n     6\\\\t\\\\n     7\\\\tuse super::{ExecutionContext, OpResult};\\\\n     8\\\\t\\\\n     9\\\\t/// Execute a patch operation: anchor→scope→find→position→insert.\\\\n    10\\\\tpub fn execute(\\\\n    11\\\\t    rendered_path: &str,\\\\n    12\\\\t    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n    13\\\\t    rendered_skip_if: Option<&str>,\\\\n    14\\\\t    anchor: &Anchor,\\\\n    15\\\\t    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\n    16\\\\t    verbose: bool,\\\\n    17\\\\t) -> OpResult {\\\\n    18\\\\t    let target = ctx.resolve_path(rendered_path);\\\\n    19\\\\t    let content_for_verbose = if verbose {\\\\n    20\\\\t        Some(rendered_content.to_string())\\\\n    21\\\\t    } else {\\\\n    22\\\\t        None\\\\n    23\\\\t    };\\\\n    24\\\\t\\\\n    25\\\\t    // Read target file: prefer virtual_files, then disk.\\\\n    26\\\\t    let file_content = if let Some(content) = ctx.virtual_files.get(&target) {\\\\n    27\\\\t        content.clone()\\\\n    28\\\\t    } else {\\\\n    29\\\\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(&target) {\\\\n    30\\\\t            Ok(content) => content,\\\\n    31\\\\t            Err(_) => {\\\\n    32\\\\t                return OpResult::Error {\\\\n    33\\\\t                    path: target.clone(),\\\\n    34\\\\t                    error: StructuredError {\\\\n    35\\\\t                        what: format!(\\\\\\\"target file not found: '{}'\\\\\\\", target.display()),\\\\n    36\\\\t                        where_: target.display().to_string(),\\\\n    37\\\\t                        why: \\\\\\\"the file does not exist and was not created by a prior operation in this run\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    38\\\\t                        hint: \\\\\\\"ensure the target file exists, or add a create operation before this patch\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    39\\\\t                    },\\\\n    40\\\\t                    rendered_content: rendered_content.to_string(),\\\\n    41\\\\t                };\\\\n    42\\\\t            }\\\\n    43\\\\t        }\\\\n    44\\\\t    };\\\\n    45\\\\t\\\\n    46\\\\t    // Check skip_if: search entire file content.\\\\n    47\\\\t    if let Some(skip_str) = rendered_skip_if {\\\\n    48\\\\t        if file_content.contains(skip_str) {\\\\n    49\\\\t            return OpResult::Skip {\\\\n    50\\\\t                path: target,\\\\n    51\\\\t                reason: format!(\\\\\\\"skip_if matched: {skip_str}\\\\\\\"),\\\\n    52\\\\t                rendered_content: content_for_verbose,\\\\n    53\\\\t            };\\\\n    54\\\\t        }\\\\n    55\\\\t    }\\\\n    56\\\\t\\\\n    57\\\\t    let lines: Vec<&str> = file_content.lines().collect();\\\\n    58\\\\t\\\\n    59\\\\t    // Find anchor line.\\\\n    60\\\\t    let anchor_re = Regex::new(&anchor.pattern).expect(\\\\\\\"regex validated at parse time\\\\\\\");\\\\n    61\\\\t    let anchor_line = match lines.iter().position(|line| anchor_re.is_match(line)) {\\\\n    62\\\\t        Some(idx) => idx,\\\\n    63\\\\t        None => {\\\\n    64\\\\t            return OpResult::Error {\\\\n    65\\\\t                path: target.clone(),\\\\n    66\\\\t                error: StructuredError {\\\\n    67\\\\t                    what: format!(\\\\\\\"anchor pattern '{}' not found\\\\\\\", anchor.pattern),\\\\n    68\\\\t                    where_: target.display().to_string(),\\\\n    69\\\\t                    why: format!(\\\\\\\"pattern '{}' did not match any line in '{}'\\\\\\\", anchor.pattern, rendered_path),\\\\n    70\\\\t                    hint: \\\\\\\"check the anchor pattern against the file contents\\\\\\\".into(),\\\\n    71\\\\t                },\\\\n    72\\\\t                rendered_content: rendered_content.to_string(),\\\\n    73\\\\t            };\\\\n    74\\\\t        }\\\\n    75\\\\t    };\\\\n    76\\\\t\\\\n    77\\\\t    // scope: line degenerate case — just insert after anchor.\\\\n    78\\\\t    if anchor.scope == ScopeType::Line {\\\\n    79\\\\t        let mut result_lines: Vec<String> = lines.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect();\\\\n    80\\\\t        let indent = detect_anchor_indent(&lines, anchor_line);\\\\n    81\\\\t        let adjusted = adjust_indentation(rendered_content, &indent);\\\\n    82\\\\t        let insert_at = anchor_line + 1;\\\\n    83\\\\t        for (j, ins_line) in adjusted.lines().enumerate() {\\\\n    84\\\\t            result_lines.insert(insert_at + j, ins_line.to_string());\\\\n    85\\\\t        }\\\\n    86\\\\t        let new_content = join_owned_lines(&result_lines, file_content.ends_with('\\\\\\\\n'));\\\\n    87\\\\t        if let Some(err) = write_back(&target, &new_content, rendered_content, ctx) {\\\\n    88\\\\t            return err;\\\\n    89\\\\t        }\\\\n    90\\\\t\\\\n    91\\\\t        return OpResult::Success {\\\\n    92\\\\t            action: \\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\",\\\\n    93\\\\t            path: target,\\\\n    94\\\\t            lines: rendered_content.lines().count(),\\\\n    95\\\\t            location: Some(format!(\\\\\\\"line({}):after\\\\\\\", anchor.pattern)),\\\\n    96\\\\t            rendered_content: content_for_verbose,\\\\n    97\\\\t            scope_diagnostics: None,\\\\n    98\\\\t        };\\\\n    99\\\\t    }\\\\n   100\\\\t\\\\n   101\\\\t    // Detect scope.\\\\n   102\\\\t    let scope_result = match scope::detect_scope(&lines, anchor_line, &anchor.scope) {\\\\n   103\\\\t        Ok(s) => s,\\\\n   104\\\\t        Err(e) => {\\\\n   105\\\\t            return OpResult::Error {\\\\n   106\\\\t                path: target,\\\\n   107\\\\t                error: StructuredError {\\\\n   108\\\\t                    what: format!(\\\\\\\"scope detection failed: {}\\\\\\\", e.what),\\\\n   109\\\\t                    where_: e.where_,\\\\n   110\\\\t              ons — after/before/prepend/append, at:first/at:last, skip_if\\n   174\\t- [ ] 5.4: Fixtures for error cases — missing vars, bad type, missing template, missing target file, regex no-match, malformed YAML, file exists without force\\n   175\\t- [ ] 5.5: Fixtures for combined operations — create + inject in one recipe, multi-file recipe, idempotency (run twice)\\n   176\\t- [ ] 5.6: insta snapshot tests for JSON output format, human output format, error message format\\n   177\\t- [ ] 5.7: Determinism test — run same recipe twice, assert byte-identical output (AC-N1.1)\\n   178\\t\\n   179\\t#### Validation Criteria\\n   180\\t- `cargo test` runs all unit + integration + snapshot tests green\\n   181\\t- Every operation mode has at least one fixture\\n   182\\t- Every error exit code (1-4) has at least one fixture\\n   183\\t- Adding a new test case requires only a new directory, no code changes. Integration fixtures are auto-discovered from directories (no code changes to add tests). Spec-level unit tests are named functions in `#[cfg(test)]` modules (e.g., `spec::fr1::ac_1_1`). The two layers serve different purposes.\\n   184\\t- Idempotency fixture: second run produces all skips, no file changes\\n   185\\t- Binary has no dynamic dependencies beyond system libc (verified via `otool -L` on macOS, `ldd` on Linux)\\n   186\\t- Every `TEST-*` ID in SPEC.md has a corresponding test function named `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}` or `spec::nfr{N}::ac_n{N}_{M}`\\n   187\\t- Error fixtures assert that error JSON contains `what`, `where`, `why`, and `hint` fields (at least one fixture per exit code)\\n   188\\t\\n   189\\t#### Key Files\\n   190\\t- `tests/integration.rs`\\n   191\\t- `tests/fixtures/` (directory tree)\\n   192\\t\\n   193\\t#### Dependencies\\n   194\\t- insta crate for snapshot testing\\n   195\\t- assert_cmd or similar for subprocess testing (or raw std::process::Command)\\n   196\\t\\n   197\\t## Dependencies\\n   198\\t\\n   199\\t- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\n   200\\t- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\n   201\\t\\n   202\\t## Key Decisions\\n   203\\t\\n   204\\t| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\n   205\\t|----------|--------|-----------|\\n   206\\t| Operations as enum, not trait objects | `FileOp` enum with match dispatch | Only 2 variants in v0.1 (create, inject). Trait abstraction adds complexity for no benefit until user-defined operations exist, which isn't planned. (ARCHITECTURE D-4) |\\n   207\\t| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages (ARCHITECTURE D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\n   208\\t| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable (ARCHITECTURE D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\n   209\\t| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism (ARCHITECTURE D-5, I-1) |\\n   210\\t| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate, good Display/Error trait impls |\\n   211\\t| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\n   212\\t| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\n   213\\t| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling. Acceptable dependency for correctness. |\\n   214\\t| \\\"did you mean?\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) against declared variable names | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Hand-rolling edit distance serves I-10 (graceful degradation) — the error tells the caller exactly what to fix. |\\n   215\\t| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves, even for ones minijinja provides (replace, indent, join) | We control exact behavior. No surprises from upstream minijinja changes. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\n   216\\t| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. Templates reference variables not created files. (D-1) |\\n   217\\t| Extra variables pass through | Don't error or warn on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness here breaks real usage. |\\n   218\\t| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later operations may depend on earlier ones (e.g., inject targets a prior create). (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\n   219\\t\\n   220\\t## Risks / Open Questions\\n   221\\t\\n   222\\t- **TTY detection on all platforms**: Need to verify `std::io::IsTerminal` works correctly on macOS + Linux. Rust 1.70+ has `IsTerminal` in std — prefer that over external crate.\\n   223\\t- **stdin variable reading conflicts**: If --vars-stdin is used, stdin is consumed for variables. This is fine since jig is non-interactive (I-3), but document that stdin can only be used for one purpose per invocation.\\n   224\\t- **Large file handling for inject**: The current design reads the entire target file into memory for injection. This is fine for source files (typically <10K lines) but should be documented as a known limitation.\\n   225\\t- **pluralizer crate coverage**: Verify `pluralizer` handles irregular plurals (person→people, child→children). Fallback: `inflector` or hand-rolled rules for common irregulars.\\n   226\\t- **minijinja built-in filter override**: Verify minijinja allows overriding built-in `indent` filter with our version. If not, use `set_unknown_method_callback` or disable built-ins before registering. Needs spike before Phase 2.\\n   227\\t\\n   228\\t## Resolved Questions\\n   229\\t\\n   230\\t- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\n   231\\t- **\\\"did you mean?\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names. minijinja doesn't provide this. Serves I-10.\\n   232\\t- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. We own the behavior, upstream changes don't affect us. Serves I-1.\\n   233\\t\\n   234\\t## Execution Order\\n   235\\t\\n   236\\t```\\n   237\\tPhase 1 ──► Phase 2 ──► Phase 3 ──► Phase 4 ──► Phase 5\\n   238\\tskeleton     vars+render  create+output inject     integration\\n   239\\t                                                    tests\\n   240\\t```\\n   241\\t\\n   242\\tEach phase produces a working, testable artifact. No phase depends on \\\"wiring up later.\\\" The CLI grows incrementally:\\n   243\\t- After Phase 1: `jig validate`, `jig vars`\\n   244\\t- After Phase 2: + `jig render`\\n   245\\t- After Phase 3: + `jig run` (create only)\\n   246\\t- After Phase 4: + `jig run` (create + inject) — **v0.1 complete**\\n   247\\t- After Phase 5: Full test coverage validates the release\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-205456.md:19:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_8\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/ARCHITECTURE.md'\",\"aggregated_output\":\"     1\\t# ARCHITECTURE.md\\n     2\\t\\n     3\\tTarget architecture for jig, the iterative build strategy to get there, and the evaluation criteria at each gate. This document grounds all workstream planning.\\n     4\\t\\n     5\\t## System Overview\\n     6\\t\\n     7\\tjig is a linear pipeline: parse CLI args, load recipe, validate variables, render templates, execute file operations, format output, exit. Every stage is independently testable. No stage has side effects except operation execution (which writes files).\\n     8\\t\\n     9\\t```\\n    10\\tCLI Input\\n    11\\t  |\\n    12\\t  v\\n    13\\t+------------------+\\n    14\\t| CLI Parser       |  args, flags, var sources\\n    15\\t| (clap)           |\\n    16\\t+------------------+\\n    17\\t  |\\n    18\\t  v\\n    19\\t+------------------+\\n    20\\t| Recipe Loader    |  YAML -> Recipe struct\\n    21\\t| (serde_yaml)     |\\n    22\\t+------------------+\\n    23\\t  |\\n    24\\t  v\\n    25\\t+------------------+\\n    26\\t| Variable         |  JSON input -> typed, validated Variables\\n    27\\t| Validator        |  merge: defaults < file < stdin < inline\\n    28\\t| (serde_json)     |\\n    29\\t+------------------+\\n    30\\t  |\\n    31\\t  v\\n    32\\t+------------------+\\n    33\\t| Template         |  template + variables -> rendered string\\n    34\\t| Renderer         |  custom filters registered here\\n    35\\t| (minijinja)      |\\n    36\\t+------------------+\\n    37\\t  |\\n    38\\t  v\\n    39\\t+------------------+\\n    40\\t| Operation        |  rendered content + file operation spec\\n    41\\t| Executor         |  -> file writes (or dry-run recording)\\n    42\\t+------------------+\\n    43\\t  |\\n    44\\t  v\\n    45\\t+------------------+\\n    46\\t| Output           |  results -> JSON (stdout) or human (stderr)\\n    47\\t| Formatter        |  TTY auto-detect, --json, --quiet\\n    48\\t+------------------+\\n    49\\t  |\\n    50\\t  v\\n    51\\tExit Code (0-4)\\n    52\\t```\\n    53\\t\\n    54\\t## Module Map\\n    55\\t\\n    56\\t### `src/main.rs` — Entry Point\\n    57\\t\\n    58\\tOwns: CLI argument parsing via clap. Wires the pipeline together.\\n    59\\tDepends on: every other module (it's the composition root).\\n    60\\t\\n    61\\tResponsibilities:\\n    62\\t- Define CLI commands: `run`, `render`, `validate`, `vars`\\n    63\\t- Parse `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose`\\n    64\\t- Call pipeline stages in order\\n    65\\t- Map errors to exit codes\\n    66\\t\\n    67\\t### `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe Parsing\\n    68\\t\\n    69\\tOwns: `Recipe`, `VariableDecl`, `FileOp` structs. YAML deserialization.\\n    70\\tDepends on: serde, serde_yaml.\\n    71\\t\\n    72\\tResponsibilities:\\n    73\\t- Deserialize `recipe.yaml` into `Recipe` struct\\n    74\\t- Validate structural correctness (required fields present, template files exist relative to recipe)\\n    75\\t- Resolve template paths relative to the recipe file location (I-7: templates live with the consumer)\\n    76\\t- Distinguish operation types: create vs inject vs replace vs patch\\n    77\\t\\n    78\\tKey types:\\n    79\\t```rust\\n    80\\tstruct Recipe {\\n    81\\t    name: Option<String>,\\n    82\\t    description: Option<String>,\\n    83\\t    variables: IndexMap<String, VariableDecl>,\\n    84\\t    files: Vec<FileOp>,\\n    85\\t}\\n    86\\t\\n    87\\tstruct VariableDecl {\\n    88\\t    var_type: VarType,       // string, number, boolean, array, object, enum\\n    89\\t    required: bool,\\n    90\\t    default: Option<Value>,\\n    91\\t    description: Option<String>,\\n    92\\t    values: Option<Vec<String>>,  // for enum type\\n    93\\t    items: Option<VarType>,       // for array type\\n    94\\t}\\n    95\\t\\n    96\\tenum FileOp {\\n    97\\t    Create { template: String, to: String, skip_if_exists: bool },\\n    98\\t    Inject { template: String, inject: String, mode: InjectMode, skip_if: Option<String> },\\n    99\\t    Replace { template: String, replace: String, spec: ReplaceSpec, fallback: Fallback },\\n   100\\t    Patch { template: String, patch: String, anchor: Anchor, skip_if: Option<String> },\\n   101\\t}\\n   102\\t```\\n   103\\t\\n   104\\t### `src/variables.rs` — Variable Validation\\n   105\\t\\n   106\\tOwns: Variable merging, type checking, default application.\\n   107\\tDepends on: serde_json, recipe types.\\n   108\\t\\n   109\\tResponsibilities:\\n   110\\t- Parse JSON from multiple sources (inline string, file, stdin)\\n   111\\t- Merge with precedence: recipe defaults < vars-file < vars-stdin < inline --vars\\n   112\\t- Type-check each value against its `VariableDecl`\\n   113\\t- Validate required fields are present\\n   114\\t- Validate enum values against allowed set\\n   115\\t- Validate array item types\\n   116\\t- Produce clear error messages with hints (I-4)\\n   117\\t\\n   118\\t### `src/renderer.rs` — Template Rendering\\n   119\\t\\n   120\\tOwns: minijinja `Environment` setup, filter registration, template loading.\\n   121\\tDepends on: minijinja, heck (for case conversion filters).\\n   122\\t\\n   123\\tResponsibilities:\\n   124\\t- Create minijinja `Environment` with templates loaded from recipe-relative paths\\n   125\\t- Register built-in filters: `snakecase`, `camelcase`, `pascalcase`, `kebabcase`, `upper`, `lower`, `capitalize`, `replace`, `pluralize`, `singularize`, `quote`, `indent`, `join`\\n   126\\t- Render a template with a variables context\\n   127\\t- Return `Result<String>` — never silently produce wrong output (I-10)\\n   128\\t\\n   129\\tDesign note: The renderer is stateless per invocation. Create environment, register filters, load templates, render, return. No caching needed at v0.1 scale.\\n   130\\t\\n   131\\t### `src/operations/mod.rs` — Operation Dispatch\\n   132\\t\\n   133\\tOwns: operation dispatch, dry-run tracking.\\n   134\\tDepends on: operation implementations, renderer output.\\n   135\\t\\n   136\\t```rust\\n   137\\tstruct ExecutionContext {\\n   138\\t    base_dir: PathBuf,\\n   139\\t    dry_run: bool,\\n   140\\t    force: bool,\\n   141\\t    virtual_files: HashMap<PathBuf, String>,  // populated by create ops in dry-run mode\\n   142\\t}\\n   143\\t\\n   144\\tenum OpResult {\\n   145\\t    Success { action: &'static str, path: PathBuf, lines: usize, location: Option<String>, rendered_content: Option<String> },\\n   146\\t    Skip { path: PathBuf, reason: String, rendered_content: Option<String> },\\n   147\\t    Error { path: PathBuf, error: StructuredError, rendered_content: String },\\n   148\\t}\\n   149\\t\\n   150\\t// Note: `verbose` is an OutputMode concern passed to the formatter, not part of ExecutionContext.\\n   151\\t\\n   152\\t// FileOp enum match dispatch — no trait needed for v0.1 (D-4)\\n   153\\tfn execute_operation(op: &FileOp, rendered: &str, ctx: &ExecutionContext) -> OpResult { ... }\\n   154\\t```\\n   155\\t\\n   156\\tOperations execute in declaration order (I-9). Each operation receives the already-rendered content — rendering happens before execution, not during.\\n   157\\t\\n   158\\t### `src/operations/create.rs` — Create Operation\\n   159\\t\\n   160\\tOwns: New file creation logic.\\n   161\\tDepends on: std::fs.\\n   162\\t\\n   163\\tResponsibilities:\\n   164\\t- Render the `to` path as a template (it can contain `{{ variables }}`)\\n   165\\t- Create parent directories as needed\\n   166\\t- Write rendered content to the target path\\n   167\\t- `skip_if_exists`: if true and target exists, return `OpResult::Skip`\\n   168\\t- If not `--force` and target exists and `skip_if_exists` is false, return error\\n   169\\t\\n   170\\t### `src/operations/inject.rs` — Inject Operation\\n   171\\t\\n   172\\tOwns: Content injection into existing files.\\n   173\\tDepends on: regex.\\n   174\\t\\n   175\\tResponsibilities:\\n   176\\t- Read the target file\\n   177\\t- `skip_if`: search for string in file content, skip if found (I-2: idempotency)\\n   178\\t- Find the anchor line using regex pattern\\n   179\\t- `at: first` (default) or `at: last` — which match to use\\n   180\\t- Insert rendered content `after` or `before` the anchor line\\n   181\\t- `prepend`: insert at beginning of file\\n   182\\t- `append`: insert at end of file\\n   183\\t- Write the modified content back\\n   184\\t\\n   185\\t### `src/operations/replace.rs` — Replace Operation\\n   186\\t\\n   187\\tOwns: Region replacement in existing files.\\n   188\\tDepends on: regex.\\n   189\\t\\n   190\\tResponsibilities:\\n   191\\t- `between`: find start pattern, find end pattern, replace everything between them (exclusive of markers)\\n   192\\t- `pattern`: find matching line(s), replace them entirely\\n   193\\t- `fallback`: what to do when pattern not found — `append`, `prepend`, `skip`, `error` (default)\\n   194\\t- Preserve line endings and file encoding\\n   195\\t\\n   196\\t### `src/operations/patch.rs` — Patch Operation\\n   197\\t\\n   198\\tOwns: Scope-aware content insertion. Delegates scope detection.\\n   199\\tDepends on: scope module, regex.\\n   200\\t\\n   201\\tResponsibilities:\\n   202\\t- Find anchor pattern in file\\n   203\\t- Determine scope boundaries using the scope module\\n   204\\t- Apply `find` narrowing within the scope\\n   205\\t- Insert rendered content at the specified position\\n   206\\t- `skip_if`: idempotency check within the scope\\n   207\\t\\n   208\\t### `src/scope/mod.rs` — Scope Detection Dispatch\\n   209\\t\\n   210\\tOwns: Scope type classification, dispatch to indent vs delimiter strategies.\\n   211\\tDepends on: indent, delimiter, position submodules.\\n   212\\t\\n   213\\t```rust\\n   214\\tenum ScopeType {\\n   215\\t    Line,              // just the matched line\\n   216\\t    Block,             // indent-based block\\n   217\\t    ClassBody,         // indent-based class body\\n   218\\t    FunctionBody,      // indent-based function body\\n   219\\t    FunctionSignature, // delimiter: ( to )\\n   220\\t    Braces,            // delimiter: { to }\\n   221\\t    Brackets,          // delimiter: [ to ]\\n   222\\t    Parens,            // delimiter: ( to )\\n   223\\t}\\n   224\\t```\\n   225\\t\\n   226\\t### `src/scope/indent.rs` — Indentation-Based Scope Detection\\n   227\\t\\n   228\\tOwns: Scope detection for indentation-significant languages (Python, YAML).\\n   229\\t\\n   230\\tAlgorithm:\\n   231\\t1. Find the anchor line's indentation level\\n   232\\t2. Walk forward: scope includes all lines indented deeper than the anchor\\n   233\\t3. Scope ends when indentation returns to the same or shallower level\\n   234\\t4. Handle blank lines within the scope (they don't end it)\\n   235\\t\\n   236\\t### `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — Delimiter-Based Scope Detection\\n   237\\t\\n   238\\tOwns: Scope detection for delimiter-based languages (C-family, JSON, TypeScript).\\n   239\\t\\n   240\\tAlgorithm:\\n   241\\t1. From the anchor line, find the opening delimiter\\n   242\\t2. Count nesting depth (handle nested delimiters)\\n   243\\t3. Scope is everything between opening and closing delimiter (exclusive)\\n   244\\t4. Handle string literals (don't count delimiters inside strings)\\n   245\\t\\n   246\\t### `src/scope/position.rs` — Semantic Position Heuristics\\n   247\\t\\n   248\\tOwns: Position determination within a scope.\\n   249\\t\\n   250\\t| Position | Heuristic |\\n   251\\t|----------|-----------|\\n   252\\t| `before` | First line of scope |\\n   253\\t| `after` | Last line of scope |\\n   254\\t| `before_close` | Line before closing delimiter or dedent |\\n   255\\t| `after_last_field` | Last line matching `^\\\\s+\\\\w+\\\\s*[:=]` |\\n   256\\t| `after_last_method` | Last line matching `^\\\\s+def \\\\w+` |\\n   257\\t| `after_last_import` | Last line matching `^\\\\s*(from\\\\|import) ` |\\n   258\\t| `sorted` | Alphabetical insertion among siblings |\\n   259\\t\\n   260\\t### `src/filters.rs` — Custom Jinja2 Filters\\n   261\\t\\n   262\\tOwns: Filter function implementations and registration.\\n   263\\tDepends on: heck (case conversions), minijinja (filter API).\\n   264\\t\\n   265\\tFilters are pure functions: `fn(value: &str, args...) -> String`. No side effects, no I/O. This module is the thinnest — most filters are one-liners wrapping `heck` or standard string operations.\\n   266\\t\\n   267\\t### `src/output.rs` — Output Formatting\\n   268\\t\\n   269\\tOwns: Dual-stream output (I-8), TTY detection.\\n   270\\tDepends on: serde_json, owo-colors.\\n   271\\t\\n   272\\tResponsibilities:\\n   273\\t- Collect `Vec<OpResult>` from operation execution\\n   274\\t- JSON mode: serialize results to stdout\\n   275\\t- Human mode: colored, summarized output to stderr\\n   276\\t- Auto-detect: if stdout is a TTY, use human mode; if piped, use JSON\\n   277\\t- `--json`: force JSON\\n   278\\t- `--quiet`: suppress all stderr output\\n   279\\t- `--verbose`: include rendered content in output\\n   280\\t\\n   281\\t### `src/error.rs` — Structured Error Types\\n   282\\t\\n   283\\tOwns: Error types, exit code mapping, error formatting.\\n   284\\tDepends on: nothing (leaf module).\\n   285\\t\\n   286\\t```rust\\n   287\\tstruct StructuredError {\\n   288\\t    what: String,\\n   289\\t    where_: String,\\n   290\\t    why: String,\\n   291\\t    hint: String,\\n   292\\t}\\n   293\\t\\n   294\\tenum JigError {\\n   295\\t    RecipeValidation(StructuredError),     // exit 1\\n   296\\t    TemplateRendering(StructuredError),    // exit 2\\n   297\\t    FileOperation(StructuredError),        // exit 3\\n   298\\t    VariableValidation(StructuredError),   // exit 4\\n   299\\t}\\n   300\\t```\\n   301\\t\\n   302\\tEvery error includes: what, where, why, hint (I-4). File operation errors include the rendered content so the caller can fall back to manual editing (I-10).\\n   303\\t\\n   304\\t### `src/workflow.rs` — Multi-Recipe Orchestration (v0.3+)\\n   305\\t\\n   306\\tOwns: Workflow definition, step execution, conditional logic, variable mapping.\\n   307\\tDepends on: recipe, variables, renderer, operations.\\n   308\\t\\n   309\\tNot built until v0.3. Included here for architectural completeness.\\n   310\\t\\n   311\\t### `src/library/` — Library Management (v0.4+)\\n   312\\t\\n   313\\tOwns: Library manifest parsing, installation, discovery, convention mapping.\\n   314\\tDepends on: recipe, filesystem, git (for remote installs).\\n   315\\t\\n   316\\tNot built until v0.4. Included here for architectural completeness.\\n   317\\t\\n   318\\t## Dependency Map\\n   319\\t\\n   320\\t```\\n   321\\t                     main.rs\\n   322\\t                    /   |   \\\\\\n   323\\t                   /    |    \\\\\\n   324\\t              recipe  variables  output\\n   325\\t                |       |         |\\n   326\\t                v       v         v\\n   327\\t             renderer  (merges)  error\\n   328\\t                |\\n   329\\t                v\\n   330\\t           operations/mod\\n   331\\t           /    |    \\\\    \\\\\\n   332\\t       create inject replace patch\\n   333\\t                              |\\n   334\\t                              v\\n   335\\t                          scope/mod\\n   336\\t                         /    |    \\\\\\n   337\\t                    indent  delim  position\\n   338\\t```\\n   339\\t\\n   340\\tExternal crate usage:\\n   341\\t- clap: main.rs only\\n   342\\t- serde + serde_yaml: recipe.rs\\n   343\\t- serde_json: variables.rs, output.rs\\n   344\\t- minijinja: renderer.rs\\n   345\\t- regex: inject.rs, replace.rs, patch.rs, scope/*.rs\\n   346\\t- heck: filters.rs\\n   347\\t- owo-colors: output.rs\\n   348\\t- thiserror: error.rs\\n   349\\t- indexmap: recipe.rs, variables.rs\\n   350\\t- pluralizer: filters.rs\\n   351\\t- strsim: renderer.rs (did-you-mean hints)\\n   352\\t- insta: tests (snapshot testing)\\n   353\\t\\n   354\\t## Key Design Decisions\\n   355\\t\\n   356\\t### D-1: Render-Then-Execute\\n   357\\t\\n   358\\tAll templates are rendered before any operation executes. The pipeline renders every template in the recipe upfront, then executes operations sequentially. This means:\\n   359\\t- A rendering error in any template prevents all file writes\\n   360\\t- The rendered content is always available for error messages (I-4)\\n   361\\t- Operations are simpler — they just deal with strings and files\\n   362\\t- Note: execution failures (e.g., inject target missing) can still produce partial writes if earlier operations succeeded. Recovery: re-run with `--force` or use rendered content from error output for manual editing.\\n   363\\t\\n   364\\t### D-2: Recipe-Relative Template Resolution\\n   365\\t\\n   366\\tTemplate paths in recipes are relative to the recipe file, not the working directory. This keeps recipes self-contained (I-7). The `--base-dir` flag only affects output paths (where files are written), not template paths (where templates are read from).\\n   367\\t\\n   368\\t### D-3: Regex for Anchoring, Not Parsing\\n   369\\t\\n   370\\tAnchors use regex, not AST parsing. This is deliberate:\\n   371\\t- Language-agnostic (same tool works for Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust)\\n   372\\t- No parser dependencies (keeps binary small, I-6)\\n   373\\t- Failure is explicit (regex didn't match = clear error, not a silent wrong parse)\\n   374\\t- Scope detection adds just enough structure without full parsing\\n   375\\t\\n   376\\t### D-4: Operations Are Values, Not Traits (Initially)\\n   377\\t\\n   378\\tFor v0.1, operations can be a simple enum with match dispatch rather than a trait-object pattern. The trait abstraction is only needed if we add user-defined operations, which isn't planned. Keep it simple — an enum with four variants covers all cases through v0.4.\\n   379\\t\\n   380\\t### D-5: No Global State\\n   381\\t\\n   382\\tNo singletons, no global config objects, no lazy_static. Every function takes its inputs as arguments. This makes testing trivial and ensures determinism (I-1).\\n   383\\t\\n   384\\t## Iterative Build Strategy\\n   385\\t\\n   386\\tEach phase produces a working, testable, demoable artifact. Nothing is \\\"wired up later.\\\" Each phase can be evaluated independently.\\n   387\\t\\n   388\\t### Phase A: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\n   389\\t\\n   390\\t**Build:**\\n   391\\t- `Cargo.toml` with dependencies: serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap\\n   392\\t- `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands\\n   393\\t- `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe struct, YAML deserialization, structural validation\\n   394\\t- `src/error.rs` — error types and exit codes\\n   395\\t- `src/variables.rs` — VariableDecl types (no validation logic yet, just the types)\\n   396\\t\\n   397\\t**Demo:**\\n   398\\t```bash\\n   399\\tjig validate recipe.yaml          # prints validation result, exits 0 or 1\\n   400\\tjig vars recipe.yaml              # prints expected variables as JSON\\n   401\\t```\\n   402\\t\\n   403\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   404\\t- Can parse the example recipe from the PRD\\n   405\\t- Rejects invalid YAML with exit code 1 and a clear message\\n   406\\t- Template file existence is checked relative to recipe location\\n   407\\t- `jig vars` output matches the PRD's expected format\\n   408\\t- Unit tests: valid recipe, missing fields, bad types, missing templates\\n   409\\t\\n   410\\t### Phase B: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\n   411\\t\\n   412\\t**Build:**\\n   413\\t- `src/variables.rs` — full validation: type checking, required fields, defaults, merging\\n   414\\t- `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja environment, template loading\\n   415\\t- `src/filters.rs` — all built-in filters\\n   416\\t- Add `render` subcommand to CLI\\n   417\\t- Add `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin` to CLI\\n   418\\t\\n   419\\t**Demo:**\\n   420\\t```bash\\n   421\\tjig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\"class_name\\\": \\\"Foo\\\"}'     # rendered output to stdout\\n   422\\tjig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\"bad\\\": 123}' 2>&1         # type error, exit 4\\n   423\\t```\\n   424\\t\\n   425\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   426\\t- All 13 built-in filters produce correct output\\n   427\\t- Variable type checking catches mismatches (string vs number, missing required, bad enum value)\\n   428\\t- Merge precedence works: defaults < file < stdin < inline\\n   429\\t- Template syntax errors produce exit code 2 with line number\\n   430\\t- Undefined variables produce a helpful error with \\\"did you mean?\\\" hint\\n   431\\t- Snapshot tests (insta) for rendered output across filter combinations\\n   432\\t\\n   433\\t### Phase C: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\n   434\\t\\n   435\\t**Build:**\\n   436\\t- `src/operations/mod.rs` — operation dispatch (create only)\\n   437\\t- `src/operations/create.rs` — file creation with directory creation, skip_if_exists\\n   438\\t- `src/output.rs` — JSON and human-readable output, TTY detection\\n   439\\t- Add `run` subcommand to CLI\\n   440\\t- Add `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose`\\n   441\\t\\n   442\\t**Demo:**\\n   443\\t```bash\\n   444\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'                 # creates files\\n   445\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...' --dry-run       # preview without writing\\n   446\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...' --json | jq .   # machine-readable output\\n   447\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'                 # second run: skip_if_exists works\\n   448\\t```\\n   449\\t\\n   450\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   451\\t- Template paths in `to` field render correctly (`tests/{{ module | replace('.', '/') }}/...`)\\n   452\\t- Parent directories are created automatically\\n   453\\t- `skip_if_exists` prevents overwriting\\n   454\\t- `--force` overrides skip_if_exists\\n   455\\t- JSON output matches the format in the PRD\\n   456\\t- Human output is colored and readable\\n   457\\t- Dry-run produces identical JSON output but writes no files\\n   458\\t- Second run with same vars shows all skips (I-2)\\n   459\\t- Integration test fixtures: recipe + vars + expected output directory\\n   460\\t\\n   461\\t### Phase D: Inject Operation\\n   462\\t\\n   463\\t**Build:**\\n   464\\t- `src/operations/inject.rs` — all injection modes\\n   465\\t- Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\n   466\\t- regex dependency added\\n   467\\t\\n   468\\t**Demo:**\\n   469\\t```bash\\n   470\\t# Recipe with create + inject operations\\n   471\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'         # creates file, injects into existing\\n   472\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'         # second run: skip_if prevents duplication\\n   473\\t```\\n   474\\t\\n   475\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   476\\t- `after` regex: content appears on the line after the match\\n   477\\t- `before` regex: content appears on the line before the match\\n   478\\t- `prepend`: content at start of file\\n   479\\t- `append`: content at end of file\\n   480\\t- `at: first` vs `at: last`: correct match selection when regex matches multiple lines\\n   481\\t- `skip_if`: string search prevents duplicate injection (I-2)\\n   482\\t- Regex match failure produces exit code 3 with the pattern and file path in the error\\n   483\\t- Integration test fixtures for each injection mode\\n   484\\t- **This completes the v0.1 feature set**\\n   485\\t\\n   486\\t### Phase E: Integration Test Framework\\n   487\\t\\n   488\\t**Build:**\\n   489\\t- `tests/` directory with fixture-based integration test harness\\n   490\\t- Fixture format: `recipe.yaml` + `vars.json` + `templates/` + `existing/` + `expected/`\\n   491\\t- Test runner: copy existing/ to temp, run jig, diff against expected/\\n   492\\t- Snapshot tests for all output formats\\n   493\\t- Error case fixtures (missing vars, bad regex, missing template, missing target file)\\n   494\\t\\n   495\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   496\\t- Every operation mode has at least one fixture\\n   497\\t- Every error case has a fixture that asserts exit code + error message content\\n   498\\t- `cargo test` runs all unit tests + integration tests + snapshot tests\\n   499\\t- Adding a new test case is just adding a directory (no code changes)\\n   500\\t\\n   501\\t### Phase F: Replace Operation (v0.2 start)\\n   502\\t\\n   503\\t**Build:**\\n   504\\t- `src/operations/replace.rs` — between, pattern, fallback modes\\n   505\\t\\n   506\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   507\\t- `between` start/end: content between markers is replaced\\n   508\\t- `pattern`: matched lines are replaced\\n   509\\t- `fallback: append/prepend/skip/error` each work correctly\\n   510\\t- Markers themselves are preserved (only content between them changes)\\n   511\\t\\n   512\\t### Phase G: Patch Operation + Scope Detection (v0.2 completion)\\n   513\\t\\n   514\\t**Build:**\\n   515\\t- `src/scope/mod.rs` — scope type dispatch\\n   516\\t- `src/scope/indent.rs` — indentation-based scope detection\\n   517\\t- `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — delimiter-based scope detection\\n   518\\t- `src/scope/position.rs` — semantic position heuristics\\n   519\\t- `src/operations/patch.rs` — anchor + scope + position + find\\n   520\\t\\n   521\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   522\\t- Indentation scope: correctly finds class/function body boundaries in Python code\\n   523\\t- Delimiter scope: correctly finds brace/bracket/paren boundaries in TypeScript/Rust\\n   524\\t- Nested delimiters handled correctly\\n   525\\t- String literals don't confuse delimiter counting\\n   526\\t- Each semantic position (after_last_field, after_last_method, etc.) tested with real code snippets\\n   527\\t- `find` narrowing works within a scope\\n   528\\t- `--verbose` shows scope boundaries and insertion point\\n   529\\t- Full patch recipe from the PRD (add-model-field) works against a Django-style fixture\\n   530\\t- Scope parse failure produces structured error with rendered content (I-4, I-10)\\n   531\\t\\n   532\\t### Phase H: Workflows (v0.3)\\n   533\\t\\n   534\\t**Build:**\\n   535\\t- `src/workflow.rs` — workflow definition, step execution\\n   536\\t- `workflow` subcommand in CLI\\n   537\\t- Conditional steps, variable mapping, error handling modes\\n   538\\t\\n   539\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   540\\t- Multi-recipe workflows execute in order\\n   541\\t- `when` expressions skip/include steps correctly\\n   542\\t- `vars_map` renames variables between steps\\n   543\\t- `on_error: stop/continue/report` behaves correctly\\n   544\\t- Per-step results in JSON output\\n   545\\t\\n   546\\t### Phase I: Libraries (v0.4)\\n   547\\t\\n   548\\t**Build:**\\n   549\\t- `src/library/mod.rs` — manifest parsing\\n   550\\t- `src/library/install.rs` — add/remove/update\\n   551\\t- `src/library/discover.rs` — recipe listing\\n   552\\t- `src/library/conventions.rs` — convention mapping and overrides\\n   553\\t- `library` subcommand in CLI\\n   554\\t\\n   555\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   556\\t- Install from local directory works\\n   557\\t- Install from git URL works\\n   558\\t- Convention overrides in .jigrc.yaml apply correctly\\n   559\\t- `jig library recipes <name>` lists all recipes\\n   560\\t- Project-local extensions and template overrides work\\n   561\\t\\n   562\\t## Testing Strategy\\n   563\\t\\n   564\\tThree layers, matching the pipeline architecture:\\n   565\\t\\n   566\\t| Layer | What | Tool | Location |\\n   567\\t|-------|------|------|----------|\\n   568\\t| **Unit** | Individual functions: parsing, validation, filter output, scope detection | `#[cfg(test)]` inline | Each `src/*.rs` |\\n   569\\t| **Integration** | Full pipeline: recipe + vars in, files out, diff against expected | Fixture directories | `tests/fixtures/` |\\n   570\\t| **Snapshot** | Rendered template output, JSON output format, error messages | `insta` crate | `tests/snapshots/` |\\n   571\\t\\n   572\\t### Fixture Directory Convention\\n   573\\t\\n   574\\t```\\n   575\\ttests/fixtures/<test-name>/\\n   576\\t  recipe.yaml              # input recipe\\n   577\\t  vars.json                # input variables\\n   578\\t  templates/               # template files referenced by recipe\\n   579\\t    *.j2\\n   580\\t  existing/                # files that exist before jig runs (for inject/replace/patch)\\n   581\\t    *.py, *.ts, etc.\\n   582\\t  expected/                # what the output directory should look like after\\n   583\\t    *.py, *.ts, etc.\\n   584\\t  expected_output.json     # optional: assert on jig's JSON stdout\\n   585\\t  expected_exit_code       # optional: file containing just \\\"0\\\", \\\"1\\\", etc.\\n   586\\t```\\n   587\\t\\n   588\\tTest runner:\\n   589\\t1. Copy `existing/` to a temp directory\\n   590\\t2. Run `jig run recipe.yaml --vars-file vars.json --base-dir $tmp --json`\\n   591\\t3. Diff temp directory against `expected/`\\n   592\\t4. If `expected_output.json` exists, assert JSON output matches\\n   593\\t5. If `expected_exit_code` exists, assert exit code matches\\n   594\\t\\n   595\\t## What This Architecture Does NOT Cover\\n   596\\t\\n   597\\tThese are explicitly out of scope for the architecture and will be addressed in later workstreams:\\n   598\\t\\n   599\\t- **Scan, Infer, Check** (v0.7-v0.8) — reverse operations, pattern learning, conformance\\n   600\\t- **Schema-first generation** (v0.9) — OpenAPI/SQL/proto/GraphQL input\\n   601\\t- **Observation engine** (post-1.0) — Claude Code hook for pattern discovery\\n   602\\t- **Custom filters via shell commands** — .jigrc.yaml `filters:` block\\n   603\\t- **Distribution** (v0.5) — CI, Homebrew, npm wrapper, Nix flake\\n   604\\t- **Claude Code plugin** (v0.6) — /jig:init, /jig:doctor skills\\n   605\\t- **Tree-sitter integration** (post-1.0) — optional AST-aware scoping\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-205456.md:25:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_11\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"awk -F'|' '/\\\\\\\\| AC-/{id=\\\"'$2; gsub(/''^[ '\\\"\\\\\\\\t]+|[ \\\\\\\\t]+\\\"'$/, \\\"\\\", id); type=$3; gsub(/''^[ '\\\"\\\\\\\\t]+|[ \\\\\\\\t]+\\\"'$/, \\\"\\\", type); crit=$4; gsub(/''^[ '\\\"\\\\\\\\t]+|[ \\\\\\\\t]+\\\"'$/, \\\"\\\", crit); test=$5; gsub(/''^[ '\\\"\\\\\\\\t]+|[ \\\\\\\\t]+\\\"'$/, \\\"\\\", test); print id \\\"'\\\"\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\" type \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\" crit \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\" test}' docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"AC-1.1\\tEvent\\tWHEN a valid recipe YAML is provided, the system SHALL parse it into a Recipe struct containing name, description, variables, and files\\tTEST-1.1\\nAC-1.2\\tEvent\\tWHEN a recipe declares variables with type, required, default, description, values (enum), and items (array), the system SHALL parse all fields into the VariableDecl struct\\tTEST-1.2\\nAC-1.3\\tEvent\\tWHEN a recipe declares `create` file operations with template, to, and skip_if_exists fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Create\\tTEST-1.3\\nAC-1.4\\tEvent\\tWHEN a recipe declares `inject` file operations with template, inject, after/before/prepend/append, at, and skip_if fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Inject\\tTEST-1.4\\nAC-1.5\\tUnwanted\\tIF the recipe YAML is malformed (invalid YAML syntax), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and an error message identifying the parse failure location\\tTEST-1.5\\nAC-1.6\\tUnwanted\\tIF a required field is missing from the recipe (e.g., files with no template), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and name the missing field\\tTEST-1.6\\nAC-1.7\\tEvent\\tWHEN a recipe references template files, the system SHALL resolve those paths relative to the recipe file location, not the working directory\\tTEST-1.7\\nAC-1.8\\tUnwanted\\tIF a referenced template file does not exist at the resolved path, the system SHALL exit with code 1 and report which template is missing and where it looked\\tTEST-1.8\\nAC-1.9\\tEvent\\tWHEN the recipe has optional metadata fields (name, description), the system SHALL accept recipes with or without them\\tTEST-1.9\\nAC-1.10\\tUnwanted\\tIF the recipe contains an unknown operation type (e.g., `patch`, `replace`), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and report \\\"unknown operation type '<name>' — this operation is not supported in v0.1\\\"\\tTEST-1.10\\nAC-1.11\\tEvent\\tWHEN the recipe has an empty `files: []` array, the system SHALL exit 0 with an empty operations array\\tTEST-1.11\\nAC-1.12\\tEvent\\tWHEN the recipe has no `variables` key or an empty `variables` map, the system SHALL accept the recipe as valid\\tTEST-1.12\\nAC-1.13\\tUnwanted\\tIF the recipe file does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 1 naming the missing path\\tTEST-1.13\\nAC-1.14\\tUnwanted\\tIF a file operation contains more than one of `to`/`inject`/`replace`/`patch` fields, the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the ambiguous operation type\\tTEST-1.14\\nAC-1.15\\tUnwanted\\tIF a file operation contains none of `to`/`inject`/`replace`/`patch` fields, the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the missing operation type\\tTEST-1.15\\nAC-2.1\\tEvent\\tWHEN `--vars` is provided with a JSON string, the system SHALL parse it as the variable input\\tTEST-2.1\\nAC-2.2\\tEvent\\tWHEN `--vars-file` is provided with a path to a JSON file, the system SHALL read and parse the file as variable input\\tTEST-2.2\\nAC-2.3\\tEvent\\tWHEN `--vars-stdin` is provided, the system SHALL read JSON from stdin as variable input\\tTEST-2.3\\nAC-2.4\\tEvent\\tWHEN multiple variable sources are provided, the system SHALL merge with precedence: recipe defaults < vars-file < vars-stdin < inline --vars\\tTEST-2.4\\nAC-2.5\\tEvent\\tWHEN a variable is declared as `required: true` and no value is provided (after merging), the system SHALL exit with code 4 naming the missing variable and providing a hint\\tTEST-2.5\\nAC-2.6\\tEvent\\tWHEN a variable is declared with a `default` and no value is provided, the system SHALL use the default value\\tTEST-2.6\\nAC-2.7\\tUnwanted\\tIF a provided variable value does not match its declared type (e.g., string given for number, object given for array), the system SHALL exit with code 4 with expected vs actual type\\tTEST-2.7\\nAC-2.8\\tEvent\\tWHEN a variable is declared as `type: enum` with `values: [a, b, c]`, the system SHALL reject values not in the allowed set with exit code 4\\tTEST-2.8\\nAC-2.9\\tEvent\\tWHEN a variable is declared as `type: array` with `items: string`, the system SHALL validate that each array element matches the item type\\tTEST-2.9\\nAC-2.10\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL accept all six variable types: string, number, boolean, array, object, enum\\tTEST-2.10\\nAC-2.11\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL accumulate all variable validation errors and report them together with exit code 4\\tTEST-2.11\\nAC-2.12\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL accept variable input containing keys not declared in the recipe's variables section without error or warning\\tTEST-2.12\\nAC-2.13\\tUnwanted\\tIF `--vars` contains invalid JSON, the system SHALL exit with code 4 with a parse error identifying the location\\tTEST-2.13\\nAC-2.14\\tUnwanted\\tIF `--vars-file` points to a nonexistent file, the system SHALL exit with code 4 naming the missing path\\tTEST-2.14\\nAC-2.15\\tUnwanted\\tIF `--vars-file` points to a file containing invalid JSON, the system SHALL exit with code 4 with a parse error identifying the file path and the JSON error location\\tTEST-2.15\\nAC-2.16\\tEvent\\tWHEN no variable sources are provided (no `--vars`, `--vars-file`, or `--vars-stdin`), the system SHALL use an empty object as input and apply recipe defaults\\tTEST-2.16\\nAC-3.1\\tEvent\\tWHEN a template is rendered with a variables context, the system SHALL substitute all `{{ variable }}` expressions with their values\\tTEST-3.1\\nAC-3.2\\tEvent\\tWHEN a template contains `{% if %}` / `{% else %}` / `{% endif %}` blocks, the system SHALL evaluate them correctly against the variable values\\tTEST-3.2\\nAC-3.3\\tEvent\\tWHEN a template contains `{% for item in list %}` loops, the system SHALL iterate and render the body for each element\\tTEST-3.3\\nAC-3.4\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL register and support all 13 built-in filters: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join\\tTEST-3.4\\nAC-3.5\\tEvent\\tWHEN `snakecase` is applied to \\\"BookingService\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\"booking_service\\\"\\tTEST-3.5\\nAC-3.6\\tEvent\\tWHEN `camelcase` is applied to \\\"booking_service\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\"bookingService\\\"\\tTEST-3.6\\nAC-3.7\\tEvent\\tWHEN `pascalcase` is applied to \\\"booking_service\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\"BookingService\\\"\\tTEST-3.7\\nAC-3.8\\tEvent\\tWHEN `kebabcase` is applied to \\\"BookingService\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\"booking-service\\\"\\tTEST-3.8\\nAC-3.9\\tEvent\\tWHEN `replace` is applied as `\\\"a.b.c\\\" \\\\\\treplace('.', '/')`, the system SHALL produce \\\"a/b/c\\\"\\nAC-3.10\\tEvent\\tWHEN `pluralize` is applied to \\\"hotel\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\"hotels\\\"\\tTEST-3.10\\nAC-3.11\\tEvent\\tWHEN `singularize` is applied to \\\"hotels\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\"hotel\\\"\\tTEST-3.11\\nAC-3.12\\tEvent\\tWHEN `quote` is applied to \\\"hello\\\", the system SHALL produce `\\\"hello\\\"` (with literal quotes)\\tTEST-3.12\\nAC-3.13\\tEvent\\tWHEN `indent(4)` is applied to a multiline string, the system SHALL indent each line by 4 spaces, including the first line. Use `indent(4, first=false)` to skip the first line. Note: this diverges from Jinja2 convention where indent() skips the first line by default\\tTEST-3.13\\nAC-3.14\\tEvent\\tWHEN `join(\\\", \\\")` is applied to `[\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\", \\\"c\\\"]`, the system SHALL produce \\\"a, b, c\\\"\\tTEST-3.14\\nAC-3.15\\tEvent\\tWHEN `{# comment #}` appears in a template, the system SHALL strip it from the output\\tTEST-3.15\\nAC-3.16\\tEvent\\tWHEN `{% raw %}...{% endraw %}` appears in a template, the system SHALL output the content literally without interpreting Jinja2 syntax\\tTEST-3.16\\nAC-3.17\\tUnwanted\\tIF a template references an undefined variable, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and include a \\\"did you mean?\\\" hint when a close match exists among the keys in the provided variable context (Levenshtein distance ≤ 3)\\tTEST-3.17\\nAC-3.18\\tUnwanted\\tIF a template has a Jinja2 syntax error, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and report the template file path and line number\\tTEST-3.18\\nAC-4.1\\tEvent\\tWHEN a create operation executes, the system SHALL render the template and write the result to the path specified in `to`\\tTEST-4.1\\nAC-4.2\\tEvent\\tWHEN the `to` path contains Jinja2 expressions (e.g., `tests/{{ module \\\\\\treplace('.', '/') }}/test_{{ class_name \\\\\\nAC-4.3\\tEvent\\tWHEN parent directories in the `to` path do not exist, the system SHALL create them automatically\\tTEST-4.3\\nAC-4.4\\tEvent\\tWHEN `skip_if_exists: true` and the target file already exists, the system SHALL skip the operation and report `\\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\"` with a reason\\tTEST-4.4\\nAC-4.5\\tUnwanted\\tIF `skip_if_exists: false` (default) and the target file already exists and `--force` is not set, the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting the conflict\\tTEST-4.5\\nAC-4.6\\tEvent\\tWHEN `--force` is set and the target file already exists, the system SHALL overwrite the file regardless of skip_if_exists\\tTEST-4.6\\nAC-4.7\\tEvent\\tWHEN `--base-dir` is set, the system SHALL resolve `to` paths relative to the base directory instead of the working directory\\tTEST-4.7\\nAC-4.8\\tEvent\\tWHEN a create operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\"action\\\": \\\"create\\\"` with the path and line count (number of lines in the written file)\\tTEST-4.8\\nAC-4.9\\tUnwanted\\tIF a filesystem write fails due to permissions, the system SHALL exit with code 3 with the path and permission error\\tTEST-4.9\\nAC-4.10\\tUnwanted\\tIF `--base-dir` specifies a directory that does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 naming the missing directory\\tTEST-4.10\\nAC-5.1\\tEvent\\tWHEN `after: \\\"regex\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line after the first matching line\\tTEST-5.1\\nAC-5.2\\tEvent\\tWHEN `before: \\\"regex\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line before the first matching line\\tTEST-5.2\\nAC-5.3\\tEvent\\tWHEN `prepend: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very beginning of the file\\tTEST-5.3\\nAC-5.4\\tEvent\\tWHEN `append: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very end of the file\\tTEST-5.4\\nAC-5.5\\tEvent\\tWHEN `at: last` is specified with `after` or `before`, the system SHALL use the last regex match instead of the first\\tTEST-5.5\\nAC-5.6\\tEvent\\tWHEN `at: first` (default) is specified, the system SHALL use the first regex match\\tTEST-5.6\\nAC-5.7\\tEvent\\tWHEN `skip_if` is specified, the system SHALL render it as a Jinja2 template with the recipe's variables, then search for the rendered string in the target file. If found, the system SHALL skip the injection and report `\\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\"` with a reason\\tTEST-5.7\\nAC-5.8\\tUnwanted\\tIF the regex pattern matches zero lines in the target file, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the pattern, the file path, and a hint\\tTEST-5.8\\nAC-5.9\\tUnwanted\\tIF the target file for injection does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the missing file path\\tTEST-5.9\\nAC-5.10\\tEvent\\tWHEN an inject operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\"action\\\": \\\"inject\\\"` with the path, location description, and line count (number of lines inserted)\\tTEST-5.10\\nAC-5.11\\tEvent\\tWHEN the inject path contains Jinja2 expressions, the system SHALL render the path before resolving it\\tTEST-5.11\\nAC-5.12\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL ignore the `at` field when `prepend` or `append` is specified\\tTEST-5.12\\nAC-5.13\\tUnwanted\\tIF `after` or `before` is specified without a regex pattern, the system SHALL exit with code 1\\tTEST-5.13\\nAC-5.14\\tUnwanted\\tIF an inject operation's `after` or `before` pattern fails to compile as a valid regex, the system SHALL exit with code 1 during recipe validation, reporting the invalid pattern and the compilation error\\tTEST-5.14\\nAC-5.15\\tUnwanted\\tIF an inject operation specifies more than one of after/before/prepend/append, the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the conflicting fields\\tTEST-5.15\\nAC-5.16\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL not apply `--force` to inject operations — the `--force` flag only affects create operations (`skip_if_exists` override)\\tTEST-5.16\\nAC-5.17\\tUnwanted\\tIF writing the modified file content fails due to permissions, the system SHALL exit with code 3 with the path, permission error, and rendered content\\tTEST-5.17\\nAC-6.1\\tState\\tWHILE stdout is a TTY, the system SHALL output human-readable colored text to stderr and produce no stdout output\\tTEST-6.1\\nAC-6.2\\tState\\tWHILE stdout is piped (not a TTY), the system SHALL output JSON to stdout and produce no stderr output except errors\\tTEST-6.2\\nAC-6.3\\tEvent\\tWHEN `--json` is specified, the system SHALL force JSON output to stdout regardless of TTY detection\\tTEST-6.3\\nAC-6.4\\tEvent\\tWHEN `--quiet` is specified, the system SHALL suppress all stderr output. Stdout behavior is determined by `--json` independently\\tTEST-6.4\\nAC-6.5\\tEvent\\tWHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include an `operations` array with action, path, lines (for success), reason (for skip), or error details (for error) for each operation\\tTEST-6.5\\nAC-6.6\\tEvent\\tWHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include `files_written` and `files_skipped` summary arrays containing unique file paths. A path appears in `files_written` if any operation wrote to it. A path appears in `files_skipped` only if all operations targeting it were skipped. In dry-run mode, `files_written` lists paths that would have been written. Paths SHALL appear in the order they were first encountered during operation execution.\\tTEST-6.6\\nAC-6.7\\tEvent\\tWHEN `--verbose` is specified, the system SHALL include rendered template content in the output\\tTEST-6.7\\nAC-6.8\\tEvent\\tWHEN `--dry-run` is specified, the system SHALL produce identical output format but write no files to disk. Create operations record their output paths and rendered content in a virtual file state. Inject operations targeting a path created earlier in the same dry-run SHALL behave as if the file exists with the rendered content from the create operation. Inject operations SHALL update the virtual file state with post-injection content so subsequent operations in the same dry-run see the cumulative result. WHEN `--dry-run` and `--force` are both specified, create operations SHALL report `action: create` for existing files, reflecting what `--force` would do.\\tTEST-6.8\\nAC-6.9\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL resolve flag interactions as: `--quiet` suppresses stderr only and has no effect on stdout or JSON content; `--verbose` adds rendered content to both human stderr and JSON stdout independently; in human mode with `--quiet`, `--verbose` has no visible effect (stderr is suppressed); in JSON mode with `--quiet`, `--verbose` still adds rendered content to JSON stdout\\tTEST-6.9\\nAC-6.10\\tEvent\\tWHEN a file operation fails, the system SHALL stop execution immediately and not execute subsequent operations. The `operations` array SHALL contain results for executed operations only\\tTEST-6.10\\nAC-6.11\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL include a top-level `dry_run` boolean field in JSON output reflecting whether `--dry-run` was specified\\tTEST-6.11\\nAC-7.1\\tEvent\\tWHEN `jig validate <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL parse the recipe and report whether it is valid, listing variables and operations\\tTEST-7.1\\nAC-7.2\\tEvent\\tWHEN `jig vars <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL output the expected variables as a JSON object with type, required, default, and description fields\\tTEST-7.2\\nAC-7.3\\tEvent\\tWHEN `jig render <template> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL render the template with the given variables and output the result to stdout. Note: `jig render` operates without recipe context — variable type validation is not available, but \\\"did you mean?\\\" hints work against the provided variable keys.\\tTEST-7.3\\nAC-7.4\\tEvent\\tWHEN `jig render` is invoked with `--to <path>`, the system SHALL write the rendered output to the specified file instead of stdout\\tTEST-7.4\\nAC-7.5\\tEvent\\tWHEN `jig run <recipe> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL execute all file operations in the recipe in declaration order\\tTEST-7.5\\nAC-7.6\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL accept global options: `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose`, `--version`\\tTEST-7.6\\nAC-7.7\\tEvent\\tWHEN `--version` is specified, the system SHALL print the version string and exit with code 0\\tTEST-7.7\\nAC-N1.1\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL produce byte-identical output when given the same recipe, variables, and existing files across multiple runs\\tTEST-N1.1\\nAC-N1.2\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL not include timestamps, random values, or machine-specific identifiers in rendered output\\tTEST-N1.2\\nAC-N2.1\\tEvent\\tWHEN a recipe designed for idempotent execution (all creates use `skip_if_exists: true` and all injects use `skip_if`) is run a second time with the same variables and the same existing files, the system SHALL report all operations as `\\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\"` with reasons\\tTEST-N2.1\\nAC-N2.2\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL not produce duplicate content when create uses `skip_if_exists: true` or inject uses `skip_if`\\tTEST-N2.2\\nAC-N3.1\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL compile to a single static binary with no runtime dependencies beyond the system C library\\tTEST-N3.1\\nAC-N3.2\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries beyond the system C library to run\\tTEST-N3.2\\nAC-N4.1\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL include what, where, why, and hint fields in every error message\\tTEST-N4.1\\nAC-N4.2\\tEvent\\tWHEN a file operation fails (exit code 3), the system SHALL include the rendered template content in the error output so the caller can fall back to manual editing. This is independent of `--verbose` — rendered content in errors is always present\\tTEST-N4.2\\nAC-N4.3\\tEvent\\tWHEN a template rendering error occurs, the system SHALL report the template file path and the line number of the error\\tTEST-N4.3\\nAC-N4.4\\tEvent\\tWHEN a variable validation error occurs, the system SHALL report the variable name, expected type, and actual value provided\\tTEST-N4.4\\nAC-N5.1\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL exit with code 0 on success, 1 for recipe validation errors, 2 for template rendering errors, 3 for file operation errors, 4 for variable validation errors\\tTEST-N5.1\\nAC-N5.2\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL use the exit code corresponding to the first pipeline stage that fails: recipe validation (1) before variable validation (4) before template rendering (2) before file operations (3)\\tTEST-N5.2\\nAC-N6.1\\tUbiquitous\\tThe system SHALL execute file operations in the order they appear in the recipe's `files` array\\tTEST-N6.1\\nAC-N6.2\\tEvent\\tWHEN an inject operation targets a file created by an earlier create operation in the same recipe, the system SHALL find and operate on the newly created file\\tTEST-N6.2\\nFR-1\\tAC-1.1 through AC-1.15\\tspec::fr1::*\\tPENDING\\nFR-2\\tAC-2.1 through AC-2.16\\tspec::fr2::*\\tPENDING\\nFR-3\\tAC-3.1 through AC-3.18\\tspec::fr3::*\\tPENDING\\nFR-4\\tAC-4.1 through AC-4.10\\tspec::fr4::*\\tPENDING\\nFR-5\\tAC-5.1 through AC-5.17\\tspec::fr5::*\\tPENDING\\nFR-6\\tAC-6.1 through AC-6.11\\tspec::fr6::*\\tPENDING\\nFR-7\\tAC-7.1 through AC-7.7\\tspec::fr7::*\\tPENDING\\nNFR-1\\tAC-N1.1, AC-N1.2\\tspec::nfr1::*\\tPENDING\\nNFR-2\\tAC-N2.1, AC-N2.2\\tspec::nfr2::*\\tPENDING\\nNFR-3\\tAC-N3.1, AC-N3.2\\tspec::nfr3::*\\tPENDING\\nNFR-4\\tAC-N4.1 through AC-N4.4\\tspec::nfr4::*\\tPENDING\\nNFR-5\\tAC-N5.1, AC-N6.2\\tspec::nfr6::*\\tPENDING\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-205456.md:49:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_25\",\"type\":\"agent_message\",\"text\":\"**Critical** (blocks implementation):\\n- Dry-run core design is internally inconsistent: the interface uses immutable context (`execute_operation(..., ctx: &ExecutionContext)`), but requirements require mutating virtual file state across operations (create then inject in dry-run). This needs an explicit mutable state contract before coding. [ARCHITECTURE.md:137](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:137), [ARCHITECTURE.md:153](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:153), [SPEC.md:147](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:147), [PLAN.md:132](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:132)\\n- Partial-write behavior is contradictory: planning docs claim “no partial writes,” while architecture/spec explicitly say execution failures can leave partial writes. This affects recovery semantics, test expectations, and operator guidance. [PLAN.md:216](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:216), [SHARED-CONTEXT.md:31](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SHARED-CONTEXT.md:31), [ARCHITECTURE.md:362](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:362), [SPEC.md:370](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:370)\\n- `jig render` behavior conflicts across docs: architecture demo expects type-validation failure on `render`, but spec says `render` has no recipe context and no type validation. This must be resolved before implementation/testing. [SPEC.md:49](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:49), [SPEC.md:161](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:161), [SPEC.md:162](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:162)\\n- Some EARS criteria are not atomic/test-friendly (multiple independent SHALLs in one row), especially AC-6.8 and AC-6.9; they should be split for deterministic traceability. items (array), the system SHALL parse all fields into the VariableDecl struct | TEST-1.2 |\\n26:| AC-1.3 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares `create` file operations with template, to, and skip_if_exists fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Create | TEST-1.3 |\\n27:| AC-1.4 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares `inject` file operations with template, inject, after/before/prepend/append, at, and skip_if fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Inject | TEST-1.4 |\\n28:| AC-1.5 | Unwanted | IF the recipe YAML is malformed (invalid YAML syntax), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and an error message identifying the parse failure location | TEST-1.5 |\\n29:| AC-1.6 | Unwanted | IF a required field is missing from the recipe (e.g., files with no template), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and name the missing field | TEST-1.6 |\\n30:| AC-1.7 | Event | WHEN a recipe references template files, the system SHALL resolve those paths relative to the recipe file location, not the working directory | TEST-1.7 |\\n31:| AC-1.8 | Unwanted | IF a referenced template file does not exist at the resolved path, the system SHALL exit with code 1 and report which template is missing and where it looked | TEST-1.8 |\\n32:| AC-1.9 | Event | WHEN the recipe has optional metadata fields (name, description), the system SHALL accept recipes with or without them | TEST-1.9 |\\n33:| AC-1.10
