Ubiquitous | The system SHALL register and support all 13 built-in filters: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join | TEST-3.4 |\\n    63\\t| AC-3.5 | Event | WHEN `snakecase` is applied to \\\"BookingService\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\"booking_service\\\" | TEST-3.5 |\\n    64\\t| AC-3.6 | Event | WHEN `camelcase` is applied to \\\"booking_service\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\"bookingService\\\" | TEST-3.6 |\\n    65\\t| AC-3.7 | Event | WHEN `pascalcase` is applied to \\\"booking_service\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\"BookingService\\\" | TEST-3.7 |\\n    66\\t| AC-3.8 | Event | WHEN `kebabcase` is applied to \\\"BookingService\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\"booking-service\\\" | TEST-3.8 |\\n    67\\t| AC-3.9 | Event | WHEN `replace` is applied as `\\\"a.b.c\\\" \\\\| replace('.', '/')`, the system SHALL produce \\\"a/b/c\\\" | TEST-3.9 |\\n    68\\t| AC-3.10 | Event | WHEN `pluralize` is applied to \\\"hotel\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\"hotels\\\" | TEST-3.10 |\\n    69\\t| AC-3.11 | Event | WHEN `singularize` is applied to \\\"hotels\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\"hotel\\\" | TEST-3.11 |\\n    70\\t| AC-3.12 | Event | WHEN `quote` is applied to \\\"hello\\\", the system SHALL produce `\\\"hello\\\"` (with literal quotes) | TEST-3.12 |\\n    71\\t| AC-3.13 | Event | WHEN `indent(4)` is applied to a multiline string, the system SHALL indent each line by 4 spaces | TEST-3.13 |\\n    72\\t| AC-3.14 | Event | WHEN `join(\\\", \\\")` is applied to `[\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\", \\\"c\\\"]`, the system SHALL produce \\\"a, b, c\\\" | TEST-3.14 |\\n    73\\t| AC-3.15 | Event | WHEN `{# comment #}` appears in a template, the system SHALL strip it from the output | TEST-3.15 |\\n    74\\t| AC-3.16 | Event | WHEN `{% raw %}...{% endraw %}` appears in a template, the system SHALL output the content literally without interpreting Jinja2 syntax | TEST-3.16 |\\n    75\\t| AC-3.17 | Unwanted | IF a template references an undefined variable, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and include a \\\"did you mean?\\\" hint when a close match exists | TEST-3.17 |\\n    76\\t| AC-3.18 | Unwanted | IF a template has a Jinja2 syntax error, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and report the template file path and line number | TEST-3.18 |\\n    77\\t\\n    78\\t#### FR-4: Create Operation\\n    79\\t\\n    80\\tRender a template and write it to a new file path. Support templated output paths, automatic directory creation, and skip-if-exists idempotency.\\n    81\\t\\n    82\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n    83\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n    84\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n    85\\t| AC-4.1 | Event | WHEN a create operation executes, the system SHALL render the template and write the result to the path specified in `to` | TEST-4.1 |\\n    86\\t| AC-4.2 | Event | WHEN the `to` path contains Jinja2 expressions (e.g., `tests/{{ module \\\\| replace('.', '/') }}/test_{{ class_name \\\\| snakecase }}.py`), the system SHALL render the path before writing | TEST-4.2 |\\n    87\\t| AC-4.3 | Event | WHEN parent directories in the `to` path do not exist, the system SHALL create them automatically | TEST-4.3 |\\n    88\\t| AC-4.4 | Event | WHEN `skip_if_exists: true` and the target file already exists, the system SHALL skip the operation and report `\\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\"` with a reason | TEST-4.4 |\\n    89\\t| AC-4.5 | Unwanted | IF `skip_if_exists: false` (default) and the target file already exists and `--force` is not set, the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting the conflict | TEST-4.5 |\\n    90\\t| AC-4.6 | Event | WHEN `--force` is set and the target file already exists, the system SHALL overwrite the file regardless of skip_if_exists | TEST-4.6 |\\n    91\\t| AC-4.7 | Event | WHEN `--base-dir` is set, the system SHALL resolve `to` paths relative to the base directory instead of the working directory | TEST-4.7 |\\n    92\\t| AC-4.8 | Event | WHEN a create operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\"action\\\": \\\"create\\\"` with the path and line count | TEST-4.8 |\\n    93\\t\\n    94\\t#### FR-5: Inject Operation\\n    95\\t\\n    96\\tRender a template and insert the result into an existing file at a location determined by regex pattern matching. Support idempotency via skip_if.\\n    97\\t\\n    98\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n    99\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   100\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   101\\t| AC-5.1 | Event | WHEN `after: \\\"regex\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line after the first matching line | TEST-5.1 |\\n   102\\t| AC-5.2 | Event | WHEN `before: \\\"regex\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line before the first matching line | TEST-5.2 |\\n   103\\t| AC-5.3 | Event | WHEN `prepend: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very beginning of the file | TEST-5.3 |\\n   104\\t| AC-5.4 | Event | WHEN `append: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very end of the file | TEST-5.4 |\\n   105\\t| AC-5.5 | Event | WHEN `at: last` is specified with `after` or `before`, the system SHALL use the last regex match instead of the first | TEST-5.5 |\\n   106\\t| AC-5.6 | Event | WHEN `at: first` (default) is specified, the system SHALL use the first regex match | TEST-5.6 |\\n   107\\t| AC-5.7 | Event | WHEN `skip_if: \\\"string\\\"` is specified and the string exists in the target file, the system SHALL skip the injection and report `\\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\"` with a reason | TEST-5.7 |\\n   108\\t| AC-5.8 | Unwanted | IF the regex pattern matches zero lines in the target file, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the pattern, the file path, and a hint | TEST-5.8 |\\n   109\\t| AC-5.9 | Unwanted | IF the target file for injection does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the missing file path | TEST-5.9 |\\n   110\\t| AC-5.10 | Event | WHEN an inject operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\"action\\\": \\\"inject\\\"` with the path, location description, and line count | TEST-5.10 |\\n   111\\t| AC-5.11 | Event | WHEN the inject path contains Jinja2 expressions, the system SHALL render the path before resolving it | TEST-5.11 |\\n   112\\t\\n   113\\t#### FR-6: Output Formatting\\n   114\\t\\n   115\\tReport operation results as JSON (stdout, for LLM callers) or human-readable text (stderr, for terminal users). Auto-detect the appropriate mode.\\n   116\\t\\n   117\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   118\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   119\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   120\\t| AC-6.1 | State | WHILE stdout is a TTY, the system SHALL output human-readable colored text to stderr by default | TEST-6.1 |\\n   121\\t| AC-6.2 | State | WHILE stdout is piped (not a TTY), the system SHALL output JSON to stdout by default | TEST-6.2 |\\n   122\\t| AC-6.3 | Event | WHEN `--json` is specified, the system SHALL force JSON output to stdout regardless of TTY detection | TEST-6.3 |\\n   123\\t| AC-6.4 | Event | WHEN `--quiet` is specified, the system SHALL suppress all output except errors | TEST-6.4 |\\n   124\\t| AC-6.5 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include an `operations` array with action, path, lines (for success), reason (for skip), or error details (for error) for each operation | TEST-6.5 |\\n   125\\t| AC-6.6 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include `files_written` and `files_skipped` summary arrays | TEST-6.6 |\\n   126\\t| AC-6.7 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified, the system SHALL include rendered template content in the output | TEST-6.7 |\\n   127\\t| AC-6.8 | Event | WHEN `--dry-run` is specified, the system SHALL produce identical output format but write no files to disk | TEST-6.8 |\\n   128\\t\\n   129\\t#### FR-7: CLI Interface\\n   130\\t\\n   131\\tProvide four subcommands for v0.1: `validate`, `vars`, `render`, and `run`. Accept global options for variable sources, output control, and execution modes.\\n   132\\t\\n   133\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   134\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   135\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   136\\t| AC-7.1 | Event | WHEN `jig validate <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL parse the recipe and report whether it is valid, listing variables and operations | TEST-7.1 |\\n   137\\t| AC-7.2 | Event | WHEN `jig vars <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL output the expected variables as a JSON object with type, required, default, and description fields | TEST-7.2 |\\n   138\\t| AC-7.3 | Event | WHEN `jig render <template> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL render the template with the given variables and output the result to stdout | TEST-7.3 |\\n   139\\t| AC-7.4 | Event | WHEN `jig render` is invoked with `--to <path>`, the system SHALL write the rendered output to the specified file instead of stdout | TEST-7.4 |\\n   140\\t| AC-7.5 | Event | WHEN `jig run <recipe> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL execute all file operations in the recipe in declaration order | TEST-7.5 |\\n   141\\t| AC-7.6 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accept global options: `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose` | TEST-7.6 |\\n   142\\t\\n   143\\t### Non-Functional Requirements\\n   144\\t\\n   145\\t#### NFR-1: Deterministic Output\\n   146\\t\\n   147\\tSame inputs must always produce the same outputs, with no randomness or environment-dependent behavior in rendered content.\\n   148\\t\\n   149\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   150\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   151\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   152\\t| AC-N1.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL produce byte-identical output when given the same recipe, variables, and existing files across multiple runs | TEST-N1.1 |\\n   153\\t| AC-N1.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not include timestamps, random values, or machine-specific identifiers in rendered output | TEST-N1.2 |\\n   154\\t\\n   155\\t#### NFR-2: Idempotent Operations\\n   156\\t\\n   157\\tRunning the same recipe twice with the same variables must produce no changes on the second run.\\n   158\\t\\n   159\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   160\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   161\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   162\\t| AC-N2.1 | Event | WHEN a recipe is run a second time with the same variables and the same existing files, the system SHALL report all operations as `\\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\"` with reasons | TEST-N2.1 |\\n   163\\t| AC-N2.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not produce duplicate content when create uses `skip_if_exists: true` or inject uses `skip_if` | TEST-N2.2 |\\n   164\\t\\n   165\\t#### NFR-3: Single Static Binary\\n   166\\t\\n   167\\tZero runtime dependencies. One binary that runs on macOS and Linux without Python, Node, JVM, or any shared libraries.\\n   168\\t\\n   169\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   170\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   171\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   172\\t| AC-N3.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL compile to a single static binary with no runtime dependencies | TEST-N3.1 |\\n   173\\t| AC-N3.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries to run | TEST-N3.2 |\\n   174\\t\\n   175\\t#### NFR-4: Structured Errors with Rendered Content\\n   176\\t\\n   177\\tErrors must include what/where/why/hint. File operation errors must include the rendered content so callers can fall back.\\n   178\\t\\n   179\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   180\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   181\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   182\\t| AC-N4.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL include what, where, why, and hint fields in every error message | TEST-N4.1 |\\n   183\\t| AC-N4.2 | Event | WHEN a file operation fails (exit code 3), the system SHALL include the rendered template content in the error output so the caller can fall back to manual editing | TEST-N4.2 |\\n   184\\t| AC-N4.3 | Event | WHEN a template rendering error occurs, the system SHALL report the template file path and the line number of the error | TEST-N4.3 |\\n   185\\t| AC-N4.4 | Event | WHEN a variable validation error occurs, the system SHALL report the variable name, expected type, and actual value provided | TEST-N4.4 |\\n   186\\t\\n   187\\t#### NFR-5: Stable Exit Codes\\n   188\\t\\n   189\\tExit codes are API. They must be deterministic and match the defined mapping.\\n   190\\t\\n   191\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   192\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   193\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   194\\t| AC-N5.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL exit with code 0 on success, 1 for recipe validation errors, 2 for template rendering errors, 3 for file operation errors, 4 for variable validation errors | TEST-N5.1 |\\n   195\\t| AC-N5.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL use the exit code of the first error encountered when multiple errors occur | TEST-N5.2 |\\n   196\\t\\n   197\\t#### NFR-6: Ordered Execution\\n   198\\t\\n   199\\tFile operations must execute in declaration order so later operations can depend on files created by earlier ones.\\n   200\\t\\n   201\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   202\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   203\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   204\\t| AC-N6.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL execute file operations in the order they appear in the recipe's `files` array | TEST-N6.1 |\\n   205\\t| AC-N6.2 | Event | WHEN an inject operation targets a file created by an earlier create operation in the same recipe, the system SHALL find and operate on the newly created file | TEST-N6.2 |\\n   206\\t\\n   207\\t## Interfaces\\n   208\\t\\n   209\\t### Public API (CLI)\\n   210\\t\\n   211\\t```\\n   212\\tjig validate <recipe>\\n   213\\tjig vars <recipe>\\n   214\\tjig render <template> [--to <path>] --vars '<json>'\\n   215\\tjig run <recipe> --vars '<json>' [options]\\n   216\\t\\n   217\\tOptions:\\n   218\\t  --vars <json>        Inline variables as JSON string\\n   219\\t  --vars-file <path>   Variables from a JSON file\\n   220\\t  --vars-stdin         Read variables from stdin\\n   221\\t  --dry-run            Preview without writing\\n   222\\t  --json               Force JSON output\\n   223\\t  --quiet              Suppress non-error output\\n   224\\t  --force              Overwrite existing files\\n   225\\t  --base-dir <path>    Resolve output paths from this directory\\n   226\\t  --verbose            Include rendered content in output\\n   227\\t```\\n   228\\t\\n   229\\t### JSON Output Schema\\n   230\\t\\n   231\\t```json\\n   232\\t{\\n   233\\t  \\\"operations\\\": [\\n   234\\t    {\\\"action\\\": \\\"create\\\", \\\"path\\\": \\\"...\\\", \\\"lines\\\": 42},\\n   235\\t    {\\\"action\\\": \\\"inject\\\", \\\"path\\\": \\\"...\\\", \\\"location\\\": \\\"after:^# fixtures\\\", \\\"lines\\\": 3},\\n   236\\t    {\\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\", \\\"path\\\": \\\"...\\\", \\\"reason\\\": \\\"skip_if matched: BookingService\\\"},\\n   237\\t    {\\\"action\\\": \\\"error\\\", \\\"path\\\": \\\"...\\\", \\\"error\\\": \\\"...\\\", \\\"hint\\\": \\\"...\\\", \\\"rendered_content\\\": \\\"...\\\"}\\n   238\\t  ],\\n   239\\t  \\\"files_written\\\": [\\\"...\\\"],\\n   240\\t  \\\"files_skipped\\\": [\\\"...\\\"]\\n   241\\t}\\n   242\\t```\\n   243\\t\\n   244\\t### Internal Interfaces\\n   245\\t\\n   246\\t```rust\\n   247\\t// Recipe → Variable Validator\\n   248\\tfn validate_variables(decls: &IndexMap<String, VariableDecl>, provided: Value) -> Result<Value, JigError>;\\n   249\\t\\n   250\\t// Recipe + Variables → Renderer\\n   251\\tfn render_template(env: &Environment, template_name: &str, vars: &Value) -> Result<String, JigError>;\\n   252\\t\\n   253\\t// Rendered content + FileOp → Operation Executor\\n   254\\tfn execute_operation(op: &FileOp, rendered: &str, ctx: &ExecutionContext) -> OpResult;\\n   255\\t\\n   256\\t// Vec<OpResult> → Output Formatter\\n   257\\tfn format_output(results: &[OpResult], mode: OutputMode) -> String;\\n   258\\t```\\n   259\\t\\n   260\\t## Data Model\\n   261\\t\\n   262\\t```rust\\n   263\\tstruct Recipe {\\n   264\\t    name: Option<String>,\\n   265\\t    description: Option<String>,\\n   266\\t    variables: IndexMap<String, VariableDecl>,\\n   267\\t    files: Vec<FileOp>,\\n   268\\t}\\n   269\\t\\n   270\\tstruct VariableDecl {\\n   271\\t    var_type: VarType,          // string, number, boolean, array, object, enum\\n   272\\t    required: bool,             // default: false\\n   273\\t    default: Option<Value>,\\n   274\\t    description: Option<String>,\\n   275\\t    values: Option<Vec<String>>, // for enum type\\n   276\\t    items: Option<VarType>,      // for array type\\n   277\\t}\\n   278\\t\\n   279\\tenum FileOp {\\n   280\\t    Create { template: String, to: String, skip_if_exists: bool },\\n   281\\t    Inject { template: String, inject: String, mode: InjectMode, skip_if: Option<String> },\\n   282\\t}\\n   283\\t\\n   284\\tenum InjectMode {\\n   285\\t    After { pattern: String, at: MatchPosition },\\n   286\\t    Before { pattern: String, at: MatchPosition },\\n   287\\t    Prepend,\\n   288\\t    Append,\\n   289\\t}\\n   290\\t\\n   291\\tenum MatchPosition { First, Last }\\n   292\\t\\n   293\\tenum OpResult {\\n   294\\t    Success { action: &'static str, path: PathBuf, lines: usize },\\n   295\\t    Skip { path: PathBuf, reason: String },\\n   296\\t    Error { path: PathBuf, error: StructuredError, rendered_content: String },\\n   297\\t}\\n   298\\t\\n   299\\tenum JigError {\\n   300\\t    RecipeValidation(String),     // exit 1\\n   301\\t    TemplateRendering(String),    // exit 2\\n   302\\t    FileOperation(String),        // exit 3\\n   303\\t    VariableValidation(String),   // exit 4\\n   304\\t}\\n   305\\t```\\n   306\\t\\n   307\\t## Error Handling\\n   308\\t\\n   309\\t| Error Category | Exit Code | Examples |\\n   310\\t|----------------|-----------|----------|\\n   311\\t| Recipe validation | 1 | Malformed YAML, missing fields, missing template files |\\n   312\\t| Template rendering | 2 | Undefined variable, Jinja2 syntax error |\\n   313\\t| File operation | 3 | Target file missing (inject), file exists (create), regex no match |\\n   314\\t| Variable validation | 4 | Missing required, wrong type, invalid enum value |\\n   315\\t\\n   316\\tEvery error includes: what happened, where (file/variable/line), why (expected vs actual), and a hint for the caller.\\n   317\\t\\n   318\\tFile operation errors additionally include `rendered_content` so the LLM caller can fall back to manual editing with the Edit tool.\\n   319\\t\\n   320\\t## Testing Strategy\\n   321\\t\\n   322\\t- **Spec tests:** One or more tests per AC-* criterion above, namespaced as `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}`\\n   323\\t- **Invariant tests:** Determinism (run twice, compare output), idempotency (run twice, second run all skips), exit code stability\\n   324\\t- **Integration fixtures:** Each fixture directory contains recipe.yaml, vars.json, templates/, existing/ (optional), expected/, expected_output.json (optional), expected_exit_code (optional)\\n   325\\t- **Snapshot tests:** `insta` for rendered template output, JSON output format, and error messages\\n   326\\t\\n   327\\t## Requirement Traceability\\n   328\\t\\n   329\\t| Requirement | Criteria | Test | Status |\\n   330\\t|-------------|----------|------|--------|\\n   331\\t| FR-1 | AC-1.1 through AC-1.9 | spec::fr1::* | PENDING |\\n   332\\t| FR-2 | AC-2.1 through AC-2.10 | spec::fr2::* | PENDING |\\n   333\\t| FR-3 | AC-3.1 through AC-3.18 | spec::fr3::* | PENDING |\\n   334\\t| FR-4 | AC-4.1 through AC-4.8 | spec::fr4::* | PENDING |\\n   335\\t| FR-5 | AC-5.1 through AC-5.11 | spec::fr5::* | PENDING |\\n   336\\t| FR-6 | AC-6.1 through AC-6.8 | spec::fr6::* | PENDING |\\n   337\\t| FR-7 | AC-7.1 through AC-7.6 | spec::fr7::* | PENDING |\\n   338\\t| NFR-1 | AC-N1.1, AC-N1.2 | spec::nfr1::* | PENDING |\\n   339\\t| NFR-2 | AC-N2.1, AC-N2.2 | spec::nfr2::* | PENDING |\\n   340\\t| NFR-3 | AC-N3.1, AC-N3.2 | spec::nfr3::* | PENDING |\\n   341\\t| NFR-4 | AC-N4.1 through AC-N4.4 | spec::nfr4::* | PENDING |\\n   342\\t| NFR-5 | AC-N5.1, AC-N5.2 | spec::nfr5::* | PENDING |\\n   343\\t| NFR-6 | AC-N6.1, AC-N6.2 | spec::nfr6::* | PENDING |\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-114234.md:14:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_4\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md'\",\"aggregated_output\":\"     1\\t# PLAN.md\\n     2\\t\\n     3\\t> Workstream: core-engine\\n     4\\t> Last updated: 2026-04-02\\n     5\\t> Status: Planned\\n     6\\t\\n     7\\t## Objective\\n     8\\t\\n     9\\tDeliver the minimum viable jig CLI: parse recipes, validate variables, render Jinja2 templates, execute create and inject file operations, and report structured results. This is the v0.1 feature set — the smallest thing that lets an LLM call `jig run` to produce files from a recipe instead of re-deriving boilerplate.\\n    10\\t\\n    11\\tCovers ARCHITECTURE.md Phases A through E (skeleton, rendering, create, inject, integration tests).\\n    12\\t\\n    13\\t## Phases\\n    14\\t\\n    15\\t### Phase 1: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\n    16\\tStatus: Planned\\n    17\\tTraces to: FR-1, FR-7 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-5\\n    18\\t\\n    19\\tBootstrap the Rust crate, wire up clap, and make recipe parsing work end-to-end. After this phase, `jig validate` and `jig vars` are functional commands.\\n    20\\t\\n    21\\t#### Milestones\\n    22\\t- [ ] 1.1: Cargo.toml with dependencies (serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap, thiserror)\\n    23\\t- [ ] 1.2: `src/error.rs` — JigError enum with exit code mapping (0-4), structured error fields (what/where/why/hint)\\n    24\\t- [ ] 1.3: `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe, VariableDecl, VarType, FileOp structs with serde deserialization; template path resolution relative to recipe location; structural validation (missing fields, missing template files)\\n    25\\t- [ ] 1.4: `src/variables.rs` — VariableDecl types and VarType enum (types only, no validation logic yet)\\n    26\\t- [ ] 1.5: `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands; wire recipe parsing; map errors to exit codes\\n    27\\t- [ ] 1.6: Unit tests for recipe parsing — valid recipe, missing fields, malformed YAML, missing template files, optional metadata\\n    28\\t\\n    29\\t#### Validation Criteria\\n    30\\t- `jig validate recipe.yaml` parses the example recipe from jig.md and exits 0\\n    31\\t- `jig validate bad.yaml` exits 1 with a clear error naming the problem\\n    32\\t- `jig vars recipe.yaml` outputs JSON matching the SPEC schema (type, required, default, description)\\n    33\\t- Template paths resolve relative to recipe file, not cwd\\n    34\\t- AC-1.1 through AC-1.9 have corresponding unit tests\\n    35\\t- AC-N5.1 exit codes are correct for recipe validation errors\\n    36\\t\\n    37\\t#### Key Files\\n    38\\t- `Cargo.toml`\\n    39\\t- `src/main.rs`\\n    40\\t- `src/recipe.rs`\\n    41\\t- `src/variables.rs` (types only)\\n    42\\t- `src/error.rs`\\n    43\\t\\n    44\\t---\\n    45\\t\\n    46\\t### Phase 2: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\n    47\\tStatus: Planned\\n    48\\tTraces to: FR-2, FR-3, NFR-1 (partial)\\n    49\\t\\n    50\\tFull variable pipeline (parse, merge, type-check) and template rendering with all 13 built-in filters. After this phase, `jig render` works.\\n    51\\t\\n    52\\t#### Milestones\\n    53\\t- [ ] 2.1: `src/variables.rs` — full validation: parse JSON from --vars/--vars-file/--vars-stdin; merge with precedence (defaults < file < stdin < inline); type-check against declarations; required field enforcement; enum validation; array item type validation\\n    54\\t- [ ] 2.2: `src/filters.rs` — all 13 built-in filters registered with minijinja: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join\\n    55\\t- [ ] 2.3: `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja Environment setup; template loading from recipe-relative paths; filter registration; render with variables context; structured error on undefined variable (with \\\"did you mean?\\\" via edit distance) and syntax errors (with file + line)\\n    56\\t- [ ] 2.4: Wire `render` subcommand in main.rs with --vars, --vars-file, --vars-stdin, --to options\\n    57\\t- [ ] 2.5: Unit tests for variable validation — every VarType, required missing, default fallback, enum rejection, array item mismatch, merge precedence\\n    58\\t- [ ] 2.6: Unit tests + insta snapshot tests for all 13 filters and template rendering (conditionals, loops, comments, raw blocks, undefined vars, syntax errors)\\n    59\\t\\n    60\\t#### Validation Criteria\\n    61\\t- All 13 filters produce correct output per AC-3.4 through AC-3.14\\n    62\\t- `jig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\"class_name\\\": \\\"BookingService\\\"}'` renders to stdout\\n    63\\t- Type mismatch exits 4 with expected vs actual type (AC-2.7)\\n    64\\t- Missing required variable exits 4 with variable name and hint (AC-2.5)\\n    65\\t- Merge precedence: inline --vars wins over --vars-file wins over defaults (AC-2.4)\\n    66\\t- Undefined template variable exits 2 with \\\"did you mean?\\\" hint (AC-3.17)\\n    67\\t- Template syntax error exits 2 with file path and line number (AC-3.18)\\n    68\\t- Same inputs produce byte-identical output across runs (AC-N1.1)\\n    69\\t\\n    70\\t#### Key Files\\n    71\\t- `src/variables.rs` (full implementation)\\n    72\\t- `src/filters.rs`\\n    73\\t- `src/renderer.rs`\\n    74\\t\\n    75\\t#### Dependencies\\n    76\\t- heck crate for case conversions\\n    77\\t- minijinja crate for template rendering\\n    78\\t\\n    79\\t---\\n    80\\t\\n    81\\t### Phase 3: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\n    82\\tStatus: Planned\\n    83\\tTraces to: FR-4, FR-6, FR-7 (complete), NFR-2 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-6\\n    84\\t\\n    85\\tFile creation with templated output paths, directory auto-creation, skip_if_exists, and dual-stream output (JSON stdout / human stderr). After this phase, `jig run` works for create-only recipes.\\n    86\\t\\n    87\\t#### Milestones\\n    88\\t- [ ] 3.1: `src/operations/mod.rs` — ExecutionContext struct (base_dir, dry_run, force); OpResult enum (Success/Skip/Error); operation dispatch (create only initially)\\n    89\\t- [ ] 3.2: `src/operations/create.rs` — render `to` path as template; create parent directories; write rendered content; skip_if_exists logic; --force override; --base-dir path resolution\\n    90\\t- [ ] 3.3: `src/output.rs` — OutputMode enum (Json/Human/Quiet); TTY auto-detection; JSON serialization of operations array + files_written/files_skipped; colored human output to stderr; --verbose includes rendered content\\n    91\\t- [ ] 3.4: Wire `run` subcommand in main.rs with --dry-run, --json, --quiet, --force, --base-dir, --verbose; execute operations in declaration order\\n    92\\t- [ ] 3.5: Unit tests for create operation — happy path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, templated paths, directory creation, file-already-exists error\\n    93\\t- [ ] 3.6: Unit tests for output formatting — JSON schema matches SPEC, human output, quiet mode, verbose mode, dry-run\\n    94\\t\\n    95\\t#### Validation Criteria\\n    96\\t- `jig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'` creates files at templated paths (AC-4.1, AC-4.2)\\n    97\\t- Parent directories created automatically (AC-4.3)\\n    98\\t- skip_if_exists: true skips existing files with action:\\\"skip\\\" (AC-4.4)\\n    99\\t- Default (skip_if_exists: false) errors on existing file without --force (AC-4.5)\\n   100\\t- --force overwrites regardless (AC-4.6)\\n   101\\t- --base-dir changes output root (AC-4.7)\\n   102\\t- --dry-run produces output but writes nothing (AC-6.8)\\n   103\\t- JSON output when piped, human output when TTY (AC-6.1, AC-6.2)\\n   104\\t- --json forces JSON (AC-6.3), --quiet suppresses non-errors (AC-6.4)\\n   105\\t- Operations execute in declaration order (AC-N6.1)\\n   106\\t- Second run with skip_if_exists: true reports all skips (AC-N2.1)\\n   107\\t\\n   108\\t#### Key Files\\n   109\\t- `src/operations/mod.rs`\\n   110\\t- `src/operations/create.rs`\\n   111\\t- `src/output.rs`\\n   112\\t- `src/main.rs` (run subcommand)\\n   113\\t\\n   114\\t#### Dependencies\\n   115\\t- owo-colors crate for terminal coloring\\n   116\\t\\n   117\\t---\\n   118\\t\\n   119\\t### Phase 4: Inject Operation\\n   120\\tStatus: Planned\\n   121\\tTraces to: FR-5, NFR-2 (complete), NFR-6\\n   122\\t\\n   123\\tContent injection into existing files via regex anchoring. All injection modes: after, before, prepend, append, with at:first/last match selection and skip_if idempotency. This completes the v0.1 operation set.\\n   124\\t\\n   125\\t#### Milestones\\n   126\\t- [ ] 4.1: `src/operations/inject.rs` — read target file; skip_if string search; regex anchor matching (after/before); at:first/at:last match selection; prepend/append modes; render inject path as template; write modified content\\n   127\\t- [ ] 4.2: Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\n   128\\t- [ ] 4.3: Unit tests for every injection mode — after (first match), after (last match), before, prepend, append, skip_if, missing target file error, regex no-match error, templated inject path\\n   129\\t- [ ] 4.4: Integration test: recipe with create + inject in same run (create file, then inject into it — tests ordered execution AC-N6.2)\\n   130\\t\\n   131\\t#### Validation Criteria\\n   132\\t- after: content on line after first match (AC-5.1)\\n   133\\t- before: content on line before first match (AC-5.2)\\n   134\\t- prepend: content at start of file (AC-5.3)\\n   135\\t- append: content at end of file (AC-5.4)\\n   136\\t- at:last uses last match (AC-5.5), at:first (default) uses first (AC-5.6)\\n   137\\t- skip_if: skips when string found in file, reports action:\\\"skip\\\" (AC-5.7)\\n   138\\t- Regex no-match exits 3 with pattern, file path, hint (AC-5.8)\\n   139\\t- Missing target file exits 3 (AC-5.9)\\n   140\\t- Inject path renders as template (AC-5.11)\\n   141\\t- Create-then-inject in same recipe works (AC-N6.2)\\n   142\\t- Second run with skip_if shows all skips — no duplicate content (AC-N2.2)\\n   143\\t\\n   144\\t#### Key Files\\n   145\\t- `src/operations/inject.rs`\\n   146\\t- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\n   147\\t\\n   148\\t#### Dependencies\\n   149\\t- regex crate\\n   150\\t\\n   151\\t---\\n   152\\t\\n   153\\t### Phase 5: Integration Test Framework\\n   154\\tStatus: Planned\\n   155\\tTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\n   156\\t\\n   157\\tFixture-based integration test harness that makes adding new test cases a matter of adding a directory. Snapshot tests for all output formats. This phase validates the entire v0.1 pipeline end-to-end.\\n   158\\t\\n   159\\t#### Milestones\\n   160\\t- [ ] 5.1: Test harness in `tests/integration.rs` — fixture discovery; copy existing/ to temp dir; run jig as subprocess; diff output against expected/; assert JSON output against expected_output.json; assert exit code against expected_exit_code\\n   161\\t- [ ] 5.2: Fixtures for create operations — simple create, templated path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, directory creation\\n   162\\t- [ ] 5.3: Fixtures for inject operations — after/before/prepend/append, at:first/at:last, skip_if\\n   163\\t- [ ] 5.4: Fixtures for error cases — missing vars, bad type, missing template, missing target file, regex no-match, malformed YAML, file exists without force\\n   164\\t- [ ] 5.5: Fixtures for combined operations — create + inject in one recipe, multi-file recipe, idempotency (run twice)\\n   165\\t- [ ] 5.6: insta snapshot tests for JSON output format, human output format, error message format\\n   166\\t- [ ] 5.7: Determinism test — run same recipe twice, assert byte-identical output (AC-N1.1)\\n   167\\t\\n   168\\t#### Validation Criteria\\n   169\\t- `cargo test` runs all unit + integration + snapshot tests green\\n   170\\t- Every operation mode has at least one fixture\\n   171\\t- Every error exit code (1-4) has at least one fixture\\n   172\\t- Adding a new test case requires only a new directory, no code changes\\n   173\\t- Idempotency fixture: second run produces all skips, no file changes\\n   174\\t\\n   175\\t#### Key Files\\n   176\\t- `tests/integration.rs`\\n   177\\t- `tests/fixtures/` (directory tree)\\n   178\\t\\n   179\\t#### Dependencies\\n   180\\t- insta crate for snapshot testing\\n   181\\t- assert_cmd or similar for subprocess testing (or raw std::process::Command)\\n   182\\t\\n   183\\t## Dependencies\\n   184\\t\\n   185\\t- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\n   186\\t- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\n   187\\t\\n   188\\t## Key Decisions\\n   189\\t\\n   190\\t| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\n   191\\t|----------|--------|-----------|\\n   192\\t| Operations as enum, not trait objects | `FileOp` enum with match dispatch | Only 2 variants in v0.1 (create, inject). Trait abstraction adds complexity for no benefit until user-defined operations exist, which isn't planned. (ARCHITECTURE D-4) |\\n   193\\t| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages (ARCHITECTURE D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\n   194\\t| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable (ARCHITECTURE D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\n   195\\t| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism (ARCHITECTURE D-5, I-1) |\\n   196\\t| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate, good Display/Error trait impls |\\n   197\\t| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\n   198\\t| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\n   199\\t| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling. Acceptable dependency for correctness. |\\n   200\\t| \\\"did you mean?\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) against declared variable names | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Hand-rolling edit distance serves I-10 (graceful degradation) — the error tells the caller exactly what to fix. |\\n   201\\t| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves, even for ones minijinja provides (replace, indent, join) | We control exact behavior. No surprises from upstream minijinja changes. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\n   202\\t| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. Templates reference variables not created files. (D-1) |\\n   203\\t| Extra variables pass through | Don't error or warn on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness here breaks real usage. |\\n   204\\t| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later operations may depend on earlier ones (e.g., inject targets a prior create). (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\n   205\\t\\n   206\\t## Risks / Open Questions\\n   207\\t\\n   208\\t- **TTY detection on all platforms**: Need to verify `std::io::IsTerminal` works correctly on macOS + Linux. Rust 1.70+ has `IsTerminal` in std — prefer that over external crate.\\n   209\\t- **stdin variable reading conflicts**: If --vars-stdin is used, stdin is consumed for variables. This is fine since jig is non-interactive (I-3), but document that stdin can only be used for one purpose per invocation.\\n   210\\t- **Large file handling for inject**: The current design reads the entire target file into memory for injection. This is fine for source files (typically <10K lines) but should be documented as a known limitation.\\n   211\\t\\n   212\\t## Resolved Questions\\n   213\\t\\n   214\\t- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\n   215\\t- **\\\"did you mean?\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names. minijinja doesn't provide this. Serves I-10.\\n   216\\t- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. We own the behavior, upstream changes don't affect us. Serves I-1.\\n   217\\t\\n   218\\t## Execution Order\\n   219\\t\\n   220\\t```\\n   221\\tPhase 1 ──► Phase 2 ──► Phase 3 ──► Phase 4 ──► Phase 5\\n   222\\tskeleton     vars+render  create+output inject     integration\\n   223\\t                                                    tests\\n   224\\t```\\n   225\\t\\n   226\\tEach phase produces a working, testable artifact. No phase depends on \\\"wiring up later.\\\" The CLI grows incrementally:\\n   227\\t- After Phase 1: `jig validate`, `jig vars`\\n   228\\t- After Phase 2: + `jig render`\\n   229\\t- After Phase 3: + `jig run` (create only)\\n   230\\t- After Phase 4: + `jig run` (create + inject) — **v0.1 complete**\\n   231\\t- After Phase 5: Full test coverage validates the release\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-114234.md:16:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_6\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/ARCHITECTURE.md'\",\"aggregated_output\":\"     1\\t# ARCHITECTURE.md\\n     2\\t\\n     3\\tTarget architecture for jig, the iterative build strategy to get there, and the evaluation criteria at each gate. This document grounds all workstream planning.\\n     4\\t\\n     5\\t## System Overview\\n     6\\t\\n     7\\tjig is a linear pipeline: parse CLI args, load recipe, validate variables, render templates, execute file operations, format output, exit. Every stage is independently testable. No stage has side effects except operation execution (which writes files).\\n     8\\t\\n     9\\t```\\n    10\\tCLI Input\\n    11\\t  |\\n    12\\t  v\\n    13\\t+------------------+\\n    14\\t| CLI Parser       |  args, flags, var sources\\n    15\\t| (clap)           |\\n    16\\t+------------------+\\n    17\\t  |\\n    18\\t  v\\n    19\\t+------------------+\\n    20\\t| Recipe Loader    |  YAML -> Recipe struct\\n    21\\t| (serde_yaml)     |\\n    22\\t+------------------+\\n    23\\t  |\\n    24\\t  v\\n    25\\t+------------------+\\n    26\\t| Variable         |  JSON input -> typed, validated Variables\\n    27\\t| Validator        |  merge: defaults < file < stdin < inline\\n    28\\t| (serde_json)     |\\n    29\\t+------------------+\\n    30\\t  |\\n    31\\t  v\\n    32\\t+------------------+\\n    33\\t| Template         |  template + variables -> rendered string\\n    34\\t| Renderer         |  custom filters registered here\\n    35\\t| (minijinja)      |\\n    36\\t+------------------+\\n    37\\t  |\\n    38\\t  v\\n    39\\t+------------------+\\n    40\\t| Operation        |  rendered content + file operation spec\\n    41\\t| Executor         |  -> file writes (or dry-run recording)\\n    42\\t+------------------+\\n    43\\t  |\\n    44\\t  v\\n    45\\t+------------------+\\n    46\\t| Output           |  results -> JSON (stdout) or human (stderr)\\n    47\\t| Formatter        |  TTY auto-detect, --json, --quiet\\n    48\\t+------------------+\\n    49\\t  |\\n    50\\t  v\\n    51\\tExit Code (0-4)\\n    52\\t```\\n    53\\t\\n    54\\t## Module Map\\n    55\\t\\n    56\\t### `src/main.rs` — Entry Point\\n    57\\t\\n    58\\tOwns: CLI argument parsing via clap. Wires the pipeline together.\\n    59\\tDepends on: every other module (it's the composition root).\\n    60\\t\\n    61\\tResponsibilities:\\n    62\\t- Define CLI commands: `run`, `render`, `validate`, `vars`\\n    63\\t- Parse `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose`\\n    64\\t- Call pipeline stages in order\\n    65\\t- Map errors to exit codes\\n    66\\t\\n    67\\t### `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe Parsing\\n    68\\t\\n    69\\tOwns: `Recipe`, `VariableDecl`, `FileOp` structs. YAML deserialization.\\n    70\\tDepends on: serde, serde_yaml.\\n    71\\t\\n    72\\tResponsibilities:\\n    73\\t- Deserialize `recipe.yaml` into `Recipe` struct\\n    74\\t- Validate structural correctness (required fields present, template files exist relative to recipe)\\n    75\\t- Resolve template paths relative to the recipe file location (I-7: templates live with the consumer)\\n    76\\t- Distinguish operation types: create vs inject vs replace vs patch\\n    77\\t\\n    78\\tKey types:\\n    79\\t```rust\\n    80\\tstruct Recipe {\\n    81\\t    name: Option<String>,\\n    82\\t    description: Option<String>,\\n    83\\t    variables: IndexMap<String, VariableDecl>,\\n    84\\t    files: Vec<FileOp>,\\n    85\\t}\\n    86\\t\\n    87\\tstruct VariableDecl {\\n    88\\t    var_type: VarType,       // string, number, boolean, array, object, enum\\n    89\\t    required: bool,\\n    90\\t    default: Option<Value>,\\n    91\\t    description: Option<String>,\\n    92\\t    values: Option<Vec<String>>,  // for enum type\\n    93\\t    items: Option<VarType>,       // for array type\\n    94\\t}\\n    95\\t\\n    96\\tenum FileOp {\\n    97\\t    Create { template: String, to: String, skip_if_exists: bool },\\n    98\\t    Inject { template: String, inject: String, mode: InjectMode, skip_if: Option<String> },\\n    99\\t    Replace { template: String, replace: String, spec: ReplaceSpec, fallback: Fallback },\\n   100\\t    Patch { template: String, patch: String, anchor: Anchor, skip_if: Option<String> },\\n   101\\t}\\n   102\\t```\\n   103\\t\\n   104\\t### `src/variables.rs` — Variable Validation\\n   105\\t\\n   106\\tOwns: Variable merging, type checking, default application.\\n   107\\tDepends on: serde_json, recipe types.\\n   108\\t\\n   109\\tResponsibilities:\\n   110\\t- Parse JSON from multiple sources (inline string, file, stdin)\\n   111\\t- Merge with precedence: recipe defaults < vars-file < vars-stdin < inline --vars\\n   112\\t- Type-check each value against its `VariableDecl`\\n   113\\t- Validate required fields are present\\n   114\\t- Validate enum values against allowed set\\n   115\\t- Validate array item types\\n   116\\t- Produce clear error messages with hints (I-4)\\n   117\\t\\n   118\\t### `src/renderer.rs` — Template Rendering\\n   119\\t\\n   120\\tOwns: minijinja `Environment` setup, filter registration, template loading.\\n   121\\tDepends on: minijinja, heck (for case conversion filters).\\n   122\\t\\n   123\\tResponsibilities:\\n   124\\t- Create minijinja `Environment` with templates loaded from recipe-relative paths\\n   125\\t- Register built-in filters: `snakecase`, `camelcase`, `pascalcase`, `kebabcase`, `upper`, `lower`, `capitalize`, `replace`, `pluralize`, `singularize`, `quote`, `indent`, `join`\\n   126\\t- Render a template with a variables context\\n   127\\t- Return `Result<String>` — never silently produce wrong output (I-10)\\n   128\\t\\n   129\\tDesign note: The renderer is stateless per invocation. Create environment, register filters, load templates, render, return. No caching needed at v0.1 scale.\\n   130\\t\\n   131\\t### `src/operations/mod.rs` — Operation Dispatch\\n   132\\t\\n   133\\tOwns: `Operation` trait, dispatch logic, dry-run tracking.\\n   134\\tDepends on: operation implementations, renderer output.\\n   135\\t\\n   136\\t```rust\\n   137\\ttrait Operation {\\n   138\\t    fn execute(&self, rendered: &str, ctx: &ExecutionContext) -> Result<OpResult>;\\n   139\\t}\\n   140\\t\\n   141\\tstruct ExecutionContext {\\n   142\\t    base_dir: PathBuf,\\n   143\\t    dry_run: bool,\\n   144\\t    force: bool,\\n   145\\t}\\n   146\\t\\n   147\\tenum OpResult {\\n   148\\t    Success { action: &'static str, path: PathBuf, lines: usize },\\n   149\\t    Skip { path: PathBuf, reason: String },\\n   150\\t    Error { path: PathBuf, error: StructuredError, rendered_content: String },\\n   151\\t}\\n   152\\t```\\n   153\\t\\n   154\\tOperations execute in declaration order (I-9). Each operation receives the already-rendered content — rendering happens before execution, not during.\\n   155\\t\\n   156\\t### `src/operations/create.rs` — Create Operation\\n   157\\t\\n   158\\tOwns: New file creation logic.\\n   159\\tDepends on: std::fs.\\n   160\\t\\n   161\\tResponsibilities:\\n   162\\t- Render the `to` path as a template (it can contain `{{ variables }}`)\\n   163\\t- Create parent directories as needed\\n   164\\t- Write rendered content to the target path\\n   165\\t- `skip_if_exists`: if true and target exists, return `OpResult::Skip`\\n   166\\t- If not `--force` and target exists and `skip_if_exists` is false, return error\\n   167\\t\\n   168\\t### `src/operations/inject.rs` — Inject Operation\\n   169\\t\\n   170\\tOwns: Content injection into existing files.\\n   171\\tDepends on: regex.\\n   172\\t\\n   173\\tResponsibilities:\\n   174\\t- Read the target file\\n   175\\t- `skip_if`: search for string in file content, skip if found (I-2: idempotency)\\n   176\\t- Find the anchor line using regex pattern\\n   177\\t- `at: first` (default) or `at: last` — which match to use\\n   178\\t- Insert rendered content `after` or `before` the anchor line\\n   179\\t- `prepend`: insert at beginning of file\\n   180\\t- `append`: insert at end of file\\n   181\\t- Write the modified content back\\n   182\\t\\n   183\\t### `src/operations/replace.rs` — Replace Operation\\n   184\\t\\n   185\\tOwns: Region replacement in existing files.\\n   186\\tDepends on: regex.\\n   187\\t\\n   188\\tResponsibilities:\\n   189\\t- `between`: find start pattern, find end pattern, replace everything between them (exclusive of markers)\\n   190\\t- `pattern`: find matching line(s), replace them entirely\\n   191\\t- `fallback`: what to do when pattern not found — `append`, `prepend`, `skip`, `error` (default)\\n   192\\t- Preserve line endings and file encoding\\n   193\\t\\n   194\\t### `src/operations/patch.rs` — Patch Operation\\n   195\\t\\n   196\\tOwns: Scope-aware content insertion. Delegates scope detection.\\n   197\\tDepends on: scope module, regex.\\n   198\\t\\n   199\\tResponsibilities:\\n   200\\t- Find anchor pattern in file\\n   201\\t- Determine scope boundaries using the scope module\\n   202\\t- Apply `find` narrowing within the scope\\n   203\\t- Insert rendered content at the specified position\\n   204\\t- `skip_if`: idempotency check within the scope\\n   205\\t\\n   206\\t### `src/scope/mod.rs` — Scope Detection Dispatch\\n   207\\t\\n   208\\tOwns: Scope type classification, dispatch to indent vs delimiter strategies.\\n   209\\tDepends on: indent, delimiter, position submodules.\\n   210\\t\\n   211\\t```rust\\n   212\\tenum ScopeType {\\n   213\\t    Line,              // just the matched line\\n   214\\t    Block,             // indent-based block\\n   215\\t    ClassBody,         // indent-based class body\\n   216\\t    FunctionBody,      // indent-based function body\\n   217\\t    FunctionSignature, // delimiter: ( to )\\n   218\\t    Braces,            // delimiter: { to }\\n   219\\t    Brackets,          // delimiter: [ to ]\\n   220\\t    Parens,            // delimiter: ( to )\\n   221\\t}\\n   222\\t```\\n   223\\t\\n   224\\t### `src/scope/indent.rs` — Indentation-Based Scope Detection\\n   225\\t\\n   226\\tOwns: Scope detection for indentation-significant languages (Python, YAML).\\n   227\\t\\n   228\\tAlgorithm:\\n   229\\t1. Find the anchor line's indentation level\\n   230\\t2. Walk forward: scope includes all lines indented deeper than the anchor\\n   231\\t3. Scope ends when indentation returns to the same or shallower level\\n   232\\t4. Handle blank lines within the scope (they don't end it)\\n   233\\t\\n   234\\t### `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — Delimiter-Based Scope Detection\\n   235\\t\\n   236\\tOwns: Scope detection for delimiter-based languages (C-family, JSON, TypeScript).\\n   237\\t\\n   238\\tAlgorithm:\\n   239\\t1. From the anchor line, find the opening delimiter\\n   240\\t2. Count nesting depth (handle nested delimiters)\\n   241\\t3. Scope is everything between opening and closing delimiter (exclusive)\\n   242\\t4. Handle string literals (don't count delimiters inside strings)\\n   243\\t\\n   244\\t### `src/scope/position.rs` — Semantic Position Heuristics\\n   245\\t\\n   246\\tOwns: Position determination within a scope.\\n   247\\t\\n   248\\t| Position | Heuristic |\\n   249\\t|----------|-----------|\\n   250\\t| `before` | First line of scope |\\n   251\\t| `after` | Last line of scope |\\n   252\\t| `before_close` | Line before closing delimiter or dedent |\\n   253\\t| `after_last_field` | Last line matching `^\\\\s+\\\\w+\\\\s*[:=]` |\\n   254\\t| `after_last_method` | Last line matching `^\\\\s+def \\\\w+` |\\n   255\\t| `after_last_import` | Last line matching `^\\\\s*(from\\\\|import) ` |\\n   256\\t| `sorted` | Alphabetical insertion among siblings |\\n   257\\t\\n   258\\t### `src/filters.rs` — Custom Jinja2 Filters\\n   259\\t\\n   260\\tOwns: Filter function implementations and registration.\\n   261\\tDepends on: heck (case conversions), minijinja (filter API).\\n   262\\t\\n   263\\tFilters are pure functions: `fn(value: &str, args...) -> String`. No side effects, no I/O. This module is the thinnest — most filters are one-liners wrapping `heck` or standard string operations.\\n   264\\t\\n   265\\t### `src/output.rs` — Output Formatting\\n   266\\t\\n   267\\tOwns: Dual-stream output (I-8), TTY detection.\\n   268\\tDepends on: serde_json, owo-colors.\\n   269\\t\\n   270\\tResponsibilities:\\n   271\\t- Collect `Vec<OpResult>` from operation execution\\n   272\\t- JSON mode: serialize results to stdout\\n   273\\t- Human mode: colored, summarized output to stderr\\n   274\\t- Auto-detect: if stdout is a TTY, use human mode; if piped, use JSON\\n   275\\t- `--json`: force JSON\\n   276\\t- `--quiet`: suppress all output except errors\\n   277\\t- `--verbose`: include rendered content in output\\n   278\\t\\n   279\\t### `src/error.rs` — Structured Error Types\\n   280\\t\\n   281\\tOwns: Error types, exit code mapping, error formatting.\\n   282\\tDepends on: nothing (leaf module).\\n   283\\t\\n   284\\t```rust\\n   285\\tenum JigError {\\n   286\\t    RecipeValidation(String),     // exit 1\\n   287\\t    TemplateRendering(String),    // exit 2\\n   288\\t    FileOperation(String),        // exit 3\\n   289\\t    VariableValidation(String),   // exit 4\\n   290\\t}\\n   291\\t```\\n   292\\t\\n   293\\tEvery error includes: what, where, why, hint (I-4). File operation errors include the rendered content so the caller can fall back to manual editing (I-10).\\n   294\\t\\n   295\\t### `src/workflow.rs` — Multi-Recipe Orchestration (v0.3+)\\n   296\\t\\n   297\\tOwns: Workflow definition, step execution, conditional logic, variable mapping.\\n   298\\tDepends on: recipe, variables, renderer, operations.\\n   299\\t\\n   300\\tNot built until v0.3. Included here for architectural completeness.\\n   301\\t\\n   302\\t### `src/library/` — Library Management (v0.4+)\\n   303\\t\\n   304\\tOwns: Library manifest parsing, installation, discovery, convention mapping.\\n   305\\tDepends on: recipe, filesystem, git (for remote installs).\\n   306\\t\\n   307\\tNot built until v0.4. Included here for architectural completeness.\\n   308\\t\\n   309\\t## Dependency Map\\n   310\\t\\n   311\\t```\\n   312\\t                     main.rs\\n   313\\t                    /   |   \\\\\\n   314\\t                   /    |    \\\\\\n   315\\t              recipe  variables  output\\n   316\\t                |       |         |\\n   317\\t                v       v         v\\n   318\\t             renderer  (merges)  error\\n   319\\t                |\\n   320\\t                v\\n   321\\t           operations/mod\\n   322\\t           /    |    \\\\    \\\\\\n   323\\t       create inject replace patch\\n   324\\t                              |\\n   325\\t                              v\\n   326\\t                          scope/mod\\n   327\\t                         /    |    \\\\\\n   328\\t                    indent  delim  position\\n   329\\t```\\n   330\\t\\n   331\\tExternal crate usage:\\n   332\\t- clap: main.rs only\\n   333\\t- serde + serde_yaml: recipe.rs\\n   334\\t- serde_json: variables.rs, output.rs\\n   335\\t- minijinja: renderer.rs\\n   336\\t- regex: inject.rs, replace.rs, patch.rs, scope/*.rs\\n   337\\t- heck: filters.rs\\n   338\\t- owo-colors: output.rs\\n   339\\t\\n   340\\t## Key Design Decisions\\n   341\\t\\n   342\\t### D-1: Render-Then-Execute\\n   343\\t\\n   344\\tTemplates are rendered *before* the operation executes. The operation receives a plain string, not a template. This means:\\n   345\\t- Rendering errors are caught before any file is touched\\n   346\\t- The rendered content is always available for error messages (I-4)\\n   347\\t- Operations are simpler — they just deal with strings and files\\n   348\\t\\n   349\\t### D-2: Recipe-Relative Template Resolution\\n   350\\t\\n   351\\tTemplate paths in recipes are relative to the recipe file, not the working directory. This keeps recipes self-contained (I-7). The `--base-dir` flag only affects output paths (where files are written), not template paths (where templates are read from).\\n   352\\t\\n   353\\t### D-3: Regex for Anchoring, Not Parsing\\n   354\\t\\n   355\\tAnchors use regex, not AST parsing. This is deliberate:\\n   356\\t- Language-agnostic (same tool works for Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust)\\n   357\\t- No parser dependencies (keeps binary small, I-6)\\n   358\\t- Failure is explicit (regex didn't match = clear error, not a silent wrong parse)\\n   359\\t- Scope detection adds just enough structure without full parsing\\n   360\\t\\n   361\\t### D-4: Operations Are Values, Not Traits (Initially)\\n   362\\t\\n   363\\tFor v0.1, operations can be a simple enum with match dispatch rather than a trait-object pattern. The trait abstraction is only needed if we add user-defined operations, which isn't planned. Keep it simple — an enum with four variants covers all cases through v0.4.\\n   364\\t\\n   365\\t### D-5: No Global State\\n   366\\t\\n   367\\tNo singletons, no global config objects, no lazy_static. Every function takes its inputs as arguments. This makes testing trivial and ensures determinism (I-1).\\n   368\\t\\n   369\\t## Iterative Build Strategy\\n   370\\t\\n   371\\tEach phase produces a working, testable, demoable artifact. Nothing is \\\"wired up later.\\\" Each phase can be evaluated independently.\\n   372\\t\\n   373\\t### Phase A: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\n   374\\t\\n   375\\t**Build:**\\n   376\\t- `Cargo.toml` with dependencies: serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap\\n   377\\t- `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands\\n   378\\t- `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe struct, YAML deserialization, structural validation\\n   379\\t- `src/error.rs` — error types and exit codes\\n   380\\t- `src/variables.rs` — VariableDecl types (no validation logic yet, just the types)\\n   381\\t\\n   382\\t**Demo:**\\n   383\\t```bash\\n   384\\tjig validate recipe.yaml          # prints validation result, exits 0 or 1\\n   385\\tjig vars recipe.yaml              # prints expected variables as JSON\\n   386\\t```\\n   387\\t\\n   388\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   389\\t- Can parse the example recipe from the PRD\\n   390\\t- Rejects invalid YAML with exit code 1 and a clear message\\n   391\\t- Template file existence is checked relative to recipe location\\n   392\\t- `jig vars` output matches the PRD's expected format\\n   393\\t- Unit tests: valid recipe, missing fields, bad types, missing templates\\n   394\\t\\n   395\\t### Phase B: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\n   396\\t\\n   397\\t**Build:**\\n   398\\t- `src/variables.rs` — full validation: type checking, required fields, defaults, merging\\n   399\\t- `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja environment, template loading\\n   400\\t- `src/filters.rs` — all built-in filters\\n   401\\t- Add `render` subcommand to CLI\\n   402\\t- Add `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin` to CLI\\n   403\\t\\n   404\\t**Demo:**\\n   405\\t```bash\\n   406\\tjig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\"class_name\\\": \\\"Foo\\\"}'     # rendered output to stdout\\n   407\\tjig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\"bad\\\": 123}' 2>&1         # type error, exit 4\\n   408\\t```\\n   409\\t\\n   410\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   411\\t- All 13 built-in filters produce correct output\\n   412\\t- Variable type checking catches mismatches (string vs number, missing required, bad enum value)\\n   413\\t- Merge precedence works: defaults < file < stdin < inline\\n   414\\t- Template syntax errors produce exit code 2 with line number\\n   415\\t- Undefined variables produce a helpful error with \\\"did you mean?\\\" hint\\n   416\\t- Snapshot tests (insta) for rendered output across filter combinations\\n   417\\t\\n   418\\t### Phase C: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\n   419\\t\\n   420\\t**Build:**\\n   421\\t- `src/operations/mod.rs` — operation dispatch (create only)\\n   422\\t- `src/operations/create.rs` — file creation with directory creation, skip_if_exists\\n   423\\t- `src/output.rs` — JSON and human-readable output, TTY detection\\n   424\\t- Add `run` subcommand to CLI\\n   425\\t- Add `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose`\\n   426\\t\\n   427\\t**Demo:**\\n   428\\t```bash\\n   429\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'                 # creates files\\n   430\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...' --dry-run       # preview without writing\\n   431\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...' --json | jq .   # machine-readable output\\n   432\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'                 # second run: skip_if_exists works\\n   433\\t```\\n   434\\t\\n   435\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   436\\t- Template paths in `to` field render correctly (`tests/{{ module | replace('.', '/') }}/...`)\\n   437\\t- Parent directories are created automatically\\n   438\\t- `skip_if_exists` prevents overwriting\\n   439\\t- `--force` overrides skip_if_exists\\n   440\\t- JSON output matches the format in the PRD\\n   441\\t- Human output is colored and readable\\n   442\\t- Dry-run produces identical JSON output but writes no files\\n   443\\t- Second run with same vars shows all skips (I-2)\\n   444\\t- Integration test fixtures: recipe + vars + expected output directory\\n   445\\t\\n   446\\t### Phase D: Inject Operation\\n   447\\t\\n   448\\t**Build:**\\n   449\\t- `src/operations/inject.rs` — all injection modes\\n   450\\t- Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\n   451\\t- regex dependency added\\n   452\\t\\n   453\\t**Demo:**\\n   454\\t```bash\\n   455\\t# Recipe with create + inject operations\\n   456\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'         # creates file, injects into existing\\n   457\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'         # second run: skip_if prevents duplication\\n   458\\t```\\n   459\\t\\n   460\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   461\\t- `after` regex: content appears on the line after the match\\n   462\\t- `before` regex: content appears on the line before the match\\n   463\\t- `prepend`: content at start of file\\n   464\\t- `append`: content at end of file\\n   465\\t- `at: first` vs `at: last`: correct match selection when regex matches multiple lines\\n   466\\t- `skip_if`: string search prevents duplicate injection (I-2)\\n   467\\t- Regex match failure produces exit code 3 with the pattern and file path in the error\\n   468\\t- Integration test fixtures for each injection mode\\n   469\\t- **This completes the v0.1 feature set**\\n   470\\t\\n   471\\t### Phase E: Integration Test Framework\\n   472\\t\\n   473\\t**Build:**\\n   474\\t- `tests/` directory with fixture-based integration test harness\\n   475\\t- Fixture format: `recipe.yaml` + `vars.json` + `templates/` + `existing/` + `expected/`\\n   476\\t- Test runner: copy existing/ to temp, run jig, diff against expected/\\n   477\\t- Snapshot tests for all output formats\\n   478\\t- Error case fixtures (missing vars, bad regex, missing template, missing target file)\\n   479\\t\\n   480\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   481\\t- Every operation mode has at least one fixture\\n   482\\t- Every error case has a fixture that asserts exit code + error message content\\n   483\\t- `cargo test` runs all unit tests + integration tests + snapshot tests\\n   484\\t- Adding a new test case is just adding a directory (no code changes)\\n   485\\t\\n   486\\t### Phase F: Replace Operation (v0.2 start)\\n   487\\t\\n   488\\t**Build:**\\n   489\\t- `src/operations/replace.rs` — between, pattern, fallback modes\\n   490\\t\\n   491\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   492\\t- `between` start/end: content between markers is replaced\\n   493\\t- `pattern`: matched lines are replaced\\n   494\\t- `fallback: append/prepend/skip/error` each work correctly\\n   495\\t- Markers themselves are preserved (only content between them changes)\\n   496\\t\\n   497\\t### Phase G: Patch Operation + Scope Detection (v0.2 completion)\\n   498\\t\\n   499\\t**Build:**\\n   500\\t- `src/scope/mod.rs` — scope type dispatch\\n   501\\t- `src/scope/indent.rs` — indentation-based scope detection\\n   502\\t- `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — delimiter-based scope detection\\n   503\\t- `src/scope/position.rs` — semantic position heuristics\\n   504\\t- `src/operations/patch.rs` — anchor + scope + position + find\\n   505\\t\\n   506\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   507\\t- Indentation scope: correctly finds class/function body boundaries in Python code\\n   508\\t- Delimiter scope: correctly finds brace/bracket/paren boundaries in TypeScript/Rust\\n   509\\t- Nested delimiters handled correctly\\n   510\\t- String literals don't confuse delimiter counting\\n   511\\t- Each semantic position (after_last_field, after_last_method, etc.) tested with real code snippets\\n   512\\t- `find` narrowing works within a scope\\n   513\\t- `--verbose` shows scope boundaries and insertion point\\n   514\\t- Full patch recipe from the PRD (add-model-field) works against a Django-style fixture\\n   515\\t- Scope parse failure produces structured error with rendered content (I-4, I-10)\\n   516\\t\\n   517\\t### Phase H: Workflows (v0.3)\\n   518\\t\\n   519\\t**Build:**\\n   520\\t- `src/workflow.rs` — workflow definition, step execution\\n   521\\t- `workflow` subcommand in CLI\\n   522\\t- Conditional steps, variable mapping, error handling modes\\n   523\\t\\n   524\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   525\\t- Multi-recipe workflows execute in order\\n   526\\t- `when` expressions skip/include steps correctly\\n   527\\t- `vars_map` renames variables between steps\\n   528\\t- `on_error: stop/continue/report` behaves correctly\\n   529\\t- Per-step results in JSON output\\n   530\\t\\n   531\\t### Phase I: Libraries (v0.4)\\n   532\\t\\n   533\\t**Build:**\\n   534\\t- `src/library/mod.rs` — manifest parsing\\n   535\\t- `src/library/install.rs` — add/remove/update\\n   536\\t- `src/library/discover.rs` — recipe listing\\n   537\\t- `src/library/conventions.rs` — convention mapping and overrides\\n   538\\t- `library` subcommand in CLI\\n   539\\t\\n   540\\t**Evaluate:**\\n   541\\t- Install from local directory works\\n   542\\t- Install from git URL works\\n   543\\t- Convention overrides in .jigrc.yaml apply correctly\\n   544\\t- `jig library recipes <name>` lists all recipes\\n   545\\t- Project-local extensions and template overrides work\\n   546\\t\\n   547\\t## Testing Strategy\\n   548\\t\\n   549\\tThree layers, matching the pipeline architecture:\\n   550\\t\\n   551\\t| Layer | What | Tool | Location |\\n   552\\t|-------|------|------|----------|\\n   553\\t| **Unit** | Individual functions: parsing, validation, filter output, scope detection | `#[cfg(test)]` inline | Each `src/*.rs` |\\n   554\\t| **Integration** | Full pipeline: recipe + vars in, files out, diff against expected | Fixture directories | `tests/fixtures/` |\\n   555\\t| **Snapshot** | Rendered template output, JSON output format, error messages | `insta` crate | `tests/snapshots/` |\\n   556\\t\\n   557\\t### Fixture Directory Convention\\n   558\\t\\n   559\\t```\\n   560\\ttests/fixtures/<test-name>/\\n   561\\t  recipe.yaml              # input recipe\\n   562\\t  vars.json                # input variables\\n   563\\t  templates/               # template files referenced by recipe\\n   564\\t    *.j2\\n   565\\t  existing/                # files that exist before jig runs (for inject/replace/patch)\\n   566\\t    *.py, *.ts, etc.\\n   567\\t  expected/                # what the output directory should look like after\\n   568\\t    *.py, *.ts, etc.\\n   569\\t  expected_output.json     # optional: assert on jig's JSON stdout\\n   570\\t  expected_exit_code       # optional: file containing just \\\"0\\\", \\\"1\\\", etc.\\n   571\\t```\\n   572\\t\\n   573\\tTest runner:\\n   574\\t1. Copy `existing/` to a temp directory\\n   575\\t2. Run `jig run recipe.yaml --vars-file vars.json --base-dir $tmp --json`\\n   576\\t3. Diff temp directory against `expected/`\\n   577\\t4. If `expected_output.json` exists, assert JSON output matches\\n   578\\t5. If `expected_exit_code` exists, assert exit code matches\\n   579\\t\\n   580\\t## What This Architecture Does NOT Cover\\n   581\\t\\n   582\\tThese are explicitly out of scope for the architecture and will be addressed in later workstreams:\\n   583\\t\\n   584\\t- **Scan, Infer, Check** (v0.7-v0.8) — reverse operations, pattern learning, conformance\\n   585\\t- **Schema-first generation** (v0.9) — OpenAPI/SQL/proto/GraphQL input\\n   586\\t- **Observation engine** (post-1.0) — Claude Code hook for pattern discovery\\n   587\\t- **Custom filters via shell commands** — .jigrc.yaml `filters:` block\\n   588\\t- **Distribution** (v0.5) — CI, Homebrew, npm wrapper, Nix flake\\n   589\\t- **Claude Code plugin** (v0.6) — /jig:init, /jig:doctor skills\\n   590\\t- **Tree-sitter integration** (post-1.0) — optional AST-aware scoping\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-114234.md:23:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_10\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-114234.md'\",\"aggregated_output\":\"     1\\tReading additional input from stdin...\\n     2\\t{\\\"type\\\":\\\"thread.started\\\",\\\"thread_id\\\":\\\"019d5439-a02a-7172-b5c4-827f5ad27fe9\\\"}\\n     3\\t{\\\"type\\\":\\\"turn.started\\\"}\\n     4\\t2026-04-03T16:42:36.988797Z ERROR rmcp::transport::worker: worker quit with fatal: Transport channel closed, when UnexpectedContentType(Some(\\\"text/plain;charset=UTF-8; body: Token expired\\\"))\\n     5\\t{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_0\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"Reviewing `core-engine` planning docs now for contradictions, EARS quality, invariant alignment, and pre-implementation risk. I’ll first locate and read the actual `SPEC.md`/`PLAN.md`/supporting docs in the repo so findings reference concrete file sections.\\\"}}\\n     6\\t{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_1\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'rg --files'\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n     7\\t{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_1\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'rg --files'\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"src/main.rs\\\\nCLAUDE.md\\\\njig.md\\\\nAGENTS.md\\\\njustfile\\\\nCargo.toml\\\\nREADME.md\\\\nCargo.lock\\\\nscripts/ws-review.sh\\\\nscripts/lib/workflow-lib.sh\\\\nscripts/ws-init.sh\\\\nscripts/ws-plan.sh\\\\nscripts/ws-execute.sh\\\\nscripts/ws-plan-review.sh\\\\nscripts/ws-consolidate.sh\\\\nscripts/validate.sh\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-claude-20260403-114234.md\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-114234.md\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/NARRATIVE.md\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md\\\\nskills/improve/SKILL.md\\\\nskills/improve/references/hook-examples.md\\\\nskills/improve/references/extension-points.md\\\\nskills/improve/references/agent-teams.md\\\\nskills/improve/references/agent-template.md\\\\nskills/improve/references/skill-template.md\\\\nskills/improve/references/best-practices.md\\\\nskills/improve/references/gotchas.md\\\\nskills/clean-copy/SKILL.md\\\\nskills/audit/SKILL.md\\\\nskills/velocity/SKILL.md\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/exec/claude-plan-20260402-223619.md\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/exec/claude-plan-20260402-223424.md\\\\nskills/velocity/references/evaluation.md\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/SHARED-CONTEXT.md\\\\ndocs/INVARIANTS.md\\\\ndocs/ARCHITECTURE.md\\\\nskills/upgrade/SKILL.md\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n     8\\t{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_2\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"I found the docs in `docs/workstreams/core-engine/`. Next I’m extracting the exact section text and line numbers from `SPEC.md`, `PLAN.md`, `INVARIANTS.md`, and `ARCHITECTURE.md` so each finding is anchored precisely.\\\"}}\\n     9\\t{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_3\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md'\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n    10\\t{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_3\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md'\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"     1\\\\t# SPEC.md\\\\n     2\\\\t\\\\n     3\\\\t> Workstream: core-engine\\\\n     4\\\\t> Last updated: 2026-04-02\\\\n     5\\\\t> Scope: v0.1 (Phases A-D from ARCHITECTURE.md)\\\\n     6\\\\t\\\\n     7\\\\t## Overview\\\\n     8\\\\t\\\\n     9\\\\tThe core engine workstream delivers the minimum pipeline that makes jig useful: parse a recipe, validate variables, render templates, execute create and inject operations, and report results. This is the v0.1 feature set — a working CLI that an LLM can call to produce files from recipes.\\\\n    10\\\\t\\\\n    11\\\\tOut of scope for this workstream: replace operations, patch operations, scope detection, workflows, libraries, scan/infer/check, custom filters via shell, .jigrc.yaml config.\\\\n    12\\\\t\\\\n    13\\\\t## Requirements\\\\n    14\\\\t\\\\n    15\\\\t### Functional Requirements\\\\n    16\\\\t\\\\n    17\\\\t#### FR-1: Recipe Parsing\\\\n    18\\\\t\\\\n    19\\\\tDeserialize a YAML recipe file into a validated internal representation. Resolve template paths relative to the recipe file location. Reject structurally invalid recipes with clear errors.\\\\n    20\\\\t\\\\n    21\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n    22\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n    23\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n    24\\\\t| AC-1.1 | Event | WHEN a valid recipe YAML is provided, the system SHALL parse it into a Recipe struct containing name, description, variables, and files | TEST-1.1 |\\\\n    25\\\\t| AC-1.2 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares variables with type, required, default, description, values (enum), and items (array), the system SHALL parse all fields into the VariableDecl struct | TEST-1.2 |\\\\n    26\\\\t| AC-1.3 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares `create` file operations with template, to, and skip_if_exists fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Create | TEST-1.3 |\\\\n    27\\\\t| AC-1.4 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares `inject` file operations with template, inject, after/before/prepend/append, at, and skip_if fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Inject | TEST-1.4 |\\\\n    28\\\\t| AC-1.5 | Unwanted | IF the recipe YAML is malformed (invalid YAML syntax), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and an error message identifying the parse failure location | TEST-1.5 |\\\\n    29\\\\t| AC-1.6 | Unwanted | IF a required field is missing from the recipe (e.g., files with no template), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and name the missing field | TEST-1.6 |\\\\n    30\\\\t| AC-1.7 | Event | WHEN a recipe references template files, the system SHALL resolve those paths relative to the recipe file location, not the working directory | TEST-1.7 |\\\\n    31\\\\t| AC-1.8 | Unwanted | IF a referenced template file does not exist at the resolved path, the system SHALL exit with code 1 and report which template is missing and where it looked | TEST-1.8 |\\\\n    32\\\\t| AC-1.9 | Event | WHEN the recipe has optional metadata fields (name, description), the system SHALL accept recipes with or without them | TEST-1.9 |\\\\n    33\\\\t\\\\n    34\\\\t#### FR-2: Variable Validation\\\\n    35\\\\t\\\\n    36\\\\tAccept variables as JSON from multiple sources, merge them with defined precedence, and type-check against recipe declarations.\\\\n    37\\\\t\\\\n    38\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n    39\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n    40\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n    41\\\\t| AC-2.1 | Event | WHEN `--vars` is provided with a JSON string, the system SHALL parse it as the variable input | TEST-2.1 |\\\\n    42\\\\t| AC-2.2 | Event | WHEN `--vars-file` is provided with a path to a JSON file, the system SHALL read and parse the file as variable input | TEST-2.2 |\\\\n    43\\\\t| AC-2.3 | Event | WHEN `--vars-stdin` is provided, the system SHALL read JSON from stdin as variable input | TEST-2.3 |\\\\n    44\\\\t| AC-2.4 | Event | WHEN multiple variable sources are provided, the system SHALL merge with precedence: recipe defaults < vars-file < vars-stdin < inline --vars | TEST-2.4 |\\\\n    45\\\\t| AC-2.5 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared as `required: true` and no value is provided (after merging), the system SHALL exit with code 4 naming the missing variable and providing a hint | TEST-2.5 |\\\\n    46\\\\t| AC-2.6 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared with a `default` and no value is provided, the system SHALL use the default value | TEST-2.6 |\\\\n    47\\\\t| AC-2.7 | Unwanted | IF a provided variable value does not match its declared type (e.g., string given for number, object given for array), the system SHALL exit with code 4 with expected vs actual type | TEST-2.7 |\\\\n    48\\\\t| AC-2.8 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared as `type: enum` with `values: [a, b, c]`, the system SHALL reject values not in the allowed set with exit code 4 | TEST-2.8 |\\\\n    49\\\\t| AC-2.9 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared as `type: array` with `items: string`, the system SHALL validate that each array element matches the item type | TEST-2.9 |\\\\n    50\\\\t| AC-2.10 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accept all six variable types: string, number, boolean, array, object, enum | TEST-2.10 |\\\\n    51\\\\t\\\\n    52\\\\t#### FR-3: Template Rendering\\\\n    53\\\\t\\\\n    54\\\\tRender Jinja2 templates using minijinja with registered built-in filters. Templates are loaded from recipe-relative paths.\\\\n    55\\\\t\\\\n    56\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n    57\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n    58\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n    59\\\\t| AC-3.1 | Event | WHEN a template is rendered with a variables context, the system SHALL substitute all `{{ variable }}` expressions with their values | TEST-3.1 |\\\\n    60\\\\t| AC-3.2 | Event | WHEN a template contains `{% if %}` / `{% else %}` / `{% endif %}` blocks, the system SHALL evaluate them correctly against the variable values | TEST-3.2 |\\\\n    61\\\\t| AC-3.3 | Event | WHEN a template contains `{% for item in list %}` loops, the system SHALL iterate and render the body for each element | TEST-3.3 |\\\\n    62\\\\t| AC-3.4 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL register and support all 13 built-in filters: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join | TEST-3.4 |\\\\n    63\\\\t| AC-3.5 | Event | WHEN `snakecase` is applied to \\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.5 |\\\\n    64\\\\t| AC-3.6 | Event | WHEN `camelcase` is applied to \\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"bookingService\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.6 |\\\\n    65\\\\t| AC-3.7 | Event | WHEN `pascalcase` is applied to \\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.7 |\\\\n    66\\\\t| AC-3.8 | Event | WHEN `kebabcase` is applied to \\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"booking-service\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.8 |\\\\n    67\\\\t| AC-3.9 | Event | WHEN `replace` is applied as `\\\\\\\"a.b.c\\\\\\\" \\\\\\\\| replace('.', '/')`, the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"a/b/c\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.9 |\\\\n    68\\\\t| AC-3.10 | Event | WHEN `pluralize` is applied to \\\\\\\"hotel\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.10 |\\\\n    69\\\\t| AC-3.11 | Event | WHEN `singularize` is applied to \\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"hotel\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.11 |\\\\n    70\\\\t| AC-3.12 | Event | WHEN `quote` is applied to \\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce `\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\"` (with literal quotes) | TEST-3.12 |\\\\n    71\\\\t| AC-3.13 | Event | WHEN `indent(4)` is applied to a multiline string, the system SHALL indent each line by 4 spaces | TEST-3.13 |\\\\n    72\\\\t| AC-3.14 | Event | WHEN `join(\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\")` is applied to `[\\\\\\\"a\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"b\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"c\\\\\\\"]`, the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"a, b, c\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.14 |\\\\n    73\\\\t| AC-3.15 | Event | WHEN `{# comment #}` appears in a template, the system SHALL strip it from the output | TEST-3.15 |\\\\n    74\\\\t| AC-3.16 | Event | WHEN `{% raw %}...{% endraw %}` appears in a template, the system SHALL output the content literally without interpreting Jinja2 syntax | TEST-3.16 |\\\\n    75\\\\t| AC-3.17 | Unwanted | IF a template references an undefined variable, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and include a \\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\" hint when a close match exists | TEST-3.17 |\\\\n    76\\\\t| AC-3.18 | Unwanted | IF a template has a Jinja2 syntax error, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and report
