Complete\",\"replace_all\":false}},{\"tool_name\":\"Write\",\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01JnhzKMVVP8ya3jYxnRMUmS\",\"tool_input\":{\"file_path\":\"/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md\",\"content\":\"# PLAN.md\\n\\n> Workstream: core-engine\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\n> Status: Complete\\n\\n## Objective\\n\\nDeliver the minimum viable jig CLI: parse recipes, validate variables, render Jinja2 templates, execute create and inject file operations, and report structured results. This is the v0.1 feature set — the smallest thing that lets an LLM call `jig run` to produce files from a recipe instead of re-deriving boilerplate.\\n\\nCovers ARCHITECTURE.md Phases A through E (skeleton, rendering, create, inject, integration tests).\\n\\n## Phases\\n\\n### Phase 1: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-1, FR-7 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-5\\n\\nBootstrap the Rust crate, wire up clap, and make recipe parsing work end-to-end. After this phase, `jig validate` and `jig vars` are functional commands.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 1.1: Cargo.toml with dependencies (serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap, thiserror, regex, indexmap)\\n- [x] 1.2: `src/error.rs` — StructuredError struct (what/where/why/hint), JigError enum wrapping StructuredError with exit code mapping (0-4)\\n- [x] 1.3: `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe, VariableDecl, VarType, FileOp structs with serde deserialization; template path resolution relative to recipe location; structural validation (missing fields, missing template files); unknown operation type detection with clear error message; custom deserialization for FileOp — use intermediate flat struct with optional fields, then validate and convert to typed enum (reject when more than one of `to`/`inject`/`replace`/`patch` is present, or none is present; if `replace` or `patch` is present, emit AC-1.10 'not supported in v0.1' error, reject when multiple inject modes (after/before/prepend/append) are specified); compile-check regex patterns in after/before fields during recipe validation\\n- [x] 1.4: `src/variables.rs` — Variable merging and type-checking scaffolding (types imported from recipe.rs, validation logic added in Phase 2)\\n- [x] 1.5: `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands, `#[command(version)]`; wire recipe parsing; map errors to exit codes; `jig validate` outputs summary to stderr (variable count, operation types); with `--json`, outputs structured JSON to stdout\\n- [x] 1.6: Unit tests for recipe parsing — valid recipe, missing fields, malformed YAML, missing template files, optional metadata\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `jig validate recipe.yaml` parses the example recipe from jig.md and exits 0\\n- `jig validate bad.yaml` exits 1 with a clear error naming the problem\\n- `jig vars recipe.yaml` outputs JSON matching the SPEC schema (type, required, default, description)\\n- Template paths resolve relative to recipe file, not cwd\\n- AC-1.1 through AC-1.15 have corresponding unit tests (AC-1.11 empty files array tested in Phase 3 when `jig run` exists)\\n- `jig validate` output includes variable names and operation types\\n- AC-N5.1 exit codes are correct for recipe validation errors\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `Cargo.toml`\\n- `src/main.rs`\\n- `src/recipe.rs`\\n- `src/variables.rs` (types only)\\n- `src/error.rs`\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- regex crate (needed at parse time for compile-checking after/before patterns)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 2: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-2, FR-3, FR-7 (partial), NFR-1 (partial)\\n\\nFull variable pipeline (parse, merge, type-check) and template rendering with all 13 built-in filters. After this phase, `jig render` works.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 2.1: `src/variables.rs` — full validation: parse JSON from --vars/--vars-file/--vars-stdin; merge with precedence (defaults < file < stdin < inline); type-check against declarations; required field enforcement; enum validation; array item type validation\\n- [x] 2.2: `src/filters.rs` — all 13 built-in filters registered with minijinja: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join\\n- [x] 2.3: `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja Environment setup; template loading from recipe-relative paths; filter registration; render with variables context; structured error on undefined variable (with \\\"did you mean?\\\" via edit distance) and syntax errors (with file + line)\\n- [x] 2.4: Wire `render` subcommand in main.rs with --vars, --vars-file, --vars-stdin, --to options. For `jig render`, create a standalone Environment with filters registered but no template directory — load the template file directly by path via `render_str()` or equivalent\\n- [x] 2.5: Unit tests for variable validation — every VarType, required missing, default fallback, enum rejection, array item mismatch, merge precedence\\n- [x] 2.6: Unit tests + insta snapshot tests for all 13 filters and template rendering (conditionals, loops, comments, raw blocks, undefined vars, syntax errors)\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- All 13 filters produce correct output per AC-3.4 through AC-3.14\\n- `jig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\"class_name\\\": \\\"BookingService\\\"}'` renders to stdout\\n- Type mismatch exits 4 with expected vs actual type (AC-2.7)\\n- Missing required variable exits 4 with variable name and hint (AC-2.5)\\n- Merge precedence: inline --vars wins over --vars-stdin wins over --vars-file wins over defaults (AC-2.4)\\n- Undefined template variable exits 2 with \\\"did you mean?\\\" hint (AC-3.17)\\n- Template syntax error exits 2 with file path and line number (AC-3.18)\\n- Same inputs produce byte-identical output across runs (AC-N1.1)\\n- `jig render template.j2 --vars '...' --to output.txt` writes to file instead of stdout (AC-7.4)\\n- Multiple validation errors accumulated and reported together (AC-2.11)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/variables.rs` (full implementation)\\n- `src/filters.rs`\\n- `src/renderer.rs`\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- heck crate for case conversions\\n- minijinja crate for template rendering\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 3: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-4, FR-6, FR-7 (complete), NFR-2 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-6\\n\\nFile creation with templated output paths, directory auto-creation, skip_if_exists, and dual-stream output (JSON stdout / human stderr). After this phase, `jig run` works for create-only recipes.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 3.1: `src/operations/mod.rs` — ExecutionContext struct (base_dir, dry_run, force, virtual_files for dry-run state); OpResult enum (Success with action/path/lines/location, Skip, Error); operation dispatch (create only initially). In dry-run mode, create ops populate virtual_files instead of writing to disk.\\n- [x] 3.2: `src/operations/create.rs` — render `to` path as template; create parent directories; write rendered content; skip_if_exists logic; --force override; --base-dir path resolution\\n- [x] 3.3: `src/output.rs` — OutputMode enum (Json/Human/Quiet); TTY auto-detection; JSON serialization of operations array + files_written/files_skipped; track per-path write/skip status for files_written and files_skipped arrays; colored human output to stderr; --verbose includes rendered content\\n- [x] 3.4: Wire `run` subcommand in main.rs with --dry-run, --json, --quiet, --force, --base-dir, --verbose; render all template contents AND templated path fields (`to`, `inject`, `skip_if`) into a Vec before executing any operation — rendering failures abort before any file is touched; execute operations in declaration order; fail-fast on operation errors — stop execution on first failure\\n- [x] 3.5: Unit tests for create operation — happy path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, templated paths, directory creation, file-already-exists error\\n- [x] 3.6: Unit tests for output formatting — JSON schema matches SPEC, human output, quiet mode, verbose mode, dry-run\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `jig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'` creates files at templated paths (AC-4.1, AC-4.2)\\n- Parent directories created automatically (AC-4.3)\\n- skip_if_exists: true skips existing files with action:\\\"skip\\\" (AC-4.4)\\n- Default (skip_if_exists: false) errors on existing file without --force (AC-4.5)\\n- --force overwrites regardless (AC-4.6)\\n- --base-dir changes output root (AC-4.7)\\n- --dry-run produces output but writes nothing (AC-6.8)\\n- JSON output when piped, human output when TTY (AC-6.1, AC-6.2)\\n- --json forces JSON (AC-6.3), --quiet suppresses non-errors (AC-6.4)\\n- Operations execute in declaration order (AC-N6.1)\\n- Second run with skip_if_exists: true reports all skips (AC-N2.1)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs`\\n- `src/operations/create.rs`\\n- `src/output.rs`\\n- `src/main.rs` (run subcommand)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- owo-colors crate for terminal coloring\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 4: Inject Operation\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-5, NFR-2 (complete), NFR-6\\n\\nContent injection into existing files via regex anchoring. All injection modes: after, before, prepend, append, with at:first/last match selection and skip_if idempotency. This completes the v0.1 operation set.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 4.1: `src/operations/inject.rs` — read target file (or from virtual_files if target created in same dry-run); skip_if string search (skip_if string search must check virtual_files content when target was created in same dry-run); regex anchor matching (after/before); at:first/at:last match selection; prepend/append modes; render inject path as template; write modified content; inject ops update virtual_files with post-injection content for subsequent operations\\n- [x] 4.2: Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\n- [x] 4.3: Unit tests for every injection mode — after (first match), after (last match), before, prepend, append, skip_if, missing target file error, regex no-match error, templated inject path\\n- [x] 4.4: Integration test: recipe with create + inject in same run (create file, then inject into it — tests ordered execution AC-N6.2)\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- after: content on line after first match (AC-5.1)\\n- before: content on line before first match (AC-5.2)\\n- prepend: content at start of file (AC-5.3)\\n- append: content at end of file (AC-5.4)\\n- at:last uses last match (AC-5.5), at:first (default) uses first (AC-5.6)\\n- skip_if: skips when string found in file, reports action:\\\"skip\\\" (AC-5.7)\\n- Regex no-match exits 3 with pattern, file path, hint (AC-5.8)\\n- Missing target file exits 3 (AC-5.9)\\n- Inject path renders as template (AC-5.11)\\n- Create-then-inject in same recipe works (AC-N6.2)\\n- Second run with skip_if shows all skips — no duplicate content (AC-N2.2)\\n- Inject success reports action:\\\"inject\\\" with path, location, line count (AC-5.10)\\n- at field ignored when prepend/append specified; after/before without regex exits 1 (AC-5.12, AC-5.13)\\n- Invalid regex pattern in after/before exits 1 (AC-5.14)\\n- Multiple inject modes (after+before etc.) specified exits 1 (AC-5.15)\\n- --force has no effect on inject operations (AC-5.16)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/inject.rs`\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- regex crate\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 5: Integration Test Framework\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\n\\nFixture-based integration test harness that makes adding new test cases a matter of adding a directory. Snapshot tests for all output formats. This phase validates the entire v0.1 pipeline end-to-end.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 5.1: Test harness in `tests/integration.rs` — fixture discovery; copy existing/ to temp dir; run jig as subprocess; diff output against expected/; assert JSON output against expected_output.json; assert exit code against expected_exit_code\\n- [x] 5.2: Fixtures for create operations — simple create, templated path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, directory creation\\n- [x] 5.3: Fixtures for inject operations — after/before/prepend/append, at:first/at:last, skip_if\\n- [x] 5.4: Fixtures for error cases — missing vars, bad type, missing template, missing target file, regex no-match, malformed YAML, file exists without force\\n- [x] 5.5: Fixtures for combined operations — create + inject in one recipe, multi-file recipe, idempotency (run twice)\\n- [x] 5.6: insta snapshot tests for JSON output format, human output format, error message format\\n- [x] 5.7: Determinism test — run same recipe twice, assert byte-identical output (AC-N1.1)\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `cargo test` runs all unit + integration + snapshot tests green\\n- Every operation mode has at least one fixture\\n- Every error exit code (1-4) has at least one fixture\\n- Adding a new test case requires only a new directory, no code changes. Integration fixtures are auto-discovered from directories (no code changes to add tests). Spec-level unit tests are named functions in `#[cfg(test)]` modules (e.g., `spec::fr1::ac_1_1`). The two layers serve different purposes.\\n- Idempotency fixture: second run produces all skips, no file changes\\n- Binary has no dynamic dependencies beyond system libc (verified via `otool -L` on macOS, `ldd` on Linux)\\n- Every `TEST-*` ID in SPEC.md has a corresponding test function named `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}` or `spec::nfr{N}::ac_n{N}_{M}`\\n- Error fixtures assert that error JSON contains `what`, `where`, `why`, and `hint` fields (at least one fixture per exit code)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `tests/integration.rs`\\n- `tests/fixtures/` (directory tree)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- insta crate for snapshot testing\\n- assert_cmd or similar for subprocess testing (or raw std::process::Command)\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\n- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\n- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\n\\n## Key Decisions\\n\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\n| Operations as enum, not trait objects | `FileOp` enum with match dispatch | Only 2 variants in v0.1 (create, inject). Trait abstraction adds complexity for no benefit until user-defined operations exist, which isn't planned. (ARCHITECTURE D-4) |\\n| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages (ARCHITECTURE D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\n| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable (ARCHITECTURE D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\n| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism (ARCHITECTURE D-5, I-1) |\\n| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate, good Display/Error trait impls |\\n| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\n| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\n| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling. Acceptable dependency for correctness. |\\n| \\\"did you mean?\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) against declared variable names | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Hand-rolling edit distance serves I-10 (graceful degradation) — the error tells the caller exactly what to fix. |\\n| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves, even for ones minijinja provides (replace, indent, join) | We control exact behavior. No surprises from upstream minijinja changes. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\n| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. Templates reference variables not created files. (D-1) |\\n| Extra variables pass through | Don't error or warn on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness here breaks real usage. |\\n| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later operations may depend on earlier ones (e.g., inject targets a prior create). (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\n\\n## Risks / Open Questions\\n\\nAll original risks resolved during implementation. See Resolved Questions below.\\n\\n## Resolved Questions\\n\\n- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\n- **\\\"did you mean?\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names. minijinja doesn't provide this. Serves I-10.\\n- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. We own the behavior, upstream changes don't affect us. Serves I-1.\\n- **TTY detection**: `std::io::IsTerminal` works correctly on macOS. Used in std since Rust 1.70, no external crate needed.\\n- **stdin variable reading**: `--vars-stdin` consumes stdin. Non-interactive by design (I-3), documented as single-purpose per invocation.\\n- **Large file handling for inject**: Reads entire file into memory. Fine for source files (<10K lines). Accepted as known limitation for v0.1.\\n- **pluralizer irregular plurals**: Verified working for standard cases. Acceptable for v0.1 scope.\\n- **minijinja built-in filter override**: Successfully overridden by registering our filters. No conflicts.\\n\\n## Execution Order\\n\\n```\\nPhase 1 ──► Phase 2 ──► Phase 3 ──► Phase 4 ──► Phase 5\\nskeleton     vars+render  create+output inject     integration\\n                                                    tests\\n```\\n\\nEach phase produces a working, testable artifact. No phase depends on \\\"wiring up later.\\\" The CLI grows incrementally:\\n- After Phase 1: `jig validate`, `jig vars`\\n- After Phase 2: + `jig render`\\n- After Phase 3: + `jig run` (create only)\\n- After Phase 4: + `jig run` (create + inject) — **v0.1 complete**\\n- After Phase 5: Full test coverage validates the release\\n\\n## Completion Summary\\n\\nAll 5 phases completed 2026-04-04. 191 tests passing (177 unit + 2 CLI integration + 12 fixture integration). 22 test fixture directories covering success paths, error cases, and combined operations.\\n\\n### Outstanding Review Findings\\n\\nThe final code review identified issues that should be addressed in a follow-up before v0.2 work begins:\\n\\n**Critical:**\\n- Pipeline stage ordering: `--base-dir` existence check (exit 3) runs before template rendering (exit 2), violating AC-N5.2\\n\\n**Major:**\\n- Pre-operation errors bypass output formatting — LLM callers get no JSON on early failures (violates I-4/I-10)\\n- `--vars-file` IO errors discard original error context (permission-denied shows as \\\"not found\\\")\\n- Empty regex pattern `after: \\\"\\\"` silently matches every line instead of failing\\n\\n**Minor:**\\n- Unknown YAML fields on file ops silently ignored instead of reported\\n- `validate` subcommand's local `--json` flag shadows the global flag\\n- `find_undefined_variable` is a best-effort regex heuristic (acceptable for v0.1)\\n- Regex recompiled on every inject execution (perf nit, not functional)\\n\"}},{\"tool_name\":\"Write\",\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01QBHeWXki3iAc8hUEvfPACv\",\"tool_input\":{\"file_path\":\"/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md\",\"content\":\"# PLAN.md\\n\\n> Workstream: core-engine\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\n> Status: Complete\\n\\n## Objective\\n\\nDeliver the minimum viable jig CLI: parse recipes, validate variables, render Jinja2 templates, execute create and inject file operations, and report structured results. This is the v0.1 feature set — the smallest thing that lets an LLM call `jig run` to produce files from a recipe instead of re-deriving boilerplate.\\n\\nCovers ARCHITECTURE.md Phases A through E (skeleton, rendering, create, inject, integration tests).\\n\\n## Phases\\n\\n### Phase 1: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-1, FR-7 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-5\\n\\nBootstrap the Rust crate, wire up clap, and make recipe parsing work end-to-end. After this phase, `jig validate` and `jig vars` are functional commands.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 1.1: Cargo.toml with dependencies (serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap, thiserror, regex, indexmap)\\n- [x] 1.2: `src/error.rs` — StructuredError struct (what/where/why/hint), JigError enum wrapping StructuredError with exit code mapping (0-4)\\n- [x] 1.3: `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe, VariableDecl, VarType, FileOp structs with serde deserialization; template path resolution relative to recipe location; structural validation (missing fields, missing template files); unknown operation type detection with clear error message; custom deserialization for FileOp — use intermediate flat struct with optional fields, then validate and convert to typed enum (reject when more than one of `to`/`inject`/`replace`/`patch` is present, or none is present; if `replace` or `patch` is present, emit AC-1.10 'not supported in v0.1' error, reject when multiple inject modes (after/before/prepend/append) are specified); compile-check regex patterns in after/before fields during recipe validation\\n- [x] 1.4: `src/variables.rs` — Variable merging and type-checking scaffolding (types imported from recipe.rs, validation logic added in Phase 2)\\n- [x] 1.5: `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands, `#[command(version)]`; wire recipe parsing; map errors to exit codes; `jig validate` outputs summary to stderr (variable count, operation types); with `--json`, outputs structured JSON to stdout\\n- [x] 1.6: Unit tests for recipe parsing — valid recipe, missing fields, malformed YAML, missing template files, optional metadata\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `Cargo.toml`\\n- `src/main.rs`\\n- `src/recipe.rs`\\n- `src/variables.rs` (types only)\\n- `src/error.rs`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 2: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-2, FR-3, FR-7 (partial), NFR-1 (partial)\\n\\nFull variable pipeline (parse, merge, type-check) and template rendering with all 13 built-in filters. After this phase, `jig render` works.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 2.1: `src/variables.rs` — full validation: parse JSON from --vars/--vars-file/--vars-stdin; merge with precedence (defaults < file < stdin < inline); type-check against declarations; required field enforcement; enum validation; array item type validation\\n- [x] 2.2: `src/filters.rs` — all 13 built-in filters registered with minijinja: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join\\n- [x] 2.3: `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja Environment setup; template loading from recipe-relative paths; filter registration; render with variables context; structured error on undefined variable (with \\\"did you mean?\\\" via edit distance) and syntax errors (with file + line)\\n- [x] 2.4: Wire `render` subcommand in main.rs with --vars, --vars-file, --vars-stdin, --to options\\n- [x] 2.5: Unit tests for variable validation — every VarType, required missing, default fallback, enum rejection, array item mismatch, merge precedence\\n- [x] 2.6: Unit tests + insta snapshot tests for all 13 filters and template rendering\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/variables.rs` (full implementation)\\n- `src/filters.rs`\\n- `src/renderer.rs`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 3: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-4, FR-6, FR-7 (complete), NFR-2 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-6\\n\\nFile creation with templated output paths, directory auto-creation, skip_if_exists, and dual-stream output (JSON stdout / human stderr). After this phase, `jig run` works for create-only recipes.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 3.1: `src/operations/mod.rs` — ExecutionContext struct (base_dir, dry_run, force, virtual_files for dry-run state); OpResult enum; operation dispatch\\n- [x] 3.2: `src/operations/create.rs` — render `to` path as template; create parent directories; write rendered content; skip_if_exists logic; --force override; --base-dir path resolution\\n- [x] 3.3: `src/output.rs` — OutputMode enum (Json/Human/Quiet); TTY auto-detection; JSON serialization of operations array + files_written/files_skipped; colored human output to stderr\\n- [x] 3.4: Wire `run` subcommand in main.rs with --dry-run, --json, --quiet, --force, --base-dir, --verbose; render-all-then-execute pipeline\\n- [x] 3.5: Unit tests for create operation\\n- [x] 3.6: Unit tests for output formatting\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs`\\n- `src/operations/create.rs`\\n- `src/output.rs`\\n- `src/main.rs` (run subcommand)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 4: Inject Operation\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-5, NFR-2 (complete), NFR-6\\n\\nContent injection into existing files via regex anchoring. All injection modes: after, before, prepend, append, with at:first/last match selection and skip_if idempotency. This completes the v0.1 operation set.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 4.1: `src/operations/inject.rs` — read target file (or from virtual_files if target created in same dry-run); skip_if string search; regex anchor matching (after/before); at:first/at:last match selection; prepend/append modes; render inject path as template; write modified content; inject ops update virtual_files with post-injection content\\n- [x] 4.2: Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\n- [x] 4.3: Unit tests for every injection mode\\n- [x] 4.4: Integration test: recipe with create + inject in same run\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/inject.rs`\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 5: Integration Test Framework\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\n\\nFixture-based integration test harness that makes adding new test cases a matter of adding a directory. Snapshot tests for all output formats. This phase validates the entire v0.1 pipeline end-to-end.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 5.1: Test harness in `tests/integration.rs` — fixture discovery; copy existing/ to temp dir; run jig as subprocess; diff output against expected/; assert exit code\\n- [x] 5.2: Fixtures for create operations — simple create, templated path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, directory creation\\n- [x] 5.3: Fixtures for inject operations — after/before/prepend/append, at:first/at:last, skip_if\\n- [x] 5.4: Fixtures for error cases — missing vars, bad type, missing template, missing target file, regex no-match, malformed YAML, file exists without force, template syntax error\\n- [x] 5.5: Fixtures for combined operations — create + inject in one recipe, multi-file recipe, idempotency (run twice)\\n- [x] 5.6: insta snapshot tests for JSON output format and error messages\\n- [x] 5.7: Determinism test — run same recipe twice, assert byte-identical output (AC-N1.1)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `tests/integration.rs`\\n- `tests/cli.rs`\\n- `tests/fixtures/` (22 fixture directories)\\n- `tests/snapshots/` (7 snapshot files)\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\n- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\n- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\n\\n## Key Decisions\\n\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\n| Operations as enum, not trait objects | `FileOp` enum with match dispatch | Only 2 variants in v0.1 (create, inject). Trait abstraction adds complexity for no benefit until user-defined operations exist, which isn't planned. (ARCHITECTURE D-4) |\\n| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages (ARCHITECTURE D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\n| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable (ARCHITECTURE D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\n| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism (ARCHITECTURE D-5, I-1) |\\n| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate, good Display/Error trait impls |\\n| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\n| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\n| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling. Acceptable dependency for correctness. |\\n| \\\"did you mean?\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) against declared variable names | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Hand-rolling edit distance serves I-10 (graceful degradation) — the error tells the caller exactly what to fix. |\\n| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves, even for ones minijinja provides (replace, indent, join) | We control exact behavior. No surprises from upstream minijinja changes. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\n| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. Templates reference variables not created files. (D-1) |\\n| Extra variables pass through | Don't error or warn on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness here breaks real usage. |\\n| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later operations may depend on earlier ones (e.g., inject targets a prior create). (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\n\\n## Resolved Questions\\n\\n- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\n- **\\\"did you mean?\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names. minijinja doesn't provide this. Serves I-10.\\n- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. We own the behavior, upstream changes don't affect us. Serves I-1.\\n- **TTY detection**: `std::io::IsTerminal` works correctly on macOS. Used in std since Rust 1.70, no external crate needed.\\n- **stdin variable reading**: `--vars-stdin` consumes stdin. Non-interactive by design (I-3), documented as single-purpose per invocation.\\n- **Large file handling for inject**: Reads entire file into memory. Fine for source files (<10K lines). Accepted as known limitation for v0.1.\\n- **pluralizer irregular plurals**: Verified working for standard cases. Acceptable for v0.1 scope.\\n- **minijinja built-in filter override**: Successfully overridden by registering our filters. No conflicts.\\n\\n## Completion Summary\\n\\nAll 5 phases completed 2026-04-04. 191 tests passing (177 unit + 2 CLI integration + 12 fixture integration). 22 test fixture directories covering success paths, error cases, and combined operations.\\n\\n### Outstanding Review Findings\\n\\nThe final code review (2026-04-04) identified issues to address before v0.2 work begins:\\n\\n**Critical:**\\n- Pipeline stage ordering: `--base-dir` existence check (exit 3) runs before template rendering (exit 2), violating AC-N5.2\\n\\n**Major:**\\n- Pre-operation errors bypass output formatting — LLM callers get no JSON on early failures (violates I-4/I-10)\\n- `--vars-file` IO errors discard original error context (permission-denied shows as \\\"not found\\\")\\n- Empty regex pattern `after: \\\"\\\"` silently matches every line instead of failing\\n\\n**Minor:**\\n- Unknown YAML fields on file ops silently ignored instead of reported\\n- `validate` subcommand's local `--json` flag shadows the global flag\\n- `find_undefined_variable` is a best-effort regex heuristic (acceptable for v0.1)\\n- Regex recompiled on every inject execution (perf nit, not functional)\\n\"}},{\"tool_name\":\"Bash\",\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01NBFkfiT12KkYySPuVGTrJV\",\"tool_input\":{\"command\":\"cat > docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md << 'PLANEOF'\\n# PLAN.md\\n\\n> Workstream: core-engine\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\n> Status: Complete\\n\\n## Objective\\n\\nDeliver the minimum viable jig CLI: parse recipes, validate variables, render Jinja2 templates, execute create and inject file operations, and report structured results. This is the v0.1 feature set — the smallest thing that lets an LLM call `jig run` to produce files from a recipe instead of re-deriving boilerplate.\\n\\nCovers ARCHITECTURE.md Phases A through E (skeleton, rendering, create, inject, integration tests).\\n\\n## Phases\\n\\n### Phase 1: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-1, FR-7 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-5\\n\\nBootstrap the Rust crate, wire up clap, and make recipe parsing work end-to-end. After this phase, `jig validate` and `jig vars` are functional commands.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 1.1: Cargo.toml with dependencies (serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap, thiserror, regex, indexmap)\\n- [x] 1.2: `src/error.rs` — StructuredError struct (what/where/why/hint), JigError enum wrapping StructuredError with exit code mapping (0-4)\\n- [x] 1.3: `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe, VariableDecl, VarType, FileOp structs with serde deserialization; template path resolution relative to recipe location; structural validation; unknown operation type detection; custom deserialization for FileOp; compile-check regex patterns during recipe validation\\n- [x] 1.4: `src/variables.rs` — Variable merging and type-checking scaffolding (types imported from recipe.rs, validation logic added in Phase 2)\\n- [x] 1.5: `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands, `#[command(version)]`; wire recipe parsing; map errors to exit codes\\n- [x] 1.6: Unit tests for recipe parsing — valid recipe, missing fields, malformed YAML, missing template files, optional metadata\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `Cargo.toml`\\n- `src/main.rs`\\n- `src/recipe.rs`\\n- `src/variables.rs` (types only)\\n- `src/error.rs`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 2: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-2, FR-3, FR-7 (partial), NFR-1 (partial)\\n\\nFull variable pipeline (parse, merge, type-check) and template rendering with all 13 built-in filters. After this phase, `jig render` works.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 2.1: `src/variables.rs` — full validation: parse JSON from --vars/--vars-file/--vars-stdin; merge with precedence; type-check against declarations; required field enforcement; enum validation; array item type validation\\n- [x] 2.2: `src/filters.rs` — all 13 built-in filters registered with minijinja\\n- [x] 2.3: `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja Environment setup; template loading from recipe-relative paths; filter registration; render with variables context; structured error on undefined variable (with \\\"did you mean?\\\" via edit distance) and syntax errors (with file + line)\\n- [x] 2.4: Wire `render` subcommand in main.rs with --vars, --vars-file, --vars-stdin, --to options\\n- [x] 2.5: Unit tests for variable validation — every VarType, required missing, default fallback, enum rejection, array item mismatch, merge precedence\\n- [x] 2.6: Unit tests + insta snapshot tests for all 13 filters and template rendering\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/variables.rs` (full implementation)\\n- `src/filters.rs`\\n- `src/renderer.rs`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 3: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-4, FR-6, FR-7 (complete), NFR-2 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-6\\n\\nFile creation with templated output paths, directory auto-creation, skip_if_exists, and dual-stream output (JSON stdout / human stderr). After this phase, `jig run` works for create-only recipes.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 3.1: `src/operations/mod.rs` — ExecutionContext struct (base_dir, dry_run, force, virtual_files for dry-run state); OpResult enum; operation dispatch\\n- [x] 3.2: `src/operations/create.rs` — render `to` path as template; create parent directories; write rendered content; skip_if_exists logic; --force override; --base-dir path resolution\\n- [x] 3.3: `src/output.rs` — OutputMode enum (Json/Human/Quiet); TTY auto-detection; JSON serialization of operations array + files_written/files_skipped; colored human output to stderr\\n- [x] 3.4: Wire `run` subcommand in main.rs with --dry-run, --json, --quiet, --force, --base-dir, --verbose; render-all-then-execute pipeline\\n- [x] 3.5: Unit tests for create operation\\n- [x] 3.6: Unit tests for output formatting\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs`\\n- `src/operations/create.rs`\\n- `src/output.rs`\\n- `src/main.rs` (run subcommand)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 4: Inject Operation\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-5, NFR-2 (complete), NFR-6\\n\\nContent injection into existing files via regex anchoring. All injection modes: after, before, prepend, append, with at:first/last match selection and skip_if idempotency. This completes the v0.1 operation set.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 4.1: `src/operations/inject.rs` — read target file (or from virtual_files if target created in same dry-run); skip_if string search; regex anchor matching (after/before); at:first/at:last match selection; prepend/append modes; inject ops update virtual_files with post-injection content\\n- [x] 4.2: Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\n- [x] 4.3: Unit tests for every injection mode\\n- [x] 4.4: Integration test: recipe with create + inject in same run\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/inject.rs`\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 5: Integration Test Framework\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\n\\nFixture-based integration test harness that makes adding new test cases a matter of adding a directory. Snapshot tests for all output formats.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 5.1: Test harness in `tests/integration.rs` — fixture discovery; copy existing/ to temp dir; run jig as subprocess; diff output against expected/; assert exit code\\n- [x] 5.2: Fixtures for create operations — simple create, templated path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, directory creation\\n- [x] 5.3: Fixtures for inject operations — after/before/prepend/append, at:first/at:last, skip_if\\n- [x] 5.4: Fixtures for error cases — missing vars, bad type, missing template, missing target file, regex no-match, malformed YAML, file exists without force, template syntax error\\n- [x] 5.5: Fixtures for combined operations — create + inject in one recipe, multi-file recipe, idempotency (run twice)\\n- [x] 5.6: insta snapshot tests for JSON output format and error messages\\n- [x] 5.7: Determinism test — run same recipe twice, assert byte-identical output (AC-N1.1)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `tests/integration.rs`\\n- `tests/cli.rs`\\n- `tests/fixtures/` (22 fixture directories)\\n- `tests/snapshots/` (7 snapshot files)\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\n- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\n- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\n\\n## Key Decisions\\n\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\n| Operations as enum, not trait objects | `FileOp` enum with match dispatch | Only 2 variants in v0.1 (create, inject). Trait abstraction adds complexity for no benefit. (D-4) |\\n| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages. (D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\n| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable. (D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\n| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism. (D-5, I-1) |\\n| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate |\\n| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\n| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\n| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling |\\n| \\\"did you mean?\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Serves I-10 (graceful degradation). |\\n| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves | We control exact behavior. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\n| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. (D-1) |\\n| Extra variables pass through | Don't error on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness breaks real usage. |\\n| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later ops may depend on earlier ones. (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\n\\n## Resolved Questions\\n\\n- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\n- **\\\"did you mean?\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names.\\n- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. No conflicts with builtins.\\n- **TTY detection**: `std::io::IsTerminal` works correctly on macOS. In std since Rust 1.70.\\n- **stdin variable reading**: `--vars-stdin` consumes stdin. Non-interactive by design (I-3).\\n- **Large file handling for inject**: Reads entire file into memory. Fine for source files (<10K lines).\\n- **minijinja built-in filter override**: Successfully overridden by registering our filters.\\n\\n## Completion Summary\\n\\nAll 5 phases completed 2026-04-04. 191 tests passing (177 unit + 2 CLI integration + 12 fixture integration). 22 test fixture directories covering success paths, error cases, and combined operations.\\n\\n### Outstanding Review Findings\\n\\nThe final code review (2026-04-04) identified issues to address before v0.2 work begins:\\n\\n**Critical:**\\n- Pipeline stage ordering: `--base-dir` existence check (exit 3) runs before template rendering (exit 2), violating AC-N5.2\\n\\n**Major:**\\n- Pre-operation errors bypass output formatting — LLM callers get no JSON on early failures (violates I-4/I-10)\\n- `--vars-file` IO errors discard original error context (permission-denied shows as \\\"not found\\\")\\n- Empty regex pattern `after: \\\"\\\"` silently matches every line instead of failing\\n\\n**Minor:**\\n- Unknown YAML fields on file ops silently ignored instead of reported\\n- `validate` subcommand's local `--json` flag shadows the global flag\\n- `find_undefined_variable` is a best-effort regex heuristic (acceptable for v0.1)\\n- Regex recompiled on every inject execution (perf nit, not functional)\\nPLANEOF\",\"description\":\"Write updated located existing workstream plans/specs (`core-engine`, `replace-patch`, `workflows`) and there isn’t a `libraries` directory yet. I’m now reading prior `PLAN.md` style so this new execution plan matches established structure and depth.\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.started\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_7\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/workflows/PLAN.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"\",\"exit_code\":null,\"status\":\"in_progress\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_7\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/workflows/PLAN.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# PLAN.md\\n\\n> Workstream: workflows\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\n> Status: Complete\\n\\n## Objective\\n\\nAdd multi-recipe orchestration to jig (v0.3). A workflow chains multiple recipes into a single invocation with conditional steps (`when`), variable mapping between steps (`vars_map`, `vars`), and configurable error handling (`on_error: stop|continue|report`). This is the prerequisite for libraries (v0.4), which organize workflows over library-namespaced recipes.\\n\\nThe deliverable is: `jig workflow <path> --vars '{...}'` executes an ordered sequence of recipe invocations, with per-step JSON results, shared filesystem state, and predictable error behavior.\\n\\n## Phases\\n\\n### Phase 1: Workflow Parsing & CLI Detection\\nStatus: Complete\\n\\nBuild the workflow YAML parser and extend `jig validate` / `jig vars` to auto-detect workflow files.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 1.1: Create `src/workflow.rs` with `Workflow`, `WorkflowStep`, `OnError` structs and serde deserialization from YAML\\n- [x] 1.2: Implement `load_workflow()` — parse YAML, resolve recipe paths relative to workflow dir, validate recipe files exist and are structurally valid\\n- [x] 1.3: Implement auto-detection in `jig validate`: read YAML, check for `steps` vs `files` key, dispatch to workflow or recipe validation\\n- [x] 1.4: Implement auto-detection in `jig vars`: same detection logic, output workflow variable declarations\\n- [x] 1.5: Add workflow validation JSON and human output formats (type, name, variables, steps with recipe/valid/conditional)\\n- [x] 1.6: Unit tests for parsing: valid workflow, empty steps, missing steps key, both steps+files, bad on_error, missing recipe file, invalid recipe, no variables, optional metadata\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `jig validate workflow.yaml` auto-detects and validates a workflow with 3+ steps, reporting each recipe's validity\\n- `jig vars workflow.yaml` outputs the workflow's variable declarations as JSON\\n- All parse-time error cases produce exit code 1 with structured error messages\\n- 20+ unit tests covering AC-1.1 through AC-1.20\\n\\n### Phase 2: Variable Resolution & Conditional Steps\\nStatus: Complete\\n\\nImplement step variable resolution (shared vars → vars_map → vars overrides) and `when` condition evaluation.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 2.1: Implement `resolve_step_variables()` — start with workflow vars, apply vars_map renaming (simultaneous, not chained), apply vars overrides\\n- [x] 2.2: Implement `evaluate_when()` — render Jinja2 template with workflow vars, apply falsy rules (empty, \\\"false\\\" case-insensitive, \\\"0\\\")\\n- [x] 2.3: Implement workflow-level variable validation — reuse `variables::validate_variables()` with workflow's `VariableDecl` map\\n- [x] 2.4: Implement step-level variable validation — validate step's resolved vars against the step recipe's `VariableDecl` map\\n- [x] 2.5: Unit tests for: vars_map rename semantics, vars_map with nonexistent source, vars_map target collision, vars override, combined vars_map+vars, when truthy/falsy cases, when with Jinja2 control flow, when with undefined variable, simultaneous vars_map\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `resolve_step_variables()` produces correct output for all documented edge cases (AC-4.1 through AC-4.10)\\n- `evaluate_when()` correctly classifies: empty → false, \\\"false\\\"/\\\"False\\\"/\\\"FALSE\\\" → false, \\\"0\\\" → false, \\\"true\\\" → true, \\\"yes\\\" → true, non-empty string → true\\n- Variable validation errors at workflow level halt before any execution (exit 4)\\n- 15+ unit tests covering FR-3 and FR-4\\n\\n### Phase 3: Execution Engine\\nStatus: Complete\\n\\nBuild the workflow execution loop that iterates steps, evaluates conditions, resolves variables, and runs recipes — sharing filesystem state (and virtual_files in dry-run) across steps.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 3.1: Implement `execute_workflow()` — sequential step loop with condition evaluation, variable resolution, and recipe execution\\n- [x] 3.2: Extract `run_recipe()` in `workflow.rs` — reusable recipe execution without CLI concerns (rendering, operation execution, error collection)\\n- [x] 3.3: Implement cross-step `virtual_files` carryover for `--dry-run` mode — a single `ExecutionContext` spans all steps\\n- [x] 3.4: Implement `on_error` handling: stop (halt on failure), continue (record and proceed, exit 0), report (record and proceed, exit 3)\\n- [x] 3.5: Implement per-step `on_error` overrides — step-level value takes precedence over workflow-level default\\n- [x] 3.6: Implement exit code selection via `compute_workflow_exit_code()` in `main.rs`\\n- [x] 3.7: Unit tests for execution: basic two-step, conditional skip, chain create→inject, chain create→patch, error stop, error continue, error report, step on_error override, dry-run chaining\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- A two-step workflow where step 1 creates a file and step 2 injects into it succeeds in both normal and dry-run mode\\n- A workflow with `when: \\\"{{ false }}\\\"` skips the step and reports status \\\"skipped\\\"\\n- `on_error: stop` halts after first failure; `continue` runs all steps; `report` runs all steps but exits 3\\n- Per-step on_error correctly overrides workflow default\\n- 20+ unit tests covering FR-5 and FR-6\\n\\n### Phase 4: CLI Command & Output Formatting\\nStatus: Complete\\n\\nAdd the `jig workflow` subcommand and implement workflow-specific JSON and human output.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 4.1: Add `Workflow` variant to `Commands` enum in `main.rs` — accepts path + same global options as `Run`\\n- [x] 4.2: Implement `cmd_workflow()` in `main.rs` — parse workflow, validate vars, execute, format output, return exit code\\n- [x] 4.3: Implement `format_workflow_json()` in `output.rs` — per-step results, aggregate files_written/files_skipped, top-level workflow/on_error/status/dry_run fields\\n- [x] 4.4: Implement `format_workflow_human()` in `output.rs` — step headers (\\\"Step 1/3: ...\\\"), nested operation lines, summary line\\n- [x] 4.5: Handle edge case: `jig workflow` passed a recipe file → exit 1 with helpful message pointing to `jig run`\\n- [x] 4.6: Wire `--verbose` to include rendered content across all steps in both JSON and human output\\n- [x] 4.7: CLI integration tests: basic workflow run, JSON output format, human output format, --dry-run, --quiet\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `jig workflow workflow.yaml --vars '...' --json` produces correct per-step JSON output matching the schema in SPEC.md\\n- Human output shows step progress headers, nested operation results, and summary line\\n- `jig workflow recipe.yaml` produces a clear error directing to `jig run`\\n- 10+ tests covering FR-7 and FR-8\\n\\n### Phase 5: Integration Testing\\nStatus: Complete\\n\\nBuild fixture-based integration tests covering the full workflow feature set, including error cases and cross-step chaining.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 5.1: Create fixture infrastructure — workflow fixtures follow the same pattern as recipe fixtures, with a `workflow.yaml` at the root and step recipe subdirectories\\n- [x] 5.2: Implement 14 success-path fixtures: basic, three-steps, when-true, when-false, when-complex, vars-map, vars-override, vars-map-and-override, chain-create-inject, chain-create-patch, empty-steps, no-variables, idempotent, dry-run-chain\\n- [x] 5.3: Implement 4 error-handling fixtures: on-error-stop, on-error-continue, on-error-report, step-on-error\\n- [x] 5.4: Implement 7 error-case fixtures: missing-recipe, bad-yaml, missing-steps, ambiguous, bad-vars, when-undef, step-missing-var\\n- [x] 5.5: Run full test suite (`cargo test`), verify all pass, update test count in CLAUDE.md — 343 tests passing\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- All 25 spec-required fixtures exist and pass\\n- `cargo test` passes with 0 failures\\n- `cargo clippy` clean\\n- Test count updated in CLAUDE.md project status\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\n- **Depends on:** core-engine (v0.1, complete), replace-patch (v0.2, complete). Workflows orchestrate all four operation types — they must all work.\\n- **Blocks:** libraries (v0.4) — libraries define workflows over library-namespaced recipes and need the workflow execution engine.\\n\\n## Key Decisions\\n\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\n| Workflow file format | Standalone YAML with `steps` key | Distinguishable from recipes (which have `files`). Clean auto-detection. Libraries (v0.4) will reference these. |\\n| Recipe path resolution | Relative to workflow file directory | Consistent with I-7 (templates live with the consumer). Keeps workflow + recipe bundles self-contained. |\\n| `when` evaluation | Render Jinja2, check string truthiness | Simple, predictable. Reuses existing renderer. No new expression language. |\\n| Falsy values | Empty, \\\"false\\\" (case-insensitive), \\\"0\\\" | Covers minijinja boolean rendering (\\\"false\\\"), zero, and empty. Minimal set — easy to document and remember. |\\n| `when` variable scope | Workflow-level variables (before vars_map/vars) | Prevents circular dependency where step vars affect step execution. Condition is about \\\"should this step run at all\\\" — a workflow-level concern. |\\n| vars_map semantics | Rename (not copy) | Spec says \\\"rename.\\\" Avoids ambiguity when recipe has different meaning for original name. Copy achievable via `vars` override. |\\n| vars_map application | Simultaneous (not chained) | `{a: b, b: c}` means original `a` → `b` and original `b` → `c`, not `a` → `b` → `c`. Prevents order-dependent surprises. |\\n| No new exit codes | Reuse 0-4 | Workflow errors map to existing categories (validation, rendering, file ops, variables). Adding exit code 5+ would break I-5 stability guarantee without clear benefit. |\\n| No nested workflows | Steps reference recipes only | Keeps v0.3 scope manageable. Nested workflows can be added later if needed. Flat is better than nested. |\\n| No inter-step variable passing | Steps share filesystem, not variables | Variables flow from workflow to steps. Steps communicate through the filesystem (one creates a file, the next reads/patches it). Keeps the model simple. |\\n| Execution context sharing | Single ExecutionContext across steps | Enables dry-run virtual_files carryover. One base_dir, one force flag, one set of virtual files. |\\n| Step variable validation timing | At execution time (not upfront) | Conditional steps might have variables that only make sense when the step runs. Validating skipped steps would produce false errors. |\\n\\n## Risks / Open Questions (Resolved)\\n\\n- **~~Risk: Refactoring `cmd_run` for reuse.~~** Resolved. Created `run_recipe()` in `workflow.rs` (lines 551-619) that handles recipe loading, rendering, and execution without CLI concerns. `cmd_run` in `main.rs` was NOT refactored — both contain similar rendering pipelines. See review finding M4 in Known Issues.\\n- **~~Risk: `virtual_files` carryover complexity.~~** Resolved. Single `ExecutionContext` spans all steps. Tested via `workflow-dry-run-chain` fixture (step 1 creates, step 2 injects into virtual file).\\n- **~~Open question: `vars` values support Jinja2?~~** Decision: no for v0.3. Literal JSON values only.\\n- **~~Open question: vars_map conflict detection?~~** Decision: `load_workflow()` now validates for duplicate targets within a single step's vars_map and rejects them at parse time. Cross-step conflicts are not checked (different steps are independent).\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.started\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_8\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/workflows/SPEC.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"\",\"exit_code\":null,\"status\":\"in_progress\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.started\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_9\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/replace-patch/PLAN.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"\",\"exit_code\":null,\"status\":\"in_progress\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_9\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/replace-patch/PLAN.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# PLAN.md\\n\\n> Workstream: replace-patch\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\n> Status: Implementation Complete — Review Findings Pending\\n\\n## Objective\\n\\nExtend jig from greenfield-only (create + inject) to brownfield operations — replacing regions in existing files and inserting content at structurally-determined locations. This is the v0.2 feature set that makes jig useful for the most common real-world task: extending existing code (adding fields, methods, endpoints) across multiple files in a single recipe.\\n\\nCovers ARCHITECTURE.md Phases F (Replace Operation) and G (Patch Operation + Scope Detection).\\n\\n## Phases\\n\\n### Phase 1: Recipe Parsing Extensions\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-1\\n\\nExtend `recipe.rs` to parse `replace` and `patch` file operations into fully typed `FileOp` variants. Currently these are accepted by `RawFileOp` but rejected at conversion time (lines 276-291). This phase removes that rejection, adds the new types to `FileOp`, and validates all fields at parse time.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 1.1: Add new types to `src/recipe.rs` — `ReplaceSpec` (Between, Pattern), `Fallback` (Append, Prepend, Skip, Error), `Anchor` struct (pattern, scope, find, position), `ScopeType` enum (8 variants), `Position` enum (7 variants). Add `FileOp::Replace` and `FileOp::Patch` variants.\\n- [x] 1.2: Extend `RawFileOp` deserialization to capture replace-specific fields (`between`, `pattern`, `fallback`) and patch-specific fields (`anchor` with `pattern`, `scope`, `find`, `position`). Add `RawAnchor` intermediate struct for serde.\\n- [x] 1.3: Implement `convert_file_op` logic for replace: validate between vs pattern (exactly one), validate fallback value, compile-check regex patterns at parse time. For patch: validate anchor.pattern required, compile-check anchor pattern, validate scope type and position type against known variants, default scope to `line` and position to `after`.\\n- [x] 1.4: Extend `FileOp::template()` to cover Replace and Patch variants.\\n- [x] 1.5: Update `validate_templates()` to check template existence for replace and patch operations.\\n- [x] 1.6: Extend `jig validate` and `jig vars` output to include replace/patch operations in their summaries.\\n- [x] 1.7: Unit tests for all parsing paths — valid replace (between, pattern, fallback variants), valid patch (all scope/position combinations, with/without find), and error cases (conflicting fields, missing fields, invalid regex, invalid scope/position/fallback strings).\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- Recipes with replace operations parse without error\\n- Recipes with patch operations parse without error\\n- All error cases from AC-1.5 through AC-1.16 produce exit code 1 with clear messages\\n- Regex patterns in between.start, between.end, pattern, and anchor.pattern are compile-checked at parse time\\n- `jig validate` reports replace/patch operation counts alongside create/inject\\n- Existing create/inject recipes still parse identically (no regressions)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/recipe.rs` (major changes — new types, new conversion logic)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- None (builds on existing recipe.rs structure)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 2: Replace Operation\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-2, FR-8 (partial)\\n\\nImplement the replace operation: match a region in an existing file and swap it with rendered content. Two modes: `between` (marker-delimited region) and `pattern` (regex-matched lines). Four fallback strategies when the match fails.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 2.1: Create `src/operations/replace.rs` — implement `execute()` function following the same signature pattern as create.rs and inject.rs. Core logic: read target file (or virtual_files), apply between or pattern matching, replace the region, write back.\\n- [x] 2.2: Implement `between` mode — find start regex match, then find end regex match after start, replace content between them (exclusive of markers). Handle adjacent markers (empty region = insert between).\\n- [x] 2.3: Implement `pattern` mode — find all contiguous lines matching regex, replace them with rendered content.\\n- [x] 2.4: Implement fallback behavior — when match not found, dispatch to append/prepend/skip/error. Append and prepend write the content; skip returns OpResult::Skip; error returns OpResult::Error with rendered_content.\\n- [x] 2.5: Wire replace dispatch into `operations/mod.rs` — add `FileOp::Replace` match arm in `execute_operation`.\\n- [x] 2.6: Wire replace into `main.rs` — extend the template preparation loop to handle replace operations (render template content and target path). Add replace to the `PreparedOp` match arms.\\n- [x] 2.7: Add verbose output for replace operations — match mode, matched region line range, lines replaced.\\n- [x] 2.8: Unit tests — between (normal, empty region, adjacent markers), pattern (single line, multi-line), all four fallbacks, missing target file, end marker not found, dry-run with virtual_files.\\n- [x] 2.9: Integration test fixtures — `replace-between`, `replace-between-empty`, `replace-pattern`, `replace-fallback-append`, `replace-fallback-prepend`, `replace-fallback-skip`, `replace-fallback-error`, `error-replace-no-match`, `error-replace-end-missing`.\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `between` mode preserves marker lines, replaces content between (AC-2.1, AC-2.7)\\n- `pattern` mode replaces matched lines entirely (AC-2.2)\\n- All four fallbacks work correctly (AC-2.3 through AC-2.6)\\n- Missing target file exits 3 with rendered content (AC-2.10)\\n- Missing end marker exits 3 with rendered content (AC-2.11)\\n- Dry-run mode uses and updates virtual_files (AC-2.13)\\n- Replace works in a recipe alongside create and inject operations\\n- All integration fixtures pass\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/replace.rs` (new)\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\n- `src/main.rs` (preparation update)\\n- `src/output.rs` (minor — replace action formatting, verbose diagnostics)\\n- `tests/fixtures/replace-*` (new)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- Phase 1 (recipe parsing for FileOp::Replace)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 3: Scope Detection\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-3, FR-4, FR-5\\n\\nImplement the scope detection module — the structural analysis layer that makes patch operations \\\"smart.\\\" Two detection strategies (indentation-based, delimiter-based) and seven semantic position heuristics.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 3.1: Create `src/scope/mod.rs` — `ScopeResult` struct, `detect_scope()` dispatch function that routes to indent or delimiter based on `ScopeType`. Export the public API.\\n- [x] 3.2: Create `src/scope/indent.rs` — indentation-based scope detection for `Block`, `ClassBody`, `FunctionBody`. Algorithm: find anchor indentation, walk forward collecting deeper-indented lines, stop when indentation returns to anchor level or shallower. Handle blank lines (don't terminate scope), multi-line class declarations (find the colon), empty scopes.\\n- [x] 3.3: Create `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — delimiter-based scope detection for `Braces`, `Brackets`, `Parens`, `FunctionSignature`. Algorithm: find opening delimiter on/after anchor, count nesting depth with string literal and comment awareness, close when depth returns to zero. Handle nested delimiters, string escaping, empty scopes.\\n- [x] 3.4: Create `src/scope/position.rs` — semantic position resolution within a scope. Implement all 7 positions: `Before`, `After`, `BeforeClose`, `AfterLastField`, `AfterLastMethod`, `AfterLastImport`, `Sorted`. Include fallback logic (field→before_close, method→before_close, import→before). Return `PositionResult` with insertion line, detected indentation, and fallback note.\\n- [x] 3.5: Implement `find_within_scope()` — search for a string within scope boundaries, detect if the found line opens a sub-scope (heuristic: line contains `[`, `{`, or `(` as the last non-whitespace character, or assignment with delimiter), return `FindResult` with sub-scope if applicable.\\n- [x] 3.6: Unit tests for indentation scopes — Python class body, Python function body, YAML nested block, empty scope, blank lines within scope, nested classes, nested functions, multi-line class declaration, decorator handling.\\n- [x] 3.7: Unit tests for delimiter scopes — Rust struct braces, TypeScript interface braces, Go function braces, Python list brackets, function signature parens, nested delimiters, delimiters in strings, delimiters in comments, escaped delimiters, empty scope, missing delimiter errors.\\n- [x] 3.8: Unit tests for position resolution — each of the 7 positions tested against realistic code blocks, fallback behavior when pattern not found, sorted insertion order.\\n- [x] 3.9: Unit tests for find narrowing — find within class body, find with sub-scope detection (list_display = [...]), find not found error.\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- Python class body correctly identified (AC-3.2): `class Foo:` through dedent\\n- Python function body correctly identified (AC-3.3): `def bar():` through dedent\\n- Blank lines don't terminate indentation scope (AC-3.4)\\n- Nested delimiters tracked correctly (AC-4.5)\\n- String literals don't affect delimiter counting (AC-4.6)\\n- Comments don't affect delimiter counting (AC-4.7)\\n- `after_last_field` finds `^\\\\s+\\\\w+\\\\s*[:=]` pattern (AC-5.4)\\n- `after_last_method` finds complete method body end (AC-5.5, AC-5.11)\\n- `sorted` inserts in alphabetical order (AC-5.7)\\n- Fallbacks work: field→before_close, method→before_close, import→before (AC-5.8-5.10)\\n- Find narrows to sub-scope when found line opens one (AC-6.2)\\n- All scope types work across languages without configuration (AC-N1.3)\\n- Deterministic: same input = same scope boundaries (AC-N4.1)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/scope/mod.rs` (new)\\n- `src/scope/indent.rs` (new)\\n- `src/scope/delimiter.rs` (new)\\n- `src/scope/position.rs` (new)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- None (scope module is independent — tested in isolation before wiring to patch)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 4: Patch Operation\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-6, FR-7, FR-8 (complete)\\n\\nImplement the patch operation: anchor-based, scope-aware content insertion. This wires together the scope detection module (Phase 3) with the file operation pipeline, adds indentation matching, and completes verbose diagnostics.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 4.1: Create `src/operations/patch.rs` — implement `execute()` function. Core pipeline: read target file → skip_if check → find anchor via regex → detect scope → apply find narrowing (if any) → resolve position → match indentation → insert rendered content → write back.\\n- [x] 4.2: Implement indentation matching — detect the indentation level at the insertion point from surrounding lines, adjust the rendered content's base indentation to match. Handle the case where the rendered template already has its own indentation (preserve relative indentation within the template, adjust only the base level).\\n- [x] 4.3: Implement `scope: line` as the degenerate case — just insert after the anchor line, identical to inject's `after` mode. This ensures patch can always fall back to the simplest behavior.\\n- [x] 4.4: Wire patch dispatch into `operations/mod.rs` — add `FileOp::Patch` match arm in `execute_operation`.\\n- [x] 4.5: Wire patch into `main.rs` — extend the template preparation loop to handle patch operations (render template content, target path, and skip_if). Add patch to the `PreparedOp` match arms.\\n- [ ] 4.6: Complete verbose diagnostics for patch and replace — `ScopeDiagnostics` struct in output, displayed in both JSON and human modes. Show anchor line, scope range, insertion point, find match, position fallback.\\n- [x] 4.7: Unit tests — full pipeline (anchor→scope→position→insert), skip_if, scope: line, indentation matching, anchor not found, scope detection failure, find narrowing, dry-run with virtual_files.\\n- [x] 4.8: Integration test fixtures — `patch-class-body-field`, `patch-braces`, `patch-skip-if`, `patch-scope-line`, `error-patch-no-anchor` implemented. Missing: `patch-function-body`, `patch-brackets`, `patch-function-signature`, `patch-find-narrowing`, `patch-sorted`, `patch-indent-matching`, `patch-nested-delimiters`, `patch-string-delimiters`, `error-patch-unbalanced`, `error-patch-find-missing`.\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- Full patch pipeline works end-to-end: anchor→scope→position→insert (AC-7.1)\\n- skip_if prevents duplicate content (AC-7.2, AC-N3.1, AC-N3.3)\\n- Indentation matching adjusts inserted content (AC-7.7)\\n- scope: line behaves like inject after (AC-7.12)\\n- Scope detection errors include rendered content (AC-7.5, AC-N2.1)\\n- Find narrowing works with sub-scopes (AC-6.2, AC-6.4)\\n- Verbose output shows complete scope diagnostics (AC-8.1, AC-8.2, AC-8.5)\\n- All integration fixtures pass\\n- Existing create/inject tests still pass (no regressions)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/patch.rs` (new)\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\n- `src/main.rs` (preparation update)\\n- `src/output.rs` (verbose scope diagnostics)\\n- `tests/fixtures/patch-*` (new)\\n- `tests/fixtures/error-patch-*` (new)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- Phase 1 (recipe parsing for FileOp::Patch)\\n- Phase 3 (scope detection module)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 5: Integration Testing + Full Validation\\nStatus: Partial — core passing, fixture gaps remain\\nTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\n\\nComprehensive integration testing across all four operation types. Multi-operation recipes, idempotency, determinism, cross-language scope detection, and the full fixture suite.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 5.1: Create `combined-all-ops` fixture — a single recipe that uses create, inject, replace, and patch in sequence, demonstrating all four operation types working together.\\n- [ ] 5.2: Create `combined-patch-idempotent` fixture — a patch-heavy recipe run twice, second run should show all skips. (existing `combined-idempotency` covers create+inject but not patch-specific idempotency)\\n- [ ] 5.3: Cross-language scope detection fixtures — Python class body and Rust struct braces covered. Missing: TypeScript interface braces, Go function braces, YAML nested block.\\n- [x] 5.4: Determinism test — integration tests confirm same inputs produce same outputs.\\n- [x] 5.5: Error recovery test — `error-replace-no-match`, `error-replace-end-missing`, `error-patch-no-anchor` all produce structured errors with rendered_content.\\n- [ ] 5.6: Snapshot tests — insta snapshots for verbose scope diagnostics not implemented (FR-8 verbose diagnostics not yet built).\\n- [x] 5.7: Verify `cargo clippy` passes clean and `cargo test` passes all tests. 298 tests passing (284 unit + 2 CLI + 12 integration).\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- All four operation types work in a single recipe (ordered execution preserved)\\n- Idempotent recipes produce all skips on second run\\n- Same inputs = same output across runs (determinism)\\n- Every error case from the Error Handling table in SPEC.md is covered by at least one fixture\\n- All scope types tested against at least two different languages\\n- `cargo test` green, `cargo clippy` clean\\n- Total test count significantly above 191 (target: 300+)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `tests/fixtures/combined-*` (new)\\n- `tests/fixtures/patch-*-python`, `patch-*-rust`, etc. (new, language-specific)\\n- `tests/integration.rs` (may need minor updates for new fixture types)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- Phase 2 (replace operation)\\n- Phase 4 (patch operation)\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\n- **Depends on:** core-engine workstream (v0.1 — complete). Specifically: recipe.rs parsing infrastructure, operations/mod.rs dispatch, output.rs formatting, error.rs types, the existing integration test harness.\\n- **Blocks:** workflows (v0.3) — workflows chain recipes that include patch/replace operations. Libraries (v0.4) — library recipes use patch operations extensively (the \\\"add-model-field\\\" example from the spec).\\n\\n## Key Decisions\\n\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\n| Scope type in recipe, not auto-detected from file extension | Recipe author specifies `scope: class_body` or `scope: braces` explicitly | Language detection is fragile and adds complexity. The recipe author knows the target language. Scope type is the right abstraction level. (ARCHITECTURE D-3, NFR-1 AC-N1.3) |\\n| Indentation vs delimiter are separate code paths | `scope/indent.rs` and `scope/delimiter.rs` as distinct modules | They share no logic. Indentation walks lines measuring whitespace. Delimiters count balanced pairs. Forcing a common abstraction would be artificial. |\\n| Semantic positions are heuristic, not parsed | Regex patterns like `^\\\\s+\\\\w+\\\\s*[:=]` for field detection | Full parsing (tree-sitter) is out of scope. Heuristics cover 90%+ of real code. When they fail, the error includes rendered content for fallback. (ARCHITECTURE D-3, I-10) |\\n| Position fallback rather than error | `after_last_field` falls back to `before_close` if no fields found | A recipe that works on a class with 5 fields should also work on an empty class. Erroring on empty scopes would make recipes fragile. The fallback is noted in verbose output. |\\n| Indentation matching on insert | Patch auto-adjusts rendered content indentation to match insertion context | Template authors shouldn't need to know the exact indentation of every insertion point. Templates define relative indentation; jig adjusts the base level. |\\n| `find` narrowing auto-detects sub-scopes | If the `find` line contains an opening delimiter, jig detects the sub-scope | This enables the `list_display = [...]` pattern from the spec without requiring the recipe author to specify a second scope type. Keeps recipe YAML simple. |\\n| Replace preserves markers (between mode) | Start and end marker lines are kept; only content between is swapped | Markers serve as stable anchors for future re-runs. Removing them would break idempotency. The spec is explicit about this. |\\n| First match for anchor pattern | Patch uses first regex match (like inject's `at: first` default) | Predictable behavior. If the user needs a specific match, they write a more specific regex. Adding `at: first/last` to patch is a future option if needed. |\\n\\n## Risks / Open Questions\\n\\n- **Risk: Indentation heuristics fail on unusual code.** Python with mixed indentation, or code with unusual formatting, may confuse the indentation-based scope detection. Mitigation: clear error messages with rendered content, verbose mode showing scope boundaries, and the fallback-to-LLM design (I-10). Accept that edge cases exist and fail gracefully rather than trying to handle every case.\\n\\n- **Risk: String literal detection in delimiter scopes is imperfect.** Multi-line strings (Python triple-quotes, Rust raw strings, JavaScript template literals with nested expressions) are hard to detect without full parsing. Mitigation: handle the common cases (single/double quoted, backtick), document the limitation, rely on the error-with-rendered-content fallback for edge cases.\\n\\n- **Risk: `after_last_method` body detection.** Finding the end of a method's body (not just the `def` line) requires running scope detection recursively. This is correct but adds complexity. Mitigation: implement it — the alternative (inserting after the `def` line) would be wrong in most cases.\\n\\n- **~~Open question~~ Resolved: Should `pattern` mode in replace match a single contiguous block or all matches?** Decision: match the first contiguous block of lines matching the pattern. If users need all occurrences, they can use multiple replace operations. This keeps behavior predictable.\\n\\n- **~~Open question~~ Resolved: Indentation matching precision.** Decision: match the immediate context (indentation of the line at the insertion point). This is simpler and always correct for the local context, even in files with mixed indentation.\\n\\n## Review Findings (2026-04-04)\\n\\nCode review completed (1 round, clean). The review identified issues across three severity levels. **None have been fixed in code yet** — the fix commit (6f9b69b) only added review artifacts.\\n\\n### Critical (must fix before merge)\\n\\n1. **`write_back` silently swallows write errors.** `patch.rs:228` uses `let _ = std::fs::write(...)` for every non-dry-run write; `replace.rs:278` does the same in fallback append/prepend. Permission-denied returns `OpResult::Success`, violating AC-7.11, AC-2.14, I-10. The main replace path (`replace.rs:106`) handles this correctly — apply the same pattern.\\n2. **`Position::Sorted` is stub-implemented.** `position.rs:170-184` just inserts at end-of-scope. AC-5.7 requires alphabetically correct insertion. Either implement or reject at parse time.\\n3. **Byte index / char index mismatch in delimiter scope detection.** `delimiter.rs:87` slices with `CharScanner.col` (char position) but Rust string slicing uses byte offsets. Multi-byte UTF-8 causes panic.\\n\\n### Major (should fix)\\n\\n4. **11 of 26 spec-required integration fixtures missing (42% gap).** Missing: `patch-function-body`, `patch-brackets`, `patch-function-signature`, `patch-find-narrowing`, `patch-sorted`, `patch-indent-matching`, `patch-nested-delimiters`, `patch-string-delimiters`, `error-patch-unbalanced`, `error-patch-find-missing`, `combined-patch-idempotent`.\\n5. **Verbose scope diagnostics (FR-8) not implemented.** No `ScopeDiagnostics` struct, no `scope_diagnostics` JSON field. Verbose only adds `rendered_content`.\\n6. **`find` narrowing doesn't re-anchor when no sub-scope is detected.** AC-6.1 requires position resolution relative to the found line, not the original scope.\\n7. **`scope: line` auto-adjusts indentation, diverging from inject-after behavior.** AC-7.12 says \\\"SHALL behave identically to inject's `after` mode.\\\"\\n8. **`find_opening` doesn't skip strings/comments when searching for the opening delimiter.** AC-4.6/4.7 — only the nesting-depth loop has string/comment awareness.\\n9. **`AfterLastMethod` regex too narrow for Rust.** Misses `const fn`, `unsafe fn`, `pub(crate) fn`, `extern \\\"C\\\" fn`.\\n10. **Location string uses `Debug` not `Display`.** Produces `classbody` instead of `class_body`.\\n11. **Mixed tab/space scope detection uses length, not literal prefix comparison.** AC-3.8 requires character-by-character whitespace comparison.\\n\\n### Minor\\n\\n12. **`AfterLastField` regex requires leading whitespace (`\\\\s+` not `\\\\s*`).** Fails on top-level fields (Go structs).\\n13. **Regex recompiled at runtime in replace.** Pre-compile during recipe parsing.\\n14. **`chars().count()` called on every scanner step.** O(n^2) per line in delimiter detection.\\n\\n## Execution Order\\n\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_8\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/workflows/SPEC.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# SPEC.md\\n\\n> Workstream: workflows\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\n> Scope: v0.3 (Phase H from ARCHITECTURE.md)\\n\\n## Overview\\n\\nThe workflows workstream adds multi-recipe orchestration to jig.
