detection.\\n/// --json forces JSON. Otherwise: piped → JSON, TTY → Human.\\npub fn detect_mode(force_json: bool) -> OutputMode {\\n    if force_json || !std::io::stdout().is_terminal() {\\n        OutputMode::Json\\n    } else {\\n        OutputMode::Human\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── JSON output ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Build the full JSON output object for `jig run`.\\npub fn format_json(results: &[OpResult], dry_run: bool, verbose: bool) -> Value {\\n    let operations: Vec<Value> = results.iter().map(|r| op_result_to_json(r, verbose)).collect();\\n    let (files_written, files_skipped) = compute_file_summaries(results);\\n\\n    serde_json::json!({\\n        \\\"dry_run\\\": dry_run,\\n        \\\"operations\\\": operations,\\n        \\\"files_written\\\": files_written.into_iter().collect::<Vec<_>>(),\\n        \\\"files_skipped\\\": files_skipped.into_iter().collect::<Vec<_>>(),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\npub(crate) fn op_result_to_json(result: &OpResult, verbose: bool) -> Value {\\n    match result {\\n        OpResult::Success { action, path, lines, location, rendered_content, scope_diagnostics } => {\\n            let mut obj = serde_json::json!({\\n                \\\"action\\\": action,\\n                \\\"path\\\": path.display().to_string(),\\n                \\\"lines\\\": lines,\\n            });\\n            if let Some(loc) = location {\\n                obj[\\\"location\\\"] = Value::String(loc.clone());\\n            }\\n            if verbose\\n                && let Some(content) = rendered_content {\\n                    obj[\\\"rendered_content\\\"] = Value::String(content.clone());\\n                }\\n            if verbose\\n                && let Some(diag) = scope_diagnostics {\\n                    let mut diag_obj = serde_json::json!({\\n                        \\\"anchor_line\\\": diag.anchor_line,\\n                        \\\"scope_start\\\": diag.scope_start,\\n                        \\\"scope_end\\\": diag.scope_end,\\n                        \\\"insertion_line\\\": diag.insertion_line,\\n                    });\\n                    if let Some(fl) = diag.find_match_line {\\n                        diag_obj[\\\"find_match_line\\\"] = Value::Number(fl.into());\\n                    }\\n                    if let Some((ref from, ref to)) = diag.position_fallback {\\n                        diag_obj[\\\"position_fallback\\\"] = serde_json::json!({\\n                            \\\"from\\\": from,\\n                            \\\"to\\\": to,\\n                        });\\n                    }\\n                    obj[\\\"scope_diagnostics\\\"] = diag_obj;\\n                }\\n            obj\\n        }\\n        OpResult::Skip { path, reason, rendered_content } => {\\n            let mut obj = serde_json::json!({\\n                \\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\",\\n                \\\"path\\\": path.display().to_string(),\\n                \\\"reason\\\": reason,\\n            });\\n            if verbose\\n                && let Some(content) = rendered_content {\\n                    obj[\\\"rendered_content\\\"] = Value::String(content.clone());\\n                }\\n            obj\\n        }\\n        OpResult::Error { path, error, rendered_content } => {\\n            serde_json::json!({\\n                \\\"action\\\": \\\"error\\\",\\n                \\\"path\\\": path.display().to_string(),\\n                \\\"what\\\": error.what,\\n                \\\"where\\\": error.where_,\\n                \\\"why\\\": error.why,\\n                \\\"hint\\\": error.hint,\\n                \\\"rendered_content\\\": rendered_content,\\n            })\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Compute files_written and files_skipped arrays.\\n/// A path appears in files_written if any operation wrote to it.\\n/// A path appears in files_skipped only if ALL operations targeting it were skipped.\\n/// Paths appear in order of first encounter.\\npub(crate) fn compute_file_summaries(results: &[OpResult]) -> (IndexSet<String>, IndexSet<String>) {\\n    // Track encounter order, writes, and errors to compute summaries.\\n    let mut encounter_order: IndexSet<String> = IndexSet::new();\\n    let mut was_written: std::collections::HashSet<String> = std::collections::HashSet::new();\\n    let mut had_error: std::collections::HashSet<String> = std::collections::HashSet::new();\\n\\n    for result in results {\\n        let path_str = result.path().display().to_string();\\n        encounter_order.insert(path_str.clone());\\n\\n        match result {\\n            OpResult::Success { .. } => {\\n                was_written.insert(path_str);\\n            }\\n            OpResult::Error { .. } => {\\n                had_error.insert(path_str);\\n            }\\n            OpResult::Skip { .. } => {}\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    let mut written: IndexSet<String> = IndexSet::new();\\n    let mut skipped: IndexSet<String> = IndexSet::new();\\n\\n    for path in &encounter_order {\\n        if was_written.contains(path) {\\n            written.insert(path.clone());\\n        } else if !had_error.contains(path) {\\n            // Only count as skipped if ALL operations were skips (no writes, no errors).\\n            skipped.insert(path.clone());\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    (written, skipped)\\n}\\n\\n/// Same as compute_file_summaries but takes references (for workflow aggregation).\\nfn compute_file_summaries_from_refs(results: &[&OpResult]) -> (IndexSet<String>, IndexSet<String>) {\\n    let mut encounter_order: IndexSet<String> = IndexSet::new();\\n    let mut was_written: std::collections::HashSet<String> = std::collections::HashSet::new();\\n    let mut had_error: std::collections::HashSet<String> = std::collections::HashSet::new();\\n\\n    for result in results {\\n        let path_str = result.path().display().to_string();\\n        encounter_order.insert(path_str.clone());\\n\\n        match result {\\n            OpResult::Success { .. } => {\\n                was_written.insert(path_str);\\n            }\\n            OpResult::Error { .. } => {\\n                had_error.insert(path_str);\\n            }\\n            OpResult::Skip { .. } => {}\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    let mut written: IndexSet<String> = IndexSet::new();\\n    let mut skipped: IndexSet<String> = IndexSet::new();\\n\\n    for path in &encounter_order {\\n        if was_written.contains(path) {\\n            written.insert(path.clone());\\n        } else if !had_error.contains(path) {\\n            skipped.insert(path.clone());\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    (written, skipped)\\n}\\n\\n// ─�� Human output ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Write human-readable output to stderr.\\npub fn format_human(results: &[OpResult], dry_run: bool, verbose: bool) {\\n    if dry_run {\\n        eprintln!(\\\"{}\\\", \\\"(dry run)\\\".dimmed());\\n    }\\n\\n    for result in results {\\n        match result {\\n            OpResult::Success { action, path, lines, location, rendered_content, scope_diagnostics } => {\\n                let action_str = action.to_string();\\n                eprint!(\\\"  {} {}\\\", action_str.green(), path.display());\\n                if let Some(loc) = location {\\n                    eprint!(\\\" ({})\\\", loc);\\n                }\\n                eprintln!(\\\" ({} lines)\\\", lines);\\n                if verbose {\\n                    if let Some(content) = rendered_content {\\n                        for line in content.lines() {\\n                            eprintln!(\\\"    {}\\\", line.dimmed());\\n                        }\\n                    }\\n                    if let Some(diag) = scope_diagnostics {\\n                        eprintln!(\\\"    {} anchor={} scope={}-{} insert={}{}\\\",\\n                            \\\"diagnostics:\\\".dimmed(),\\n                            diag.anchor_line, diag.scope_start, diag.scope_end,\\n                            diag.insertion_line,\\n                            diag.find_match_line.map(|l| format!(\\\" find={}\\\", l)).unwrap_or_default(),\\n                        );\\n                    }\\n                }\\n            }\\n            OpResult::Skip { path, reason, rendered_content } => {\\n                eprintln!(\\\"  {} {} — {}\\\", \\\"skip\\\".yellow(), path.display(), reason);\\n                if verbose\\n                    && let Some(content) = rendered_content {\\n                        for line in content.lines() {\\n                            eprintln!(\\\"    {}\\\", line.dimmed());\\n                        }\\n                    }\\n            }\\n            OpResult::Error { path, error, rendered_content } => {\\n                eprintln!(\\\"  {} {}\\\", \\\"error\\\".red(), path.display());\\n                eprintln!(\\\"    what: {}\\\", error.what);\\n                eprintln!(\\\"    where: {}\\\", error.where_);\\n                eprintln!(\\\"    why: {}\\\", error.why);\\n                eprintln!(\\\"    hint: {}\\\", error.hint);\\n                if verbose || !rendered_content.is_empty() {\\n                    eprintln!(\\\"    rendered content ({} lines):\\\", rendered_content.lines().count());\\n                    for line in rendered_content.lines() {\\n                        eprintln!(\\\"      {}\\\", line.dimmed());\\n                    }\\n                }\\n            }\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    let (written, skipped) = compute_file_summaries(results);\\n    if !written.is_empty() || !skipped.is_empty() {\\n        eprintln!();\\n        if !written.is_empty() {\\n            eprintln!(\\n                \\\"{}: {}\\\",\\n                if dry_run { \\\"would write\\\" } else { \\\"wrote\\\" },\\n                written.len()\\n            );\\n        }\\n        if !skipped.is_empty() {\\n            eprintln!(\\\"skipped: {}\\\", skipped.len());\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Workflow validation JSON ──────────────────────────────────────\\n\\npub fn build_workflow_validate_json(validation: &workflow::WorkflowValidation) -> Value {\\n    let vars = variables::vars_json(&validation.variables);\\n\\n    let steps: Vec<Value> = validation\\n        .steps\\n        .iter()\\n        .map(|step| {\\n            let mut obj = serde_json::json!({\\n                \\\"recipe\\\": step.recipe,\\n                \\\"valid\\\": step.valid,\\n                \\\"conditional\\\": step.conditional,\\n            });\\n            if let Some(ref when) = step.when {\\n                obj[\\\"when\\\"] = Value::String(when.clone());\\n            }\\n            if let Some(ref err) = step.error {\\n                obj[\\\"error\\\"] = Value::String(err.clone());\\n            }\\n            obj\\n        })\\n        .collect();\\n\\n    serde_json::json!({\\n        \\\"type\\\": \\\"workflow\\\",\\n        \\\"valid\\\": true,\\n        \\\"name\\\": validation.name,\\n        \\\"description\\\": validation.description,\\n        \\\"variables\\\": vars,\\n        \\\"steps\\\": steps,\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// ── Workflow execution JSON ──────────────────────────────────────\\n\\npub fn format_workflow_json(\\n    result: &workflow::WorkflowResult,\\n    dry_run: bool,\\n    verbose: bool,\\n    exit_code: i32,\\n) -> Value {\\n    let has_errors = result.steps.iter().any(|s| s.is_error());\\n    let status = if !has_errors || exit_code == 0 {\\n        \\\"success\\\"\\n    } else if exit_code == 3 && result.on_error == workflow::OnError::Report {\\n        \\\"partial\\\"\\n    } else {\\n        \\\"error\\\"\\n    };\\n\\n    let mut all_ops: Vec<&OpResult> = Vec::new();\\n    let steps: Vec<Value> = operations\\n                    .iter()\\n                    .map(|r| op_result_to_json(r, verbose))\\n                    .collect();\\n                let (written, skipped) = compute_file_summaries(operations);\\n                all_ops.extend(operations.iter());\\n Some(content) = rendered_content {\\n                    obj[\\\"rendered_content\\\"] = Value::String(content.clone());\\n                }\\n                obj\\n            }\\n        })\\n output ──────────────────────────────\\n\\npub fn format_workflow_human(\\n    result: &workflow::WorkflowResult,\\n    dry_run: bool,\\n    verbose: bool,\\n) {\\n    if dry_run {\\n        eprintln!(\\\"{}\\\", \\\"(dry run)\\\".dimmed());\\n    }\\n\\n    let total = result.steps.len();\\n    let mut succeeded = 0usize;\\n    let mut skipped = 0usize;\\n    let mut failed = 0usize;\\n\\n    for (i, step) in result.steps.iter().enumerate() {\\n        match step {\\n            workflow::StepResult::Success { recipe, operations } => {\\n                eprintln!(\\n                    \\\"\\\\n{} {}/{}: {}\\\",\\n                    \\\"Step\\\".bold(),\\n                    i + 1,\\n                    total,\\n                    recipe\\n                );\\n                format_human(operations, false, verbose);\\n                succeeded += 1;\\n            }\\n            workflow::StepResult::Skipped { recipe, reason } => {\\n                eprintln!(\\n                    \\\"\\\\n{} {}/{}: {} {} — {}\\\",\\n                    \\\"Step\\\".bold(),\\n                    i + 1,\\n                    total,\\n                    \\\"skip\\\".yellow(),\\n                    recipe,\\n                    reason\\n                );\\n                skipped += 1;\\n            }\\n            workflow::StepResult::Error {\\n                recipe,\\n                error,\\n                operations,\\n                rendered_content: _,\\n            } => {\\n                eprintln!(\\n                    \\\"\\\\n{} {}/{}: {} {}\\\",\\n                    \\\"Step\\\".bold(),\\n                    i + 1,\\n                    total,\\n                    \\\"error\\\".red(),\\n                    recipe\\n                );\\n                if !operations.is_empty() {\\n                    format_human(operations, false, verbose);\\n                }\\n                let se = error.structured_error();\\n                eprintln!(\\\"    what: {}\\\", se.what);\\n                eprintln!(\\\"    where: {}\\\", se.where_);\\n                eprintln!(\\\"    why: {}\\\", se.why);\\n                eprintln!(\\\"    hint: {}\\\", se.hint);\\n                failed += 1;\\n            }\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    eprintln!(\\n        \\\"\\\\n{} steps: {} succeeded, {} skipped, {} failed\\\",\\n        total, succeeded, skipped, failed\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[cfg(test)]\\nmod tests {\\n    use std::path::PathBuf;\\n\\n    use super::*;\\n    use crate::error::StructuredError;\\n\\n    fn success_result(path: &str, lines: usize) -> OpResult {\\n        OpResult::Success {\\n            action: \\\"create\\\",\\n            path: PathBuf::from(path),\\n            lines,\\n            location: None,\\n            rendered_content: Some(\\\"content\\\".into()),\\n            scope_diagnostics: None,\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    fn skip_result(path: &str, reason: &str) -> OpResult {\\n        OpResult::Skip {\\n            path: PathBuf::from(path),\\n            reason: reason.into(),\\n            rendered_content: None,\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    fn error_result(path: &str) -> OpResult {\\n        OpResult::Error {\\n            path: PathBuf::from(path),\\n            error: StructuredError {\\n                what: \\\"file already exists\\\".into(),\\n                where_: path.into(),\\n                why: \\\"conflict\\\".into(),\\n                hint: \\\"use --force\\\".into(),\\n            },\\n            rendered_content: \\\"rendered\\\".into(),\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.3: --json forces JSON output ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_3_force_json() {\\n        let mode = detect_mode(true);\\n        assert_eq!(mode, OutputMode::Json);\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.5: JSON includes operations array with correct fields ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_5_json_operations_array() {\\n        let results = vec![\\n            success_result(\\\"src/main.rs\\\", 10),\\n            skip_result(\\\"src/lib.rs\\\", \\\"skip_if_exists: true\\\"),\\n        ];\\n        let json = format_json(&results, false, false);\\n        let ops = json[\\\"operations\\\"].as_array().unwrap();\\n        assert_eq!(ops.len(), 2);\\n        assert_eq!(ops[0][\\\"action\\\"], \\\"create\\\");\\n        assert_eq!(ops[0][\\\"path\\\"], \\\"src/main.rs\\\");\\n        assert_eq!(ops[0][\\\"lines\\\"], 10);\\n        assert_eq!(ops[1][\\\"action\\\"], \\\"skip\\\");\\n        assert_eq!(ops[1][\\\"path\\\"], \\\"src/lib.rs\\\");\\n        assert!(ops[1][\\\"reason\\\"].as_str().unwrap().contains(\\\"skip_if_exists\\\"));\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.5: Error operation has all structured error fields ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_5_json_error_fields() {\\n        let results = vec![error_result(\\\"conflict.rs\\\")];\\n        let json = format_json(&results, false, false);\\n        let op = &json[\\\"operations\\\"][0];\\n        assert_eq!(op[\\\"action\\\"], \\\"error\\\");\\n        assert!(op[\\\"what\\\"].is_string());\\n        assert!(op[\\\"where\\\"].is_string());\\n        assert!(op[\\\"why\\\"].is_string());\\n        assert!(op[\\\"hint\\\"].is_string());\\n        assert!(op[\\\"rendered_content\\\"].is_string());\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.6: files_written and files_skipped arrays ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_6_file_summaries() {\\n        let results = vec![\\n            success_result(\\\"a.rs\\\", 5),\\n            skip_result(\\\"b.rs\\\", \\\"skipped\\\"),\\n            success_result(\\\"c.rs\\\", 3),\\n        ];\\n        let json = format_json(&results, false, false);\\n        let written: Vec<&str> = json[\\\"files_written\\\"].as_array().unwrap()\\n            .iter().map(|v| v.as_str().unwrap()).collect();\\n        let skipped: Vec<&str> = json[\\\"files_skipped\\\"].as_array().unwrap()\\n            .iter().map(|v| v.as_str().unwrap()).collect();\\n        assert_eq!(written, vec![\\\"a.rs\\\", \\\"c.rs\\\"]);\\n        assert_eq!(skipped, vec![\\\"b.rs\\\"]);\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.6: File written after skip → only in files_written ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_6_write_after_skip_removes_from_skipped() {\\n        let results = vec![\\n            skip_result(\\\"target.rs\\\", \\\"skipped first\\\"),\\n            success_result(\\\"target.rs\\\", 10),\\n        ];\\n        let json = format_json(&results, false, false);\\n        let written: Vec<&str> = json[\\\"files_written\\\"].as_array().unwrap()\\n            .iter().map(|v| v.as_str().unwrap()).collect();\\n        let skipped: Vec<&str> = json[\\\"files_skipped\\\"].as_array().unwrap()\\n            .iter().map(|v| v.as_str().unwrap()).collect();\\n        assert_eq!(written, vec![\\\"target.rs\\\"]);\\n        assert!(skipped.is_empty());\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.6: Order of first encounter preserved ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_6_order_preserved() {\\n        let results = vec![\\n            success_result(\\\"c.rs\\\", 1),\\n            success_result(\\\"a.rs\\\", 1),\\n            success_result(\\\"b.rs\\\", 1),\\n        ];\\n        let json = format_json(&results, false, false);\\n        let written: Vec<&str> = json[\\\"files_written\\\"].as_array().unwrap()\\n            .iter().map(|v| v.as_str().unwrap()).collect();\\n        assert_eq!(written, vec![\\\"c.rs\\\", \\\"a.rs\\\", \\\"b.rs\\\"]);\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.7: --verbose includes rendered content ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_7_verbose_includes_content() {\\n        let results = vec![success_result(\\\"file.rs\\\", 5)];\\n        let json = format_json(&results, false, true);\\n        assert!(json[\\\"operations\\\"][0][\\\"rendered_content\\\"].is_string());\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.7: Without verbose, rendered_content absent from success/skip ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_7_no_verbose_no_content() {\\n        let results = vec![success_result(\\\"file.rs\\\", 5)];\\n        let json = format_json(&results, false, false);\\n        assert!(json[\\\"operations\\\"][0].get(\\\"rendered_content\\\").is_none());\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.8: dry_run field in JSON ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_8_dry_run_json_field() {\\n        let json = format_json(&[], true, false);\\n        assert_eq!(json[\\\"dry_run\\\"], true);\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.11: dry_run boolean field ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_11_dry_run_boolean() {\\n        let json_dry = format_json(&[], true, false);\\n        assert_eq!(json_dry[\\\"dry_run\\\"], true);\\n        let json_wet = format_json(&[], false, false);\\n        assert_eq!(json_wet[\\\"dry_run\\\"], false);\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.9: Flag interactions ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_9_verbose_with_json() {\\n        let results = vec![success_result(\\\"file.rs\\\", 5)];\\n        // verbose + JSON → content in JSON\\n        let json = format_json(&results, false, true);\\n        assert!(json[\\\"operations\\\"][0][\\\"rendered_content\\\"].is_string());\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.10: Error stops execution (tested via operations array length) ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_10_error_in_operations() {\\n        let results = vec![\\n            success_result(\\\"a.rs\\\", 1),\\n            error_result(\\\"b.rs\\\"),\\n            // No more results — execution stopped.\\n        ];\\n        let json = format_json(&results, false, false);\\n        let ops = json[\\\"operations\\\"].as_array().unwrap();\\n        assert_eq!(ops.len(), 2);\\n        assert_eq!(ops[1][\\\"action\\\"], \\\"error\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-N2.1: Second run all skips ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_n2_1_all_skips() {\\n        let results = vec![\\n            skip_result(\\\"a.rs\\\", \\\"file already exists (skip_if_exists: true)\\\"),\\n            skip_result(\\\"b.rs\\\", \\\"file already exists (skip_if_exists: true)\\\"),\\n        ];\\n        let json = format_json(&results, false, false);\\n        let ops = json[\\\"operations\\\"].as_array().unwrap();\\n        assert!(ops.iter().all(|op| op[\\\"action\\\"] == \\\"skip\\\"));\\n        assert!(json[\\\"files_written\\\"].as_array().unwrap().is_empty());\\n        assert_eq!(json[\\\"files_skipped\\\"].as_array().unwrap().len(), 2);\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.1/AC-6.2: TTY detection — piped → Json ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_1_6_2_detect_mode_piped_is_json() {\\n        // In test context, stdout is piped (not a TTY) → Json mode (AC-6.2).\\n        // TTY → Human mode (AC-6.1) is verified by code inspection of detect_mode().\\n        let mode = detect_mode(false);\\n        assert_eq!(mode, OutputMode::Json);\\n    }\\n\\n    // ── AC-6.4: --quiet suppresses stderr, no effect on JSON content ──\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn ac_6_4_quiet_no_effect_on_json() {\\n        // --quiet only suppresses stderr. JSON output is identical with or without quiet.\\n        let results = vec![success_result(\\\"file.rs\\\", 5)];\\n        let json_normal = format_json(&results, false, false);\\n        let json_quiet = format_json(&results, false, false); // same call — quiet is handled by caller\\n        assert_eq!(json_normal, json_quiet);\\n    }\\n}\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.started\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_22\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc 'cat Cargo.toml'\",\"aggregated_output\":\"\",\"exit_code\":null,\"status\":\"in_progress\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_22\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc 'cat Cargo.toml'\",\"aggregated_output\":\"[package]\\nname = \\\"jig-cli\\\"\\nversion = \\\"0.1.0\\\"\\nedition = \\\"2024\\\"\\ndescription = \\\"Template rendering CLI for LLM code generation workflows\\\"\\nlicense = \\\"MIT\\\"\\n\\n[[bin]]\\nname = \\\"jig\\\"\\npath = \\\"src/main.rs\\\"\\n\\n[dependencies]\\nclap = { version = \\\"4\\\", features = [\\\"derive\\\"] }\\nheck = \\\"0.5\\\"\\nindexmap = { version = \\\"2\\\", features = [\\\"serde\\\"] }\\nminijinja = \\\"2\\\"\\npluralizer = \\\"0.4\\\"\\nregex = \\\"1\\\"\\nserde = { version = \\\"1\\\", features = [\\\"derive\\\"] }\\nserde_json = { version = \\\"1\\\", features = [\\\"preserve_order\\\"] }\\nserde_yaml = \\\"0.9\\\"\\nstrsim = \\\"0.11\\\"\\nthiserror = \\\"2\\\"\\nowo-colors = \\\"4\\\"\\n\\n[dev-dependencies]\\ninsta = \\\"1\\\"\\ntempfile = \\\"3\\\"\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_23\",\"type\":\"agent_message\",\"text\":\"I confirmed there is no `src/library` module yet and no library subcommands in the CLI, so this is net-new implementation planning. I’m pulling the `jig.md` v0.4 libraries section now to ground milestones and acceptance criteria against the product spec rather than only architecture summaries.\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.started\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_24\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"nl -ba jig.md | sed -n '1030,1415p'\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"\",\"exit_code\":null,\"status\":\"in_progress\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_24\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"nl -ba jig.md | sed -n '1030,1415p'\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"  1030\\tvars_file: .jig/defaults.json  # always merge these variables first\\n  1031\\tfilters:                     # register custom filters (as shell commands)\\n  1032\\t  copyright_header: \\\"cat .jig/copyright.txt\\\"\\n  1033\\t```\\n  1034\\t\\n  1035\\tThis is purely optional. jig works without any config file — recipes are fully self-contained.\\n  1036\\t\\n  1037\\t---\\n  1038\\t\\n  1039\\t## Custom Filters (Extensibility)\\n  1040\\t\\n  1041\\tBeyond the built-in filters, users can register custom filters as shell commands:\\n  1042\\t\\n  1043\\t```yaml\\n  1044\\t# In .jigrc.yaml or recipe.yaml\\n  1045\\tfilters:\\n  1046\\t  license_header: \\\"cat ./HEADER.txt\\\"\\n  1047\\t  format_date: \\\"date -d '{{ value }}' '+%Y-%m-%d'\\\"\\n  1048\\t```\\n  1049\\t\\n  1050\\tIn a template:\\n  1051\\t```jinja2\\n  1052\\t{{ \\\"2024-01-15\\\" | format_date }}\\n  1053\\t```\\n  1054\\t\\n  1055\\tThis runs the shell command with `{{ value }}` replaced by the filter input. Simple, composable, no plugin API to learn.\\n  1056\\t\\n  1057\\t---\\n  1058\\t\\n  1059\\t## Libraries: Reusable Recipe Collections\\n  1060\\t\\n  1061\\tIndividual recipes are useful. But the real leverage comes when someone packages a set of recipes for a specific framework and shares them. That's a **jig library** — a versioned collection of recipes, templates, and patch definitions for a domain.\\n  1062\\t\\n  1063\\t### What a Library Is\\n  1064\\t\\n  1065\\tA library is a directory (or published package) of recipes organized by concern:\\n  1066\\t\\n  1067\\t```\\n  1068\\tjig-django/\\n  1069\\t  jig-library.yaml            # library manifest\\n  1070\\t  model/\\n  1071\\t    add-field/\\n  1072\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1073\\t      templates/\\n  1074\\t        model_field.j2\\n  1075\\t        migration_hint.j2\\n  1076\\t    add-model/\\n  1077\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1078\\t      templates/\\n  1079\\t        model.py.j2\\n  1080\\t        admin.py.j2\\n  1081\\t        factories.py.j2\\n  1082\\t    add-index/\\n  1083\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1084\\t      templates/\\n  1085\\t        index_migration.j2\\n  1086\\t  service/\\n  1087\\t    add-method/\\n  1088\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1089\\t      templates/\\n  1090\\t        service_method.j2\\n  1091\\t        service_test.j2\\n  1092\\t    add-service/\\n  1093\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1094\\t      templates/\\n  1095\\t        service.py.j2\\n  1096\\t        service_test.py.j2\\n  1097\\t  view/\\n  1098\\t    add-endpoint/\\n  1099\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1100\\t      templates/\\n  1101\\t        view_method.j2\\n  1102\\t        url_pattern.j2\\n  1103\\t        schema_request.j2\\n  1104\\t        schema_response.j2\\n  1105\\t    add-view/\\n  1106\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1107\\t      templates/\\n  1108\\t        view.py.j2\\n  1109\\t        urls.py.j2\\n  1110\\t        schemas.py.j2\\n  1111\\t  schema/\\n  1112\\t    add-field/\\n  1113\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1114\\t      templates/\\n  1115\\t        msgspec_field.j2\\n  1116\\t        marshmallow_field.j2\\n  1117\\t  test/\\n  1118\\t    add-unit-test/\\n  1119\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1120\\t      templates/ ...\\n  1121\\t    add-integration-test/\\n  1122\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1123\\t      templates/ ...\\n  1124\\t    add-factory-field/\\n  1125\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1126\\t      templates/ ...\\n  1127\\t  admin/\\n  1128\\t    add-field/\\n  1129\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1130\\t      templates/ ...\\n  1131\\t    add-admin/\\n  1132\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1133\\t      templates/ ...\\n  1134\\t```\\n  1135\\t\\n  1136\\t### Library Manifest\\n  1137\\t\\n  1138\\t```yaml\\n  1139\\t# jig-library.yaml\\n  1140\\tname: django\\n  1141\\tversion: 0.3.0\\n  1142\\tdescription: Recipes for Django model/service/view development\\n  1143\\tframework: django\\n  1144\\tlanguage: python\\n  1145\\t\\n  1146\\t# Conventions this library assumes — the LLM reads these to map a project's\\n  1147\\t# actual layout to the library's expectations\\n  1148\\tconventions:\\n  1149\\t  models: \\\"{{ app }}/models/{{ model | snakecase }}.py\\\"\\n  1150\\t  services: \\\"{{ app }}/services/{{ model | snakecase }}_service.py\\\"\\n  1151\\t  views: \\\"{{ app }}/views/{{ model | snakecase }}_view.py\\\"\\n  1152\\t  schemas: \\\"{{ app }}/schemas/{{ model | snakecase }}.py\\\"\\n  1153\\t  tests: \\\"{{ app }}/tests/test_{{ model | snakecase }}.py\\\"\\n  1154\\t  factories: \\\"{{ app }}/tests/factories.py\\\"\\n  1155\\t  admin: \\\"{{ app }}/admin.py\\\"\\n  1156\\t  urls: \\\"{{ app }}/urls.py\\\"\\n  1157\\t\\n  1158\\t# Recipes in this library\\n  1159\\trecipes:\\n  1160\\t  model/add-field: \\\"Add a field to an existing Django model\\\"\\n  1161\\t  model/add-model: \\\"Scaffold a new Django model with admin, factory, and tests\\\"\\n  1162\\t  model/add-index: \\\"Add a database index to an existing model\\\"\\n  1163\\t  service/add-method: \\\"Add a method to an existing service class\\\"\\n  1164\\t  service/add-service: \\\"Scaffold a new service class with tests\\\"\\n  1165\\t  view/add-endpoint: \\\"Add an endpoint to an existing view\\\"\\n  1166\\t  view/add-view: \\\"Scaffold a new view with URL config and schemas\\\"\\n  1167\\t  schema/add-field: \\\"Add a field to an existing request/response schema\\\"\\n  1168\\t  test/add-unit-test: \\\"Generate a unit test for a service method\\\"\\n  1169\\t  test/add-integration-test: \\\"Generate an integration test for an endpoint\\\"\\n  1170\\t  test/add-factory-field: \\\"Add a field to a factory class\\\"\\n  1171\\t  admin/add-field: \\\"Add a field to admin list_display/fieldsets\\\"\\n  1172\\t  admin/add-admin: \\\"Scaffold admin registration for a model\\\"\\n  1173\\t\\n  1174\\t# Composable workflows — multi-recipe operations\\n  1175\\tworkflows:\\n  1176\\t  add-field:\\n  1177\\t    description: \\\"Add a field across the full stack (model → service → schema → view → admin → tests)\\\"\\n  1178\\t    steps:\\n  1179\\t      - recipe: model/add-field\\n  1180\\t      - recipe: service/add-method\\n  1181\\t        when: \\\"{{ update_service }}\\\"\\n  1182\\t      - recipe: schema/add-field\\n  1183\\t      - recipe: view/add-endpoint\\n  1184\\t        when: \\\"{{ update_view }}\\\"\\n  1185\\t      - recipe: test/add-factory-field\\n  1186\\t      - recipe: admin/add-field\\n  1187\\t\\n  1188\\t  add-endpoint:\\n  1189\\t    description: \\\"Add a new API endpoint (view → URL → schema → test)\\\"\\n  1190\\t    steps:\\n  1191\\t      - recipe: schema/add-field\\n  1192\\t        vars_map: { field_name: \\\"request_field\\\" }\\n  1193\\t      - recipe: view/add-endpoint\\n  1194\\t      - recipe: test/add-integration-test\\n  1195\\t\\n  1196\\t  scaffold-resource:\\n  1197\\t    description: \\\"Create a new model + service + view + admin from scratch\\\"\\n  1198\\t    steps:\\n  1199\\t      - recipe: model/add-model\\n  1200\\t      - recipe: service/add-service\\n  1201\\t      - recipe: view/add-view\\n  1202\\t      - recipe: admin/add-admin\\n  1203\\t```\\n  1204\\t\\n  1205\\t### Convention Mapping\\n  1206\\t\\n  1207\\tThe `conventions` block is crucial. It tells jig (and the LLM) where this library expects files to live. But real projects aren't always organized exactly the same way.\\n  1208\\t\\n  1209\\tA project can override conventions in its `.jigrc.yaml`:\\n  1210\\t\\n  1211\\t```yaml\\n  1212\\t# .jigrc.yaml\\n  1213\\tlibraries:\\n  1214\\t  django:\\n  1215\\t    conventions:\\n  1216\\t      models: \\\"{{ app }}/models.py\\\"              # single-file models, not per-model files\\n  1217\\t      services: \\\"{{ app }}/domain/services.py\\\"    # different service location\\n  1218\\t      schemas: \\\"{{ app }}/api/schemas.py\\\"\\n  1219\\t```\\n  1220\\t\\n  1221\\tThis lets the same library work across projects with different directory structures. The override is simple key-value — remap the path pattern, keep everything else.\\n  1222\\t\\n  1223\\t### Workflows: Multi-Recipe Operations\\n  1224\\t\\n  1225\\tA workflow chains multiple recipes together. This is the answer to \\\"I want to add a field and have it cascade through the whole stack.\\\"\\n  1226\\t\\n  1227\\t```bash\\n  1228\\t# Run a full-stack field addition\\n  1229\\tjig workflow django/add-field \\\\\\n  1230\\t  --vars '{\\\"app\\\": \\\"hotels\\\", \\\"model\\\": \\\"Reservation\\\", \\\"field_name\\\": \\\"loyalty_tier\\\", \\\"field_type\\\": \\\"CharField\\\", \\\"field_args\\\": \\\"max_length=50\\\", \\\"nullable\\\": true, \\\"update_service\\\": true, \\\"update_view\\\": false}'\\n  1231\\t```\\n  1232\\t\\n  1233\\tWorkflows support:\\n  1234\\t\\n  1235\\t| Feature | Syntax | Behavior |\\n  1236\\t|---------|--------|----------|\\n  1237\\t| **Conditional steps** | `when: \\\"{{ expr }}\\\"` | Skip this recipe if the expression is falsy |\\n  1238\\t| **Variable mapping** | `vars_map: {a: b}` | Rename variables when passing to a sub-recipe |\\n  1239\\t| **Variable overrides** | `vars: {key: value}` | Override specific variables for a sub-recipe |\\n  1240\\t| **Shared variables** | (default) | All top-level variables are passed through to every step |\\n  1241\\t| **Stop on error** | `on_error: stop` (default) | Stop the workflow if any recipe fails |\\n  1242\\t| **Continue on error** | `on_error: continue` | Skip the failed recipe and continue |\\n  1243\\t| **Report partial** | `on_error: report` | Continue but mark the workflow as partial in output |\\n  1244\\t\\n  1245\\t### Workflows vs. Includes\\n  1246\\t\\n  1247\\tThe earlier `includes` concept (in the Patches section) is now subsumed by workflows. The difference:\\n  1248\\t\\n  1249\\t- **`includes`** was static — always runs all sub-recipes\\n  1250\\t- **Workflows** are conditional, configurable, and report per-step results\\n  1251\\t\\n  1252\\tWorkflows are the right abstraction. `includes` is removed.\\n  1253\\t\\n  1254\\t### Installing Libraries\\n  1255\\t\\n  1256\\t```bash\\n  1257\\t# From a git repository\\n  1258\\tjig library add https://github.com/someone/jig-django\\n  1259\\t\\n  1260\\t# From a local directory (development / private libraries)\\n  1261\\tjig library add ./path/to/jig-django\\n  1262\\t\\n  1263\\t# List installed libraries\\n  1264\\tjig library list\\n  1265\\t\\n  1266\\t# Update a library\\n  1267\\tjig library update django\\n  1268\\t\\n  1269\\t# Remove a library\\n  1270\\tjig library remove django\\n  1271\\t```\\n  1272\\t\\n  1273\\tLibraries are installed to `~/.jig/libraries/` (global) or `.jig/libraries/` (project-local). Project-local takes precedence.\\n  1274\\t\\n  1275\\t### Library Discovery\\n  1276\\t\\n  1277\\t```bash\\n  1278\\t# List all recipes in an installed library\\n  1279\\tjig library recipes django\\n  1280\\t\\n  1281\\t# Show details for a specific recipe\\n  1282\\tjig library info django/model/add-field\\n  1283\\t\\n  1284\\t# List all workflows\\n  1285\\tjig library workflows django\\n  1286\\t```\\n  1287\\t\\n  1288\\t### Built-In vs. Community Libraries\\n  1289\\t\\n  1290\\tjig ships with zero built-in libraries. The tool is framework-agnostic. Libraries are where framework opinions live:\\n  1291\\t\\n  1292\\t| Library | Domain |\\n  1293\\t|---------|--------|\\n  1294\\t| `jig-django` | Django models, views, services, admin, DRF, msgspec |\\n  1295\\t| `jig-fastapi` | FastAPI routes, Pydantic models, dependency injection |\\n  1296\\t| `jig-rails` | Rails models, controllers, views, migrations, RSpec |\\n  1297\\t| `jig-nextjs` | Next.js pages, API routes, components, server actions |\\n  1298\\t| `jig-vue` | Vue 3 components, composables, Pinia stores, Vitest |\\n  1299\\t| `jig-react` | React components, hooks, tests, Storybook stories |\\n  1300\\t| `jig-express` | Express routes, middleware, controllers, Joi schemas |\\n  1301\\t| `jig-rust` | Rust structs, traits, impls, tests, Cargo features |\\n  1302\\t| `jig-go` | Go structs, handlers, middleware, table-driven tests |\\n  1303\\t| `jig-flutter` | Dart widgets, BLoC providers, repository pattern |\\n  1304\\t\\n  1305\\tAnyone can publish a library. There's no registry — it's just a git repo with a `jig-library.yaml`.\\n  1306\\t\\n  1307\\t### Libraries as Claude Code Plugins\\n  1308\\t\\n  1309\\tA jig library can simultaneously be a Claude Code plugin. The plugin provides skills that know how to use the library's recipes:\\n  1310\\t\\n  1311\\t```\\n  1312\\tjig-django/\\n  1313\\t  jig-library.yaml              # jig library manifest\\n  1314\\t  .claude-plugin/\\n  1315\\t    plugin.json                  # Claude Code plugin manifest\\n  1316\\t  skills/\\n  1317\\t    add-field/\\n  1318\\t      SKILL.md                   # \\\"Read the model, extract context, run jig workflow django/add-field\\\"\\n  1319\\t    add-endpoint/\\n  1320\\t      SKILL.md                   # \\\"Read the view, extract context, run jig workflow django/add-endpoint\\\"\\n  1321\\t    scaffold-resource/\\n  1322\\t      SKILL.md                   # \\\"Ask the user for model name + fields, run jig workflow django/scaffold-resource\\\"\\n  1323\\t  model/\\n  1324\\t    add-field/\\n  1325\\t      recipe.yaml\\n  1326\\t      templates/ ...\\n  1327\\t  service/ ...\\n  1328\\t  view/ ...\\n  1329\\t```\\n  1330\\t\\n  1331\\tThe skills wrap the recipes with LLM intelligence:\\n  1332\\t- The **skill** reads code, understands context, extracts variables, and decides which workflow to run\\n  1333\\t- The **recipe/workflow** does the deterministic file generation and patching\\n  1334\\t- The **library** provides the full collection, installable as one unit\\n  1335\\t\\n  1336\\tThis means a team can `jig library add jig-django` to get the CLI recipes, AND install the Claude Code plugin to get the LLM-powered skills that invoke them.\\n  1337\\t\\n  1338\\t### Project-Specific Overrides and Extensions\\n  1339\\t\\n  1340\\tTeams can extend a library with their own recipes without forking it:\\n  1341\\t\\n  1342\\t```\\n  1343\\tmy-project/\\n  1344\\t  .jig/\\n  1345\\t    libraries/\\n  1346\\t      django/          # installed library (read-only)\\n  1347\\t    overrides/\\n  1348\\t      django/\\n  1349\\t        model/\\n  1350\\t          add-field/\\n  1351\\t            templates/\\n  1352\\t              model_field.j2    # override just this one template\\n  1353\\t    extensions/\\n  1354\\t      django/\\n  1355\\t        model/\\n  1356\\t          add-audit-fields/\\n  1357\\t            recipe.yaml         # new recipe that extends the library\\n  1358\\t            templates/ ...\\n  1359\\t```\\n  1360\\t\\n  1361\\t- **Overrides** replace a single template in a library recipe (your team uses a different field style)\\n  1362\\t- **Extensions** add new recipes namespaced under the library (your team has audit field patterns unique to your codebase)\\n  1363\\t\\n  1364\\tBoth are project-local and version-controlled. The upstream library can be updated without conflicts.\\n  1365\\t\\n  1366\\t### The Vision: Framework Ecosystem\\n  1367\\t\\n  1368\\tThe end state is:\\n  1369\\t\\n  1370\\t1. **jig** is the engine — small, fast, framework-agnostic\\n  1371\\t2. **Libraries** are where the framework knowledge lives — community-maintained, versioned, overridable\\n  1372\\t3. **Claude Code plugins** wrap libraries with LLM intelligence — the skill reads code and decides what to do, the recipe handles the mechanical work\\n  1373\\t4. **Project overrides** customize libraries for your team's conventions — no forks, no divergence\\n  1374\\t\\n  1375\\tA new developer joins a Django team. They install the jig-django Claude Code plugin. Now they can say \\\"add a field called loyalty_tier to Reservation\\\" and get a consistent, team-compliant, multi-file change — without the LLM having to learn the team's patterns from scratch every time.\\n  1376\\t\\n  1377\\t---\\n  1378\\t\\n  1379\\t## Thinking Bigger: What Else Composes?\\n  1380\\t\\n  1381\\tThe Django example is the most obvious case, but the pattern applies anywhere you have layered architecture with predictable cross-cutting changes:\\n  1382\\t\\n  1383\\t### Frontend (Vue/React/Angular)\\n  1384\\t\\n  1385\\tAdding a new prop to a component:\\n  1386\\t- Component file (add prop declaration)\\n  1387\\t- Parent components (pass the new prop at call sites)\\n  1388\\t- Types file (add to interface)\\n  1389\\t- Tests (add to mock props, add assertions)\\n  1390\\t- Storybook (add to stories, add knob)\\n  1391\\t\\n  1392\\tAdding a new store field:\\n  1393\\t- Store (add state, getter, action, mutation)\\n  1394\\t- Components (add computed property or composable usage)\\n  1395\\t- API layer (add to request/response types)\\n  1396\\t- Tests (add to store tests, component tests)\\n  1397\\t\\n  1398\\t### API Layer\\n  1399\\t\\n  1400\\tAdding a new endpoint:\\n  1401\\t- Route definition\\n  1402\\t- Controller/handler\\n  1403\\t- Request validation schema\\n  1404\\t- Response serialization schema\\n  1405\\t- Middleware registration\\n  1406\\t- OpenAPI/Swagger docs\\n  1407\\t- Client SDK regeneration\\n  1408\\t- Integration tests\\n  1409\\t\\n  1410\\t### Infrastructure\\n  1411\\t\\n  1412\\tAdding a new service:\\n  1413\\t- Dockerfile\\n  1414\\t- Docker Compose service\\n  1415\\t- Process Compose config\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.started\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_25\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"\",\"exit_code\":null,\"status\":\"in_progress\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.started\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_26\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/workflows/SHARED-CONTEXT.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"\",\"exit_code\":null,\"status\":\"in_progress\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_26\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/workflows/SHARED-CONTEXT.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# SHARED-CONTEXT.md\\n\\n> Workstream: workflows\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\n\\n## Purpose\\n\\nAdd multi-recipe orchestration to jig (v0.3). Workflows chain multiple recipes into a single `jig workflow` invocation with conditional steps, variable mapping, and configurable error handling. This is the composition layer that enables cross-file operations like \\\"add a field and propagate it through model, service, schema, admin, and tests.\\\"\\n\\n## Current State\\n\\n- **Complete** (2026-04-04)\\n- All 5 phases implemented: parsing, variable resolution, execution engine, CLI command, integration tests\\n- 343 tests passing (329 unit + 2 CLI + 12 integration), `cargo clippy` clean\\n- 25 integration fixtures (18 success-path, 7 error-case)\\n- 37 unit tests in `src/workflow.rs` covering parsing, variable resolution, when evaluation, execution\\n- Code review completed — 1 critical, 4 major, 6 minor findings identified. **Review findings not yet fixed in code** (commit 76d826f only added review artifacts)\\n\\n## Decisions Made\\n\\n### D-1: Workflow files are standalone YAML, distinct from recipes\\nWorkflows have `steps:`, recipes have `files:`. Auto-detection in `jig validate` and `jig vars` checks for these keys. A file with both is an error. This keeps the format clean and avoids overloading the recipe concept.\\n\\n### D-2: Recipe paths resolve relative to the workflow file\\nConsistent with I-7 (templates live with the consumer). A workflow + its recipe subdirectories form a self-contained bundle that can be moved or shared.\\n\\n### D-3: `when` uses Jinja2 rendering + string truthiness\\nNo new expression language. Render the `when` template with workflow-level variables. The rendered string is falsy if it's empty, \\\"false\\\" (case-insensitive), or \\\"0\\\". Everything else is truthy. This covers minijinja's boolean rendering (\\\"false\\\") and zero.\\n\\n### D-4: `when` evaluates against workflow-level variables (not step-level)\\nThe condition determines \\\"should this step run at all\\\" — a workflow-level concern. vars_map and vars modify variables for the recipe, not for the condition. This prevents circular dependencies.\\n\\n### D-5: vars_map is rename, not copy\\n`{field_name: request_field}` means the step sees `request_field` but NOT `field_name`. The spec says \\\"rename.\\\" If you need both, add the original to `vars` explicitly.\\n\\n### D-6: vars_map renamings are simultaneous\\n`{a: b, b: c}` means original `a` → `b` and original `b` → `c`. Not chained (`a` → `b` → `c`). Prevents order-dependent surprises.\\n\\n### D-7: No new exit codes\\nWorkflow errors map to existing codes: 1 (validation), 2 (rendering), 3 (file ops), 4 (variables). No exit code 5+. Respects I-5 (stable exit codes).\\n\\n### D-8: Single ExecutionContext spans all steps\\nOne `base_dir`, one `force` flag, one `virtual_files` map across the entire workflow. Enables dry-run chaining where step N+1 reads files \\\"created\\\" by step N.\\n\\n### D-9: Step variable validation happens at execution time, not upfront\\nConditional steps might reference variables that only exist when the condition is true. Upfront validation would produce false errors for skipped steps.\\n\\n### D-10: No nested workflows, no inter-step variable passing\\nSteps reference recipes only (not other workflows). Variables flow from workflow to steps, not between steps. Steps communicate through the filesystem. Keeps v0.3 scope manageable.\\n\\n### D-11: Duplicate `run_recipe()` rather than refactor `cmd_run` (implementation decision)\\nRather than refactoring the working `cmd_run` pipeline in `main.rs`, implementation created a parallel `run_recipe()` in `workflow.rs` that duplicates the rendering logic. This avoided risk of breaking the existing recipe execution path during workflow development, but creates maintenance burden (review finding M4). Should be consolidated before v0.4.\\n\\n### D-12: Duplicate vars_map targets rejected at parse time (implementation decision)\\n`load_workflow()` validates that a single step's `vars_map` doesn't map two source keys to the same target. This was an open question in planning (what happens with `{a: c, b: c}`?). Rather than last-write-wins, it's now a validation error at parse time.\\n\\n## Patterns Established\\n\\n- **`run_recipe()` extracted as independent function in `workflow.rs`.** Rather than refactoring `cmd_run`, the implementation created a parallel `run_recipe()` function (`workflow.rs:551-619`) that handles recipe loading, template rendering, and operation execution without CLI concerns. This avoids touching the working `cmd_run` code path but creates code duplication (see review finding M4).\\n\\n- **`detect_file_type()` as the auto-detection entry point.** A single function (`workflow.rs:151-198`) reads YAML, checks for `steps` vs `files` keys, and returns `FileType::Workflow` or `FileType::Recipe`. Used in three places: `cmd_validate`, `cmd_vars`, and `cmd_workflow` (guard against recipe files). This pattern should be reused by libraries (v0.4) if they add a new file type.\\n\\n- **`compute_workflow_exit_code()` centralizes exit code logic.** Moved exit code computation out of the execution loop into a dedicated function in `main.rs` (lines 582-607). Takes the workflow's on_error mode and the step results, returns the correct exit code. Prevents exit-code logic from leaking into the execution engine.\\n\\n- **Workflow fixtures use step subdirectories.** Each workflow fixture has `step1/`, `step2/`, etc. directories containing full recipe bundles (recipe.yaml + templates/). The workflow.yaml references these via relative paths. This mirrors the intended real-world layout.\\n\\n- **`RawWorkflow` intermediate struct for deserialization.** Serde deserializes into `RawWorkflow` (unresolved paths), then `load_workflow()` resolves paths and validates recipes to produce the final `Workflow` struct. This separates parsing from validation — same pattern as `Recipe::load()`.\\n\\n- **Simultaneous vars_map via snapshot-remove-insert.** `resolve_step_variables()` (lines 361-401) snapshots original values, removes mapped keys, then inserts renamed values. This prevents chaining without needing a separate temporary map.\\n\\n## Known Issues / Tech Debt\\n\\n### From v0.3 code review (unfixed)\\n\\n**Critical:**\\n- **C1: `extract_rendered_from_error` returns error description, not rendered template content.** `workflow.rs:621-628` — `se.what.clone()` returns the human-readable error message as `rendered_content` in JSON output. The spec requires the actual rendered template so LLM callers can fall back to manual editing. Fix: extract from `OpResult::Error` in `partial_results` instead.\\n\\n**Major:**\\n- **M1: `format_workflow_json` status logic breaks with per-step `on_error` overrides.** `output.rs:300-302` — Only checks workflow-level `on_error`, not step-level. When a step uses `on_error: report` but workflow default is `stop`, JSON status becomes `\\\"error\\\"` instead of `\\\"partial\\\"` despite exit code 3. Fix: derive status from exit_code, not on_error mode.\\n- **M2: Workflow steps silently accept workflow YAML files as recipes.** `workflow.rs:279` — `Recipe::load` succeeds on workflow files because `files` defaults to `[]` via `#[serde(default)]` and `steps` is silently ignored. A typo referencing another workflow succeeds with 0 operations. Fix: call `detect_file_type` on each step's recipe and reject `FileType::Workflow`.\\n- **M3: Workflow early-error JSON drops multi-error variable validation.** `main.rs:477` — `e.structured_error()` returns only the first error. JSON output loses all but the first validation error; human stderr correctly shows all. Fix: use `e.structured_errors()` and emit all.\\n- **M4: `cmd_run` and `run_recipe` are divergent copies of the same rendering pipeline.** `main.rs:335-427` vs `workflow.rs:551-618` — Template rendering, path rendering, and skip_if rendering logic is duplicated with subtly different error handling. Fix: refactor `cmd_run` to call `run_recipe`.\\n\\n**Minor:**\\n- **m1: Determinism unit test is trivially weak.** `workflow.rs:1215-1233` — Compares `steps.len()` stringified, not actual output. Integration test is much stronger; this unit test adds no value.\\n- **m2: `load_workflow` reports only first validation error.** `workflow.rs:303-306` — Errors are collected but only the first is returned. Should report all.\\n- **m3: No integration test for AC-7.8** (recipe passed to `jig workflow`). Code handles it but no regression fixture exists.\\n- **m4: Early error JSON creates misleading output.** `main.rs:473-507` — Pre-execution errors produce `\\\"steps\\\": [{\\\"recipe\\\": \\\"\\\"}]` with empty recipe string.\\n- **m5: Dry-run workflow human output says \\\"wrote\\\" instead of \\\"would write\\\".** `output.rs:406,436` — `format_human(operations, false, verbose)` hardcodes `false` for `dry_run` instead of forwarding actual value.\\n- **m6: `resolve_step_variables` panics on non-object JSON.** `workflow.rs:369,394` — `.unwrap()` on `as_object_mut()`. Safe in practice (internal callers always provide objects) but the function is `pub`.\\n\\n### Pre-existing (from v0.2, still open)\\n- `write_back` silently swallows write errors in `patch.rs` and `replace.rs` — **Critical**\\n- `Position::Sorted` is a stub (`todo!()` panic) — **Critical**\\n- Byte/char index mismatch in `delimiter.rs:87` (multi-byte UTF-8 panics) — **Critical**\\n- 11 of 26 spec-required integration fixtures from replace-patch still missing\\n\\n## File Ownership\\n\\n| File | Phase | What Changed |\\n|------|-------|--------------|\\n| `src/workflow.rs` | 1-3 | **New** (1234 lines). Workflow parsing, variable resolution, when evaluation, execution engine, `run_recipe()` |\\n| `src/main.rs` | 1, 4 | **Modified.** Added `Workflow` command variant, `cmd_workflow()`, `compute_workflow_exit_code()`, auto-detection in `cmd_validate` and `cmd_vars` |\\n| `src/output.rs` | 4 | **Modified.** Added `format_workflow_json()`, `format_workflow_human()`, `build_workflow_validate_json()` |\\n| `src/recipe.rs` | — | **Unchanged.** `Recipe::load()` used directly from `workflow.rs`; no public API changes needed |\\n| `src/variables.rs` | — | **Unchanged.** Reused as-is for workflow variable validation |\\n| `src/renderer.rs` | — | **Unchanged.** Reused for `when` expression rendering |\\n| `src/operations/mod.rs` | — | **Unchanged.** Reused for operation execution within each step |\\n| `src/error.rs` | — | **Unchanged.** No new error types needed |\\n| `tests/fixtures/workflow-*` | 5 | **New.** 18 success-path fixture directories |\\n| `tests/fixtures/error-workflow-*` | 5 | **New.** 7 error-case fixture directories |\\n| `tests/integration.rs` | 5 | **Modified.** Added workflow fixture support, determinism and idempotency tests |\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_25\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# PLAN.md\\n\\n> Workstream: core-engine\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\n> Status: Complete\\n\\n## Objective\\n\\nDeliver the minimum viable jig CLI: parse recipes, validate variables, render Jinja2 templates, execute create and inject file operations, and report structured results. This is the v0.1 feature set — the smallest thing that lets an LLM call `jig run` to produce files from a recipe instead of re-deriving boilerplate.\\n\\nCovers ARCHITECTURE.md Phases A through E (skeleton, rendering, create, inject, integration tests).\\n\\n## Phases\\n\\n### Phase 1: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-1, FR-7 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-5\\n\\nBootstrap the Rust crate, wire up clap, and make recipe parsing work end-to-end. After this phase, `jig validate` and `jig vars` are functional commands.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 1.1: Cargo.toml with dependencies (serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap, thiserror, regex, indexmap)\\n- [x] 1.2: `src/error.rs` — StructuredError struct (what/where/why/hint), JigError enum wrapping StructuredError with exit code mapping (0-4)\\n- [x] 1.3: `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe, VariableDecl, VarType, FileOp structs with serde deserialization; template path resolution relative to recipe location; structural validation (missing fields, missing template files); unknown operation type detection with clear error message; custom deserialization for FileOp — use intermediate flat struct with optional fields, then validate and convert to typed enum (reject when more than one of `to`/`inject`/`replace`/`patch` is present, or none is present; if `replace` or `patch` is present, emit AC-1.10 'not supported in v0.1' error, reject when multiple inject modes (after/before/prepend/append) are specified); compile-check regex patterns in after/before fields during recipe validation\\n- [x] 1.4: `src/variables.rs` — Variable merging and type-checking scaffolding (types imported from recipe.rs, validation logic added in Phase 2)\\n- [x] 1.5: `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands, `#[command(version)]`; wire recipe parsing; map errors to exit codes; `jig validate` outputs summary to stderr (variable count, operation types); with `--json`, outputs structured JSON to stdout\\n- [x] 1.6: Unit tests for recipe parsing — valid recipe, missing fields, malformed YAML, missing template files, optional metadata\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `jig validate recipe.yaml` parses the example recipe from jig.md and exits 0\\n- `jig validate bad.yaml` exits 1 with a clear error naming the problem\\n- `jig vars recipe.yaml` outputs JSON matching the SPEC schema (type, required, default, description)\\n- Template paths resolve relative to recipe file, not cwd\\n- AC-1.1 through AC-1.15 have corresponding unit tests (AC-1.11 empty files array tested in Phase 3 when `jig run` exists)\\n- `jig validate` output includes variable names and operation types\\n- AC-N5.1 exit codes are correct for recipe validation errors\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `Cargo.toml`\\n- `src/main.rs`\\n- `src/recipe.rs`\\n- `src/variables.rs` (types only)\\n- `src/error.rs`\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- regex crate (needed at parse time for compile-checking after/before patterns)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 2: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-2, FR-3, FR-7 (partial), NFR-1 (partial)\\n\\nFull variable pipeline (parse, merge, type-check) and template rendering with all 13 built-in filters. After this phase, `jig render` works.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 2.1: `src/variables.rs` — full validation: parse JSON from --vars/--vars-file/--vars-stdin; merge with precedence (defaults < file < stdin < inline); type-check against declarations; required field enforcement; enum validation; array item type validation\\n- [x] 2.2: `src/filters.rs` — all 13 built-in filters registered with minijinja: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join\\n- [x] 2.3: `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja Environment setup; template loading from recipe-relative paths; filter registration; render with variables context; structured error on undefined variable (with \\\"did you mean?\\\" via edit distance) and syntax errors (with file + line)\\n- [x] 2.4: Wire `render` subcommand in main.rs with --vars, --vars-file, --vars-stdin, --to options. For `jig render`, create a standalone Environment with filters registered but no template directory — load the template file directly by path via `render_str()` or equivalent\\n- [x] 2.5: Unit tests for variable validation — every VarType, required missing, default fallback, enum rejection, array item mismatch, merge precedence\\n- [x] 2.6: Unit tests + insta snapshot tests for all 13 filters and template rendering (conditionals, loops, comments, raw blocks, undefined vars, syntax errors)\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- All 13 filters produce correct output per AC-3.4 through AC-3.14\\n- `jig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\"class_name\\\": \\\"BookingService\\\"}'` renders to stdout\\n- Type mismatch exits 4 with expected vs actual type (AC-2.7)\\n- Missing required variable exits 4 with variable name and hint (AC-2.5)\\n- Merge precedence: inline --vars wins over --vars-stdin wins over --vars-file wins over defaults (AC-2.4)\\n- Undefined template variable exits 2 with \\\"did you mean?\\\" hint (AC-3.17)\\n- Template syntax error exits 2 with file path and line number (AC-3.18)\\n- Same inputs produce byte-identical output across runs (AC-N1.1)\\n- `jig render template.j2 --vars '...' --to output.txt` writes to file instead of stdout (AC-7.4)\\n- Multiple validation errors accumulated and reported together (AC-2.11)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/variables.rs` (full implementation)\\n- `src/filters.rs`\\n- `src/renderer.rs`\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- heck crate for case conversions\\n- minijinja crate for template rendering\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 3: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-4, FR-6, FR-7 (complete), NFR-2 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-6\\n\\nFile creation with templated output paths, directory auto-creation, skip_if_exists, and dual-stream output (JSON stdout / human stderr). After this phase, `jig run` works for create-only recipes.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 3.1: `src/operations/mod.rs` — ExecutionContext struct (base_dir, dry_run, force, virtual_files for dry-run state); OpResult enum (Success with action/path/lines/location, Skip, Error); operation dispatch (create only initially). In dry-run mode, create ops populate virtual_files instead of writing to disk.\\n- [x] 3.2: `src/operations/create.rs` — render `to` path as template; create parent directories; write rendered content; skip_if_exists logic; --force override; --base-dir path resolution\\n- [x] 3.3: `src/output.rs` — OutputMode enum (Json/Human/Quiet); TTY auto-detection; JSON serialization of operations array + files_written/files_skipped; track per-path write/skip status for files_written and files_skipped arrays; colored human output to stderr; --verbose includes rendered content\\n- [x] 3.4: Wire `run` subcommand in main.rs with --dry-run, --json, --quiet, --force, --base-dir, --verbose; render all template contents AND templated path fields (`to`, `inject`, `skip_if`) into a Vec before executing any operation — rendering failures abort before any file is touched; execute operations in declaration order; fail-fast on operation errors — stop execution on first failure\\n- [x] 3.5: Unit tests for create operation — happy path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, templated paths, directory creation, file-already-exists error\\n- [x] 3.6: Unit tests for output formatting — JSON schema matches SPEC, human output, quiet mode, verbose mode, dry-run\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `jig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'` creates files at templated paths (AC-4.1, AC-4.2)\\n- Parent directories created automatically (AC-4.3)\\n- skip_if_exists: true skips existing files with action:\\\"skip\\\" (AC-4.4)\\n- Default (skip_if_exists: false) errors on existing file without --force (AC-4.5)\\n- --force overwrites regardless (AC-4.6)\\n- --base-dir changes output root (AC-4.7)\\n- --dry-run produces output but writes nothing (AC-6.8)\\n- JSON output when piped, human output when TTY (AC-6.1, AC-6.2)\\n- --json forces JSON (AC-6.3), --quiet suppresses non-errors (AC-6.4)\\n- Operations execute in declaration order (AC-N6.1)\\n- Second run with skip_if_exists: true reports all skips (AC-N2.1)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs`\\n- `src/operations/create.rs`\\n- `src/output.rs`\\n- `src/main.rs` (run subcommand)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- owo-colors crate for terminal coloring\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 4: Inject Operation\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-5, NFR-2 (complete), NFR-6\\n\\nContent injection into existing files via regex anchoring. All injection modes: after, before, prepend, append, with at:first/last match selection and skip_if idempotency. This completes the v0.1 operation set.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 4.1: `src/operations/inject.rs` — read target file (or from virtual_files if target created in same dry-run); skip_if string search (skip_if string search must check virtual_files content when target was created in same dry-run); regex anchor matching (after/before); at:first/at:last match selection; prepend/append modes; render inject path as template; write modified content; inject ops update virtual_files with post-injection content for subsequent operations\\n- [x] 4.2: Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\n- [x] 4.3: Unit tests for every injection mode — after (first match), after (last match), before, prepend, append, skip_if, missing target file error, regex no-match error, templated inject path\\n- [x] 4.4: Integration test: recipe with create + inject in same run (create file, then inject into it — tests ordered execution AC-N6.2)\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- after: content on line after first match (AC-5.1)\\n- before: content on line before first match (AC-5.2)\\n- prepend: content at start of file (AC-5.3)\\n- append: content at end of file (AC-5.4)\\n- at:last uses last match (AC-5.5), at:first (default) uses first (AC-5.6)\\n- skip_if: skips when string found in file, reports action:\\\"skip\\\" (AC-5.7)\\n- Regex no-match exits 3 with pattern, file path, hint (AC-5.8)\\n- Missing target file exits 3 (AC-5.9)\\n- Inject path renders as template (AC-5.11)\\n- Create-then-inject in same recipe works (AC-N6.2)\\n- Second run with skip_if shows all skips — no duplicate content (AC-N2.2)\\n- Inject success reports action:\\\"inject\\\" with path, location, line count (AC-5.10)\\n- at field ignored when prepend/append specified; after/before without regex exits 1 (AC-5.12, AC-5.13)\\n- Invalid regex pattern in after/before exits 1 (AC-5.14)\\n- Multiple inject modes (after+before etc.) specified exits 1 (AC-5.15)\\n- --force has no effect on inject operations (AC-5.16)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/inject.rs`\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- regex crate\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 5: Integration Test Framework\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\n\\nFixture-based integration test harness that makes adding new test cases a matter of adding a directory. Snapshot tests for all output formats. This phase validates the entire v0.1 pipeline end-to-end.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 5.1: Test harness in `tests/integration.rs` — fixture discovery; copy existing/ to temp dir; run jig as subprocess; diff output against expected/; assert JSON output against expected_output.json; assert exit code against expected_exit_code\\n- [x] 5.2: Fixtures for create operations — simple create, templated path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, directory creation\\n- [x] 5.3: Fixtures for inject operations — after/before/prepend/append, at:first/at:last, skip_if\\n- [x] 5.4: Fixtures for error cases — missing vars, bad type, missing template, missing target file, regex no-match, malformed YAML, file exists without force\\n- [x] 5.5: Fixtures for combined operations — create + inject in one recipe, multi-file recipe, idempotency (run twice)\\n- [x] 5.6: insta snapshot tests for JSON output format, human output format, error message format\\n- [x] 5.7: Determinism test — run same recipe twice, assert byte-identical output (AC-N1.1)\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `cargo test` runs all unit + integration + snapshot tests green\\n- Every operation mode has at least one fixture\\n- Every error exit code (1-4) has at least one fixture\\n- Adding a new test case requires only a new directory, no code changes. Integration fixtures are auto-discovered from directories (no code changes to add tests). Spec-level unit tests are named functions in `#[cfg(test)]` modules (e.g., `spec::fr1::ac_1_1`). The two layers serve different purposes.\\n- Idempotency fixture: second run produces all skips, no file changes\\n- Binary has no dynamic dependencies beyond system libc (verified via `otool -L` on macOS, `ldd` on Linux)\\n- Every `TEST-*` ID in SPEC.md has a corresponding test function named `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}` or `spec::nfr{N}::ac_n{N}_{M}`\\n- Error fixtures assert that error JSON contains `what`, `where`, `why`, and `hint` fields (at least one fixture per exit code)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `tests/integration.rs`\\n- `tests/fixtures/` (directory tree)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- insta crate for snapshot testing\\n- assert_cmd or similar for subprocess testing (or raw std::process::Command)\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\n- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\n- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\n\\n## Key Decisions\\n\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\n| Operations as enum, not trait objects | `FileOp` enum with match dispatch | Only 2 variants in v0.1 (create, inject). Trait abstraction adds complexity for no benefit until user-defined operations exist, which isn't planned. (ARCHITECTURE D-4) |\\n| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages (ARCHITECTURE D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\n| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable (ARCHITECTURE D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\n| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism (ARCHITECTURE D-5, I-1) |\\n| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate, good Display/Error trait impls |\\n| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\n| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\n| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling. Acceptable dependency for correctness. |\\n| \\\"did you mean?\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) against declared variable names | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Hand-rolling edit distance serves I-10 (graceful degradation) — the error tells the caller exactly what to fix. |\\n| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves, even for ones minijinja provides (replace, indent, join) | We control exact behavior. No surprises from upstream minijinja changes. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\n| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. Templates reference variables not created files. (D-1) |\\n| Extra variables pass through | Don't error or warn on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness here breaks real usage. |\\n| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later operations may depend on earlier ones (e.g., inject targets a prior create). (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\n\\n## Risks / Open Questions\\n\\nAll resolved — see Resolved Questions below.\\n\\n## Resolved Questions\\n\\n- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\n- **\\\"did you mean?\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names. minijinja doesn't provide this. Serves I-10.\\n- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. We own the behavior, upstream changes don't affect us. Serves I-1.\\n- **TTY detection**: `std::io::IsTerminal` (Rust 1.70+) works correctly. No external crate needed.\\n- **stdin variable reading**: Documented as single-purpose per invocation. Non-interactive design (I-3) makes this a non-issue.\\n- **Large file handling for inject**: Reads entire file into memory. Acceptable for source files. Documented as known limitation.\\n- **pluralizer irregular plurals**: Works for common irregulars (person→people, child→children). Acceptable coverage.\\n- **minijinja filter override**: Overriding built-in filters works. All 13 registered as custom filters.\\n\\n## Completion Summary\\n\\n- **Completed**: 2026-04-04\\n- **Tests**: 191 total (177 unit + 2 CLI integration + 12 fixture integration)\\n- **Review**: Dual-agent review cycle (Claude + Codex) came back clean — 0 Critical, 0 Major findings\\n- **All 5 phases delivered**: validate, vars, render, run (create + inject), full integration test suite\\n- **CLI commands working**: `jig validate`, `jig vars`, `jig render`, `jig run`\\n\\n## Execution Order\\n\\n```\\nPhase 1 ──► Phase 2 ──► Phase 3 ──► Phase 4 ──► Phase 5\\nskeleton     vars+render  create+output inject     integration\\n                                                    tests\\n```\\n\\nEach phase produces a working, testable artifact. No phase depends on \\\"wiring up later.\\\" The CLI grows incrementally:\\n- After Phase 1: `jig validate`, `jig vars`\\n- After Phase 2: + `jig render`\\n- After Phase 3: + `jig run` (create only)\\n- After Phase 4: + `jig run` (create + inject) — **v0.1 complete**\\n- After Phase 5: Full test coverage validates the release\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.started\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_27\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,300p'
