AC-N3.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries to run | TEST-N3.2 |\\\\n188:| AC-N4.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL include what, where, why, and hint fields in every error message | TEST-N4.1 |\\\\n189:| AC-N4.2 | Event | WHEN a file operation fails (exit code 3), the system SHALL include the rendered template content in the error output so the caller can fall back to manual editing | TEST-N4.2 |\\\\n190:| AC-N4.3 | Event | WHEN a template rendering error occurs, the system SHALL report the template file path and the line number of the error | TEST-N4.3 |\\\\n191:| AC-N4.4 | Event | WHEN a variable validation error occurs, the system SHALL report the variable name, expected type, and actual value provided | TEST-N4.4 |\\\\n200:| AC-N5.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL exit with code 0 on success, 1 for recipe validation errors, 2 for template rendering errors, 3 for file operation errors, 4 for variable validation errors | TEST-N5.1 |\\\\n201:| AC-N5.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL use the exit code of the first error encountered when multiple errors occur | TEST-N5.2 |\\\\n210:| AC-N6.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL execute file operations in the order they appear in the recipe's `files` array | TEST-N6.1 |\\\\n211:| AC-N6.2 | Event | WHEN an inject operation targets a file created by an earlier create operation in the same recipe, the system SHALL find and operate on the newly created file | TEST-N6.2 |\\\\n364:| FR-1 | AC-1.1 through AC-1.12 | spec::fr1::* | PENDING |\\\\n365:| FR-2 | AC-2.1 through AC-2.11 | spec::fr2::* | PENDING |\\\\n366:| FR-3 | AC-3.1 through AC-3.18 | spec::fr3::* | PENDING |\\\\n367:| FR-4 | AC-4.1 through AC-4.8 | spec::fr4::* | PENDING |\\\\n368:| FR-5 | AC-5.1 through AC-5.12 | spec::fr5::* | PENDING |\\\\n369:| FR-6 | AC-6.1 through AC-6.9 | spec::fr6::* | PENDING |\\\\n370:| FR-7 | AC-7.1 through AC-7.6 | spec::fr7::* | PENDING |\\\\n371:| NFR-1 | AC-N1.1, AC-N1.2 | spec::nfr1::* | PENDING |\\\\n372:| NFR-2 | AC-N2.1, AC-N2.2 | spec::nfr2::* | PENDING |\\\\n373:| NFR-3 | AC-N3.1, AC-N3.2 | spec::nfr3::* | PENDING |\\\\n374:| NFR-4 | AC-N4.1 through AC-N4.4 | spec::nfr4::* | PENDING |\\\\n375:| NFR-5 | AC-N5.1, AC-N5.2 | spec::nfr5::* | PENDING |\\\\n376:| NFR-6 | AC-N6.1, AC-N6.2 | spec::nfr6::* | PENDING |\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-115748.md:29:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_13\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md | sed -n '171,180p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"   171\\\\t#### NFR-3: Single Static Binary\\\\n   172\\\\t\\\\n   173\\\\tZero runtime dependencies. One binary that runs on macOS and Linux without Python, Node, JVM, or any shared libraries.\\\\n   174\\\\t\\\\n   175\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   176\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   177\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   178\\\\t| AC-N3.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL compile to a single static binary with no runtime dependencies | TEST-N3.1 |\\\\n   179\\\\t| AC-N3.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries to run | TEST-N3.2 |\\\\n   180\\\\t\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-115748.md:71:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_34\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md | sed -n '7,12p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"     7\\\\t## Overview\\\\n     8\\\\t\\\\n     9\\\\tThe core engine workstream delivers the minimum pipeline that makes jig useful: parse a recipe, validate variables, render templates, execute create and inject operations, and report results. This is the v0.1 feature set — a working CLI that an LLM can call to produce files from recipes.\\\\n    10\\\\t\\\\n    11\\\\tOut of scope for this workstream: replace operations, patch operations, scope detection, workflows, libraries, scan/infer/check, custom filters via shell, .jigrc.yaml config.\\\\n    12\\\\t\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-115748.md:82:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_40\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md | sed -n '186,190p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"   186\\\\t## Dependencies\\\\n   187\\\\t\\\\n   188\\\\t- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\\\n   189\\\\t- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\\\n   190\\\\t\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-115748.md:93:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_46\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"**Critical** (blocks implementation):\\\\n- `--quiet` behavior is self-contradictory: [AC-6.4](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:128) says suppress all output except errors, [AC-6.9](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:133) says `--quiet` does not affect `--json` stdout, while [I-8](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/INVARIANTS.md:43) and [ARCH output responsibilities](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:275) say quiet suppresses normal output. One behavior must be chosen.\\\\n- Output-stream invariant scope is unclear/contradictory: [I-8](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/INVARIANTS.md:43) says TTY mode writes nothing to stdout, but [AC-7.2](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:143) and [AC-7.3](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:144) require stdout output for `vars`/`render`. Define whether I-8 is `run`-only or global with exceptions.\\\\n- Static-binary requirement is inconsistent with validation gate: [I-6](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/INVARIANTS.md:33) and [NFR-3](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:171) require no shared libraries, but [Phase 5 validation](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:174) allows system libc dynamic dependency. Acceptance target is currently impossible to evaluate consistently.\\\\n- Type ownership is contradictory across docs: [ARCH `recipe.rs` owns `VariableDecl`](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:69), [ARCH Phase A says `variables.rs` owns VariableDecl types](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:380), and [PLAN 1.3 vs 1.4](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:24) duplicate ownership. Pick one canonical module before coding.\\\\n\\\\n**Major** (should fix before implementation):\\\\n- Dry-run virtual file behavior is underspecified in implementation plan: [AC-6.8](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:132) requires injects to see earlier dry-run creates, but [Phase 4 inject milestone](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:126) and [ExecutionContext shape](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:137) do not include virtual FS state.\\\\n- EARS format violation/atomicity issue in [AC-5.12](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:116): one row mixes a ubiquitous rule and an unwanted `IF` rule. Split into two ACs.\\\\n- Undefined-variable hint scope is contradictory: [AC-3.17](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:79) requires “did you mean?” hints, but [AC-7.3 note](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:144) says `render` has no hints. Constrain AC-3.17 to `run` or define separate behavior by command.\\\\n- Phase gates miss explicit coverage for v0.1 ACs: [Phase 1 validation](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:34) only cites AC-1.1..1.9 (omits AC-1.10..1.12), and [Phase 4 validation](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:131) omits AC-5.10 and AC-5.12.\\\\n- Behavior “extra variables pass through silently” is decided in plan/context ([PLAN decision](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:206), [SHARED-CONTEXT](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SHARED-CONTEXT.md:32)) but not specified in [FR-2 ACs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:37). This is externally visible and should be in SPEC.\\\\n- Missing failure-mode requirements: FR-2 has no explicit malformed JSON error AC for [--vars/--vars-file/--vars-stdin](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:44), and FR-5 lacks invalid-regex-compilation behavior (only no-match is defined in [AC-5.8](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:112)).\\\\n- Determinism risk in output ordering: [AC-6.6](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:130) requires unique path arrays but not deterministic order, which can violate byte-identical output in [AC-N1.1](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:158).\\\\n\\\\n**Minor** (nice to fix):\\\\n- CLI docs mismatch: [AC-7.6](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:147) includes `--version`, but [Public API options list](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:223) omits it.\\\\n- Testability ambiguity in [AC-7.1](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:142): “report whether valid, listing variables and operations” has no output format contract.\\\\n- Line-count semantics are not defined for [AC-4.8](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:96) and [AC-5.10](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:114) (rendered lines vs modified lines).\\\\n- Architecture doc drift: [ARCH `JigError(String)` snippet](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:284) conflicts with structured-error model in [SPEC data model](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:313).\\\\n\\\\n**Strengths** (what's done well):\\\\n- Strong requirement structure: all 90 ACs have IDs and test trace IDs in [SPEC AC tables](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:24).\\\\n- Clear FR/NFR coverage table in [Requirement Traceability](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:360).\\\\n- Phase-based execution is concrete and incremental in [PLAN phases](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:15).\\\\n- Invariant-driven decisions (render-before-execute, recipe-relative templates, ordered ops) are consistently reinforced across [INVARIANTS](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/INVARIANTS.md:1), [ARCHITECTURE D-1/D-2](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:341), and [PLAN key decisions](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:191).\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-synthesized-20260403-121146.md:1:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"result\\\",\\\"subtype\\\":\\\"success\\\",\\\"is_error\\\":false,\\\"duration_ms\\\":183757,\\\"duration_api_ms\\\":183712,\\\"num_turns\\\":6,\\\"result\\\":\\\"Good. I've verified the key cross-references. Now I can produce the synthesized review.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n# Synthesized Plan Review: core-engine\\\\n\\\\n## Critical (blocks implementation)\\\\n\\\\n### C-1: `--quiet`/`--verbose`/`--json` flag interaction is internally contradictory\\\\n**[AGREED — A:Major, B:Critical]**\\\\n\\\\n`SPEC.md:AC-6.9` says \\\\\\\"--verbose is ignored when --quiet is set\\\\\\\" but also \\\\\\\"--json and --verbose are independent.\\\\\\\" For `--json --quiet --verbose`, the spec contradicts itself: --quiet kills --verbose, but --verbose should independently add rendered content to JSON. `INVARIANTS.md:I-8` adds a third voice: \\\\\\\"--quiet suppresses stderr only; stdout behavior is determined by --json independently.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Rewrite AC-6.9 to cleanly separate streams. `--quiet` suppresses stderr only and has no effect on stdout/JSON content. `--verbose` adds rendered content to both human (stderr) and JSON (stdout) output independently. In human mode with `--quiet`, `--verbose` has no visible effect (stderr is suppressed). In JSON mode with `--quiet`, `--verbose` still adds rendered content to JSON stdout.\\\\n\\\\n### C-2: OpResult::Success missing `location` field required by AC-5.10\\\\n**[B-only — verified]**\\\\n\\\\n`SPEC.md:AC-5.10` requires inject success to report `\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\"` with `path`, `location`, and `lines`. The JSON Output Schema shows `\\\\\\\"location\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"after:^# fixtures\\\\\\\"` for inject. But `OpResult::Success` in both `SPEC.md:309` and `ARCHITECTURE.md:144` only has `{ action, path, lines }` — no `location` field. An implementer following the data model will fail to satisfy the AC and JSON schema.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Add `location: Option<String>` to `OpResult::Success` in SPEC.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, and PLAN.md Phase 3 milestone 3.1. Populated for inject operations, `None` for create.\\\\n\\\\n### C-3: I-8 vs AC-7.4 — `jig render` output destination conflict\\\\n**[B-only — verified]**\\\\n\\\\n`INVARIANTS.md:I-8` says \\\\\\\"jig vars and jig render always write to stdout regardless of TTY mode.\\\\\\\" `SPEC.md:AC-7.4` says \\\\\\\"WHEN jig render is invoked with --to <path>, the system SHALL write the rendered output to the specified file instead of stdout.\\\\\\\" Direct contradiction — I-8 says always stdout, AC-7.4 says sometimes file.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Amend I-8 to: \\\\\\\"jig vars and jig render write to stdout by default regardless of TTY mode. jig render --to <path> redirects output to a file.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n### C-4: PRD contradicts ARCHITECTURE/SPEC on render ordering\\\\n**[A-only — valid]**\\\\n\\\\n`jig.md:789-802` Core Flow says render-per-operation: \\\\\\\"For each file operation: a. Render the template... b. Execute the operation.\\\\\\\" `ARCHITECTURE.md:D-1` and `SPEC.md:Rendering Lifecycle` say render-all-upfront. The plan correctly chose upfront (fail-fast), but the PRD disagrees. If someone reads jig.md later and \\\\\\\"corrects\\\\\\\" the code to match, they'll break the fail-fast guarantee.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Add a note in jig.md at the Core Flow section: \\\\\\\"Note: the implementation renders all templates upfront before any operation executes (see ARCHITECTURE.md D-1). This section describes the logical flow, not execution order.\\\\\\\" Or amend jig.md directly.\\\\n\\\\n### C-5: No AC for inject mode mutual exclusivity\\\\n**[AGREED — A:Critical, B:Minor]**\\\\n\\\\nNo AC specifies what happens when a recipe has both `after` and `before`, or `after` and `prepend`, on the same inject operation. The `InjectMode` enum in the data model makes this impossible at the type level, but YAML deserialization must reject the ambiguity — and there's no AC requiring that.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Add under FR-5: \\\\\\\"IF an inject operation specifies more than one of after/before/prepend/append, the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the conflicting fields.\\\\\\\" Add to PLAN Phase 1 milestone 1.3 for custom deserialization.\\\\n\\\\n### C-6: No AC for `to`/`inject` field mutual exclusivity\\\\n**[A-only — valid]**\\\\n\\\\nPLAN milestone 1.3 says \\\\\\\"reject when both `to` and `inject` are present, or neither.\\\\\\\" This validation has no corresponding AC in SPEC.md — a future reviewer could remove the check.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Add two ACs under FR-1: \\\\\\\"IF a file operation contains both `to` and `inject` fields, SHALL exit code 1 reporting ambiguity.\\\\\\\" \\\\\\\"IF a file operation contains neither `to` nor `inject`, SHALL exit code 1 reporting missing field.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n### C-7: AC-5.14 exit code vs pipeline stage mismatch\\\\n**[B-only — valid]**\\\\n\\\\n`AC-5.14` classifies invalid inject regex as exit code 1 (recipe validation). But regex compilation happens during inject execution (PLAN Phase 4), not recipe parsing (Phase 1). If exit code 1 means recipe validation, the regex must be validated during parsing — but the current architecture doesn't do this.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Either (a) move regex pattern compilation to recipe parsing in Phase 1 milestone 1.3, making exit code 1 correct, or (b) change AC-5.14 to exit code 3 (file operation error) since it occurs during execution. Option (a) is better — catch bad regexes early.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Major (should fix before implementation)\\\\n\\\\n### M-1: `skip_if` rendering not explicit in AC-5.7\\\\n**[A-only]**\\\\n\\\\nAC-5.7 says \\\\\\\"WHEN skip_if: 'string' is specified and the string exists in the target file.\\\\\\\" The Rendering Lifecycle section clarifies skip_if is rendered, but the AC itself could be read as searching for the literal YAML value. For `skip_if: \\\\\\\"{{ class_name }}\\\\\\\"`, an implementer reading only the AC could search for `{{ class_name }}` literally.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Reword AC-5.7: \\\\\\\"WHEN skip_if is specified, the system SHALL render it as a Jinja2 template, then search for the rendered string in the target file. If found, skip and report action: 'skip'.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n### M-2: `--force` undefined for inject operations\\\\n**[A-only]**\\\\n\\\\nFR-4 defines `--force` for create (AC-4.6: overwrite regardless of skip_if_exists). FR-5 never mentions `--force` for inject. Does --force bypass `skip_if`? Injecting duplicate content is far more destructive than overwriting a file.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Add AC under FR-5: \\\\\\\"The system SHALL not apply --force to inject operations. The --force flag only affects create operations (skip_if_exists override).\\\\\\\" Or if --force should bypass skip_if on inject, specify that explicitly with appropriate warnings.\\\\n\\\\n### M-3: `--vars-file` with invalid JSON content has no AC\\\\n**[AGREED — A:Major, B:Minor]**\\\\n\\\\nAC-2.13 covers invalid inline JSON. AC-2.14 covers nonexistent file. No AC for a valid file path containing invalid JSON — the more common error case.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Add: \\\\\\\"IF --vars-file points to a file containing invalid JSON, the system SHALL exit with code 4 with a parse error identifying the file and the JSON error location.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n### M-4: `indent(4)` behavior contradicts Jinja2 convention\\\\n**[A-only]**\\\\n\\\\nAC-3.13 says `indent(4)` SHALL indent each line including the first. Jinja2/minijinja convention: `indent(4)` skips the first line. Since jig registers all filters itself this is implementable, but it will confuse anyone familiar with Jinja2.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Either (a) match Jinja2 convention — first line NOT indented by default — and update AC-3.13, or (b) keep current behavior but add a prominent note in SPEC.md and future user docs about the divergence.\\\\n\\\\n### M-5: Dry-run virtual state incomplete for inject-after-inject\\\\n**[A-only]**\\\\n\\\\nAC-6.8 says creates populate virtual file state and injects use it. But if op 1 creates file A, op 2 injects into A, and op 3 injects into A — op 3 needs the post-op-2 state for skip_if and anchoring. The spec only says creates populate virtual state.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Add to AC-6.8: \\\\\\\"Inject operations SHALL update the virtual file state with post-injection content so subsequent operations in the same dry-run see the cumulative result.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n### M-6: \\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\" hint source inconsistency\\\\n**[B-only]**\\\\n\\\\nAC-3.17 says hints against \\\\\\\"provided variable context\\\\\\\" keys. PLAN Key Decisions and SHARED-CONTEXT say hints against \\\\\\\"declared variable names.\\\\\\\" These differ when extra variables are passed through (AC-2.12 allows undeclared keys).\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** For `jig run` (recipe context), suggest against declared variable names (the recipe defines the contract). For `jig render` (no recipe, per AC-7.3), suggest against provided variable keys. Update PLAN and SHARED-CONTEXT to distinguish these cases.\\\\n\\\\n### M-7: Fail-fast on operation errors not specified in SPEC\\\\n**[B-only]**\\\\n\\\\nPLAN Key Decisions says \\\\\\\"stop on first operation failure.\\\\\\\" But SPEC AC-6.5 describes an `operations` array that reads like all operations are reported. An implementer reading only the SPEC could run all operations and collect results.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Add AC under FR-6: \\\\\\\"WHEN a file operation fails, the system SHALL stop execution immediately and not execute subsequent operations. The operations array SHALL contain results for executed operations only.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n### M-8: Rendered content in errors is unconditional, not verbose-only\\\\n**[B-only]**\\\\n\\\\nI-4 and NFR-4/AC-N4.2 require `rendered_content` in file operation errors unconditionally. But AC-6.7 (\\\\\\\"--verbose includes rendered content in output\\\\\\\") and PLAN Phase 3 milestone 3.3 could be read as rendered content only appearing with --verbose.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Clarify: `rendered_content` in OpResult::Error is always present regardless of --verbose (per I-4). `--verbose` adds rendered content to *success* output. These are orthogonal.\\\\n\\\\n### M-9: No AC for zero variable sources\\\\n**[A-only]**\\\\n\\\\nNo AC covers when none of --vars/--vars-file/--vars-stdin are provided. If the recipe has all-optional variables with defaults, this should work.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Add: \\\\\\\"WHEN no variable sources are provided, the system SHALL use an empty object as input and apply recipe defaults.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n### M-10: NFR-3 wording conflict — \\\\\\\"zero dependencies\\\\\\\" vs system C library\\\\n**[B-only]**\\\\n\\\\nNFR-3 heading says \\\\\\\"zero runtime dependencies... no shared libraries.\\\\\\\" AC-N3.1 allows \\\\\\\"system C library.\\\\\\\" PLAN Phase 5 uses otool/ldd checks that acknowledge dynamic linkage exists.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Update NFR-3 description to: \\\\\\\"No runtime dependencies beyond the system C library.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n### M-11: Phase 1 validation criteria overstated for AC-1.11\\\\n**[B-only]**\\\\n\\\\nPhase 1 claims AC-1.1 through AC-1.13 are unit-tested, but AC-1.11 (empty `files: []` exits 0 with empty operations array) is run-output behavior requiring `jig run`, which doesn't exist until Phase 3.\\\\n\\\\n**Fix:** Move AC-1.11 testing to Phase 3, or clarify that `jig validate` handles the empty-files case in Phase 1 and the exit-0-with-empty-array behavior is tested in Phase 3.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Minor (nice to fix)\\\\n\\\\n### m-1: AC-2.11 phrasing is vacuous\\\\n**[A-only]** \\\\\\\"Exit code of the first error encountered\\\\\\\" — all variable errors are exit code 4. Simplify to: \\\\\\\"SHALL accumulate all variable validation errors and report them together with exit code 4.\\\\\\\" Also add explicit aggregation test to PLAN Phase 2 milestones (B flagged this gap).\\\\n\\\\n### m-2: No AC for `--version`\\\\n**[A-only]** AC-7.6 lists --version as a global option but no AC tests it. Add: \\\\\\\"WHEN --version is specified, SHALL print version string and exit 0.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n### m-3: PLAN Phase 1 milestone 1.3 incomplete for inject mode deserialization\\\\n**[A-only]** after/before/prepend/append mutual exclusivity validation isn't mentioned in milestone 1.3's custom deserialization. Call it out explicitly.\\\\n\\\\n### m-4: `jig vars` output format ambiguity\\\\n**[A-only]** AC-7.2 says include \\\\\\\"type, required, default, description.\\\\\\\" Should `required: false` be shown? Should `default` be omitted when absent? Specify field omission rules.\\\\n\\\\n### m-5: `jig validate` output content vague\\\\n**[A-only]** AC-7.1 says \\\\\\\"listing variables and operations.\\\\\\\" jig.md shows checkmarks and counts. Specify what \\\\\\\"listing\\\\\\\" means — structured vs summary.\\\\n\\\\n### m-6: `pluralizer` crate risk understated\\\\n**[A-only]** Obscure crate, last updated 2022. Pin version and add snapshot tests for known irregulars (person/people, child/children) in Phase 2, not as an afterthought.\\\\n\\\\n### m-7: Test count vs AC count mismatch\\\\n**[A-only]** Execution plan claims 79 acceptance criteria. SPEC has 98 (84 functional + 14 non-functional). Correct the count.\\\\n\\\\n### m-8: ARCHITECTURE external crate list stale\\\\n**[B-only]** Missing thiserror, indexmap, pluralizer, strsim, insta from the dependency map. Update to match PLAN/SHARED-CONTEXT decisions.\\\\n\\\\n### m-9: AC-3.13 and AC-6.9 bundle multiple assertions\\\\n**[B-only]** Reduces atomic testability. Split AC-3.13 (indent first line + indent(4, first=false)) and AC-6.9 (three separate flag interaction rules) into separate ACs.\\\\n\\\\n### m-10: Test naming enforcement has no mechanism\\\\n**[AGREED — A:m-4, B:Major]** SPEC says tests should be `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}` but nothing verifies coverage. Add a script that parses SPEC.md AC IDs and checks test function existence.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Strengths (confirmed by both reviewers)\\\\n\\\\n- **EARS format rigorously applied** — all 98 ACs have IDs, type classifications, and TEST-* trace references. Both reviewers independently verified syntactic correctness.\\\\n- **Rendering Lifecycle section** — explicitly listing which fields are rendered as templates and which are not prevents \\\\\\\"should this be dynamic?\\\\\\\" debates during implementation.\\\\n- **Error design with rendered content (I-4, I-10)** — including rendered content in errors so the LLM caller never re-derives is a genuine differentiator. Both reviewers called this out as the right trade-off.\\\\n- **Fixture-based integration test design** — \\\\\\\"adding a test = adding a directory\\\\\\\" scales well. The fixture format (recipe, vars, templates, existing, expected) is complete.\\\\n- **Phased delivery with working artifacts at each gate** — each phase produces a demoable CLI. No \\\\\\\"skeleton now, wire later\\\\\\\" patterns.\\\\n- **Traceability matrix** connecting requirements to criteria to tests, enabling mechanical completeness verification.\\\\n- **Key Decisions table with rationale** — captures the \\\\\\\"why\\\\\\\" (IndexMap for ordering, extra variables pass-through, fail-fast) so decisions aren't revisited without context.\\\\n- **Strong invariant alignment** — PLAN and implementation decisions consistently trace back to INVARIANTS.md for determinism (I-1), idempotency (I-2), ordered execution (I-9), and recipe-relative templates have skipped files\\\\\\\\nfiles_skipped: {:?}\\\\\\\",\\\\n        skipped\\\\n    );\\\\n}\\\\n\\\\n// ── Snapshot tests ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n\\\\n/// Snapshot let pretty = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap();\\\\n    insta::assert_snapshot!(\\\\\\\"json_output_create\\\\\\\", pretty);\\\\n}\\\\n\\\\n/// Snapshot test: JSON output format for an inject operation.\\\\n#[test]\\\\nfn snapshot_json_output_inject() {\\\\n    let fixture = fixtures_dir().join(\\\\\\\"inject-after\\\\\\\");\\\\n    let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap();\\\\n skip.\\\\n#[test]\\\\nfn snapshot_json_output_skip() {\\\\n    let fixture = fixtures_dir().join(\\\\\\\"inject-skip-if\\\\\\\");\\\\n    let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap();\\\\n    let work_dir = tmp.path();\\\\n    setup_existing(&fixture, work_dir);\\\\n\\\\n    let (_, stdout, _) = run_fixture(&fixture, work_dir);\\\\n    let json = normalize_json(&stdout, work_dir);\\\\n    let pretty = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap();\\\\n    insta::assert_snapshot!(\\\\\\\"json_output_skip\\\\\\\", pretty);\\\\n}\\\\n\\\\n/// Snapshot test: JSON output for a file-operation error (file exists without force).\\\\n#[test]\\\\nfn snapshot_json_output_error() {\\\\n    let fixture = fixtures_dir().join(\\\\\\\"error-file-exists\\\\\\\");\\\\n    let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap();\\\\n    let work_dir = tmp.path();\\\\n    setup_existing(&fixture, work_dir);\\\\n\\\\n    let (code, stdout, _) = run_fixture(&fixture, work_dir);\\\\n    assert_eq!(code, 3);\\\\n    let json = normalize_json(&stdout, work_dir);\\\\n    let pretty = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap();\\\\n    insta::assert_snapshot!(\\\\\\\"json_output_error\\\\\\\", pretty);\\\\n}\\\\n\\\\n/// Snapshot test: stderr error message for missing required variable.\\\\n#[test]\\\\nfn snapshot_error_missing_var() {\\\\n    let fixture = fixtures_dir().join(\\\\\\\"error-missing-vars\\\\\\\");\\\\n    let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap();\\\\n    let work_dir = tmp.path();\\\\n\\\\n    let (code, _, stderr) = run_fixture(&fixture, work_dir);\\\\n    assert_eq!(code, 4);\\\\n    insta::assert_snapshot!(\\\\\\\"error_missing_var\\\\\\\", stderr);\\\\n}\\\\n\\\\n/// Snapshot test: stderr error message for malformed YAML.\\\\n#[test]\\\\nfn snapshot_error_malformed_yaml() {\\\\n    let fixture = fixtures_dir().join(\\\\\\\"error-malformed-yaml\\\\\\\");\\\\n    let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap();\\\\n    let work_dir = tmp.path();\\\\n\\\\n    let (code, _, stderr) = run_fixture(&fixture, work_dir);\\\\n    assert_eq!(code, 1);\\\\n    // Normalize the path in stderr to be fixture-relative for stable snapshots.\\\\n    let normalized = stderr.replace(\\\\n        &fixture.join(\\\\\\\"recipe.yaml\\\\\\\").display().to_string(),\\\\n        \\\\\\\"<recipe-path>\\\\\\\",\\\\n    );\\\\n    insta::assert_snapshot!(\\\\\\\"error_malformed_yaml\\\\\\\", normalized);\\\\n}\\\\n\\\\n/// Snapshot test: combined create+inject JSON output.\\\\n#[test]\\\\nfn snapshot_json_output_combined() {\\\\n    let fixture = fixtures_dir().join(\\\\\\\"combined-create-inject\\\\\\\");\\\\n    let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap();\\\\n    let work_dir = tmp.path();\\\\n\\\\n    let (_, stdout, _) = run_fixture(&fixture, work_dir);\\\\n    let json = normalize_json(&stdout, work_dir);\\\\n    let pretty = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap();\\\\n    insta::assert_snapshot!(\\\\\\\"json_output_combined\\\\\\\", pretty);\\\\n}\\\\n\\\\n// ── Static binary check ──────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n\\\\n/// AC-N3.1: Binary has no dynamic dependencies beyond system libc.\\\\n#[test]\\\\nfn binary_no_extra_dynamic_deps() {\\\\n    let binary = jig_bin();\\\\n\\\\n    if cfg!(target_os = \\\\\\\"macos\\\\\\\") {\\\\n        let output = Command::new(\\\\\\\"otool\\\\\\\")\\\\n            .args([\\\\\\\"-L\\\\\\\", &binary.display().to_string()])\\\\n            .output()\\\\n            .expect(\\\\\\\"otool failed\\\\\\\");\\\\n        let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);\\\\n        // On macOS, only system libraries should appear.\\\\n        for line in stdout.lines().skip(1) {\\\\n            let line = line.trim();\\\\n            if line.is_empty() {\\\\n                continue;\\\\n            }\\\\n            assert!(\\\\n                line.contains(\\\\\\\"/usr/lib/\\\\\\\") || line.contains(\\\\\\\"/System/\\\\\\\"),\\\\n                \\\\\\\"unexpected dynamic dependency: {}\\\\\\\",\\\\n                line\\\\n            );\\\\n        }\\\\n    } else if cfg!(target_os = \\\\\\\"linux\\\\\\\") {\\\\n        let output = Command::new(\\\\\\\"ldd\\\\\\\")\\\\n            .arg(binary.display().to_string())\\\\n            .output()\\\\n            .expect(\\\\\\\"ldd failed\\\\\\\");\\\\n        let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);\\\\n        for line in stdout.lines() {\\\\n            let line = line.trim();\\\\n            if line.is_empty() || line.contains(\\\\\\\"statically linked\\\\\\\") {\\\\n                continue;\\\\n            }\\\\n            // Allow libc, libpthread, libdl, libm, libgcc, ld-linux\\\\n            let allowed = [\\\\n                \\\\\\\"libc.\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"libpthread.\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"libdl.\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"libm.\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"libgcc_s.\\\\\\\",\\\\n                \\\\\\\"ld-linux\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"linux-vdso\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"librt.\\\\\\\",\\\\n            ];\\\\n            assert!(\\\\n                allowed.iter().any(|a| line.contains(a)),\\\\n                \\\\\\\"unexpected dynamic dependency: {}\\\\\\\",\\\\n                line\\\\n            );\\\\n        }\\\\n    }\\\\n}\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-synthesized-20260403-180424.md:1:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"result\\\",\\\"subtype\\\":\\\"success\\\",\\\"is_error\\\":false,\\\"duration_ms\\\":201520,\\\"duration_api_ms\\\":201496,\\\"num_turns\\\":7,\\\"result\\\":\\\"Now I have enough verified context. Here's the synthesized review:\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n# Synthesized Adversarial Review: core-engine Workstream\\\\n\\\\n## Critical (blocks implementation)\\\\n\\\\n**C-1: `--verbose` cannot be implemented with the current data model.**\\\\nBoth reviewers independently identified this gap. `OpResult::Success` (SPEC.md:318) carries `action, path, lines, location` but no `rendered_content`. AC-6.7 and AC-6.9 require `--verbose` to include rendered template content for *all* operations — success and skip, not just errors. The `format_output` signature (SPEC.md:281) only receives `&[OpResult]`, so the rendered strings from the upfront render pass have no path to the formatter.\\\\n**Fix:** Add `rendered_content: Option<String>` to `OpResult::Success` and `OpResult::Skip`. Populate it when `--verbose` is set. Update the `format_output` signature or the `OpResult` enum in SPEC.md's Data Model section.\\\\n\\\\n**C-2: `--quiet` semantics are contradictory across documents.**\\\\nCodex caught this; verified against all sources. The *authoritative ACs* are internally consistent (AC-6.4: \\\\\\\"suppress all stderr output\\\\\\\"; AC-6.9: \\\\\\\"suppresses stderr only\\\\\\\"; INVARIANTS.md:43: \\\\\\\"suppresses stderr only\\\\\\\"). But the *informal descriptions* say different things — ARCHITECTURE.md:278 says \\\\\\\"suppress all output except errors\\\\\\\" and SPEC.md:245 CLI help says \\\\\\\"Suppress non-error output\\\\\\\". An implementer reading the CLI help description would build the wrong thing.\\\\n**Fix:** Align ARCHITECTURE.md:278 to \\\\\\\"suppress all stderr output\\\\\\\" and SPEC.md:245 to \\\\\\\"--quiet  Suppress stderr output\\\\\\\". The ACs are correct — fix the prose.\\\\n\\\\n## Major (should fix before implementation)\\\\n\\\\n**M-1: `replace`/`patch` fields in FileOp flat-struct deserialization will cause silent serde failures instead of clean \\\\\\\"not supported\\\\\\\" errors.**\\\\nReview A flagged this (C-1 + M-5). The PLAN calls for an intermediate flat struct with `to` and `inject` as `Option<_>` fields, but `jig.md` recipes use `replace`, `between`, `pattern`, `fallback`, and `patch` fields. If the flat struct doesn't include these as `Option<_>`, serde_yaml will either silently ignore them (with `deny_unknown_fields` off) or hard-fail (with it on) — neither produces the clean AC-1.10 error \\\\\\\"unknown operation type 'replace' — not supported in v0.1\\\\\\\". Additionally, AC-1.14 only covers `to`+`inject` ambiguity but not the case where `replace` or `patch` appears alone (AC-1.15 would erroneously fire).\\\\n**Fix:** In PLAN Phase 1.3 and SPEC AC-1.14/AC-1.15, expand the flat struct to include `replace: Option<String>` and `patch: Option<String>`. Validation logic: exactly one of `{to, inject, replace, patch}` must be present. If `replace` or `patch` is the one present, emit the AC-1.10 error. Update AC-1.14 to cover \\\\\\\"more than one of to/inject/replace/patch\\\\\\\" and AC-1.15 to cover \\\\\\\"none of to/inject/replace/patch\\\\\\\".\\\\n\\\\n**M-2: Variable-source optionality contradicts the CLI interface block.**\\\\nCodex caught this. AC-2.16 (SPEC.md:62) says \\\\\\\"WHEN no variable sources are provided... the system SHALL use an empty object.\\\\\\\" But the CLI interface (SPEC.md:236-237) shows `--vars '<json>'` without brackets for both `render` and `run`, implying it's required.\\\\n**Fix:** Change SPEC.md:236 to `jig render <template> [--to <path>] [--vars '<json>']` and SPEC.md:237 to `jig run <recipe> [--vars '<json>'] [options]`. In clap, make `--vars` optional.\\\\n\\\\n**M-3: `jig render` has no code path for creating a minijinja Environment without recipe context.**\\\\nReview A flagged this (C-3). AC-7.3 says `jig render` operates \\\\\\\"without recipe context.\\\\\\\" But the renderer (PLAN Phase 2.3) sets up the Environment with recipe-relative template loading and filter registration. Who creates the Environment for `jig render`? The internal interface (SPEC.md:275) assumes an `Environment` already exists.\\\\n**Fix:** Add to PLAN Phase 2.4: \\\\\\\"For `jig render`, create a standalone Environment with filters registered but no template directory — load the template file directly by path. The `render_string()` path in the renderer handles this.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**M-4: `indent` filter override may be blocked by minijinja.**\\\\nReview A flagged this (M-1). AC-3.13 specifies `indent(4)` indents all lines including the first, diverging from Jinja2/minijinja convention. PLAN says \\\\\\\"register all 13 filters ourselves.\\\\\\\" But minijinja may reject registering a filter named `indent` if it's a built-in.\\\\n**Fix:** Add to PLAN Phase 2.3 or Risks: \\\\\\\"Verify minijinja allows overriding built-in `indent` filter. If not, use `set_unknown_method_callback` or disable the built-in before registering ours.\\\\\\\" This needs a spike before Phase 2 implementation.\\\\n\\\\n**M-5: `skip_if` search must check virtual_files during dry-run, not just the filesystem.**\\\\nReview A flagged this (C-2). AC-5.7 says skip_if searches the target file for the rendered string. AC-6.8 says dry-run inject reads from virtual_files. But PLAN Phase 4.1 says \\\\\\\"read target file (or from virtual_files)\\\\\\\" for the inject content but doesn't mention skip_if also needing to search virtual file content.\\\\n**Fix:** In PLAN Phase 4.1, add explicit note: \\\\\\\"skip_if string search must check virtual_files content when the target was created in the same dry-run, not just the on-disk file.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**M-6: `jig validate` output contract is unspecified.**\\\\nBoth reviewers flagged this. AC-7.1 says \\\\\\\"report whether it is valid, listing variables and operations.\\\\\\\" But no AC specifies the output format (JSON/human), destination (stdout/stderr), or whether `--json` affects it. Phase 1 validation criteria only check exit codes, not output.\\\\n**Fix:** Add AC-7.1a: \\\\\\\"WHEN `jig validate` succeeds, the system SHALL output a summary to stderr listing variable count and operation count. WHEN `--json` is specified, output structured JSON to stdout instead.\\\\\\\" Add to Phase 1 validation criteria: \\\\\\\"validate output includes variable names and operation types.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**M-7: `--force --dry-run` interaction unspecified.**\\\\nReview A flagged this (M-6). What does dry-run report for a create with `skip_if_exists: true` when `--force` is also set? Force should win — dry-run should report \\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\" (what force would do), not \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\".\\\\n**Fix:** Add to AC-6.8 or as new AC-6.8a: \\\\\\\"WHEN `--dry-run` and `--force` are both specified, create operations SHALL report `action: create` for existing files (reflecting what --force would do).\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**M-8: Inject has no AC for permission-denied write failures.**\\\\nCodex caught this. AC-4.9 covers create permission errors (exit 3), but FR-5 has no equivalent. Inject writes modified content back to the file and can also fail on permissions.\\\\n**Fix:** Add AC-5.9a: \\\\\\\"IF writing the modified file content fails due to permissions, the system SHALL exit with code 3 with the path, permission error, and rendered content.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**M-9: FR-5 completion criteria in PLAN omit AC-5.15 and AC-5.16.**\\\\nCodex caught this. Phase 4 validation criteria (PLAN.md:149-150) mention AC-5.12 and AC-5.13 in passing but don't explicitly check AC-5.15 (multiple inject modes) or AC-5.16 (--force doesn't apply to inject).\\\\n**Fix:** Add to Phase 4 validation criteria: \\\\\\\"Multiple inject modes specified exits 1 (AC-5.15)\\\\\\\" and \\\\\\\"--force has no effect on inject operations (AC-5.16)\\\\\\\".\\\\n\\\\n**M-10: Test strategy is internally inconsistent.**\\\\nCodex caught this. PLAN.md:180 says \\\\\\\"Adding a new test case requires only a new directory, no code changes\\\\\\\" but PLAN.md:183 says \\\\\\\"every TEST-* has a corresponding test function named `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}`.\\\\\\\" These can't both be true — either tests are auto-discovered from directories or each has a named function.\\\\n**Fix:** Clarify: integration fixtures are auto-discovered (no code changes), while spec-level unit tests ARE named functions in source files. The two layers serve different purposes and don't need to be unified. Reword PLAN.md:183 to clarify that spec test functions cover unit-level ACs, while fixture directories cover integration-level ACs.\\\\n\\\\n**M-11: `regex` and `indexmap` crates missing from Phase 1 dependency list.**\\\\nReview A flagged both (M-4, m-7). Phase 1.3 requires compile-checking regex patterns and Phase 1 uses IndexMap for variable ordering, but Phase 1 milestone 1.1 lists only \\\\\\\"serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap, thiserror.\\\\\\\"\\\\n**Fix:** Add `regex` and `indexmap` to PLAN Phase 1 milestone 1.1 dependency list.\\\\n\\\\n**M-12: Stale execution plan conflicts with canonical planning state.**\\\\nBoth reviewers flagged this (Review A m-6, Codex major). `claude-plan-20260402-223619.md` says \\\\\\\"79 acceptance criteria\\\\\\\" (actual: 106), lists 3 \\\\\\\"HUMAN DECISION NEEDED\\\\\\\" items that are already decided in SHARED-CONTEXT.md.\\\\n**Fix:** Either delete the stale execution plan or add a header marking it superseded by PLAN.md. Do not let it be treated as authoritative.\\\\n\\\\n## Minor (nice to fix)\\\\n\\\\n**m-1: AC-2.11 \\\\\\\"exit code of the first error encountered\\\\\\\" is meaningless.**\\\\nBoth reviewers flagged this. All variable validation errors produce exit code 4, so \\\\\\\"first error encountered\\\\\\\" adds nothing. The clause appears designed for a future that doesn't exist.\\\\n**Fix:** Simplify to \\\\\\\"The system SHALL accumulate all variable validation errors and report them together with exit code 4.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**m-2: `dry_run` boolean in JSON output schema has no AC.**\\\\nReview A flagged this (M-2). The JSON schema example (SPEC.md:253) includes `\\\\\\\"dry_run\\\\\\\": false` at the top level, but no AC requires it.\\\\n**Fix:** Add AC-6.5a: \\\\\\\"The JSON output SHALL include a top-level `dry_run` boolean field reflecting whether `--dry-run` was specified.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**m-3: `files_skipped` per-path tracking is non-trivial, not called out in PLAN.**\\\\nReview A flagged this (m-2). AC-6.6 requires \\\\\\\"A path appears in `files_skipped` only if ALL operations targeting it were skipped\\\\\\\" — this needs per-path state tracking across operations.\\\\n**Fix:** Add to PLAN Phase 3.3 or 3.1: \\\\\\\"Track per-path write/skip status. A path enters files_written if any op wrote to it. A path enters files_skipped only if every op targeting it was skipped.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**m-4: Partial-write recovery guidance overstates `--force`.**\\\\nCodex caught this. SPEC.md:368 says \\\\\\\"re-run with `--force` to overwrite\\\\\\\" as recovery, but AC-5.16 explicitly says `--force` doesn't apply to inject. Recovery via `--force` only works for create-related partial writes.\\\\n**Fix:** Amend SPEC.md:368 to: \\\\\\\"Recovery: for partial writes involving create operations, re-run with `--force`. For inject failures, use the rendered content from the error output to manually apply the operation.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**m-5: AC-6.8 and AC-6.9 each bundle multiple independent behaviors.**\\\\nCodex caught this. AC-6.8 covers dry-run output format, virtual file creation, virtual file reads by inject, AND virtual state accumulation — all in one AC. This makes failure localization harder.\\\\n**Fix:** Consider splitting AC-6.8 into AC-6.8a (dry-run writes no files), AC-6.8b (virtual file state from creates), AC-6.8c (inject reads virtual state). Low priority — the current form is testable, just coarse.\\\\n\\\\n**m-6: Exit code ownership for `--vars` JSON parse errors is ambiguous.**\\\\nReview A flagged this (M-3). AC-2.13 says invalid JSON in `--vars` exits with code 4 (variable validation). But clap could catch malformed `--vars` at the CLI parse stage. The pipeline ordering (AC-N5.2) implies variable validation owns this, but implementation may differ.\\\\n**Fix:** Add a note to AC-2.13 or PLAN Phase 2.2: \\\\\\\"JSON parse errors in `--vars`/`--vars-file`/`--vars-stdin` are classified as variable validation errors (exit 4), not CLI parse errors. Parse the JSON string in the variable validation stage, not in clap.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**m-7: Path rendering timing not explicit in PLAN.**\\\\nReview A flagged this (M-8). PLAN Phase 3.4 says \\\\\\\"render all templates into a `Vec<(FileOp, String)>`\\\\\\\" — this is template *content*. But `to` and `inject` paths also contain Jinja2 expressions and need rendering. The SPEC's Rendering Lifecycle section says they're rendered fields, but the PLAN doesn't say when.\\\\n**Fix:** Amend PLAN Phase 3.4: \\\\\\\"Render all template contents AND all templated path fields (`to`, `inject`, `skip_if`) upfront before executing any operation.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**m-8: NFR-3 wording is slightly contradictory.**\\\\nCodex caught this. AC-N3.1 says \\\\\\\"single static binary with no runtime dependencies beyond the system C library\\\\\\\" while AC-N3.2 says \\\\\\\"not require... shared libraries.\\\\\\\" Linking against libc IS a shared library dependency.\\\\n**Fix:** Amend AC-N3.2 to: \\\\\\\"The system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries beyond the system C library to run.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**m-9: `python_type` filter in jig.md PRD example is not in the 13 built-in filters.**\\\\nReview A flagged this (m-1). The PRD patch example uses `{{ field_type | python_type }}` but this is a library-specific filter. Won't affect v0.1 (patch is out of scope) but could confuse readers.\\\\n**Fix:** No code change needed. Optionally add a note to SHARED-CONTEXT.md: \\\\\\\"PRD example recipes reference library-specific filters like `python_type` that are out of scope for v0.1.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**m-10: No AC covers combining all three variable sources simultaneously.**\\\\nReview A flagged this (m-8). AC-2.4 defines merge precedence for multiple sources, but no fixture tests the three-way merge (`--vars-file` + `--vars-stdin` + `--vars`).\\\\n**Fix:** Add a fixture in Phase 5.4 that provides all three sources and asserts the precedence chain.\\\\n\\\\n## Strengths (confirmed by both reviewers)\\\\n\\\\n- **EARS format coverage is thorough.** All 106 AC rows have consistent ID naming, type classification, and TEST-* traceability. No orphaned rows.\\\\n- **Render-then-execute (D-1) is the right architecture.** Prevents partial writes from template failures, simplifies operations, and ensures rendered content is always available for error messages.\\\\n- **Fixture-based integration tests** — \\\\\\\"adding a test = adding a directory\\\\\\\" is well-designed for a CLI tool.\\\\n- **Dry-run virtual file state** is well-specified. AC-6.8's requirement for cumulative virtual state across operations means dry-run output actually matches what a real run would produce.\\\\n- **Error design with rendered content (I-4, I-10)** is the key differentiator for LLM callers. Including rendered content in file operation errors means the LLM never re-derives what to insert.\\\\n- **Extra variables pass through silently** — pragmatically correct for LLM callers that construct variable objects with extra fields.\\\\n- **`--force` correctly scoped to create only (AC-5.16)** — force-injecting would bypass idempotency and produce duplicate content.\\\\n- **Phased incremental build** with working artifacts at each gate — each phase is independently demoable and testable.\\\\n- **Invariant alignment** is solid across determinism (I-1), ordered execution (I-9), recipe-relative templates (I-7), and non-interactive JSON workflow (I-3).\\\",\\\"stop_reason\\\":\\\"end_turn\\\",\\\"session_id\\\":\\\"7f635780-1d9e-47d7-87c6-1c769fe27191\\\",\\\"total_cost_usd\\\":0.8015329999999999,\\\"usage\\\":{\\\"input_tokens\\\":5,\\\"cache_creation_input_tokens\\\":77760,\\\"cache_read_input_tokens\\\":182816,\\\"output_tokens\\\":8964,\\\"server_tool_use\\\":{\\\"web_search_requests\\\":0,\\\"web_fetch_requests\\\":0},\\\"service_tier\\\":\\\"standard\\\",\\\"cache_creation\\\":{\\\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\\\":77760,\\\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\\\":0},\\\"inference_geo\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"iterations\\\":[],\\\"speed\\\":\\\"standard\\\"},\\\"modelUsage\\\":{\\\"claude-opus-4-6[1m]\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":5,\\\"outputTokens\\\":8964,\\\"cacheReadInputTokens\\\":182816,\\\"cacheCreationInputTokens\\\":77760,\\\"webSearchRequests\\\":0,\\\"costUSD\\\":0.8015329999999999,\\\"contextWindow\\\":1000000,\\\"maxOutputTokens\\\":64000}},\\\"permission_denials\\\":[],\\\"terminal_reason\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"fast_mode_state\\\":\\\"off\\\",\\\"uuid\\\":\\\"bdc15d75-af40-4709-9319-5e6c0a5f2297\\\"}\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-114234.md:10:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_3\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md'\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"     1\\\\t# SPEC.md\\\\n     2\\\\t\\\\n     3\\\\t> Workstream: core-engine\\\\n     4\\\\t> Last updated: 2026-04-02\\\\n     5\\\\t> Scope: v0.1 (Phases A-D from ARCHITECTURE.md)\\\\n     6\\\\t\\\\n     7\\\\t## Overview\\\\n     8\\\\t\\\\n     9\\\\tThe core engine workstream delivers the minimum pipeline that makes jig useful: parse a recipe, validate variables, render templates, execute create and inject operations, and report results. This is the v0.1 feature set — a working CLI that an LLM can call to produce files from recipes.\\\\n    10\\\\t\\\\n    11\\\\tOut of scope for this workstream: replace operations, patch operations, scope detection, workflows, libraries, scan/infer/check, custom filters via shell, .jigrc.yaml config.\\\\n    12\\\\t\\\\n    13\\\\t## Requirements\\\\n    14\\\\t\\\\n    15\\\\t### Functional Requirements\\\\n    16\\\\t\\\\n    17\\\\t#### FR-1: Recipe Parsing\\\\n    18\\\\t\\\\n    19\\\\tDeserialize a YAML recipe file into a validated internal representation. Resolve template paths relative to the recipe file location. Reject structurally invalid recipes with clear errors.\\\\n    20\\\\t\\\\n    21\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n    22\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n    23\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n    24\\\\t| AC-1.1 | Event | WHEN a valid recipe YAML is provided, the system SHALL parse it into a Recipe struct containing name, description, variables, and files | TEST-1.1 |\\\\n    25\\\\t| AC-1.2 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares variables with type, required, default, description, values (enum), and items (array), the system SHALL parse all fields into the VariableDecl struct | TEST-1.2 |\\\\n    26\\\\t| AC-1.3 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares `create` file operations with template, to, and skip_if_exists fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Create | TEST-1.3 |\\\\n    27\\\\t| AC-1.4 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares `inject` file operations with template, inject, after/before/prepend/append, at, and skip_if fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Inject | TEST-1.4 |\\\\n    28\\\\t| AC-1.5 | Unwanted | IF the recipe YAML is malformed (invalid YAML syntax), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and an error message identifying the parse failure location | TEST-1.5 |\\\\n    29\\\\t| AC-1.6 | Unwanted | IF a required field is missing from the recipe (e.g., files with no template), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and name the missing field | TEST-1.6 |\\\\n    30\\\\t| AC-1.7 | Event | WHEN a recipe references template files, the system SHALL resolve those paths relative to the recipe file location, not the working directory | TEST-1.7 |\\\\n    31\\\\t| AC-1.8 | Unwanted | IF a referenced template file does not exist at the resolved path, the system SHALL exit with code 1 and report which template is missing and where it looked | TEST-1.8 |\\\\n    32\\\\t| AC-1.9 | Event | WHEN the recipe has optional metadata fields (name, description), the system SHALL accept recipes with or without them | TEST-1.9 |\\\\n    33\\\\t\\\\n    34\\\\t#### FR-2: Variable Validation\\\\n    35\\\\t\\\\n    36\\\\tAccept variables as JSON from multiple sources, merge them with defined precedence, and type-check against recipe declarations.\\\\n    37\\\\t\\\\n    38\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n    39\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n    40\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n    41\\\\t| AC-2.1 | Event | WHEN `--vars` is provided with a JSON string, the system SHALL parse it as the variable input | TEST-2.1 |\\\\n    42\\\\t| AC-2.2 | Event | WHEN `--vars-file` is provided with a path to a JSON file, the system SHALL read and parse the file as variable input | TEST-2.2 |\\\\n    43\\\\t| AC-2.3 | Event | WHEN `--vars-stdin` is provided, the system SHALL read JSON from stdin as variable input | TEST-2.3 |\\\\n    44\\\\t| AC-2.4 | Event | WHEN multiple variable sources are provided, the system SHALL merge with precedence: recipe defaults < vars-file < vars-stdin < inline --vars | TEST-2.4 |\\\\n    45\\\\t| AC-2.5 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared as `required: true` and no value is provided (after merging), the system SHALL exit with code 4 naming the missing variable and providing a hint | TEST-2.5 |\\\\n    46\\\\t| AC-2.6 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared with a `default` and no value is provided, the system SHALL use the default value | TEST-2.6 |\\\\n    47\\\\t| AC-2.7 | Unwanted | IF a provided variable value does not match its declared type (e.g., string given for number, object given for array), the system SHALL exit with code 4 with expected vs actual type | TEST-2.7 |\\\\n    48\\\\t| AC-2.8 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared as `type: enum` with `values: [a, b, c]`, the system SHALL reject values not in the allowed set with exit code 4 | TEST-2.8 |\\\\n    49\\\\t| AC-2.9 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared as `type: array` with `items: string`, the system SHALL validate that each array element matches the item type | TEST-2.9 |\\\\n    50\\\\t| AC-2.10 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accept all six variable types: string, number, boolean, array, object, enum | TEST-2.10 |\\\\n    51\\\\t\\\\n    52\\\\t#### FR-3: Template Rendering\\\\n    53\\\\t\\\\n    54\\\\tRender Jinja2 templates using minijinja with registered built-in filters. Templates are loaded from recipe-relative paths.\\\\n    55\\\\t\\\\n    56\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n    57\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n    58\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n    59\\\\t| AC-3.1 | Event | WHEN a template is rendered with a variables context, the system SHALL substitute all `{{ variable }}` expressions with their values | TEST-3.1 |\\\\n    60\\\\t| AC-3.2 | Event | WHEN a template contains `{% if %}` / `{% else %}` / `{% endif %}` blocks, the system SHALL evaluate them correctly against the variable values | TEST-3.2 |\\\\n    61\\\\t| AC-3.3 | Event | WHEN a template contains `{% for item in list %}` loops, the system SHALL iterate and render the body for each element | TEST-3.3 |\\\\n    62\\\\t| AC-3.4 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL register and support all 13 built-in filters: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join | TEST-3.4 |\\\\n    63\\\\t| AC-3.5 | Event | WHEN `snakecase` is applied to \\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.5 |\\\\n    64\\\\t| AC-3.6 | Event | WHEN `camelcase` is applied to \\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"bookingService\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.6 |\\\\n    65\\\\t| AC-3.7 | Event | WHEN `pascalcase` is applied to \\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.7 |\\\\n    66\\\\t| AC-3.8 | Event | WHEN `kebabcase` is applied to \\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"booking-service\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.8 |\\\\n    67\\\\t| AC-3.9 | Event | WHEN `replace` is applied as `\\\\\\\"a.b.c\\\\\\\" \\\\\\\\| replace('.', '/')`, the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"a/b/c\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.9 |\\\\n    68\\\\t| AC-3.10 | Event | WHEN `pluralize` is applied to \\\\\\\"hotel\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.10 |\\\\n    69\\\\t| AC-3.11 | Event | WHEN `singularize` is applied to \\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"hotel\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.11 |\\\\n    70\\\\t| AC-3.12 | Event | WHEN `quote` is applied to \\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce `\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\"` (with literal quotes) | TEST-3.12 |\\\\n    71\\\\t| AC-3.13 | Event | WHEN `indent(4)` is applied to a multiline string, the system SHALL indent each line by 4 spaces | TEST-3.13 |\\\\n    72\\\\t| AC-3.14 | Event | WHEN `join(\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\")` is applied to `[\\\\\\\"a\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"b\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"c\\\\\\\"]`, the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\"a, b, c\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.14 |\\\\n    73\\\\t| AC-3.15 | Event | WHEN `{# comment #}` appears in a template, the system SHALL strip it from the output | TEST-3.15 |\\\\n    74\\\\t| AC-3.16 | Event | WHEN `{% raw %}...{% endraw %}` appears in a template, the system SHALL output the content literally without interpreting Jinja2 syntax | TEST-3.16 |\\\\n    75\\\\t| AC-3.17 | Unwanted | IF a template references an undefined variable, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and include a \\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\" hint when a close match exists | TEST-3.17 |\\\\n    76\\\\t| AC-3.18 | Unwanted | IF a template has a Jinja2 syntax error, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and report the template file path and line number | TEST-3.18 |\\\\n    77\\\\t\\\\n    78\\\\t#### FR-4: Create Operation\\\\n    79\\\\t\\\\n    80\\\\tRender a template and write it to a new file path. Support templated output paths, automatic directory creation, and skip-if-exists idempotency.\\\\n    81\\\\t\\\\n    82\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n    83\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n    84\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n    85\\\\t| AC-4.1 | Event | WHEN a create operation executes, the system SHALL render the template and write the result to the path specified in `to` | TEST-4.1 |\\\\n    86\\\\t| AC-4.2 | Event | WHEN the `to` path contains Jinja2 expressions (e.g., `tests/{{ module \\\\\\\\| replace('.', '/') }}/test_{{ class_name \\\\\\\\| snakecase }}.py`), the system SHALL render the path before writing | TEST-4.2 |\\\\n    87\\\\t| AC-4.3 | Event | WHEN parent directories in the `to` path do not exist, the system SHALL create them automatically | TEST-4.3 |\\\\n    88\\\\t| AC-4.4 | Event | WHEN `skip_if_exists: true` and the target file already exists, the system SHALL skip the operation and report `\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\"` with a reason | TEST-4.4 |\\\\n    89\\\\t| AC-4.5 | Unwanted | IF `skip_if_exists: false` (default) and the target file already exists and `--force` is not set, the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting the conflict | TEST-4.5 |\\\\n    90\\\\t| AC-4.6 | Event | WHEN `--force` is set and the target file already exists, the system SHALL overwrite the file regardless of skip_if_exists | TEST-4.6 |\\\\n    91\\\\t| AC-4.7 | Event | WHEN `--base-dir` is set, the system SHALL resolve `to` paths relative to the base directory instead of the working directory | TEST-4.7 |\\\\n    92\\\\t| AC-4.8 | Event | WHEN a create operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\"` with the path and line count | TEST-4.8 |\\\\n    93\\\\t\\\\n    94\\\\t#### FR-5: Inject Operation\\\\n    95\\\\t\\\\n    96\\\\tRender a template and insert the result into an existing file at a location determined by regex pattern matching. Support idempotency via skip_if.\\\\n    97\\\\t\\\\n    98\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n    99\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   100\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   101\\\\t| AC-5.1 | Event | WHEN `after: \\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line after the first matching line | TEST-5.1 |\\\\n   102\\\\t| AC-5.2 | Event | WHEN `before: \\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line before the first matching line | TEST-5.2 |\\\\n   103\\\\t| AC-5.3 | Event | WHEN `prepend: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very beginning of the file | TEST-5.3 |\\\\n   104\\\\t| AC-5.4 | Event | WHEN `append: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very end of the file | TEST-5.4 |\\\\n   105\\\\t| AC-5.5 | Event | WHEN `at: last` is specified with `after` or `before`, the system SHALL use the last regex match instead of the first | TEST-5.5 |\\\\n   106\\\\t| AC-5.6 | Event | WHEN `at: first` (default) is specified, the system SHALL use the first regex match | TEST-5.6 |\\\\n   107\\\\t| AC-5.7 | Event | WHEN `skip_if: \\\\\\\"string\\\\\\\"` is specified and the string exists in the target file, the system SHALL skip the injection and report `\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\"` with a reason | TEST-5.7 |\\\\n   108\\\\t| AC-5.8 | Unwanted | IF the regex pattern matches zero lines in the target file, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the pattern, the file path, and a hint | TEST-5.8 |\\\\n   109\\\\t| AC-5.9 | Unwanted | IF the target file for injection does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the missing file path | TEST-5.9 |\\\\n   110\\\\t| AC-5.10 | Event | WHEN an inject operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\"` with the path, location description, and line count | TEST-5.10 |\\\\n   111\\\\t| AC-5.11 | Event | WHEN the inject path contains Jinja2 expressions, the system SHALL render the path before resolving it | TEST-5.11 |\\\\n   112\\\\t\\\\n   113\\\\t#### FR-6: Output Formatting\\\\n   114\\\\t\\\\n   115\\\\tReport operation results as JSON (stdout, for LLM callers) or human-readable text (stderr, for terminal users). Auto-detect the appropriate mode.\\\\n   116\\\\t\\\\n   117\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   118\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   119\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   120\\\\t| AC-6.1 | State | WHILE stdout is a TTY, the system SHALL output human-readable colored text to stderr by default | TEST-6.1 |\\\\n   121\\\\t| AC-6.2 | State | WHILE stdout is piped (not a TTY), the system SHALL output JSON to stdout by default | TEST-6.2 |\\\\n   122\\\\t| AC-6.3 | Event | WHEN `--json` is specified, the system SHALL force JSON output to stdout regardless of TTY detection | TEST-6.3 |\\\\n   123\\\\t| AC-6.4 | Event | WHEN `--quiet` is specified, the system SHALL suppress all output except errors | TEST-6.4 |\\\\n   124\\\\t| AC-6.5 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include an `operations` array with action, path, lines (for success), reason (for skip), or error details (for error) for each operation | TEST-6.5 |\\\\n   125\\\\t| AC-6.6 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include `files_written` and `files_skipped` summary arrays | TEST-6.6 |\\\\n   126\\\\t| AC-6.7 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified, the system SHALL include rendered template content in the output | TEST-6.7 |\\\\n   127\\\\t| AC-6.8 | Event | WHEN `--dry-run` is specified, the system SHALL produce identical output format but write no files to disk | TEST-6.8 |\\\\n   128\\\\t\\\\n   129\\\\t#### FR-7: CLI Interface\\\\n   130\\\\t\\\\n   131\\\\tProvide four subcommands for v0.1: `validate`, `vars`, `render`, and `run`. Accept global options for variable sources, output control, and execution modes.\\\\n   132\\\\t\\\\n   133\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   134\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   135\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   136\\\\t| AC-7.1 | Event | WHEN `jig validate <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL parse the recipe and report whether it is valid, listing variables and operations | TEST-7.1 |\\\\n   137\\\\t| AC-7.2 | Event | WHEN `jig vars <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL output the expected variables as a JSON object with type, required, default, and description fields | TEST-7.2 |\\\\n   138\\\\t| AC-7.3 | Event | WHEN `jig render <template> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL render the template with the given variables and output the result to stdout | TEST-7.3 |\\\\n   139\\\\t| AC-7.4 | Event | WHEN `jig render` is invoked with `--to <path>`, the system SHALL write the rendered output to the specified file instead of stdout | TEST-7.4 |\\\\n   140\\\\t| AC-7.5 | Event | WHEN `jig run <recipe> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL execute all file operations in the recipe in declaration order | TEST-7.5 |\\\\n   141\\\\t| AC-7.6 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accept global options: `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose` | TEST-7.6 |\\\\n   142\\\\t\\\\n   143\\\\t### Non-Functional Requirements\\\\n   144\\\\t\\\\n   145\\\\t#### NFR-1: Deterministic Output\\\\n   146\\\\t\\\\n   147\\\\tSame inputs must always produce the same outputs, with no randomness or environment-dependent behavior in rendered content.\\\\n   148\\\\t\\\\n   149\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   150\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   151\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   152\\\\t| AC-N1.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL produce byte-identical output when given the same recipe, variables, and existing files across multiple runs | TEST-N1.1 |\\\\n   153\\\\t| AC-N1.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not include timestamps, random values, or machine-specific identifiers in rendered output | TEST-N1.2 |\\\\n   154\\\\t\\\\n   155\\\\t#### NFR-2: Idempotent Operations\\\\n   156\\\\t\\\\n   157\\\\tRunning the same recipe twice with the same variables must produce no changes on the second run.\\\\n   158\\\\t\\\\n   159\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   160\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   161\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   162\\\\t| AC-N2.1 | Event | WHEN a recipe is run a second time with the same variables and the same existing files, the system SHALL report all operations as `\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\"` with reasons | TEST-N2.1 |\\\\n   163\\\\t| AC-N2.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not produce duplicate content when create uses `skip_if_exists: true` or inject uses `skip_if` | TEST-N2.2 |\\\\n   164\\\\t\\\\n   165\\\\t#### NFR-3: Single Static Binary\\\\n   166\\\\t\\\\n   167\\\\tZero runtime dependencies. One binary that runs on macOS and Linux without Python, Node, JVM, or any shared libraries.\\\\n   168\\\\t\\\\n   169\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   170\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   171\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   172\\\\t| AC-N3.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL compile to a single static binary with no runtime dependencies | TEST-N3.1 |\\\\n   173\\\\t| AC-N3.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries to run | TEST-N3.2 |\\\\n   174\\\\t\\\\n   175\\\\t#### NFR-4: Structured Errors with Rendered Content\\\\n   176\\\\t\\\\n   177\\\\tErrors must include what/where/why/hint. File operation errors must include the rendered content so callers can fall back.\\\\n   178\\\\t\\\\n   179\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   180\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   181\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   182\\\\t| AC-N4.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL include what, where, why, and hint fields in every error message | TEST-N4.1 |\\\\n   183\\\\t| AC-N4.2 | Event | WHEN a file operation fails (exit code 3), the system SHALL include the rendered template content in the error output so the caller can fall back to manual editing | TEST-N4.2 |\\\\n   184\\\\t| AC-N4.3 | Event | WHEN a template rendering error occurs, the system SHALL report the template file path and the line number of the error | TEST-N4.3 |\\\\n   185\\\\t| AC-N4.4 | Event | WHEN a variable validation error occurs, the system SHALL report the variable name, expected type, and actual value provided | TEST-N4.4 |\\\\n   186\\\\t\\\\n   187\\\\t#### NFR-5: Stable Exit Codes\\\\n   188\\\\t\\\\n   189\\\\tExit codes are API. They must be deterministic and match the defined mapping.\\\\n   190\\\\t\\\\n   191\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   192\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   193\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   194\\\\t| AC-N5.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL exit with code 0 on success, 1 for recipe validation errors, 2 for template rendering errors, 3 for file operation errors, 4 for variable validation errors | TEST-N5.1 |\\\\n   195\\\\t| AC-N5.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL use the exit code of the first error encountered when multiple errors occur | TEST-N5.2 |\\\\n   196\\\\t\\\\n   197\\\\t#### NFR-6: Ordered Execution\\\\n   198\\\\t\\\\n   199\\\\tFile operations must execute in declaration order so later operations can depend on files created by earlier ones.\\\\n   200\\\\t\\\\n   201\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   202\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   203\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   204\\\\t| AC-N6.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL execute file operations in the order they appear in the recipe's `files` array | TEST-N6.1 |\\\\n   205\\\\t| AC-N6.2 | Event | WHEN an inject operation targets a file created by an earlier create operation in the same recipe, the system SHALL find and operate on the newly created file | TEST-N6.2 |\\\\n   206\\\\t\\\\n   207\\\\t## Interfaces\\\\n   208\\\\t\\\\n   209\\\\t### Public API (CLI)\\\\n   210\\\\t\\\\n   211\\\\t```\\\\n   212\\\\tjig validate <recipe>\\\\n   213\\\\tjig vars <recipe>\\\\n   214\\\\tjig render <template> [--to <path>] --vars '<json>'\\\\n   215\\\\tjig run <recipe> --vars '<json>' [options]\\\\n   216\\\\t\\\\n   217\\\\tOptions:\\\\n   218\\\\t  --vars <json>        Inline variables as JSON string\\\\n   219\\\\t  --vars-file <path>   Variables from a JSON file\\\\n   220\\\\t  --vars-stdin         Read variables from stdin\\\\n   221\\\\t  --dry-run            Preview without writing\\\\n   222\\\\t  --json               Force JSON output\\\\n   223\\\\t  --quiet              Suppress non-error output\\\\n   224\\\\t  --force              Overwrite existing files\\\\n   225\\\\t  --base-dir <path>    Resolve output paths from this directory\\\\n   226\\\\t  --verbose            Include rendered content in output\\\\n   227\\\\t```\\\\n   228\\\\t\\\\n   229\\\\t### JSON Output Schema\\\\n   230\\\\t\\\\n   231\\\\t```json\\\\n   232\\\\t{\\\\n   233\\\\t  \\\\\\\"operations\\\\\\\": [\\\\n   234\\\\t    {\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"lines\\\\\\\": 42},\\\\n   235\\\\t    {\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"location\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"after:^# fixtures\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"lines\\\\\\\": 3},\\\\n   236\\\\t    {\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"reason\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"skip_if matched: BookingService\\\\\\\"},\\\\n   237\\\\t    {\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"hint\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"rendered_content\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\"}\\\\n   238\\\\t  ],\\\\n   239\\\\t  \\\\\\\"files_written\\\\\\\": [\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\"],\\\\n   240\\\\t  \\\\\\\"files_skipped\\\\\\\": [\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\"]\\\\n   241\\\\t}\\\\n   242\\\\t```\\\\n   243\\\\t\\\\n   244\\\\t### Internal Interfaces\\\\n   245\\\\t\\\\n   246\\\\t```rust\\\\n   247\\\\t// Recipe → Variable Validator\\\\n   248\\\\tfn validate_variables(decls: &IndexMap<String, VariableDecl>, provided: Value) -> Result<Value, JigError>;\\\\n   249\\\\t\\\\n   250\\\\t// Recipe + Variables → Renderer\\\\n   251\\\\tfn render_template(env: &Environment, template_name: &str, vars: &Value) -> Result<String, JigError>;\\\\n   252\\\\t\\\\n   253\\\\t// Rendered content + FileOp → Operation Executor\\\\n   254\\\\tfn execute_operation(op: &FileOp, rendered: &str, ctx: &ExecutionContext) -> OpResult;\\\\n   255\\\\t\\\\n   256\\\\t// Vec<OpResult> → Output Formatter\\\\n   257\\\\tfn format_output(results: &[OpResult], mode: OutputMode) -> String;\\\\n   258\\\\t```\\\\n   259\\\\t\\\\n   260\\\\t## Data Model\\\\n   261\\\\t\\\\n   262\\\\t```rust\\\\n   263\\\\tstruct Recipe {\\\\n   264\\\\t    name: Option<String>,\\\\n   265\\\\t    description: Option<String>,\\\\n   266\\\\t    variables: IndexMap<String, VariableDecl>,\\\\n   267\\\\t    files: Vec<FileOp>,\\\\n   268\\\\t}\\\\n   269\\\\t\\\\n   270\\\\tstruct VariableDecl {\\\\n   271\\\\t    var_type: VarType,          // string, number, boolean, array, object, enum\\\\n   272\\\\t    required: bool,             // default: false\\\\n   273\\\\t    default: Option<Value>,\\\\n   274\\\\t    description: Option<String>,\\\\n   275\\\\t    values: Option<Vec<String>>, // for enum type\\\\n   276\\\\t    items: Option<VarType>,      // for array type\\\\n   277\\\\t}\\\\n   278\\\\t\\\\n   279\\\\tenum FileOp {\\\\n   280\\\\t    Create { template: String, to: String, skip_if_exists: bool },\\\\n   281\\\\t    Inject { template: String, inject: String, mode: InjectMode, skip_if: Option<String> },\\\\n   282\\\\t}\\\\n   283\\\\t\\\\n   284\\\\tenum InjectMode {\\\\n   285\\\\t    After { pattern: String, at: MatchPosition },\\\\n   286\\\\t    Before { pattern: String, at: MatchPosition },\\\\n   287\\\\t    Prepend,\\\\n   288\\\\t    Append,\\\\n   289\\\\t}\\\\n   290\\\\t\\\\n   291\\\\tenum MatchPosition { First, Last }\\\\n   292\\\\t\\\\n   293\\\\tenum OpResult {\\\\n   294\\\\t    Success { action: &'static str, path: PathBuf, lines: usize },\\\\n   295\\\\t    Skip { path: PathBuf, reason: String },\\\\n   296\\\\t    Error { path: PathBuf, error: StructuredError, rendered_content: String },\\\\n   297\\\\t}\\\\n   298\\\\t\\\\n   299\\\\tenum JigError {\\\\n   300\\\\t    RecipeValidation(String),     // exit 1\\\\n   301\\\\t    TemplateRendering(String),    // exit 2\\\\n   302\\\\t    FileOperation(String),        // exit 3\\\\n   303\\\\t    VariableValidation(String),   // exit 4\\\\n   304\\\\t}\\\\n   305\\\\t```\\\\n   306\\\\t\\\\n   307\\\\t## Error Handling\\\\n   308\\\\t\\\\n   309\\\\t| Error Category | Exit Code | Examples |\\\\n   310\\\\t|----------------|-----------|----------|\\\\n   311\\\\t| Recipe validation | 1 | Malformed YAML, missing fields, missing template files |\\\\n   312\\\\t| Template rendering | 2 | Undefined variable, Jinja2 syntax error |\\\\n   313\\\\t| File operation | 3 | Target file missing (inject), file exists (create), regex no match |\\\\n   314\\\\t| Variable validation | 4 | Missing required, wrong type, invalid enum value |\\\\n   315\\\\t\\\\n   316\\\\tEvery error includes: what happened, where (file/variable/line), why (expected vs actual), and a hint for the caller.\\\\n   317\\\\t\\\\n   318\\\\tFile operation errors additionally include `rendered_content` so the LLM caller can fall back to manual editing with the Edit tool.\\\\n   319\\\\t\\\\n   320\\\\t## Testing Strategy\\\\n   321\\\\t\\\\n   322\\\\t- **Spec tests:** One or more tests per AC-* criterion above, namespaced as `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}`\\\\n   323\\\\t- **Invariant tests:** Determinism (run twice, compare output), idempotency (run twice, second run all skips), exit code stability\\\\n   324\\\\t- **Integration fixtures:** Each fixture directory contains recipe.yaml, vars.json, templates/, existing/ (optional), expected/, expected_output.json (optional), expected_exit_code (optional)\\\\n   325\\\\t- **Snapshot tests:** `insta` for rendered template output, JSON output format, and error messages\\\\n   326\\\\t\\\\n   327\\\\t## Requirement Traceability\\\\n   328\\\\t\\\\n   329\\\\t| Requirement | Criteria | Test | Status |\\\\n   330\\\\t|-------------|----------|------|--------|\\\\n   331\\\\t| FR-1 | AC-1.1 through AC-1.9 | spec::fr1::* | PENDING |\\\\n   332\\\\t| FR-2 | AC-2.1 through AC-2.10 | spec::fr2::* | PENDING |\\\\n   333\\\\t| FR-3 | AC-3.1 through AC-3.18 | spec::fr3::* | PENDING |\\\\n   334\\\\t| FR-4 | AC-4.1 through AC-4.8 | spec::fr4::* | PENDING |\\\\n   335\\\\t| FR-5 | AC-5.1 through AC-5.11 | spec::fr5::* | PENDING |\\\\n   336\\\\t| FR-6 | AC-6.1 through AC-6.8 | spec::fr6::* | PENDING |\\\\n   337\\\\t| FR-7 | AC-7.1 through AC-7.6 | spec::fr7::* | PENDING |\\\\n   338\\\\t| NFR-1 | AC-N1.1, AC-N1.2 | spec::nfr1::* | PENDING |\\\\n   339\\\\t| NFR-2 | AC-N2.1, AC-N2.2 | spec::nfr2::* | PENDING |\\\\n   340\\\\t| NFR-3 | AC-N3.1, AC-N3.2 | spec::nfr3::* | PENDING |\\\\n   341\\\\t| NFR-4 | AC-N4.1 through AC-N4.4 | spec::nfr4::* | PENDING |\\\\n   342\\\\t| NFR-5 | AC-N5.1, AC-N5.2 | spec::nfr5::* | PENDING |\\\\n   343\\\\t| NFR-6 | AC-N6.1, AC-N6.2 | spec::nfr6::* | PENDING -lc 'nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md'\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"     1\\\\t# PLAN.md\\\\n     2\\\\t\\\\n     3\\\\t> Workstream: core-engine\\\\n     4\\\\t> Last updated: 2026-04-02\\\\n     5\\\\t> Status: Planned\\\\n     6\\\\t\\\\n     7\\\\t## Objective\\\\n     8\\\\t\\\\n     9\\\\tDeliver the minimum viable jig CLI: parse recipes, validate variables, render Jinja2 templates, execute create and inject file operations, and report structured results.
