-lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"nl -ba tests/integration.rs | sed -n '1,360p'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"     1\\\\\\\\t//! Fixture-based integration tests for jig CLI.\\\\\\\\n     2\\\\\\\\t//!\\\\\\\\n     3\\\\\\\\t//! Each subdirectory under `tests/fixtures/` is a test case. Adding a new test\\\\\\\\n     4\\\\\\\\t//! case requires only adding a directory — no code changes.\\\\\\\\n     5\\\\\\\\t//!\\\\\\\\n     6\\\\\\\\t//! Fixture layout:\\\\\\\\n     7\\\\\\\\t//!   recipe.yaml          — recipe to run\\\\\\\\n     8\\\\\\\\t//!   vars.json            — variables (JSON)\\\\\\\\n     9\\\\\\\\t//!   templates/           — template files (referenced by recipe)\\\\\\\\n    10\\\\\\\\t//!   existing/            — pre-existing files, copied to temp dir before run\\\\\\\\n    11\\\\\\\\t//!   expected/            — expected files after run (diffed against actual)\\\\\\\\n    12\\\\\\\\t//!   expected_output.json — (optional) expected JSON output structure\\\\\\\\n    13\\\\\\\\t//!   expected_exit_code   — (optional) expected exit code (default: 0)\\\\\\\\n    14\\\\\\\\t//!   force                — (optional) if present, pass --force\\\\\\\\n    15\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    16\\\\\\\\tuse std::fs;\\\\\\\\n    17\\\\\\\\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\\\\\\\\n    18\\\\\\\\tuse std::process::Command;\\\\\\\\n    19\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    20\\\\\\\\t// ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\\\\\n    21\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    22\\\\\\\\tfn jig_bin() -> PathBuf {\\\\\\\\n    23\\\\\\\\t    PathBuf::from(env!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"CARGO_BIN_EXE_jig\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"))\\\\\\\\n    24\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\n    25\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    26\\\\\\\\tfn fixtures_dir() -> PathBuf {\\\\\\\\n    27\\\\\\\\t    PathBuf::from(env!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")).join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"tests/fixtures\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n    28\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\n    29\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    30\\\\\\\\t/// Discover all fixture directories (immediate children of tests/fixtures/).\\\\\\\\n    31\\\\\\\\t/// Supports both recipe fixtures (recipe.yaml) and workflow fixtures (workflow.yaml).\\\\\\\\n    32\\\\\\\\tfn discover_fixtures() -> Vec<PathBuf> {\\\\\\\\n    33\\\\\\\\t    let dir = fixtures_dir();\\\\\\\\n    34\\\\\\\\t    let mut fixtures: Vec<PathBuf> = fs::read_dir(&dir)\\\\\\\\n    35\\\\\\\\t        .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"cannot read fixtures dir {}: {}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", dir.display(), e))\\\\\\\\n    36\\\\\\\\t        .filter_map(|entry| {\\\\\\\\n    37\\\\\\\\t            let entry = entry.ok()?;\\\\\\\\n    38\\\\\\\\t            let path = entry.path();\\\\\\\\n    39\\\\\\\\t            if path.is_dir()\\\\\\\\n    40\\\\\\\\t                && (path.join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"recipe.yaml\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").exists() || path.join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"workflow.yaml\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").exists())\\\\\\\\n    41\\\\\\\\t            {\\\\\\\\n    42\\\\\\\\t                Some(path)\\\\\\\\n    43\\\\\\\\t            } else {\\\\\\\\n    44\\\\\\\\t                None\\\\\\\\n    45\\\\\\\\t            }\\\\\\\\n    46\\\\\\\\t        })\\\\\\\\n    47\\\\\\\\t        .collect();\\\\\\\\n    48\\\\\\\\t    fixtures.sort();\\\\\\\\n    49\\\\\\\\t    fixtures\\\\\\\\n    50\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\n    51\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    52\\\\\\\\t/// Determine if a fixture is a workflow fixture (has workflow.yaml).\\\\\\\\n    53\\\\\\\\tfn is_workflow_fixture(fixture: &Path) -> bool {\\\\\\\\n    54\\\\\\\\t    fixture.join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"workflow.yaml\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").exists()\\\\\\\\n    55\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\n    56\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    57\\\\\\\\t/// Read expected exit code from fixture (default 0).\\\\\\\\n    58\\\\\\\\tfn expected_exit_code(fixture: &Path) -> i32 {\\\\\\\\n    59\\\\\\\\t    let path = fixture.join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"expected_exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n    60\\\\\\\\t    if path.exists() {\\\\\\\\n    61\\\\\\\\t        fs::read_to_string(&path)\\\\\\\\n    62\\\\\\\\t            .unwrap()\\\\\\\\n    63\\\\\\\\t            .trim()\\\\\\\\n    64\\\\\\\\t            .parse()\\\\\\\\n    65\\\\\\\\t            .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"bad expected_exit_code in {}: {}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", fixture.display(), e))\\\\\\\\n    66\\\\\\\\t    } else {\\\\\\\\n    67\\\\\\\\t        0\\\\\\\\n    68\\\\\\\\t    }\\\\\\\\n    69\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\n    70\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    71\\\\\\\\t/// Run jig on a fixture, returning (exit_code, stdout, stderr).\\\\\\\\n    72\\\\\\\\t/// Automatically detects recipe vs workflow fixtures.\\\\\\\\n    73\\\\\\\\tfn run_fixture(fixture: &Path, work_dir: &Path) -> (i32, String, String) {\\\\\\\\n    74\\\\\\\\t    let vars_json = fs::read_to_string(fixture.join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"vars.json\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")).unwrap_or_else(|_| \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".into());\\\\\\\\n    75\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    76\\\\\\\\t    let mut cmd = Command::new(jig_bin());\\\\\\\\n    77\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    78\\\\\\\\t    if is_workflow_fixture(fixture) {\\\\\\\\n    79\\\\\\\\t        let workflow = fixture.join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"workflow.yaml\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n    80\\\\\\\\t        cmd.args([\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"workflow\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", &workflow.display().to_string()]);\\\\\\\\n    81\\\\\\\\t    } else {\\\\\\\\n    82\\\\\\\\t        let recipe = fixture.join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"recipe.yaml\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n    83\\\\\\\\t        cmd.args([\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"run\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", &recipe.display().to_string()]);\\\\\\\\n    84\\\\\\\\t    }\\\\\\\\n    85\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    86\\\\\\\\t    cmd.args([\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"--vars\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", &vars_json])\\\\\\\\n    87\\\\\\\\t        .args([\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"--base-dir\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", &work_dir.display().to_string()])\\\\\\\\n    88\\\\\\\\t        .arg(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"--json\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n    89\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    90\\\\\\\\t    if fixture.join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"force\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").exists() {\\\\\\\\n    91\\\\\\\\t        cmd.arg(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"--force\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n    92\\\\\\\\t    }\\\\\\\\n    93\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    94\\\\\\\\t    if fixture.join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"dry_run\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").exists() {\\\\\\\\n    95\\\\\\\\t        cmd.arg(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"--dry-run\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n    96\\\\\\\\t    }\\\\\\\\n    97\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n    98\\\\\\\\t    let output = cmd.output().expect(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"failed to run jig\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n    99\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   100\\\\\\\\t    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);\\\\\\\\n   101\\\\\\\\t    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string();\\\\\\\\n   102\\\\\\\\t    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string();\\\\\\\\n   103\\\\\\\\t    (code, stdout, stderr)\\\\\\\\n   104\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\n   105\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   106\\\\\\\\t/// Copy existing/ files into work_dir.\\\\\\\\n   107\\\\\\\\tfn setup_existing(fixture: &Path, work_dir: &Path) {\\\\\\\\n   108\\\\\\\\t    let existing = fixture.join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"existing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n   109\\\\\\\\t    if existing.is_dir() {\\\\\\\\n   110\\\\\\\\t        copy_dir_recursive(&existing, work_dir);\\\\\\\\n   111\\\\\\\\t    }\\\\\\\\n   112\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\n   113\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   114\\\\\\\\t/// Recursively copy src/ contents into dst/.\\\\\\\\n   115\\\\\\\\tfn copy_dir_recursive(src: &Path, dst: &Path) {\\\\\\\\n   116\\\\\\\\t    for entry in fs::read_dir(src).unwrap() {\\\\\\\\n   117\\\\\\\\t        let entry = entry.unwrap();\\\\\\\\n   118\\\\\\\\t        let src_path = entry.path();\\\\\\\\n   119\\\\\\\\t        let dst_path = dst.join(entry.file_name());\\\\\\\\n   120\\\\\\\\t        if src_path.is_dir() {\\\\\\\\n   121\\\\\\\\t            fs::create_dir_all(&dst_path).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n   122\\\\\\\\t            copy_dir_recursive(&src_path, &dst_path);\\\\\\\\n   123\\\\\\\\t        } else {\\\\\\\\n   124\\\\\\\\t            if let Some(parent) = dst_path.parent() {\\\\\\\\n   125\\\\\\\\t                fs::create_dir_all(parent).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n   126\\\\\\\\t            }\\\\\\\\n   127\\\\\\\\t            fs::copy(&src_path, &dst_path).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n   128\\\\\\\\t        }\\\\\\\\n   129\\\\\\\\t    }\\\\\\\\n   130\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\n   131\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   132\\\\\\\\t/// Compare work_dir contents against expected/ directory.\\\\\\\\n   133\\\\\\\\t/// Returns a list of (relative_path, diff_description) for mismatches.\\\\\\\\n   134\\\\\\\\tfn diff_expected(fixture: &Path, work_dir: &Path) -> Vec<(String, String)> {\\\\\\\\n   135\\\\\\\\t    let expected = fixture.join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"expected\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n   136\\\\\\\\t    if !expected.is_dir() {\\\\\\\\n   137\\\\\\\\t        return vec![];\\\\\\\\n   138\\\\\\\\t    }\\\\\\\\n   139\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   140\\\\\\\\t    let mut diffs = vec![];\\\\\\\\n   141\\\\\\\\t    diff_dir_recursive(&expected, work_dir, &expected, &mut diffs);\\\\\\\\n   142\\\\\\\\t    diffs\\\\\\\\n   143\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\n   144\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   145\\\\\\\\tfn diff_dir_recursive(\\\\\\\\n   146\\\\\\\\t    expected_root: &Path,\\\\\\\\n   147\\\\\\\\t    work_dir: &Path,\\\\\\\\n   148\\\\\\\\t    current: &Path,\\\\\\\\n   149\\\\\\\\t    diffs: &mut Vec<(String, String)>,\\\\\\\\n   150\\\\\\\\t) {\\\\\\\\n   151\\\\\\\\t    for entry in fs::read_dir(current).unwrap() {\\\\\\\\n   152\\\\\\\\t        let entry = entry.unwrap();\\\\\\\\n   153\\\\\\\\t        let path = entry.path();\\\\\\\\n   154\\\\\\\\t        let rel = path.strip_prefix(expected_root).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n   155\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   156\\\\\\\\t        if path.is_dir() {\\\\\\\\n   157\\\\\\\\t            diff_dir_recursive(expected_root, work_dir, &path, diffs);\\\\\\\\n   158\\\\\\\\t        } else {\\\\\\\\n   159\\\\\\\\t            let actual_path = work_dir.join(rel);\\\\\\\\n   160\\\\\\\\t            if !actual_path.exists() {\\\\\\\\n   161\\\\\\\\t                diffs.push((rel.display().to_string(), \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"file missing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\".into()));\\\\\\\\n   162\\\\\\\\t                continue;\\\\\\\\n   163\\\\\\\\t            }\\\\\\\\n   164\\\\\\\\t            let expected_bytes = fs::read(&path).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n   165\\\\\\\\t            let actual_bytes = fs::read(&actual_path).unwrap();\\\\\\\\n   166\\\\\\\\t            if expected_bytes != actual_bytes {\\\\\\\\n   167\\\\\\\\t                let expected_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&expected_bytes);\\\\\\\\n   168\\\\\\\\t                let actual_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&actual_bytes);\\\\\\\\n   169\\\\\\\\t                diffs.push((\\\\\\\\n   170\\\\\\\\t                    rel.display().to_string(),\\\\\\\\n   171\\\\\\\\t                    format!(\\\\\\\\n   172\\\\\\\\t                        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"content mismatch:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n--- expected\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n+++ actual\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n-{}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n+{}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n   173\\\\\\\\t                        expected_str.replace('\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n', \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n-\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"),\\\\\\\\n   174\\\\\\\\t                        actual_str.replace('\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n', \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"),\\\\\\\\n   175\\\\\\\\t                    ),\\\\\\\\n   176\\\\\\\\t                ));\\\\\\\\n   177\\\\\\\\t            }\\\\\\\\n   178\\\\\\\\t        }\\\\\\\\n   179\\\\\\\\t    }\\\\\\\\n   180\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\n   181\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   182\\\\\\\\t/// Normalize JSON output: replace absolute paths with relative paths\\\\\\\\n   183\\\\\\\\t/// (strip the work_dir prefix).\\\\\\\\n   184\\\\\\\\tfn normalize_json(json_str: &str, work_dir: &Path) -> serde_json::Value {\\\\\\\\n   185\\\\\\\\t    let work_dir_str = work_dir.display().to_string();\\\\\\\\n   186\\\\\\\\t    let normalized = json_str.replace(&format!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{}/\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", work_dir_str), \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n   187\\\\\\\\t    serde_json::from_str(&normalized).unwrap_or_else(|e| {\\\\\\\\n   188\\\\\\\\t        panic!(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"failed to parse JSON output: {}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nraw output:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n{}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", e, json_str)\\\\\\\\n   189\\\\\\\\t    })\\\\\\\\n   190\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\n   191\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   192\\\\\\\\t/// Assert JSON output matches expected_output.json.\\\\\\\\n   193\\\\\\\\t/// Only checks fields present in expected — extra fields in actual are allowed.\\\\\\\\n   194\\\\\\\\tfn assert_json_matches(expected: &serde_json::Value, actual: &serde_json::Value, path: &str) {\\\\\\\\n   195\\\\\\\\t    match (expected, actual) {\\\\\\\\n   196\\\\\\\\t        (serde_json::Value::Object(exp_map), serde_json::Value::Object(act_map)) => {\\\\\\\\n   197\\\\\\\\t            for (key, exp_val) in exp_map {\\\\\\\\n   198\\\\\\\\t                let act_val = act_map.get(key).unwrap_or_else(|| assert!(\\\\\\\\n   634\\\\\\\\t                line.contains(\\\\\\cross multiple runs\\\\\\\\tTEST-N1.1\\\\\\\\nAC-N1.2\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL not include timestamps, random values, or machine-specific identifiers in rendered output\\\\\\\\tTEST-N1.2\\\\\\\\nAC-N2.1\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a recipe designed for idempotent execution (all creates use `skip_if_exists: true` and all injects use `skip_if`) is run a second time with the same variables and the same existing files, the system SHALL report all operations as `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with reasons\\\\\\\\tTEST-N2.1\\\\\\\\nAC-N2.2\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL not produce duplicate content when create uses `skip_if_exists: true` or inject uses `skip_if`\\\\\\\\tTEST-N2.2\\\\\\\\nAC-N3.1\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL compile to a single static binary with no runtime dependencies beyond the system C library\\\\\\\\tTEST-N3.1\\\\\\\\nAC-N3.2\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries beyond the system C library to run\\\\\\\\tTEST-N3.2\\\\\\\\nAC-N4.1\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL include what, where, why, and hint fields in every error message\\\\\\\\tTEST-N4.1\\\\\\\\nAC-N4.2\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a file operation fails (exit code 3), the system SHALL include the rendered template content in the error output so the caller can fall back to manual editing. This is independent of `--verbose` — rendered content in errors is always present\\\\\\\\tTEST-N4.2\\\\\\\\nAC-N4.3\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a template rendering error occurs, the system SHALL report the template file path and the line number of the error\\\\\\\\tTEST-N4.3\\\\\\\\nAC-N4.4\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a variable validation error occurs, the system SHALL report the variable name, expected type, and actual value provided\\\\\\\\tTEST-N4.4\\\\\\\\nAC-N5.1\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL exit with code 0 on success, 1 for recipe validation errors, 2 for template rendering errors, 3 for file operation errors, 4 for variable validation errors\\\\\\\\tTEST-N5.1\\\\\\\\nAC-N5.2\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL use the exit code corresponding to the first pipeline stage that fails: recipe validation (1) before variable validation (4) before template rendering (2) before file operations (3)\\\\\\\\tTEST-N5.2\\\\\\\\nAC-N6.1\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL execute file operations in the order they appear in the recipe's `files` array\\\\\\\\tTEST-N6.1\\\\\\\\nAC-N6.2\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN an inject operation targets a file created by an earlier create operation in the same recipe, the system SHALL find and operate on the newly created file\\\\\\\\tTEST-N6.2\\\\\\\\nFR-1\\\\\\\\tAC-1.1 through AC-1.15\\\\\\\\tspec::fr1::*\\\\\\\\tPENDING\\\\\\\\nFR-2\\\\\\\\tAC-2.1 through (blocks implementation):\\\\\\\\n- Dry-run core design is internally inconsistent: the interface uses immutable context (`execute_operation(..., ctx: &ExecutionContext)`), but requirements require mutating virtual file state across operations (create then inject in dry-run). This needs an explicit mutable state contract before coding. [ARCHITECTURE.md:137](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:137), [ARCHITECTURE.md:153](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:153), [SPEC.md:147](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:147), [PLAN.md:132](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:132)\\\\\\\\n- Partial-write behavior is contradictory: planning docs claim “no partial writes,” while architecture/spec explicitly say execution failures can leave partial writes. This affects recovery semantics, test expectations, and operator guidance. [PLAN.md:216](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:216), [SHARED-CONTEXT.md:31](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SHARED-CONTEXT.md:31), [ARCHITECTURE.md:362](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:362), [SPEC.md:370](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:370)\\\\\\\\n- `jig render` behavior conflicts across docs: architecture demo expects type-validation failure on `render`, but spec says `render` has no recipe context and no type validation. This must be resolved before implementation/testing. [ARCHITECTURE.md:421](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:421), [ARCHITECTURE.md:423](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:423), [SPEC.md:161](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:161)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Major** (should fix before implementation):\\\\\\\\n- `--quiet` semantics conflict: plan says suppress “non-errors,” but spec/invariant say suppress all stderr. [PLAN.md:110](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:110), [SPEC.md:143](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:143), [INVARIANTS.md:43](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/INVARIANTS.md:43)\\\\\\\\n- Error precedence is ambiguous for operation typing: AC-1.10 (unsupported operation) can overlap AC-1.14 (ambiguous operation fields), but precedence is not specified in spec. [SPEC.md:33](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:33), [SPEC.md:37](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:37)\\\\\\\\n- Key failure modes are underspecified: invalid JSON from `--vars-stdin`, missing template path for `jig render`, and `jig render --to` write failures lack explicit AC/error-code coverage. [SPEC.md:49](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:49), [SPEC.md:161](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:161), [SPEC.md:162](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:162)\\\\\\\\n- Some EARS criteria are not atomic/test-friendly (multiple independent SHALLs in one row), especially AC-6.8 and AC-6.9; they should be split for deterministic traceability. [SPEC.md:147](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:147), [SPEC.md:148](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:148)\\\\\\\\n- Plan validation criteria do not explicitly cover several SPEC ACs (notably AC-4.9/4.10, AC-5.17, AC-6.5/6.6/6.7/6.9/6.11, AC-N5.2), creating coverage ambiguity pre-implementation. [PLAN.md:101](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:101), [PLAN.md:137](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:137), [PLAN.md:179](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:179)\\\\\\\\n- PLAN introduces behavior not in SPEC (`jig validate --json`), so either SPEC should include it or PLAN should remove it. [PLAN.md:26](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:26), [SPEC.md:235](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:235)\\\\\\\\n- Artifact drift is significant: shared context says 28 milestones but PLAN has 29; execution plan says 79 AC and marks key decisions as unresolved while PLAN marks them decided/resolved. [SHARED-CONTEXT.md:14](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SHARED-CONTEXT.md:14), [PLAN.md:21](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:21), [claude-plan-20260402-223619.md:7](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/exec/claude-plan-20260402-223619.md:7), [claude-plan-20260402-223619.md:147](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/exec/claude-plan-20260402-223619.md:147), [PLAN.md:202](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:202), [PLAN.md:228](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:228)\\\\\\\\n- Invariant/NFR wording around “single static binary” vs “beyond system C library” is ambiguous and could create false pass/fail in validation gates. [INVARIANTS.md:33](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/INVARIANTS.md:33), [SPEC.md:191](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:191), [PLAN.md:185](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:185)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Minor** (nice to fix):\\\\\\\\n- Two table rows embed escaped pipes in examples, which can break machine parsing of the markdown table in some tooling. [SPEC.md:33](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:33)\\\\\\\\n- Requirement traceability table maps by wildcard (`spec::frX::*`) rather than explicit TEST-ID-to-test-case mapping, reducing auditability. [SPEC.md:379](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:379)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Strengths** (what’s done well):\\\\\\\\n- EARS structure is broadly disciplined: all AC rows are typed, have IDs, and include a `Traces To` reference token. [SPEC.md:21](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:21), [SPEC.md:173](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:173)\\\\\\\\n- Phase plan is concrete and incremental with milestones, validation gates, file ownership, and dependency declarations. -lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"rg -n 'TEST-[A-Za-z0-9.:-]+' docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"24:| AC-1.1 | Event | WHEN a valid recipe YAML is provided, the system SHALL parse it into a Recipe struct containing name, description, variables, and files | TEST-1.1 |\\\\\\\\n25:| AC-1.2 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares variables with type, required, default, description, values (enum), and items (array), the system SHALL parse all fields into the VariableDecl struct | TEST-1.2 |\\\\\\\\n26:| AC-1.3 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares `create` file operations with template, to, and skip_if_exists fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Create | TEST-1.3 |\\\\\\\\n27:| AC-1.4 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares `inject` file operations with template, inject, after/before/prepend/append, at, and skip_if fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Inject | TEST-1.4 |\\\\\\\\n28:| AC-1.5 | Unwanted | IF the recipe YAML is malformed (invalid YAML syntax), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and an error message identifying the parse failure location | TEST-1.5 |\\\\\\\\n29:| AC-1.6 | Unwanted | IF a required field is missing from the recipe (e.g., files with no template), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and name the missing field | TEST-1.6 |\\\\\\\\n30:| AC-1.7 | Event | WHEN a recipe references template files, the system SHALL resolve those paths relative to the recipe file location, not the working directory | TEST-1.7 |\\\\\\\\n31:| AC-1.8 | Unwanted | IF a referenced template file does not exist at the resolved path, the system SHALL exit with code 1 and report which template is missing and where it looked | TEST-1.8 |\\\\\\\\n32:| AC-1.9 | Event | WHEN the recipe has optional metadata fields (name, description), the system SHALL accept recipes with or without them | TEST-1.9 |\\\\\\\\n33:| AC-1.10 | Unwanted | IF the recipe contains an unknown operation type (e.g., `patch`, `replace`), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and report \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"unknown operation type '<name>' — this operation is not supported in v0.1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-1.10 |\\\\\\\\n34:| AC-1.11 | Event | WHEN the recipe has an empty `files: []` array, the system SHALL exit 0 with an empty operations array | TEST-1.11 |\\\\\\\\n35:| AC-1.12 | Event | WHEN the recipe has no `variables` key or an empty `variables` map, the system SHALL accept the recipe as valid | TEST-1.12 |\\\\\\\\n44:| AC-2.1 | Event | WHEN `--vars` is provided with a JSON string, the system SHALL parse it as the variable input | TEST-2.1 |\\\\\\\\n45:| AC-2.2 | Event | WHEN `--vars-file` is provided with a path to a JSON file, the system SHALL read and parse the file as variable input | TEST-2.2 |\\\\\\\\n46:| AC-2.3 | Event | WHEN `--vars-stdin` is provided, the system SHALL read JSON from stdin as variable input | TEST-2.3 |\\\\\\\\n47:| AC-2.4 | Event | WHEN multiple variable sources are provided, the system SHALL merge with precedence: recipe defaults < vars-file < vars-stdin < inline --vars | TEST-2.4 |\\\\\\\\n48:| AC-2.5 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared as `required: true` and no value is provided (after merging), the system SHALL exit with code 4 naming the missing variable and providing a hint | TEST-2.5 |\\\\\\\\n49:| AC-2.6 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared with a `default` and no value is provided, the system SHALL use the default value | TEST-2.6 |\\\\\\\\n50:| AC-2.7 | Unwanted | IF a provided variable value does not match its declared type (e.g., string given for number, object given for array), the system SHALL exit with code 4 with expected vs actual type | TEST-2.7 |\\\\\\\\n51:| AC-2.8 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared as `type: enum` with `values: [a, b, c]`, the system SHALL reject values not in the allowed set with exit code 4 | TEST-2.8 |\\\\\\\\n52:| AC-2.9 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared as `type: array` with `items: string`, the system SHALL validate that each array element matches the item type | TEST-2.9 |\\\\\\\\n53:| AC-2.10 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accept all six variable types: string, number, boolean, array, object, enum | TEST-2.10 |\\\\\\\\n54:| AC-2.11 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accumulate all variable validation errors and report them together, using the exit code of the first error encountered | TEST-2.11 |\\\\\\\\n63:| AC-3.1 | Event | WHEN a template is rendered with a variables context, the system SHALL substitute all `{{ variable }}` expressions with their values | TEST-3.1 |\\\\\\\\n64:| AC-3.2 | Event | WHEN a template contains `{% if %}` / `{% else %}` / `{% endif %}` blocks, the system SHALL evaluate them correctly against the variable values | TEST-3.2 |\\\\\\\\n65:| AC-3.3 | Event | WHEN a template contains `{% for item in list %}` loops, the system SHALL iterate and render the body for each element | TEST-3.3 |\\\\\\\\n66:| AC-3.4 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL register and support all 13 built-in filters: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join | TEST-3.4 |\\\\\\\\n67:| AC-3.5 | Event | WHEN `snakecase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.5 |\\\\\\\\n68:| AC-3.6 | Event | WHEN `camelcase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"bookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.6 |\\\\\\\\n69:| AC-3.7 | Event | WHEN `pascalcase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.7 |\\\\\\\\n70:| AC-3.8 | Event | WHEN `kebabcase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking-service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.8 |\\\\\\\\n71:| AC-3.9 | Event | WHEN `replace` is applied as `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a.b.c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\| replace('.', '/')`, the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a/b/c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.9 |\\\\\\\\n72:| AC-3.10 | Event | WHEN `pluralize` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotel\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.10 |\\\\\\\\n73:| AC-3.11 | Event | WHEN `singularize` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotel\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.11 |\\\\\\\\n74:| AC-3.12 | Event | WHEN `quote` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` (with literal quotes) | TEST-3.12 |\\\\\\\\n75:| AC-3.13 | Event | WHEN `indent(4)` is applied to a multiline string, the system SHALL indent each line by 4 spaces, including the first line. Use `indent(4, first=false)` to skip the first line. | TEST-3.13 |\\\\\\\\n76:| AC-3.14 | Event | WHEN `join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")` is applied to `[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"]`, the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a, b, c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.14 |\\\\\\\\n77:| AC-3.15 | Event | WHEN `{# comment #}` appears in a template, the system SHALL strip it from the output | TEST-3.15 |\\\\\\\\n78:| AC-3.16 | Event | WHEN `{% raw %}...{% endraw %}` appears in a template, the system SHALL output the content literally without interpreting Jinja2 syntax | TEST-3.16 |\\\\\\\\n79:| AC-3.17 | Unwanted | IF a template references an undefined variable, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and include a \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" hint when a close match exists (Levenshtein distance ≤ 3) | TEST-3.17 |\\\\\\\\n80:| AC-3.18 | Unwanted | IF a template has a Jinja2 syntax error, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and report the template file path and line number | TEST-3.18 |\\\\\\\\n89:| AC-4.1 | Event | WHEN a create operation executes, the system SHALL render the template and write the result to the path specified in `to` | TEST-4.1 |\\\\\\\\n90:| AC-4.2 | Event | WHEN the `to` path contains Jinja2 expressions (e.g., `tests/{{ module \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\| replace('.', '/') }}/test_{{ class_name \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\| snakecase }}.py`), the system SHALL render the path before writing | TEST-4.2 |\\\\\\\\n91:| AC-4.3 | Event | WHEN parent directories in the `to` path do not exist, the system SHALL create them automatically | TEST-4.3 |\\\\\\\\n92:| AC-4.4 | Event | WHEN `skip_if_exists: true` and the target file already exists, the system SHALL skip the operation and report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with a reason | TEST-4.4 |\\\\\\\\n93:| AC-4.5 | Unwanted | IF `skip_if_exists: false` (default) and the target file already exists and `--force` is not set, the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting the conflict | TEST-4.5 |\\\\\\\\n94:| AC-4.6 | Event | WHEN `--force` is set and the target file already exists, the system SHALL overwrite the file regardless of skip_if_exists | TEST-4.6 |\\\\\\\\n95:| AC-4.7 | Event | WHEN `--base-dir` is set, the system SHALL resolve `to` paths relative to the base directory instead of the working directory | TEST-4.7 |\\\\\\\\n96:| AC-4.8 | Event | WHEN a create operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with the path and line count | TEST-4.8 |\\\\\\\\n105:| AC-5.1 | Event | WHEN `after: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line after the first matching line | TEST-5.1 |\\\\\\\\n106:| AC-5.2 | Event | WHEN `before: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line before the first matching line | TEST-5.2 |\\\\\\\\n107:| AC-5.3 | Event | WHEN `prepend: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very beginning of the file | TEST-5.3 |\\\\\\\\n108:| AC-5.4 | Event | WHEN `append: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very end of the file | TEST-5.4 |\\\\\\\\n109:| AC-5.5 | Event | WHEN `at: last` is specified with `after` or `before`, the system SHALL use the last regex match instead of the first | TEST-5.5 |\\\\\\\\n110:| AC-5.6 | Event | WHEN `at: first` (default) is specified, the system SHALL use the first regex match | TEST-5.6 |\\\\\\\\n111:| AC-5.7 | Event | WHEN `skip_if: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"string\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified and the string exists in the target file, the system SHALL skip the injection and report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with a reason | TEST-5.7 |\\\\\\\\n112:| AC-5.8 | Unwanted | IF the regex pattern matches zero lines in the target file, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the pattern, the file path, and a hint | TEST-5.8 |\\\\\\\\n113:| AC-5.9 | Unwanted | IF the target file for injection does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the missing file path | TEST-5.9 |\\\\\\\\n114:| AC-5.10 | Event | WHEN an inject operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with the path, location description, and line count | TEST-5.10 |\\\\\\\\n115:| AC-5.11 | Event | WHEN the inject path contains Jinja2 expressions, the system SHALL render the path before resolving it | TEST-5.11 |\\\\\\\\n116:| AC-5.12 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL ignore the `at` field when `prepend` or `append` is specified. IF `after` or `before` is specified without a regex pattern, the system SHALL exit with code 1 | TEST-5.12 |\\\\\\\\n125:| AC-6.1 | State | WHILE stdout is a TTY, the system SHALL output human-readable colored text to stderr and produce no stdout output | TEST-6.1 |\\\\\\\\n126:| AC-6.2 | State | WHILE stdout is piped (not a TTY), the system SHALL output JSON to stdout and produce no stderr output except errors | TEST-6.2 |\\\\\\\\n127:| AC-6.3 | Event | WHEN `--json` is specified, the system SHALL force JSON output to stdout regardless of TTY detection | TEST-6.3 |\\\\\\\\n128:| AC-6.4 | Event | WHEN `--quiet` is specified, the system SHALL suppress all output except errors | TEST-6.4 |\\\\\\\\n129:| AC-6.5 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include an `operations` array with action, path, lines (for success), reason (for skip), or error details (for error) for each operation | TEST-6.5 |\\\\\\\\n130:| AC-6.6 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include `files_written` and `files_skipped` summary arrays containing unique file paths. A path appears in `files_written` if any operation wrote to it. A path appears in `files_skipped` only if all operations targeting it were skipped. In dry-run mode, `files_written` lists paths that would have been written. | TEST-6.6 |\\\\\\\\n131:| AC-6.7 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified, the system SHALL include rendered template content in the output | TEST-6.7 |\\\\\\\\n132:| AC-6.8 | Event | WHEN `--dry-run` is specified, the system SHALL produce identical output format but write no files to disk. Create operations record their output paths and rendered content in a virtual file state. Inject operations targeting a path created earlier in the same dry-run SHALL behave as if the file exists with the rendered content from the create operation. | TEST-6.8 |\\\\\\\\n133:| AC-6.9 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL resolve flag conflicts as: `--quiet` suppresses stderr but does not affect `--json` stdout; `--verbose` is ignored when `--quiet` is set; `--json` and `--verbose` are independent (verbose adds rendered content to JSON output) | TEST-6.9 |\\\\\\\\n142:| AC-7.1 | Event | WHEN `jig validate <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL parse the recipe and report whether it is valid, listing variables and operations | TEST-7.1 |\\\\\\\\n143:| AC-7.2 | Event | WHEN `jig vars <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL output the expected variables as a JSON object with type, required, default, and description fields | TEST-7.2 |\\\\\\\\n144:| AC-7.3 | Event | WHEN `jig render <template> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL render the template with the given variables and output the result to stdout. Note: `jig render` operates without recipe context — variable type validation and \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" hints are not available. Use `jig run` for full validation. | TEST-7.3 |\\\\\\\\n145:| AC-7.4 | Event | WHEN `jig render` is invoked with `--to <path>`, the system SHALL write the rendered output to the specified file instead of stdout | TEST-7.4 |\\\\\\\\n146:| AC-7.5 | Event | WHEN `jig run <recipe> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL execute all file operations in the recipe in declaration order | TEST-7.5 |\\\\\\\\n147:| AC-7.6 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accept global options: `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose`, `--version` | TEST-7.6 |\\\\\\\\n158:| AC-N1.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL produce byte-identical output when given the same recipe, variables, and existing files across multiple runs | TEST-N1.1 |\\\\\\\\n159:| AC-N1.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not include timestamps, random values, or machine-specific identifiers in rendered output | TEST-N1.2 |\\\\\\\\n168:| AC-N2.1 | Event | WHEN a recipe designed for idempotent execution (all creates use `skip_if_exists: true` and all injects use `skip_if`) is run a second time with the same variables and the same existing files, the system SHALL report all operations as `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with reasons | TEST-N2.1 |\\\\\\\\n169:| AC-N2.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not produce duplicate content when create uses `skip_if_exists: true` or inject uses `skip_if` | TEST-N2.2 |\\\\\\\\n178:| AC-N3.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL compile to a single static binary with no runtime dependencies | TEST-N3.1 |\\\\\\\\n179:| AC-N3.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries to run | TEST-N3.2 |\\\\\\\\n188:| AC-N4.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL include what, where, why, and hint fields in every error message | TEST-N4.1 |\\\\\\\\n189:| AC-N4.2 | Event | WHEN a file operation fails (exit code 3), the system SHALL include the rendered template content in the error output so the caller can fall back to manual editing | TEST-N4.2 |\\\\\\\\n190:| AC-N4.3 | Event | WHEN a template rendering error occurs, the system SHALL report the template file path and the line number of the error | TEST-N4.3 |\\\\\\\\n191:| AC-N4.4 | Event | WHEN a variable validation error occurs, the system SHALL report the variable name, expected type, and actual value provided | TEST-N4.4 |\\\\\\\\n200:| AC-N5.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL exit with code 0 on success, 1 for recipe validation errors, 2 for template rendering errors, 3 for file operation errors, 4 for variable validation errors | TEST-N5.1 |\\\\\\\\n201:| AC-N5.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL use the exit code of the first error encountered when multiple errors occur | TEST-N5.2 |\\\\\\\\n210:| AC-N6.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL execute file operations in the order they appear in the recipe's `files` array | TEST-N6.1 |\\\\\\\\n211:| AC-N6.2 | Event | WHEN an inject operation targets a file created by an earlier create operation in the same recipe, the system SHALL find and operate on the newly created file | TEST-N6.2 | TEST-3.1 |\\\\\\\\n64:| AC-3.2 | Event | WHEN a template contains `{% if %}` / `{% else %}` / `{% endif %}` blocks, the system SHALL evaluate them correctly against the variable values | TEST-3.2 |\\\\\\\\n65:| AC-3.3 | Event | WHEN a template contains `{% for item in list %}` loops, the system SHALL iterate and render the body for each element | TEST-3.3 |\\\\\\\\n66:| AC-3.4 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL register and support all 13 built-in filters: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join | TEST-3.4 |\\\\\\\\n67:| AC-3.5 | Event | WHEN `snakecase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.5 |\\\\\\\\n68:| AC-3.6 | Event | WHEN `camelcase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"bookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.6 |\\\\\\\\n69:| AC-3.7 | Event | WHEN `pascalcase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.7 |\\\\\\\\n70:| AC-3.8 | Event | WHEN `kebabcase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking-service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.8 |\\\\\\\\n71:| AC-3.9 | Event | WHEN `replace` is applied as `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a.b.c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\| replace('.', '/')`, the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a/b/c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.9 |\\\\\\\\n72:| AC-3.10 | Event | WHEN `pluralize` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotel\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.10 |\\\\\\\\n73:| AC-3.11 | Event | WHEN `singularize` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotel\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.11 |\\\\\\\\n74:| AC-3.12 | Event | WHEN `quote` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` (with literal quotes) | TEST-3.12 |\\\\\\\\n75:| AC-3.13 | Event | WHEN `indent(4)` is applied to a multiline string, the system SHALL indent each line by 4 spaces, including the first line. Use `indent(4, first=false)` to skip the first line. | TEST-3.13 |\\\\\\\\n76:| AC-3.14 | Event | WHEN `join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")` is applied to `[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"]`, the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a, b, c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.14 |\\\\\\\\n77:| AC-3.15 | Event | WHEN `{# comment #}` appears in a template, the system SHALL strip it from the output | TEST-3.15 |\\\\\\\\n78:| AC-3.16 | Event | WHEN `{% raw %}...{% endraw %}` appears in a template, the system SHALL output the content literally without interpreting Jinja2 syntax | TEST-3.16 |\\\\\\\\n79:| AC-3.17 | Unwanted | IF a template references an undefined variable, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and include a \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" hint when a close match exists (Levenshtein distance ≤ 3) | TEST-3.17 |\\\\\\\\n80:| AC-3.18 | Unwanted | IF a template has a Jinja2 syntax error, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and report the template file path and line number | TEST-3.18 |\\\\\\\\n89:| AC-4.1 | Event | WHEN a create operation executes, the system SHALL render the template and write the result to the path specified in `to` | TEST-4.1 |\\\\\\\\n90:| AC-4.2 | Event | WHEN the `to` path contains Jinja2 expressions (e.g., `tests/{{ module \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\| replace('.', '/') }}/test_{{ class_name \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\| snakecase }}.py`), the system SHALL render the path before writing | TEST-4.2 |\\\\\\\\n91:| AC-4.3 | Event | WHEN parent directories in the `to` path do not exist, the system SHALL create them automatically | TEST-4.3 |\\\\\\\\n92:| AC-4.4 | Event | WHEN `skip_if_exists: true` and the target file already exists, the system SHALL skip the operation and report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with a reason | TEST-4.4 |\\\\\\\\n93:| AC-4.5 | Unwanted | IF `skip_if_exists: false` (default) and the target file already exists and `--force` is not set, the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting the conflict | TEST-4.5 |\\\\\\\\n94:| AC-4.6 | Event | WHEN `--force` is set and the target file already exists, the system SHALL overwrite the file regardless of skip_if_exists | TEST-4.6 |\\\\\\\\n95:| AC-4.7 | Event | WHEN `--base-dir` is set, the system SHALL resolve `to` paths relative to the base directory instead of the working directory | TEST-4.7 |\\\\\\\\n96:| AC-4.8 | Event | WHEN a create operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with the path and line count | TEST-4.8 |\\\\\\\\n105:| AC-5.1 | Event | WHEN `after: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line after the first matching line | TEST-5.1 |\\\\\\\\n106:| AC-5.2 | Event | WHEN `before: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line before the first matching line | TEST-5.2 |\\\\\\\\n107:| AC-5.3 | Event | WHEN `prepend: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very beginning of the file | TEST-5.3 |\\\\\\\\n108:| AC-5.4 | Event | WHEN `append: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very end of the file | TEST-5.4 |\\\\\\\\n109:| AC-5.5 | Event | WHEN `at: last` is specified with `after` or `before`, the system SHALL use the last regex match instead of the first | TEST-5.5 |\\\\\\\\n110:| AC-5.6 | Event | WHEN `at: first` (default) is specified, the system SHALL use the first regex match | TEST-5.6 |\\\\\\\\n111:| AC-5.7 | Event | WHEN `skip_if: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"string\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified and the string exists in the target file, the system SHALL skip the injection and report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with a reason | TEST-5.7 |\\\\\\\\n112:| AC-5.8 | Unwanted | IF the regex pattern matches zero lines in the target file, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the pattern, the file path, and a hint | TEST-5.8 |\\\\\\\\n113:| AC-5.9 | Unwanted | IF the target file for injection does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the missing file path | TEST-5.9 |\\\\\\\\n114:| AC-5.10 | Event | WHEN an inject operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with the path, location description, and line count | TEST-5.10 |\\\\\\\\n115:| AC-5.11 | Event | WHEN the inject path contains Jinja2 expressions, the system SHALL render the path before resolving it | TEST-5.11 |\\\\\\\\n116:| AC-5.12 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL ignore the `at` field when `prepend` or `append` is specified. IF `after` or `before` is specified without a regex pattern, the system SHALL exit with code 1 | TEST-5.12 |\\\\\\\\n125:| AC-6.1 | State | WHILE stdout is a TTY, the system SHALL output human-readable colored text to stderr and produce no stdout output | TEST-6.1 |\\\\\\\\n126:| AC-6.2 | State | WHILE stdout is piped (not a TTY), the system SHALL output JSON to stdout and produce no stderr output except errors | TEST-6.2 |\\\\\\\\n127:| AC-6.3 | Event | WHEN `--json` is specified, the system SHALL force JSON output to stdout regardless of TTY detection | TEST-6.3 |\\\\\\\\n128:| AC-6.4 | Event | WHEN `--quiet` is specified, the system SHALL suppress all output except errors | TEST-6.4 |\\\\\\\\n129:| AC-6.5 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include an `operations` array with action, path, lines (for success), reason (for skip), or error details (for error) for each operation | TEST-6.5 |\\\\\\\\n130:| AC-6.6 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include `files_written` and `files_skipped` summary arrays containing unique file paths. A path appears in `files_written` if any operation wrote to it. A path appears in `files_skipped` only if all operations targeting it were skipped. In dry-run mode, `files_written` lists paths that would have been written. | TEST-6.6 |\\\\\\\\n131:| AC-6.7 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified, the system SHALL include rendered template content in the output | TEST-6.7 |\\\\\\\\n132:| AC-6.8 | Event | WHEN `--dry-run` is specified, the system SHALL produce identical output format but write no files to disk. Create operations record their output paths and rendered content in a virtual file state. Inject operations targeting a path created earlier in the same dry-run SHALL behave as if the file exists with the rendered content from the create operation. | TEST-6.8 |\\\\\\\\n133:| AC-6.9 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL resolve flag conflicts as: `--quiet` suppresses stderr but does not affect `--json` stdout; `--verbose` is ignored when `--quiet` is set; `--json` and `--verbose` are independent (verbose adds rendered content to JSON output) | TEST-6.9 |\\\\\\\\n142:| AC-7.1 | Event | WHEN `jig validate <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL parse the recipe and report whether it is valid, listing variables and operations | TEST-7.1 |\\\\\\\\n143:| AC-7.2 | Event | WHEN `jig vars <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL output the expected variables as a JSON object with type, required, default, and description fields | TEST-7.2 |\\\\\\\\n144:| AC-7.3 | Event | WHEN `jig render <template> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL render the template with the given variables and output the result to stdout. Note: `jig render` operates without recipe context — variable type validation and \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" hints are not available. Use `jig run` for full validation. | TEST-7.3 |\\\\\\\\n145:| AC-7.4 | Event | WHEN `jig render` is invoked with `--to <path>`, the system SHALL write the rendered output to the specified file instead of stdout | TEST-7.4 |\\\\\\\\n146:| AC-7.5 | Event | WHEN `jig run <recipe> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL execute all file operations in the recipe in declaration order | TEST-7.5 |\\\\\\\\n147:| AC-7.6 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accept global options: `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose`, `--version` | TEST-7.6 |\\\\\\\\n158:| AC-N1.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL produce byte-identical output when given the same recipe, variables, and existing files across multiple runs | TEST-N1.1 |\\\\\\\\n159:| AC-N1.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not include timestamps, random values, or machine-specific identifiers in rendered output | TEST-N1.2 |\\\\\\\\n168:| AC-N2.1 | Event | WHEN a recipe designed for idempotent execution (all creates use `skip_if_exists: true` and all injects use `skip_if`) is run a second time with the same variables and the same existing files, the system SHALL report all operations as `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with reasons | TEST-N2.1 |\\\\\\\\n169:| AC-N2.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not produce duplicate content when create uses `skip_if_exists: true` or inject uses `skip_if` | TEST-N2.2 |\\\\\\\\n178:| AC-N3.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL compile to a single static binary with no runtime dependencies | TEST-N3.1 |\\\\\\\\n179:| AC-N3.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries to run | TEST-N3.2 |\\\\\\\\n188:| AC-N4.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL include what, where, why, and hint fields in every error message | TEST-N4.1 |\\\\\\\\n189:| AC-N4.2 | Event | WHEN a file operation fails (exit code 3), the system SHALL include the rendered template content in the error output so the caller can fall back to manual editing | TEST-N4.2 |\\\\\\\\n190:| AC-N4.3 | Event | WHEN a template rendering error occurs, the system SHALL report the template file path and the line number of the error | TEST-N4.3 |\\\\\\\\n191:| AC-N4.4 | Event | WHEN a variable validation error occurs, the system SHALL report the variable name, expected type, and actual value provided | TEST-N4.4 |\\\\\\\\n200:| AC-N5.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL exit with code 0 on success, 1 for recipe validation errors, 2 for template rendering errors, 3 for file operation errors, 4 for variable validation errors | TEST-N5.1 |\\\\\\\\n201:| AC-N5.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL use the exit code of the first error encountered when multiple errors occur | TEST-N5.2 |\\\\\\\\n210:| AC-N6.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL execute file operations in the order they appear in the recipe's `files` array | TEST-N6.1 |\\\\\\\\n211:| AC-N6.2 | Event | WHEN an inject operation targets a file created by an earlier create operation in the same recipe, the system SHALL find and operate on the newly created file | TEST-N6.2 |\\\\\\\\n364:| FR-1 | AC-1.1 through AC-1.12 | spec::fr1::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\n365:| FR-2 | AC-2.1 through AC-2.11 | spec::fr2::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\n366:| FR-3 | AC-3.1 through AC-3.18 | spec::fr3::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\n367:| FR-4 | AC-4.1 through AC-4.8 | spec::fr4::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\n368:| FR-5 | AC-5.1 through AC-5.12 | spec::fr5::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\n369:| FR-6 | AC-6.1 through AC-6.9 | spec::fr6::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\n370:| FR-7 | AC-7.1 through AC-7.6 | spec::fr7::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\n371:| NFR-1 | AC-N1.1, AC-N1.2 | spec::nfr1::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\n372:| NFR-2 | AC-N2.1, AC-N2.2 | spec::nfr2::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\n373:| NFR-3 | AC-N3.1, AC-N3.2 | spec::nfr3::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\n374:| NFR-4 | AC-N4.1 through AC-N4.4 | spec::nfr4::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\n375:| NFR-5 | AC-N5.1, AC-N5.2 | spec::nfr5::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\n376:| NFR-6 | AC-N6.1, AC-N6.2 | spec::nfr6::* | PENDING
