--files'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"src/main.rs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCLAUDE.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\njig.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nAGENTS.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\njustfile\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCargo.toml\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nREADME.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCargo.lock\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nscripts/ws-review.sh\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nscripts/lib/workflow-lib.sh\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nscripts/ws-init.sh\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nscripts/ws-plan.sh\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nscripts/ws-execute.sh\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nscripts/ws-plan-review.sh\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nscripts/ws-consolidate.sh\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nscripts/validate.sh\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-claude-20260403-114234.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-114234.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/NARRATIVE.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/improve/SKILL.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/improve/references/hook-examples.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/improve/references/extension-points.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/improve/references/agent-teams.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/improve/references/agent-template.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/improve/references/skill-template.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/improve/references/best-practices.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/improve/references/gotchas.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/clean-copy/SKILL.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/audit/SKILL.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/velocity/SKILL.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/exec/claude-plan-20260402-223619.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/exec/claude-plan-20260402-223424.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/velocity/references/evaluation.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/SHARED-CONTEXT.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndocs/INVARIANTS.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndocs/ARCHITECTURE.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nskills/upgrade/SKILL.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n     8\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"agent_message\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"I found the docs in `docs/workstreams/core-engine/`. Next I’m extracting the exact section text and line numbers from `SPEC.md`, `PLAN.md`, `INVARIANTS.md`, and `ARCHITECTURE.md` so each finding is anchored precisely.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n     9\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.started\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":null,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"in_progress\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n    10\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"     1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t# SPEC.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t> Workstream: core-engine\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     4\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t> Last updated: 2026-04-02\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     5\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t> Scope: v0.1 (Phases A-D from ARCHITECTURE.md)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     6\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     7\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Overview\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     8\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     9\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tThe core engine workstream delivers the minimum pipeline that makes jig useful: parse a recipe, validate variables, render templates, execute create and inject operations, and report results. This is the v0.1 feature set — a working CLI that an LLM can call to produce files from recipes.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    10\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    11\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOut of scope for this workstream: replace operations, patch operations, scope detection, workflows, libraries, scan/infer/check, custom filters via shell, .jigrc.yaml config.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    12\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    13\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Requirements\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    14\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    15\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Functional Requirements\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    16\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    17\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### FR-1: Recipe Parsing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    18\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    19\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDeserialize a YAML recipe file into a validated internal representation. Resolve template paths relative to the recipe file location. Reject structurally invalid recipes with clear errors.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    20\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    21\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    22\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    23\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    24\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-1.1 | Event | WHEN a valid recipe YAML is provided, the system SHALL parse it into a Recipe struct containing name, description, variables, and files | TEST-1.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    25\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-1.2 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares variables with type, required, default, description, values (enum), and items (array), the system SHALL parse all fields into the VariableDecl struct | TEST-1.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    26\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-1.3 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares `create` file operations with template, to, and skip_if_exists fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Create | TEST-1.3 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    27\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-1.4 | Event | WHEN a recipe declares `inject` file operations with template, inject, after/before/prepend/append, at, and skip_if fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Inject | TEST-1.4 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    28\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-1.5 | Unwanted | IF the recipe YAML is malformed (invalid YAML syntax), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and an error message identifying the parse failure location | TEST-1.5 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    29\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-1.6 | Unwanted | IF a required field is missing from the recipe (e.g., files with no template), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and name the missing field | TEST-1.6 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    30\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-1.7 | Event | WHEN a recipe references template files, the system SHALL resolve those paths relative to the recipe file location, not the working directory | TEST-1.7 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    31\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-1.8 | Unwanted | IF a referenced template file does not exist at the resolved path, the system SHALL exit with code 1 and report which template is missing and where it looked | TEST-1.8 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    32\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-1.9 | Event | WHEN the recipe has optional metadata fields (name, description), the system SHALL accept recipes with or without them | TEST-1.9 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    33\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    34\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### FR-2: Variable Validation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    35\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    36\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tAccept variables as JSON from multiple sources, merge them with defined precedence, and type-check against recipe declarations.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    37\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    38\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    39\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    40\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    41\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-2.1 | Event | WHEN `--vars` is provided with a JSON string, the system SHALL parse it as the variable input | TEST-2.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    42\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-2.2 | Event | WHEN `--vars-file` is provided with a path to a JSON file, the system SHALL read and parse the file as variable input | TEST-2.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    43\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-2.3 | Event | WHEN `--vars-stdin` is provided, the system SHALL read JSON from stdin as variable input | TEST-2.3 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    44\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-2.4 | Event | WHEN multiple variable sources are provided, the system SHALL merge with precedence: recipe defaults < vars-file < vars-stdin < inline --vars | TEST-2.4 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    45\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-2.5 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared as `required: true` and no value is provided (after merging), the system SHALL exit with code 4 naming the missing variable and providing a hint | TEST-2.5 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    46\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-2.6 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared with a `default` and no value is provided, the system SHALL use the default value | TEST-2.6 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    47\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-2.7 | Unwanted | IF a provided variable value does not match its declared type (e.g., string given for number, object given for array), the system SHALL exit with code 4 with expected vs actual type | TEST-2.7 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    48\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-2.8 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared as `type: enum` with `values: [a, b, c]`, the system SHALL reject values not in the allowed set with exit code 4 | TEST-2.8 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    49\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-2.9 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared as `type: array` with `items: string`, the system SHALL validate that each array element matches the item type | TEST-2.9 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    50\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-2.10 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accept all six variable types: string, number, boolean, array, object, enum | TEST-2.10 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    51\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    52\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### FR-3: Template Rendering\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    53\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    54\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tRender Jinja2 templates using minijinja with registered built-in filters. Templates are loaded from recipe-relative paths.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    55\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    56\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    57\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    58\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    59\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.1 | Event | WHEN a template is rendered with a variables context, the system SHALL substitute all `{{ variable }}` expressions with their values | TEST-3.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    60\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.2 | Event | WHEN a template contains `{% if %}` / `{% else %}` / `{% endif %}` blocks, the system SHALL evaluate them correctly against the variable values | TEST-3.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    61\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.3 | Event | WHEN a template contains `{% for item in list %}` loops, the system SHALL iterate and render the body for each element | TEST-3.3 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    62\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.4 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL register and support all 13 built-in filters: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join | TEST-3.4 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    63\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.5 | Event | WHEN `snakecase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.5 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    64\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.6 | Event | WHEN `camelcase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"bookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.6 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    65\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.7 | Event | WHEN `pascalcase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.7 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    66\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.8 | Event | WHEN `kebabcase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking-service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.8 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    67\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.9 | Event | WHEN `replace` is applied as `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a.b.c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\| replace('.', '/')`, the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a/b/c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.9 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    68\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.10 | Event | WHEN `pluralize` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotel\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.10 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    69\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.11 | Event | WHEN `singularize` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotel\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.11 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    70\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.12 | Event | WHEN `quote` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` (with literal quotes) | TEST-3.12 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    71\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.13 | Event | WHEN `indent(4)` is applied to a multiline string, the system SHALL indent each line by 4 spaces | TEST-3.13 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    72\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.14 | Event | WHEN `join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")` is applied to `[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"]`, the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a, b, c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.14 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    73\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.15 | Event | WHEN `{# comment #}` appears in a template, the system SHALL strip it from the output | TEST-3.15 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    74\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.16 | Event | WHEN `{% raw %}...{% endraw %}` appears in a template, the system SHALL output the content literally without interpreting Jinja2 syntax | TEST-3.16 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    75\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.17 | Unwanted | IF a template references an undefined variable, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and include a \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" hint when a close match exists | TEST-3.17 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    76\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-3.18 | Unwanted | IF a template has a Jinja2 syntax error, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and report the template file path and line number | TEST-3.18 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    77\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    78\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### FR-4: Create Operation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    79\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    80\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tRender a template and write it to a new file path. Support templated output paths, automatic directory creation, and skip-if-exists idempotency.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    81\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    82\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    83\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    84\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    85\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-4.1 | Event | WHEN a create operation executes, the system SHALL render the template and write the result to the path specified in `to` | TEST-4.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    86\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-4.2 | Event | WHEN the `to` path contains Jinja2 expressions (e.g., `tests/{{ module \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\| replace('.', '/') }}/test_{{ class_name \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\| snakecase }}.py`), the system SHALL render the path before writing | TEST-4.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    87\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-4.3 | Event | WHEN parent directories in the `to` path do not exist, the system SHALL create them automatically | TEST-4.3 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    88\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-4.4 | Event | WHEN `skip_if_exists: true` and the target file already exists, the system SHALL skip the operation and report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with a reason | TEST-4.4 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    89\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-4.5 | Unwanted | IF `skip_if_exists: false` (default) and the target file already exists and `--force` is not set, the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting the conflict | TEST-4.5 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    90\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-4.6 | Event | WHEN `--force` is set and the target file already exists, the system SHALL overwrite the file regardless of skip_if_exists | TEST-4.6 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    91\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-4.7 | Event | WHEN `--base-dir` is set, the system SHALL resolve `to` paths relative to the base directory instead of the working directory | TEST-4.7 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    92\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-4.8 | Event | WHEN a create operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with the path and line count | TEST-4.8 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    93\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    94\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### FR-5: Inject Operation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    95\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    96\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tRender a template and insert the result into an existing file at a location determined by regex pattern matching. Support idempotency via skip_if.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    97\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    98\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    99\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   100\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   101\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-5.1 | Event | WHEN `after: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line after the first matching line | TEST-5.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   102\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-5.2 | Event | WHEN `before: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line before the first matching line | TEST-5.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   103\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-5.3 | Event | WHEN `prepend: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very beginning of the file | TEST-5.3 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   104\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-5.4 | Event | WHEN `append: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very end of the file | TEST-5.4 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   105\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-5.5 | Event | WHEN `at: last` is specified with `after` or `before`, the system SHALL use the last regex match instead of the first | TEST-5.5 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   106\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-5.6 | Event | WHEN `at: first` (default) is specified, the system SHALL use the first regex match | TEST-5.6 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   107\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-5.7 | Event | WHEN `skip_if: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"string\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified and the string exists in the target file, the system SHALL skip the injection and report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with a reason | TEST-5.7 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   108\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-5.8 | Unwanted | IF the regex pattern matches zero lines in the target file, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the pattern, the file path, and a hint | TEST-5.8 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   109\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-5.9 | Unwanted | IF the target file for injection does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the missing file path | TEST-5.9 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   110\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-5.10 | Event | WHEN an inject operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with the path, location description, and line count | TEST-5.10 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   111\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-5.11 | Event | WHEN the inject path contains Jinja2 expressions, the system SHALL render the path before resolving it | TEST-5.11 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   112\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   113\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### FR-6: Output Formatting\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   114\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   115\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tReport operation results as JSON (stdout, for LLM callers) or human-readable text (stderr, for terminal users). Auto-detect the appropriate mode.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   116\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   117\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   118\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   119\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   120\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-6.1 | State | WHILE stdout is a TTY, the system SHALL output human-readable colored text to stderr by default | TEST-6.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   121\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-6.2 | State | WHILE stdout is piped (not a TTY), the system SHALL output JSON to stdout by default | TEST-6.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   122\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-6.3 | Event | WHEN `--json` is specified, the system SHALL force JSON output to stdout regardless of TTY detection | TEST-6.3 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   123\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-6.4 | Event | WHEN `--quiet` is specified, the system SHALL suppress all output except errors | TEST-6.4 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   124\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-6.5 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include an `operations` array with action, path, lines (for success), reason (for skip), or error details (for error) for each operation | TEST-6.5 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   125\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-6.6 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include `files_written` and `files_skipped` summary arrays | TEST-6.6 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   126\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-6.7 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified, the system SHALL include rendered template content in the output | TEST-6.7 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   127\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-6.8 | Event | WHEN `--dry-run` is specified, the system SHALL produce identical output format but write no files to disk | TEST-6.8 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   128\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   129\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### FR-7: CLI Interface\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   130\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   131\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tProvide four subcommands for v0.1: `validate`, `vars`, `render`, and `run`. Accept global options for variable sources, output control, and execution modes.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   132\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   133\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   134\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   135\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   136\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-7.1 | Event | WHEN `jig validate <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL parse the recipe and report whether it is valid, listing variables and operations | TEST-7.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   137\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-7.2 | Event | WHEN `jig vars <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL output the expected variables as a JSON object with type, required, default, and description fields | TEST-7.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   138\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-7.3 | Event | WHEN `jig render <template> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL render the template with the given variables and output the result to stdout | TEST-7.3 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   139\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-7.4 | Event | WHEN `jig render` is invoked with `--to <path>`, the system SHALL write the rendered output to the specified file instead of stdout | TEST-7.4 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   140\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-7.5 | Event | WHEN `jig run <recipe> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL execute all file operations in the recipe in declaration order | TEST-7.5 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   141\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-7.6 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accept global options: `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose` | TEST-7.6 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   142\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   143\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Non-Functional Requirements\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   144\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   145\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### NFR-1: Deterministic Output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   146\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   147\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tSame inputs must always produce the same outputs, with no randomness or environment-dependent behavior in rendered content.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   148\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   149\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   150\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   151\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   152\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N1.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL produce byte-identical output when given the same recipe, variables, and existing files across multiple runs | TEST-N1.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   153\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N1.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not include timestamps, random values, or machine-specific identifiers in rendered output | TEST-N1.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   154\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   155\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### NFR-2: Idempotent Operations\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   156\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   157\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tRunning the same recipe twice with the same variables must produce no changes on the second run.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   158\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   159\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   160\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   161\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   162\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N2.1 | Event | WHEN a recipe is run a second time with the same variables and the same existing files, the system SHALL report all operations as `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with reasons | TEST-N2.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   163\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N2.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not produce duplicate content when create uses `skip_if_exists: true` or inject uses `skip_if` | TEST-N2.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   164\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   165\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### NFR-3: Single Static Binary\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   166\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   167\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tZero runtime dependencies. One binary that runs on macOS and Linux without Python, Node, JVM, or any shared libraries.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   168\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   169\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   170\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   171\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   172\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N3.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL compile to a single static binary with no runtime dependencies | TEST-N3.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   173\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N3.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries to run | TEST-N3.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   174\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   175\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### NFR-4: Structured Errors with Rendered Content\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   176\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   177\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tErrors must include what/where/why/hint. File operation errors must include the rendered content so callers can fall back.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   178\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   179\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   180\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   181\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   182\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N4.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL include what, where, why, and hint fields in every error message | TEST-N4.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   183\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N4.2 | Event | WHEN a file operation fails (exit code 3), the system SHALL include the rendered template content in the error output so the caller can fall back to manual editing | TEST-N4.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   184\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N4.3 | Event | WHEN a template rendering error occurs, the system SHALL report the template file path and the line number of the error | TEST-N4.3 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   185\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N4.4 | Event | WHEN a variable validation error occurs, the system SHALL report the variable name, expected type, and actual value provided | TEST-N4.4 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   186\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   187\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### NFR-5: Stable Exit Codes\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   188\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   189\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tExit codes are API. They must be deterministic and match the defined mapping.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   190\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   191\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   192\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   193\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   194\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N5.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL exit with code 0 on success, 1 for recipe validation errors, 2 for template rendering errors, 3 for file operation errors, 4 for variable validation errors | TEST-N5.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   195\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N5.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL use the exit code of the first error encountered when multiple errors occur | TEST-N5.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   196\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   197\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### NFR-6: Ordered Execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   198\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   199\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tFile operations must execute in declaration order so later operations can depend on files created by earlier ones.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   200\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   201\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   202\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   203\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   204\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N6.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL execute file operations in the order they appear in the recipe's `files` array | TEST-N6.1 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   205\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| AC-N6.2 | Event | WHEN an inject operation targets a file created by an earlier create operation in the same recipe, the system SHALL find and operate on the newly created file | TEST-N6.2 |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   206\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   207\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Interfaces\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   208\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   209\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Public API (CLI)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   210\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   211\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   212\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig validate <recipe>\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   213\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig vars <recipe>\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   214\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig render <template> [--to <path>] --vars '<json>'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   215\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig run <recipe> --vars '<json>' [options]\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   216\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   217\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOptions:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   218\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  --vars <json>        Inline variables as JSON string\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   219\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  --vars-file <path>   Variables from a JSON file\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   220\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  --vars-stdin         Read variables from stdin\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   221\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  --dry-run            Preview without writing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   222\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  --json               Force JSON output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   223\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  --quiet              Suppress non-error output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   224\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  --force              Overwrite existing files\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   225\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  --base-dir <path>    Resolve output paths from this directory\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   226\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  --verbose            Include rendered content in output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   227\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   228\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   229\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### JSON Output Schema\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   230\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   231\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```json\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   232\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   233\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"operations\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": [\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   234\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"lines\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 42},\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   235\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"location\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"after:^# fixtures\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"lines\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 3},\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   236\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"reason\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip_if matched: BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   237\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hint\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"rendered_content\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   238\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  ],\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   239\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"files_written\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": [\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"],\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   240\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"files_skipped\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": [\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"]\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   241\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   242\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   243\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   244\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Internal Interfaces\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   245\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   246\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```rust\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   247\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t// Recipe → Variable Validator\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   248\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tfn validate_variables(decls: &IndexMap<String, VariableDecl>, provided: Value) -> Result<Value, JigError>;\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   249\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   250\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t// Recipe + Variables → Renderer\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   251\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tfn render_template(env: &Environment, template_name: &str, vars: &Value) -> Result<String, JigError>;\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   252\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   253\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t// Rendered content + FileOp → Operation Executor\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   254\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tfn execute_operation(op: &FileOp, rendered: &str, ctx: &ExecutionContext) -> OpResult;\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   255\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   256\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t// Vec<OpResult> → Output Formatter\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   257\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tfn format_output(results: &[OpResult], mode: OutputMode) -> String;\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   258\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   259\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   260\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Data Model\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   261\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   262\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```rust\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   263\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tstruct Recipe {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   264\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    name: Option<String>,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   265\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    description: Option<String>,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   266\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    variables: IndexMap<String, VariableDecl>,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   267\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    files: Vec<FileOp>,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   268\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   269\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   270\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tstruct VariableDecl {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   271\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    var_type: VarType,          // string, number, boolean, array, object, enum\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   272\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    required: bool,             // default: false\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   273\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    default: Option<Value>,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   274\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    description: Option<String>,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   275\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    values: Option<Vec<String>>, // for enum type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   276\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    items: Option<VarType>,      // for array type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   277\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   278\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   279\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tenum FileOp {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   280\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Create { template: String, to: String, skip_if_exists: bool },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   281\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Inject { template: String, inject: String, mode: InjectMode, skip_if: Option<String> },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   282\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   283\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   284\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tenum InjectMode {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   285\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    After { pattern: String, at: MatchPosition },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   286\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Before { pattern: String, at: MatchPosition },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   287\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Prepend,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   288\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Append,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   289\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   290\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   291\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tenum MatchPosition { First, Last }\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   292\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   293\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tenum OpResult {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   294\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Success { action: &'static str, path: PathBuf, lines: usize },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   295\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Skip { path: PathBuf, reason: String },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   296\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Error { path: PathBuf, error: StructuredError, rendered_content: String },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   297\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   298\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   299\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tenum JigError {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   300\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    RecipeValidation(String),     // exit 1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   301\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    TemplateRendering(String),    // exit 2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   302\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    FileOperation(String),        // exit 3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   303\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    VariableValidation(String),   // exit 4\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   304\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   305\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   306\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   307\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Error Handling\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   308\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   309\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Error Category | Exit Code | Examples |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   310\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----------------|-----------|----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   311\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Recipe validation | 1 | Malformed YAML, missing fields, missing template files |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   312\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Template rendering | 2 | Undefined variable, Jinja2 syntax error |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   313\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| File operation | 3 | Target file missing (inject), file exists (create), regex no match |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   314\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Variable validation | 4 | Missing required, wrong type, invalid enum value |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   315\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   316\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tEvery error includes: what happened, where (file/variable/line), why (expected vs actual), and a hint for the caller.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   317\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   318\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tFile operation errors additionally include `rendered_content` so the LLM caller can fall back to manual editing with the Edit tool.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   319\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   320\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Testing Strategy\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   321\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   322\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Spec tests:** One or more tests per AC-* criterion above, namespaced as `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   323\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Invariant tests:** Determinism (run twice, compare output), idempotency (run twice, second run all skips), exit code stability\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   324\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Integration fixtures:** Each fixture directory contains recipe.yaml, vars.json, templates/, existing/ (optional), expected/, expected_output.json (optional), expected_exit_code (optional)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   325\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Snapshot tests:** `insta` for rendered template output, JSON output format, and error messages\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   326\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   327\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Requirement Traceability\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   328\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   329\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Requirement | Criteria | Test | Status |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   330\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|-------------|----------|------|--------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   331\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| FR-1 | AC-1.1 through AC-1.9 | spec::fr1::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   332\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| FR-2 | AC-2.1 through AC-2.10 | spec::fr2::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   333\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| FR-3 | AC-3.1 through AC-3.18 | spec::fr3::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   334\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| FR-4 | AC-4.1 through AC-4.8 | spec::fr4::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   335\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| FR-5 | AC-5.1 through AC-5.11 | spec::fr5::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   336\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| FR-6 | AC-6.1 through AC-6.8 | spec::fr6::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   337\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| FR-7 | AC-7.1 through AC-7.6 | spec::fr7::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   338\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| NFR-1 | AC-N1.1, AC-N1.2 | spec::nfr1::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   339\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| NFR-2 | AC-N2.1, AC-N2.2 | spec::nfr2::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   340\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| NFR-3 | AC-N3.1, AC-N3.2 | spec::nfr3::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   341\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| NFR-4 | AC-N4.1 through AC-N4.4 | spec::nfr4::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   342\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| NFR-5 | AC-N5.1, AC-N5.2 | spec::nfr5::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   343\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| NFR-6 | AC-N6.1, AC-N6.2 | spec::nfr6::* | PENDING |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n    11\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.started\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_4\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":null,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"in_progress\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n    12\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.started\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_5\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/INVARIANTS.md'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":null,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"in_progress\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n    13\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_5\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/INVARIANTS.md'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"     1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t# INVARIANTS.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tProject-wide constraints. Every workstream must respect these. If a change would violate an invariant, it requires explicit discussion and sign-off before proceeding.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     4\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     5\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## I-1: Deterministic Output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     6\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     7\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tSame recipe + same variables + same existing files = same output. Always. No randomness, no timestamps, no machine-specific behavior in rendered output. Tests can assert exact equality.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     8\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     9\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## I-2: Idempotent Operations\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    10\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    11\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tEvery file operation is safe to re-run. `skip_if` and `skip_if_exists` prevent duplicate content. Running the same recipe twice with the same variables produces no changes on the second run. JSON output reports `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with a reason for every skipped operation.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    12\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    13\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## I-3: JSON In, Files Out\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    14\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    15\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tVariables are structured JSON. Output is files on disk. The CLI is non-interactive — no prompts, no wizards, no \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"press y to continue.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" An LLM (or script) can call jig without any human in the loop.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    16\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    17\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## I-4: Structured Errors with Rendered Content\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    18\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    19\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tWhen an operation fails (anchor not found, scope parse fails, file missing), jig still renders the template and includes the rendered content in the error output. The caller never has to re-derive what to insert — only where to put it. Errors include what/where/why + a hint.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    20\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    21\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## I-5: Stable Exit Codes\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    22\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    23\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tExit codes are API. They do not change between minor versions.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    24\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    25\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Code | Meaning |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    26\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|------|---------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    27\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| 0 | Success |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    28\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| 1 | Recipe validation error |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    29\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| 2 | Template rendering error |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    30\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| 3 | File operation error |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    31\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| 4 | Variable validation error |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    32\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    33\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## I-6: Single Static Binary, Zero Runtime Dependencies\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    34\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    35\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tNo Python, no Node, no JVM. One binary, runs anywhere. Startup must be fast — jig may be called dozens of times in a single LLM session.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    36\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    37\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## I-7: Templates Live With the Consumer\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    38\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    39\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tThere is no central template directory. Templates are co-located with the recipe that uses them. A recipe + its templates form a self-contained unit that can be moved, versioned, or shared independently.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    40\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    41\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## I-8: Dual Output Streams\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    42\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    43\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tJSON to stdout (machine-readable, for LLM callers). Human-readable to stderr (colored, for terminal users). Auto-detect TTY vs piped. `--json` forces JSON. `--quiet` suppresses everything except errors.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    44\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    45\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## I-9: Operations Are Ordered\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    46\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    47\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tFile operations in a recipe execute in declaration order. Later operations can depend on files created or modified by earlier operations in the same recipe run.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    48\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    49\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## I-10: Graceful Degradation to LLM\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    50\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    51\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig handles the deterministic part (rendering). When structural analysis fails (can't find anchor, can't parse scope), it fails with enough information for the LLM to fall back to its native Edit tool. jig never silently produces wrong output — it either succeeds or fails clearly.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n    14\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_4\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"     1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t# PLAN.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t> Workstream: core-engine\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     4\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t> Last updated: 2026-04-02\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     5\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t> Status: Planned\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     6\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     7\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Objective\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     8\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     9\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDeliver the minimum viable jig CLI: parse recipes, validate variables, render Jinja2 templates, execute create and inject file operations, and report structured results. This is the v0.1 feature set — the smallest thing that lets an LLM call `jig run` to produce files from a recipe instead of re-deriving boilerplate.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    10\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    11\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tCovers ARCHITECTURE.md Phases A through E (skeleton, rendering, create, inject, integration tests).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    12\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    13\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Phases\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    14\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    15\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Phase 1: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    16\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tStatus: Planned\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    17\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tTraces to: FR-1, FR-7 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-5\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    18\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    19\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tBootstrap the Rust crate, wire up clap, and make recipe parsing work end-to-end. After this phase, `jig validate` and `jig vars` are functional commands.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    20\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    21\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Milestones\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    22\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 1.1: Cargo.toml with dependencies (serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap, thiserror)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    23\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 1.2: `src/error.rs` — JigError enum with exit code mapping (0-4), structured error fields (what/where/why/hint)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    24\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 1.3: `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe, VariableDecl, VarType, FileOp structs with serde deserialization; template path resolution relative to recipe location; structural validation (missing fields, missing template files)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    25\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 1.4: `src/variables.rs` — VariableDecl types and VarType enum (types only, no validation logic yet)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    26\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 1.5: `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands; wire recipe parsing; map errors to exit codes\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    27\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 1.6: Unit tests for recipe parsing — valid recipe, missing fields, malformed YAML, missing template files, optional metadata\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    28\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    29\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Validation Criteria\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    30\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `jig validate recipe.yaml` parses the example recipe from jig.md and exits 0\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    31\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `jig validate bad.yaml` exits 1 with a clear error naming the problem\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    32\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `jig vars recipe.yaml` outputs JSON matching the SPEC schema (type, required, default, description)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    33\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Template paths resolve relative to recipe file, not cwd\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    34\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- AC-1.1 through AC-1.9 have corresponding unit tests\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    35\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- AC-N5.1 exit codes are correct for recipe validation errors\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    36\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    37\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Key Files\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    38\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `Cargo.toml`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    39\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/main.rs`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    40\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/recipe.rs`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    41\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/variables.rs` (types only)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    42\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/error.rs`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    43\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    44\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t---\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    45\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    46\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Phase 2: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    47\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tStatus: Planned\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    48\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tTraces to: FR-2, FR-3, NFR-1 (partial)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    49\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    50\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tFull variable pipeline (parse, merge, type-check) and template rendering with all 13 built-in filters. After this phase, `jig render` works.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    51\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    52\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Milestones\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    53\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 2.1: `src/variables.rs` — full validation: parse JSON from --vars/--vars-file/--vars-stdin; merge with precedence (defaults < file < stdin < inline); type-check against declarations; required field enforcement; enum validation; array item type validation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    54\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 2.2: `src/filters.rs` — all 13 built-in filters registered with minijinja: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    55\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 2.3: `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja Environment setup; template loading from recipe-relative paths; filter registration; render with variables context; structured error on undefined variable (with \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" via edit distance) and syntax errors (with file + line)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    56\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 2.4: Wire `render` subcommand in main.rs with --vars, --vars-file, --vars-stdin, --to options\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    57\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 2.5: Unit tests for variable validation — every VarType, required missing, default fallback, enum rejection, array item mismatch, merge precedence\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    58\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 2.6: Unit tests + insta snapshot tests for all 13 filters and template rendering (conditionals, loops, comments, raw blocks, undefined vars, syntax errors)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    59\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    60\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Validation Criteria\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    61\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- All 13 filters produce correct output per AC-3.4 through AC-3.14\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    62\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `jig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"class_name\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}'` renders to stdout\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    63\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Type mismatch exits 4 with expected vs actual type (AC-2.7)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    64\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Missing required variable exits 4 with variable name and hint (AC-2.5)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    65\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Merge precedence: inline --vars wins over --vars-file wins over defaults (AC-2.4)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    66\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Undefined template variable exits 2 with \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" hint (AC-3.17)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    67\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Template syntax error exits 2 with file path and line number (AC-3.18)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    68\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Same inputs produce byte-identical output across runs (AC-N1.1)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    69\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    70\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Key Files\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n 3: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    82\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tStatus: Planned\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    83\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tTraces to: FR-4, FR-6, FR-7 (complete), NFR-2 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-6\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    84\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    85\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tFile creation with templated output paths, directory auto-creation, skip_if_exists, and dual-stream output (JSON stdout / human stderr). After this phase, `jig run` works for create-only recipes.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    86\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    87\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Milestones\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    88\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 3.1: `src/operations/mod.rs` — ExecutionContext struct (base_dir, dry_run, force); OpResult enum (Success/Skip/Error); operation dispatch (create only initially)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    89\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 3.2: `src/operations/create.rs` — render `to` path as template; create parent directories; write rendered content; skip_if_exists logic; --force override; --base-dir path resolution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    90\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 3.3: `src/output.rs` — OutputMode enum (Json/Human/Quiet); TTY auto-detection; JSON serialization of operations array + files_written/files_skipped; colored human output to stderr; --verbose includes rendered content\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    91\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 3.4: Wire `run` subcommand in main.rs with --dry-run, output, quiet mode, verbose mode, dry-run\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    94\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    95\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Validation Criteria\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    96\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `jig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'` creates files at templated paths (AC-4.1, AC-4.2)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    97\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Parent directories created automatically (AC-4.3)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    98\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- skip_if_exists: true skips existing files with action:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" (AC-4.4)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    99\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Default (skip_if_exists: false) errors on existing file without --force (AC-4.5)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   100\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- --force overwrites regardless (AC-4.6)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n 123\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tContent injection into existing files via regex anchoring. All injection modes: after, before, prepend, append, with at:first/last match selection and skip_if idempotency. This completes the v0.1 operation set.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   124\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   125\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Milestones\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   126\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 4.1: `src/operations/inject.rs` — read target file; skip_if string search; regex anchor matching (after/before); at:first/at:last match selection; prepend/append modes; render inject path as template; write modified content\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   127\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 4.2: Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   128\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 4.3: Unit tests for every injection mode — after (first match), after (last match), before, prepend, append, skip_if, missing target file error, regex no-match error, templated inject path\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   129\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 4.4: Integration test: recipe with create + inject in same run (create file, then inject into it — tests ordered execution AC-N6.2)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   130\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   131\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Validation Criteria\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   132\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- after: content on line after first match (AC-5.1)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   133\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- before: content on line before first match (AC-5.2)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   134\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- prepend: content at start of file (AC-5.3)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   135\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- append: content at end of file (AC-5.4)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   136\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- at:last uses last match (AC-5.5), at:first (default) uses first (AC-5.6)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   137\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- skip_if: skips when string found in file, reports action:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" (AC-5.7)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   138\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Regex no-match exits 3 with pattern, file path, hint (AC-5.8)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   139\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Missing target file exits 3 (AC-5.9)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   140\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Inject path renders as template (AC-5.11)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   141\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Create-then-inject in same recipe works (AC-N6.2)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   142\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Second run with skip_if shows all skips — no duplicate content (AC-N2.2)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   143\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   144\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Key Files\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   145\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/operations/inject.rs`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n
