Phase 5: Integration Test Framework\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   154\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tStatus: Planned\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   155\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   156\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   157\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tFixture-based integration test harness that makes adding new test cases a matter of adding a directory. Snapshot tests for all output formats. This phase validates the entire v0.1 pipeline end-to-end.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   158\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   159\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Milestones\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   160\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 5.1: Test harness in `tests/integration.rs` — fixture discovery; copy existing/ to temp dir; run jig as subprocess; diff output against expected/; assert JSON output against expected_output.json; assert exit code against expected_exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   161\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 5.2: Fixtures for create operations — simple create, templated path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, directory creation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   162\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 5.3: Fixtures for inject operations — after/before/prepend/append, at:first/at:last, skip_if\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   163\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 5.4: Fixtures for error cases — missing vars, bad type, missing template, missing target file, regex no-match, malformed YAML, file exists without force\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   164\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 5.5: Fixtures for combined operations — create + inject in one recipe, multi-file recipe, idempotency (run twice)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   165\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 5.6: insta snapshot tests for JSON output format, human output format, error message format\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   166\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- [ ] 5.7: Determinism test — run same recipe twice, assert byte-identical output (AC-N1.1)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   167\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   168\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Validation Criteria\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   169\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `cargo test` runs all unit + integration + snapshot tests green\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   170\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Every operation mode has at least one fixture\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   171\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Every error exit code (1-4) has at least one fixture\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   172\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Adding a new test case requires only a new directory, no code changes\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   173\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Idempotency fixture: second run produces all skips, no file changes\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   174\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   175\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Key Files\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   176\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `tests/integration.rs`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   177\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `tests/fixtures/` (directory tree)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   178\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   179\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t#### Dependencies\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   180\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- insta crate for snapshot testing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   181\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- assert_cmd or similar for subprocess testing (or raw std::process::Command)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   182\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   183\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Dependencies\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   184\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   185\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   186\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   187\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   188\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Key Decisions\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   189\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   190\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   191\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----------|--------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   192\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Operations as enum, not trait objects | `FileOp` enum with match dispatch | Only 2 variants in v0.1 (create, inject). Trait abstraction adds complexity for no benefit until user-defined operations exist, which isn't planned. (ARCHITECTURE D-4) |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   193\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages (ARCHITECTURE D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   194\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable (ARCHITECTURE D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   195\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism (ARCHITECTURE D-5, I-1) |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   196\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate, good Display/Error trait impls |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   197\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   198\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   199\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling. Acceptable dependency for correctness. |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   200\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) against declared variable names | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Hand-rolling edit distance serves I-10 (graceful degradation) — the error tells the caller exactly what to fix. |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   201\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves, even for ones minijinja provides (replace, indent, join) | We control exact behavior. No surprises from upstream minijinja changes. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   202\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. Templates reference variables not created files. (D-1) |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   203\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Extra variables pass through | Don't error or warn on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness here breaks real usage. |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   204\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later operations may depend on earlier ones (e.g., inject targets a prior create). (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   205\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   206\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Risks / Open Questions\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   207\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   208\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **TTY detection on all platforms**: Need to verify `std::io::IsTerminal` works correctly on macOS + Linux. Rust 1.70+ has `IsTerminal` in std — prefer that over external crate.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   209\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **stdin variable reading conflicts**: If --vars-stdin is used, stdin is consumed for variables. This is fine since jig is non-interactive (I-3), but document that stdin can only be used for one purpose per invocation.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   210\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Large file handling for inject**: The current design reads the entire target file into memory for injection. This is fine for source files (typically <10K lines) but should be documented as a known limitation.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   211\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   212\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Resolved Questions\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   213\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   214\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   215\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names. minijinja doesn't provide this. Serves I-10.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   216\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. We own the behavior, upstream changes don't affect us. Serves I-1.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   217\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   218\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Execution Order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   219\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   220\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   221\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tPhase 1 ──► Phase 2 ──► Phase 3 ──► Phase 4 ──► Phase 5\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   222\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tskeleton     vars+render  create+output inject     integration\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   223\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                                                    tests\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   224\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   225\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   226\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tEach phase produces a working, testable artifact. No phase depends on \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"wiring up later.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" The CLI grows incrementally:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   227\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- After Phase 1: `jig validate`, `jig vars`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   228\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- After Phase 2: + `jig render`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   229\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- After Phase 3: + `jig run` (create only)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   230\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- After Phase 4: + `jig run` (create + inject) — **v0.1 complete**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   231\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- After Phase 5: Full test coverage validates the release\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n    15\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.started\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_6\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/ARCHITECTURE.md'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":null,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"in_progress\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n    16\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_6\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/ARCHITECTURE.md'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"     1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t# ARCHITECTURE.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tTarget architecture for jig, the iterative build strategy to get there, and the evaluation criteria at each gate. This document grounds all workstream planning.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     4\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     5\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## System Overview\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     6\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     7\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig is a linear pipeline: parse CLI args, load recipe, validate variables, render templates, execute file operations, format output, exit. Every stage is independently testable. No stage has side effects except operation execution (which writes files).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     8\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     9\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    10\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tCLI Input\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    11\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    12\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  v\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    13\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t+------------------+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    14\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| CLI Parser       |  args, flags, var sources\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    15\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| (clap)           |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    16\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t+------------------+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    17\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    18\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  v\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    19\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t+------------------+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    20\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Recipe Loader    |  YAML -> Recipe struct\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    21\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| (serde_yaml)     |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    22\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t+------------------+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    23\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    24\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  v\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    25\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t+------------------+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    26\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Variable         |  JSON input -> typed, validated Variables\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    27\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Validator        |  merge: defaults < file < stdin < inline\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    28\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| (serde_json)     |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    29\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t+------------------+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    30\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    31\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  v\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    32\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t+------------------+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    33\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Template         |  template + variables -> rendered string\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    34\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Renderer         |  custom filters registered here\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    35\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| (minijinja)      |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    36\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t+------------------+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    37\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    38\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  v\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    39\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t+------------------+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    40\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Operation        |  rendered content + file operation spec\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    41\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Executor         |  -> file writes (or dry-run recording)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    42\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t+------------------+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    43\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    44\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  v\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    45\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t+------------------+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    46\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Output           |  results -> JSON (stdout) or human (stderr)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    47\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Formatter        |  TTY auto-detect, --json, --quiet\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    48\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t+------------------+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    49\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    50\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  v\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    51\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tExit Code (0-4)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    52\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    53\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    54\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Module Map\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    55\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    56\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/main.rs` — Entry Point\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    57\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    58\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: CLI argument parsing via clap. Wires the pipeline together.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    59\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: every other module (it's the composition root).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    60\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    61\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tResponsibilities:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    62\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Define CLI commands: `run`, `render`, `validate`, `vars`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    63\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Parse `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    64\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Call pipeline stages in order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    65\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Map errors to exit codes\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    66\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    67\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe Parsing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    68\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    69\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: `Recipe`, `VariableDecl`, `FileOp` structs. YAML deserialization.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    70\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: serde, serde_yaml.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    71\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    72\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tResponsibilities:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    73\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Deserialize `recipe.yaml` into `Recipe` struct\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    74\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Validate structural correctness (required fields present, template files exist relative to recipe)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    75\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Resolve template paths relative to the recipe file location (I-7: templates live with the consumer)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    76\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Distinguish operation types: create vs inject vs replace vs patch\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    77\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    78\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tKey types:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    79\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```rust\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    80\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tstruct Recipe {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    81\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    name: Option<String>,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    82\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    description: Option<String>,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    83\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    variables: IndexMap<String, VariableDecl>,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    84\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    files: Vec<FileOp>,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    85\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    86\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    87\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tstruct VariableDecl {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    88\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    var_type: VarType,       // string, number, boolean, array, object, enum\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    89\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    required: bool,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    90\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    default: Option<Value>,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    91\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    description: Option<String>,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    92\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    values: Option<Vec<String>>,  // for enum type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    93\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    items: Option<VarType>,       // for array type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    94\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    95\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    96\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tenum FileOp {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    97\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Create { template: String, to: String, skip_if_exists: bool },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    98\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Inject { template: String, inject: String, mode: InjectMode, skip_if: Option<String> },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    99\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Replace { template: String, replace: String, spec: ReplaceSpec, fallback: Fallback },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   100\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Patch { template: String, patch: String, anchor: Anchor, skip_if: Option<String> },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   101\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   102\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   103\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   104\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/variables.rs` — Variable Validation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   105\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   106\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Variable merging, type checking, default application.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   107\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: serde_json, recipe types.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   108\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   109\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tResponsibilities:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   110\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Parse JSON from multiple sources (inline string, file, stdin)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   111\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Merge with precedence: recipe defaults < vars-file < vars-stdin < inline --vars\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   112\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Type-check each value against its `VariableDecl`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   113\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Validate required fields are present\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   114\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Validate enum values against allowed set\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   115\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Validate array item types\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   116\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Produce clear error messages with hints (I-4)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   117\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   118\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/renderer.rs` — Template Rendering\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   119\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   120\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: minijinja `Environment` setup, filter registration, template loading.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   121\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: minijinja, heck (for case conversion filters).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   122\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   123\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tResponsibilities:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   124\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Create minijinja `Environment` with templates loaded from recipe-relative paths\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   125\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Register built-in filters: `snakecase`, `camelcase`, `pascalcase`, `kebabcase`, `upper`, `lower`, `capitalize`, `replace`, `pluralize`, `singularize`, `quote`, `indent`, `join`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   126\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Render a template with a variables context\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   127\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Return `Result<String>` — never silently produce wrong output (I-10)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   128\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   129\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDesign note: The renderer is stateless per invocation. Create environment, register filters, load templates, render, return. No caching needed at v0.1 scale.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   130\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   131\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/operations/mod.rs` — Operation Dispatch\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   132\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   133\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: `Operation` trait, dispatch logic, dry-run tracking.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   134\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: operation implementations, renderer output.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   135\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   136\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```rust\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   137\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ttrait Operation {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   138\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    fn execute(&self, rendered: &str, ctx: &ExecutionContext) -> Result<OpResult>;\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   139\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   140\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   141\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tstruct ExecutionContext {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   142\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    base_dir: PathBuf,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   143\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    dry_run: bool,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   144\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    force: bool,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   145\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   146\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   147\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tenum OpResult {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   148\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Success { action: &'static str, path: PathBuf, lines: usize },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   149\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Skip { path: PathBuf, reason: String },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   150\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Error { path: PathBuf, error: StructuredError, rendered_content: String },\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   151\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   152\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   153\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   154\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOperations execute in declaration order (I-9). Each operation receives the already-rendered content — rendering happens before execution, not during.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   155\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   156\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/operations/create.rs` — Create Operation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   157\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   158\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: New file creation logic.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   159\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: std::fs.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   160\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   161\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tResponsibilities:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   162\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Render the `to` path as a template (it can contain `{{ variables }}`)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   163\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Create parent directories as needed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   164\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Write rendered content to the target path\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   165\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `skip_if_exists`: if true and target exists, return `OpResult::Skip`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   166\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- If not `--force` and target exists and `skip_if_exists` is false, return error\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   167\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   168\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/operations/inject.rs` — Inject Operation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   169\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   170\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Content injection into existing files.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   171\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: regex.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   172\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   173\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tResponsibilities:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   174\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Read the target file\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   175\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `skip_if`: search for string in file content, skip if found (I-2: idempotency)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   176\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Find the anchor line using regex pattern\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   177\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `at: first` (default) or `at: last` — which match to use\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   178\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Insert rendered content `after` or `before` the anchor line\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   179\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `prepend`: insert at beginning of file\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   180\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `append`: insert at end of file\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   181\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Write the modified content back\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   182\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   183\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/operations/replace.rs` — Replace Operation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   184\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   185\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Region replacement in existing files.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   186\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: regex.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   187\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   188\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tResponsibilities:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   189\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `between`: find start pattern, find end pattern, replace everything between them (exclusive of markers)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   190\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `pattern`: find matching line(s), replace them entirely\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   191\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `fallback`: what to do when pattern not found — `append`, `prepend`, `skip`, `error` (default)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   192\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Preserve line endings and file encoding\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   193\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   194\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/operations/patch.rs` — Patch Operation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   195\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   196\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Scope-aware content insertion. Delegates scope detection.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   197\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: scope module, regex.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   198\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   199\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tResponsibilities:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   200\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Find anchor pattern in file\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   201\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Determine scope boundaries using the scope module\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   202\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Apply `find` narrowing within the scope\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   203\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Insert rendered content at the specified position\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   204\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `skip_if`: idempotency check within the scope\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   205\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   206\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/scope/mod.rs` — Scope Detection Dispatch\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   207\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   208\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Scope type classification, dispatch to indent vs delimiter strategies.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   209\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: indent, delimiter, position submodules.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   210\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   211\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```rust\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   212\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tenum ScopeType {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   213\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Line,              // just the matched line\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   214\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Block,             // indent-based block\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   215\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    ClassBody,         // indent-based class body\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   216\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    FunctionBody,      // indent-based function body\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   217\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    FunctionSignature, // delimiter: ( to )\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   218\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Braces,            // delimiter: { to }\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   219\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Brackets,          // delimiter: [ to ]\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   220\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    Parens,            // delimiter: ( to )\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   221\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   222\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   223\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   224\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/scope/indent.rs` — Indentation-Based Scope Detection\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   225\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   226\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Scope detection for indentation-significant languages (Python, YAML).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   227\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   228\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tAlgorithm:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   229\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t1. Find the anchor line's indentation level\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   230\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t2. Walk forward: scope includes all lines indented deeper than the anchor\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   231\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t3. Scope ends when indentation returns to the same or shallower level\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   232\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t4. Handle blank lines within the scope (they don't end it)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   233\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   234\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — Delimiter-Based Scope Detection\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   235\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   236\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Scope detection for delimiter-based languages (C-family, JSON, TypeScript).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   237\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   238\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tAlgorithm:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   239\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t1. From the anchor line, find the opening delimiter\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   240\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t2. Count nesting depth (handle nested delimiters)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   241\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t3. Scope is everything between opening and closing delimiter (exclusive)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   242\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t4. Handle string literals (don't count delimiters inside strings)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   243\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   244\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/scope/position.rs` — Semantic Position Heuristics\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   245\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   246\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Position determination within a scope.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   247\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   248\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Position | Heuristic |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   249\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   250\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `before` | First line of scope |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   251\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `after` | Last line of scope |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   252\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `before_close` | Line before closing delimiter or dedent |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   253\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `after_last_field` | Last line matching `^\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s*[:=]` |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   254\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `after_last_method` | Last line matching `^\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s+def \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\w+` |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   255\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `after_last_import` | Last line matching `^\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s*(from\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\|import) ` |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   256\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `sorted` | Alphabetical insertion among siblings |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   257\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   258\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/filters.rs` — Custom Jinja2 Filters\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   259\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   260\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Filter function implementations and registration.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   261\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: heck (case conversions), minijinja (filter API).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   262\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   263\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tFilters are pure functions: `fn(value: &str, args...) -> String`. No side effects, no I/O. This module is the thinnest — most filters are one-liners wrapping `heck` or standard string operations.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   264\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   265\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/output.rs` — Output Formatting\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   266\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   267\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Dual-stream output (I-8), TTY detection.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   268\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: serde_json, owo-colors.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   269\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   270\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tResponsibilities:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   271\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Collect `Vec<OpResult>` from operation execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   272\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- JSON mode: serialize results to stdout\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   273\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Human mode: colored, summarized output to stderr\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   274\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Auto-detect: if stdout is a TTY, use human mode; if piped, use JSON\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   275\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `--json`: force JSON\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   276\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `--quiet`: suppress all output except errors\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   277\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `--verbose`: include rendered content in output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   278\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   279\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/error.rs` — Structured Error Types\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   280\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   281\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Error types, exit code mapping, error formatting.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   282\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: nothing (leaf module).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   283\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   284\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```rust\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   285\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tenum JigError {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   286\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    RecipeValidation(String),     // exit 1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   287\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    TemplateRendering(String),    // exit 2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   288\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    FileOperation(String),        // exit 3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   289\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    VariableValidation(String),   // exit 4\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   290\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   291\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   292\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   293\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tEvery error includes: what, where, why, hint (I-4). File operation errors include the rendered content so the caller can fall back to manual editing (I-10).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   294\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   295\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/workflow.rs` — Multi-Recipe Orchestration (v0.3+)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   296\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   297\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Workflow definition, step execution, conditional logic, variable mapping.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   298\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: recipe, variables, renderer, operations.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   299\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   300\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tNot built until v0.3. Included here for architectural completeness.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   301\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   302\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### `src/library/` — Library Management (v0.4+)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   303\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   304\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tOwns: Library manifest parsing, installation, discovery, convention mapping.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   305\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDepends on: recipe, filesystem, git (for remote installs).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   306\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   307\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tNot built until v0.4. Included here for architectural completeness.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   308\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   309\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Dependency Map\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   310\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   311\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   312\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                     main.rs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   313\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                    /   |   \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   314\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                   /    |    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   315\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t              recipe  variables  output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   316\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                |       |         |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   317\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                v       v         v\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   318\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t             renderer  (merges)  error\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   319\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   320\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                v\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   321\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t           operations/mod\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   322\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t           /    |    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   323\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t       create inject replace patch\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   324\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                              |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   325\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                              v\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   326\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                          scope/mod\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   327\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                         /    |    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   328\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t                    indent  delim  position\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   329\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   330\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   331\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tExternal crate usage:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   332\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- clap: main.rs only\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   333\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- serde + serde_yaml: recipe.rs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   334\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- serde_json: variables.rs, output.rs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   335\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- minijinja: renderer.rs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   336\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- regex: inject.rs, replace.rs, patch.rs, scope/*.rs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   337\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- heck: filters.rs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   338\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- owo-colors: output.rs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   339\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   340\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Key Design Decisions\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   341\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   342\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### D-1: Render-Then-Execute\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   343\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   344\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tTemplates are rendered *before* the operation executes. The operation receives a plain string, not a template. This means:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   345\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Rendering errors are caught before any file is touched\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   346\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- The rendered content is always available for error messages (I-4)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   347\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Operations are simpler — they just deal with strings and files\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   348\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   349\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### D-2: Recipe-Relative Template Resolution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   350\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   351\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tTemplate paths in recipes are relative to the recipe file, not the working directory. This keeps recipes self-contained (I-7). The `--base-dir` flag only affects output paths (where files are written), not template paths (where templates are read from).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   352\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   353\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### D-3: Regex for Anchoring, Not Parsing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   354\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   355\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tAnchors use regex, not AST parsing. This is deliberate:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   356\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Language-agnostic (same tool works for Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   357\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- No parser dependencies (keeps binary small, I-6)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   358\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Failure is explicit (regex didn't match = clear error, not a silent wrong parse)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   359\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Scope detection adds just enough structure without full parsing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   360\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   361\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### D-4: Operations Are Values, Not Traits (Initially)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   362\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   363\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tFor v0.1, operations can be a simple enum with match dispatch rather than a trait-object pattern. The trait abstraction is only needed if we add user-defined operations, which isn't planned. Keep it simple — an enum with four variants covers all cases through v0.4.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   364\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   365\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### D-5: No Global State\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   366\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   367\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tNo singletons, no global config objects, no lazy_static. Every function takes its inputs as arguments. This makes testing trivial and ensures determinism (I-1).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   368\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   369\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Iterative Build Strategy\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   370\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   371\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tEach phase produces a working, testable, demoable artifact. Nothing is \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"wired up later.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" Each phase can be evaluated independently.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   372\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   373\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Phase A: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   374\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   375\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Build:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   376\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `Cargo.toml` with dependencies: serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   377\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   378\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe struct, YAML deserialization, structural validation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   379\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/error.rs` — error types and exit codes\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   380\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/variables.rs` — VariableDecl types (no validation logic yet, just the types)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   381\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   382\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Demo:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   383\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```bash\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   384\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig validate recipe.yaml          # prints validation result, exits 0 or 1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   385\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig vars recipe.yaml              # prints expected variables as JSON\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   386\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   387\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   388\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   389\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Can parse the example recipe from the PRD\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   390\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Rejects invalid YAML with exit code 1 and a clear message\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   391\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Template file existence is checked relative to recipe location\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   392\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `jig vars` output matches the PRD's expected format\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   393\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Unit tests: valid recipe, missing fields, bad types, missing templates\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   394\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   395\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Phase B: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   396\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   397\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Build:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   398\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/variables.rs` — full validation: type checking, required fields, defaults, merging\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   399\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja environment, template loading\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   400\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/filters.rs` — all built-in filters\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   401\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Add `render` subcommand to CLI\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   402\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Add `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin` to CLI\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   403\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   404\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Demo:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   405\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```bash\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   406\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"class_name\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Foo\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}'     # rendered output to stdout\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   407\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"bad\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 123}' 2>&1         # type error, exit 4\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   408\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   409\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   410\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   411\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- All 13 built-in filters produce correct output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   412\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Variable type checking catches mismatches (string vs number, missing required, bad enum value)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   413\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Merge precedence works: defaults < file < stdin < inline\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   414\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Template syntax errors produce exit code 2 with line number\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   415\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Undefined variables produce a helpful error with \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" hint\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   416\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Snapshot tests (insta) for rendered output across filter combinations\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   417\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   418\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Phase C: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   419\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   420\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Build:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   421\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/operations/mod.rs` — operation dispatch (create only)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   422\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/operations/create.rs` — file creation with directory creation, skip_if_exists\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   423\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/output.rs` — JSON and human-readable output, TTY detection\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   424\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Add `run` subcommand to CLI\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   425\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Add `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   426\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   427\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Demo:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   428\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```bash\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   429\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'                 # creates files\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   430\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...' --dry-run       # preview without writing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   431\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...' --json | jq .   # machine-readable output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   432\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'                 # second run: skip_if_exists works\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   433\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   434\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   435\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   436\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Template paths in `to` field render correctly (`tests/{{ module | replace('.', '/') }}/...`)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   437\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Parent directories are created automatically\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   438\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `skip_if_exists` prevents overwriting\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   439\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `--force` overrides skip_if_exists\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   440\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- JSON output matches the format in the PRD\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   441\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Human output is colored and readable\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   442\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Dry-run produces identical JSON output but writes no files\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   443\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Second run with same vars shows all skips (I-2)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   444\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Integration test fixtures: recipe + vars + expected output directory\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   445\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   446\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Phase D: Inject Operation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   447\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   448\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Build:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   449\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/operations/inject.rs` — all injection modes\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   450\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   451\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- regex dependency added\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   452\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   453\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Demo:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   454\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```bash\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   455\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t# Recipe with create + inject operations\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   456\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'         # creates file, injects into existing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   457\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tjig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'         # second run: skip_if prevents duplication\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   458\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   459\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   460\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   461\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `after` regex: content appears on the line after the match\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   462\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `before` regex: content appears on the line before the match\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   463\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `prepend`: content at start of file\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   464\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `append`: content at end of file\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   465\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `at: first` vs `at: last`: correct match selection when regex matches multiple lines\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   466\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `skip_if`: string search prevents duplicate injection (I-2)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   467\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Regex match failure produces exit code 3 with the pattern and file path in the error\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   468\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Integration test fixtures for each injection mode\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   469\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **This completes the v0.1 feature set**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   470\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   471\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Phase E: Integration Test Framework\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   472\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   473\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Build:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   474\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `tests/` directory with fixture-based integration test harness\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   475\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Fixture format: `recipe.yaml` + `vars.json` + `templates/` + `existing/` + `expected/`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   476\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Test runner: copy existing/ to temp, run jig, diff against expected/\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   477\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Snapshot tests for all output formats\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   478\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Error case fixtures (missing vars, bad regex, missing template, missing target file)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   479\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   480\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   481\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Every operation mode has at least one fixture\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   482\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Every error case has a fixture that asserts exit code + error message content\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   483\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `cargo test` runs all unit tests + integration tests + snapshot tests\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   484\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Adding a new test case is just adding a directory (no code changes)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   485\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   486\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Phase F: Replace Operation (v0.2 start)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   487\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   488\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Build:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   489\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/operations/replace.rs` — between, pattern, fallback modes\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   490\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   491\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   492\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `between` start/end: content between markers is replaced\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   493\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `pattern`: matched lines are replaced\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   494\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `fallback: append/prepend/skip/error` each work correctly\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   495\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Markers themselves are preserved (only content between them changes)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   496\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   497\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Phase G: Patch Operation + Scope Detection (v0.2 completion)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   498\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   499\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Build:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   500\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/scope/mod.rs` — scope type dispatch\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   501\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/scope/indent.rs` — indentation-based scope detection\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   502\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — delimiter-based scope detection\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   503\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/scope/position.rs` — semantic position heuristics\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   504\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/operations/patch.rs` — anchor + scope + position + find\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   505\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   506\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   507\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Indentation scope: correctly finds class/function body boundaries in Python code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   508\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Delimiter scope: correctly finds brace/bracket/paren boundaries in TypeScript/Rust\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   509\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Nested delimiters handled correctly\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   510\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- String literals don't confuse delimiter counting\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   511\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Each semantic position (after_last_field, after_last_method, etc.) tested with real code snippets\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   512\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `find` narrowing works within a scope\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   513\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `--verbose` shows scope boundaries and insertion point\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   514\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Full patch recipe from the PRD (add-model-field) works against a Django-style fixture\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   515\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Scope parse failure produces structured error with rendered content (I-4, I-10)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   516\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   517\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Phase H: Workflows (v0.3)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   518\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   519\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Build:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   520\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/workflow.rs` — workflow definition, step execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   521\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `workflow` subcommand in CLI\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   522\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Conditional steps, variable mapping, error handling modes\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   523\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   524\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   525\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Multi-recipe workflows execute in order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   526\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `when` expressions skip/include steps correctly\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   527\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `vars_map` renames variables between steps\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   528\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `on_error: stop/continue/report` behaves correctly\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   529\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Per-step results in JSON output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   530\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   531\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Phase I: Libraries (v0.4)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   532\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   533\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Build:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   534\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/library/mod.rs` — manifest parsing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   535\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/library/install.rs` — add/remove/update\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   536\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/library/discover.rs` — recipe listing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   537\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `src/library/conventions.rs` — convention mapping and overrides\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   538\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `library` subcommand in CLI\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   539\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   540\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   541\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Install from local directory works\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   542\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Install from git URL works\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   543\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Convention overrides in .jigrc.yaml apply correctly\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   544\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- `jig library recipes <name>` lists all recipes\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   545\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Project-local extensions and template overrides work\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   546\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   547\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Testing Strategy\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   548\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   549\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tThree layers, matching the pipeline architecture:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   550\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   551\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Layer | What | Tool | Location |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   552\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|-------|------|------|----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   553\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| **Unit** | Individual functions: parsing, validation, filter output, scope detection | `#[cfg(test)]` inline | Each `src/*.rs` |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   554\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| **Integration** | Full pipeline: recipe + vars in, files out, diff against expected | Fixture directories | `tests/fixtures/` |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   555\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| **Snapshot** | Rendered template output, JSON output format, error messages | `insta` crate | `tests/snapshots/` |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   556\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   557\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t### Fixture Directory Convention\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   558\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   559\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   560\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ttests/fixtures/<test-name>/\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   561\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  recipe.yaml              # input recipe\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   562\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  vars.json                # input variables\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   563\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  templates/               # template files referenced by recipe\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   564\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    *.j2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   565\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  existing/                # files that exist before jig runs (for inject/replace/patch)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   566\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    *.py, *.ts, etc.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   567\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  expected/                # what the output directory should look like after\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   568\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t    *.py, *.ts, etc.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   569\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  expected_output.json     # optional: assert on jig's JSON stdout\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   570\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t  expected_exit_code       # optional: file containing just \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", etc.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   571\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t```\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   572\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   573\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tTest runner:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   574\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t1. Copy `existing/` to a temp directory\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   575\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t2. Run `jig run recipe.yaml --vars-file vars.json --base-dir $tmp --json`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   576\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t3. Diff temp directory against `expected/`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   577\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t4. If `expected_output.json` exists, assert JSON output matches\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   578\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t5. If `expected_exit_code` exists, assert exit code matches\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   579\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   580\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## What This Architecture Does NOT Cover\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   581\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   582\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tThese are explicitly out of scope for the architecture and will be addressed in later workstreams:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   583\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   584\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Scan, Infer, Check** (v0.7-v0.8) — reverse operations, pattern learning, conformance\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   585\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Schema-first generation** (v0.9) — OpenAPI/SQL/proto/GraphQL input\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   586\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Observation engine** (post-1.0) — Claude Code hook for pattern discovery\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   587\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Custom filters via shell commands** — .jigrc.yaml `filters:` block\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   588\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Distribution** (v0.5) — CI, Homebrew, npm wrapper, Nix flake\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   589\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Claude Code plugin** (v0.6) — /jig:init, /jig:doctor skills\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n   590\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Tree-sitter integration** (post-1.0) — optional AST-aware scoping\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n    17\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_7\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"agent_message\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"I’ve pulled `SPEC` and `PLAN` with line numbers and already see a few concrete spec/plan mismatches. I’m now checking `SHARED-CONTEXT` and `NARRATIVE` for additional contradictions that could confuse implementation or validation gating.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n    18\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.started\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_8\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/SHARED-CONTEXT.md'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":null,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"in_progress\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n    19\\\\\\\\t{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"item_8\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/SHARED-CONTEXT.md'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"     1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t# SHARED-CONTEXT.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t> Workstream: core-engine\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     4\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t> Last updated: 2026-04-02\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     5\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     6\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Purpose\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     7\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     8\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tDeliver the v0.1 jig CLI: recipe parsing, variable validation, Jinja2 template rendering, create and inject file operations, and structured output. This is the minimum feature set that makes jig useful — an LLM can call `jig run recipe.yaml --vars '{...}'` to produce files deterministically instead of re-deriving boilerplate.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n     9\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    10\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Current State\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    11\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    12\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Initialized (2026-04-02)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    13\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- SPEC.md complete with 7 functional requirements (67 acceptance criteria) and 6 non-functional requirements (12 acceptance criteria)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    14\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- PLAN.md complete with 5 phases, 28 milestones\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    15\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- No Rust code exists yet — greenfield implementation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    16\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    17\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Decisions Made\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    18\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    19\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Decision | Rationale |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    20\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    21\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Operations as enum (FileOp), not trait objects | Only 2 variants in v0.1. Trait abstraction adds no value without user-defined operations. (D-4) |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    22\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Render-then-execute pipeline | Errors caught before file writes; rendered content always available for error fallback. (D-1) |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    23\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Recipe-relative template paths | Keeps recipes self-contained. --base-dir only affects output paths. (D-2, I-7) |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    24\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| No global state | Pure functions with explicit inputs. Trivial testing, determinism guaranteed. (D-5) |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    25\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| thiserror for error types | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    26\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| IndexMap for variable ordering | Preserves declaration order for deterministic output (I-1) |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    27\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| std::io::IsTerminal for TTY detection | In std since Rust 1.70, no external crate needed |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    28\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `pluralizer` crate for pluralize/singularize | Full English rules without hand-rolling. Acceptable dependency for correctness. |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    29\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `strsim` crate for \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" hints | minijinja doesn't provide suggestions on undefined vars. Edit distance against declared names serves I-10 (graceful degradation). |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    30\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Register all 13 filters ourselves | Even for filters minijinja provides natively (replace, indent, join). We own exact behavior — upstream changes don't break us. Serves I-1 (determinism). |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    31\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL before ANY file write. Fail-fast, no partial writes. Templates reference variables not created files. |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    32\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Extra variables pass through silently | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness breaks real usage. |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    33\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first failure. Later ops may depend on earlier ones (inject targets prior create). |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    34\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| 5-phase incremental build | Each phase produces a working CLI that grows: validate/vars → render → run(create) → run(create+inject) → full test suite |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    35\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    36\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Patterns Established\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    37\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    38\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Acceptance criteria use EARS format** — every AC has a type (Event/State/Ubiquitous/Unwanted) and a traces-to test ID\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    39\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Fixture-based integration tests** — each test case is a directory with recipe.yaml, vars.json, templates/, existing/, expected/. No code changes needed to add tests.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    40\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Dual output streams** — JSON to stdout for machines, colored text to stderr for humans. Auto-detect via IsTerminal.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    41\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- **Exit codes are API** — 0 success, 1 recipe, 2 template, 3 file op, 4 variable. Never changes.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    42\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    43\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## Known Issues / Tech Debt\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    44\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    45\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- Large file handling for inject reads entire file into memory (fine for source files <10K lines, document as limitation)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    46\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t- stdin can only serve one purpose per invocation (--vars-stdin consumes it)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    47\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    48\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t## File Ownership\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    49\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    50\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tThis workstream owns the entire `src/` tree for v0.1:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    51\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    52\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| File | Phase | Purpose |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    53\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t|------|-------|---------|\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    54\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `Cargo.toml` | 1 | Dependencies and crate config |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    55\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `src/main.rs` | 1-4 | CLI entry point, clap commands |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    56\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `src/error.rs` | 1 | JigError enum, exit code mapping |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    57\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `src/recipe.rs` | 1 | Recipe/VariableDecl/FileOp structs, YAML parsing |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    58\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `src/variables.rs` | 1-2 | Variable types (P1), validation + merging (P2) |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    59\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `src/filters.rs` | 2 | 13 built-in Jinja2 filters |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    60\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `src/renderer.rs` | 2 | minijinja environment, template loading |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    61\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `src/operations/mod.rs` | 3 | Operation dispatch, ExecutionContext, OpResult |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    62\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `src/operations/create.rs` | 3 | File creation logic |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    63\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `src/operations/inject.rs` | 4 | Content injection logic |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    64\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `src/output.rs` | 3 | JSON + human output formatting |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    65\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `tests/integration.rs` | 5 | Fixture-based integration test harness |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    66\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t| `tests/fixtures/` | 5 | Test fixture directories |\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\"}}\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-114234.md:26:{\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item_12\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh and parse the file as variable input | TEST-2.2 |\\\\\\\\n43:| AC-2.3 | Event | WHEN `--vars-stdin` is provided, the system SHALL read JSON from stdin as variable input | TEST-2.3 |\\\\\\\\n44:| | WHEN a variable is declared as `required: true` and no value is provided (after merging), the system SHALL exit with code 4 naming the missing variable and providing a hint | TEST-2.5 |\\\\\\\\n46:| AC-2.6 | Event | WHEN a variable is declared with a `default` and no value is provided, the system SHALL use the default value | TEST-2.6 |\\\\\\\\n47:| AC-2.7 | Unwanted | IF a provided variable TEST-5.1 |\\\\\\\\n102:| AC-5.2 | Event | WHEN `before: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line before the first matching line | TEST-5.2 |\\\\\\\\n103:| AC-5.3 | Event | WHEN `prepend: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very beginning of the file | TEST-5.3 |\\\\\\\\n104:| AC-5.4 | Event | WHEN system SHALL use the last regex match instead of the first | TEST-5.5 |\\\\\\\\n106:| AC-5.6 | Event | WHEN `at: first` (default) is specified, the system SHALL use the first regex match | TEST-5.6 |\\\\\\\\n107:| AC-5.7 | Event | WHEN `skip_if: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"string\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified and the string exists in the target file, the system SHALL skip the injection and report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with a reason | TEST-5.7 |\\\\\\\\n108:| AC-5.8 | Unwanted | IF the regex pattern matches zero lines in the target file, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the pattern, the file path, and a hint | TEST-5.8 |\\\\\\\\n109:| AC-5.9 | Unwanted | IF the target file for injection does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the missing file path | TEST-5.9 |\\\\\\\\n110:| AC-5.10 | Event | WHEN an inject operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with the path, location description, and line count | TEST-5.10 |\\\\\\\\n111:| AC-5.11 | Event | WHEN the inject path contains Jinja2 expressions, the system SHALL render the path before resolving it | TEST-5.11 |\\\\\\\\n120:| AC-6.1 | State | WHILE stdout is a TTY, the system SHALL output human-readable colored text to stderr by default | TEST-6.1 |\\\\\\\\n121:| AC-6.2 | State | WHILE stdout is piped (not a TTY), the system SHALL output JSON to stdout by default | TEST-6.2 |\\\\\\\\n122:| AC-6.3 | Event | WHEN `--json` is specified, the system SHALL force JSON output to stdout regardless of TTY detection | TEST-6.3 |\\\\\\\\n123:| AC-6.4 | Event | WHEN `--quiet` is specified, the system SHALL suppress all output except errors | TEST-6.4 |\\\\\\\\n124:| AC-6.5 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include an `operations` array with action, path, lines (for success), reason (for skip), or error details (for error) for each operation | TEST-6.5 |\\\\\\\\n125:| AC-6.6 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include `files_written` and `files_skipped` summary arrays | TEST-6.6 |\\\\\\\\n126:| AC-6.7 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified, the system SHALL include rendered template content in the output | TEST-6.7 |\\\\\\\\n127:| AC-6.8 | Event | WHEN `--dry-run` is specified, the system SHALL produce identical output format but write no files to disk | TEST-6.8 |\\\\\\\\n136:| AC-7.1 | Event | WHEN `jig validate <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL parse the recipe and report whether it is valid, listing variables and operations | TEST-7.1 |\\\\\\\\n137:| AC-7.2 | Event | WHEN `jig vars <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL output the expected variables as a JSON object with type, required, default, and description fields | TEST-7.2 |\\\\\\\\n138:| AC-7.3 | Event | WHEN `jig render <template> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL render the template with the given variables and output the result to stdout | TEST-7.3 |\\\\\\\\n139:| AC-7.4 | Event | WHEN `jig render` is invoked with `--to <path>`, the system SHALL write the rendered output to the specified file instead of stdout | TEST-7.4 |\\\\\\\\n140:| AC-7.5 | Event | WHEN `jig run <recipe> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL execute all file operations in the recipe in declaration order | TEST-7.5 |\\\\\\\\n141:| AC-7.6 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accept global options: `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose` | TEST-7.6 |\\\\\\\\n152:| AC-N1.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL produce byte-identical output when given the same recipe, variables, and existing files across multiple runs | TEST-N1.1 |\\\\\\\\n153:| AC-N1.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not include timestamps, random values, or machine-specific identifiers in rendered output | TEST-N1.2 |\\\\\\\\n162:| AC-N2.1 | Event | WHEN a recipe is run a second time with the same variables and the same existing files, the system SHALL report all operations as `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with reasons | TEST-N2.1 |\\\\\\\\n163:| AC-N2.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not produce duplicate content when create uses `skip_if_exists: true` or inject uses `skip_if` | TEST-N2.2 |\\\\\\\\n172:| AC-N3.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL compile to a single static binary with no runtime dependencies | TEST-N3.1 |\\\\\\\\n173:| AC-N3.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries to run | TEST-N3.2 |\\\\\\\\n182:| AC-N4.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL include what, where, why, and hint fields in every error message | TEST-N4.1 |\\\\\\\\n183:| AC-N4.2 | Event | WHEN a file operation fails (exit code 3), the system SHALL include the rendered template content in the error output so the caller can fall back to manual editing | TEST-N4.2 |\\\\\\\\n184:| AC-N4.3 | Event | WHEN a template rendering error occurs, the system SHALL report the template file path and the line number of the error | TEST-N4.3 |\\\\\\\\n185:| AC-N4.4 | Event | WHEN a variable validation error occurs, the system SHALL report the variable name, expected type, and actual value provided | TEST-N4.4 |\\\\\\\\n194:| AC-N5.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL exit with code 0 on success, 1 for recipe validation errors, 2 for template rendering errors, 3 for file operation errors, 4 for variable validation errors | TEST-N5.1 |\\\\\\\\n195:| AC-N5.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL use the exit code of the first error encountered when multiple errors occur | TEST-N5.2 |\\\\\\\\n204:| AC-N6.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL execute file operations in the order they appear in the recipe's `files` array | TEST-N6.1 |\\\\\\\\n205:| AC-N6.2 | Event | WHEN an inject operation targets a file created by an earlier create operation in the same recipe, the system SHALL find and operate on the newly created file | TEST-N6.2 -lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sed -n '280,620p' docs/ARCHITECTURE.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n### `src/error.rs` — Structured Error Types\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOwns: Error types, exit code mapping, error formatting.\\\\\\\\nDepends on: nothing (leaf module).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\nstruct StructuredError {\\\\\\\\n    what: String,\\\\\\\\n    where_: String,\\\\\\\\n    why: String,\\\\\\\\n    hint: String,\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nenum JigError {\\\\\\\\n    RecipeValidation(StructuredError),     // exit 1\\\\\\\\n    TemplateRendering(StructuredError),    // exit 2\\\\\\\\n    FileOperation(StructuredError),        // exit 3\\\\\\\\n    VariableValidation(StructuredError),   // exit 4\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nEvery error includes: what, where, why, hint (I-4). File operation errors include the rendered content so the caller can fall back to manual editing (I-10).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### `src/workflow.rs` — Multi-Recipe Orchestration (v0.3+)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOwns: Workflow definition, step execution, conditional logic, variable mapping.\\\\\\\\nDepends on: recipe, variables, renderer, operations.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNot built until v0.3. Included here for architectural completeness.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### `src/library/` — Library Management (v0.4+)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOwns: Library manifest parsing, installation, discovery, convention mapping.\\\\\\\\nDepends on: recipe, filesystem, git (for remote installs).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNot built until v0.4. Included here for architectural completeness.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Dependency Map\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n                     main.rs\\\\\\\\n                    /   |   \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                   /    |    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n              recipe  variables  output\\\\\\\\n                |       |         |\\\\\\\\n                v       v         v\\\\\\\\n             renderer  (merges)  error\\\\\\\\n                |\\\\\\\\n                v\\\\\\\\n           operations/mod\\\\\\\\n           /    |    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n       create inject replace patch\\\\\\\\n                              |\\\\\\\\n                              v\\\\\\\\n                          scope/mod\\\\\\\\n                         /    |    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    indent  delim  position\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExternal crate usage:\\\\\\\\n- clap: main.rs only\\\\\\\\n- serde + serde_yaml: recipe.rs\\\\\\\\n- serde_json: variables.rs, output.rs\\\\\\\\n- minijinja: renderer.rs\\\\\\\\n- regex: inject.rs, replace.rs, patch.rs, scope/*.rs\\\\\\\\n- heck: filters.rs\\\\\\\\n- owo-colors: output.rs\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Key Design Decisions\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### D-1: Render-Then-Execute\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAll templates are rendered before any operation executes. The pipeline renders every template in the recipe upfront, then executes operations sequentially. This means:\\\\\\\\n- A rendering error in any template prevents all file writes\\\\\\\\n- The rendered content is always available for error messages (I-4)\\\\\\\\n- Operations are simpler — they just deal with strings and files\\\\\\\\n- Note: execution failures (e.g., inject target missing) can still produce partial writes if earlier operations succeeded. Recovery: re-run with `--force` or use rendered content from error output for manual editing.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### D-2: Recipe-Relative Template Resolution\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nTemplate paths in recipes are relative to the recipe file, not the working directory. This keeps recipes self-contained (I-7). The `--base-dir` flag only affects output paths (where files are written), not template paths (where templates are read from).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### D-3: Regex for Anchoring, Not Parsing\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAnchors use regex, not AST parsing. This is deliberate:\\\\\\\\n- Language-agnostic (same tool works for Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust)\\\\\\\\n- No parser dependencies (keeps binary small, I-6)\\\\\\\\n- Failure is explicit (regex didn't match = clear error, not a silent wrong parse)\\\\\\\\n- Scope detection adds just enough structure without full parsing\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### D-4: Operations Are Values, Not Traits (Initially)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nFor v0.1, operations can be a simple enum with match dispatch rather than a trait-object pattern. The trait abstraction is only needed if we add user-defined operations, which isn't planned. Keep it simple — an enum with four variants covers all cases through v0.4.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### D-5: No Global State\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNo singletons, no global config objects, no lazy_static. Every function takes its inputs as arguments. This makes testing trivial and ensures determinism (I-1).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Iterative Build Strategy\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nEach phase produces a working, testable, demoable artifact. Nothing is \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"wired up later.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" Each phase can be evaluated independently.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase A: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Build:**\\\\\\\\n- `Cargo.toml` with dependencies: serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap\\\\\\\\n- `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands\\\\\\\\n- `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe struct, YAML deserialization, structural validation\\\\\\\\n- `src/error.rs` — error types and exit codes\\\\\\\\n- `src/variables.rs` — VariableDecl types (no validation logic yet, just the types)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Demo:**\\\\\\\\n```bash\\\\\\\\njig validate recipe.yaml          # prints validation result, exits 0 or 1\\\\\\\\njig vars recipe.yaml              # prints expected variables as JSON\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\n- Can parse the example recipe from the PRD\\\\\\\\n- Rejects invalid YAML with exit code 1 and a clear message\\\\\\\\n- Template file existence is checked relative to recipe location\\\\\\\\n- `jig vars` output matches the PRD's expected format\\\\\\\\n- Unit tests: valid recipe, missing fields, bad types, missing templates\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase B: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Build:**\\\\\\\\n- `src/variables.rs` — full validation: type checking, required fields, defaults, merging\\\\\\\\n- `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja environment, template loading\\\\\\\\n- `src/filters.rs` — all built-in filters\\\\\\\\n- Add `render` subcommand to CLI\\\\\\\\n- Add `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin` to CLI\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Demo:**\\\\\\\\n```bash\\\\\\\\njig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"class_name\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Foo\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}'     # rendered output to stdout\\\\\\\\njig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"bad\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 123}' 2>&1         # type error, exit
