Support idempotency via skip_if.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.1 | Event | WHEN `after: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line after the first matching line | TEST-5.1 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.2 | Event | WHEN `before: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line before the first matching line | TEST-5.2 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.3 | Event | WHEN `prepend: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very beginning of the file | TEST-5.3 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.4 | Event | WHEN `append: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very end of the file | TEST-5.4 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.5 | Event | WHEN `at: last` is specified with `after` or `before`, the system SHALL use the last regex match instead of the first | TEST-5.5 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.6 | Event | WHEN `at: first` (default) is specified, the system SHALL use the first regex match | TEST-5.6 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\"}}\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-180424.md:20:{\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item_8\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sed -n '121,260p' docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"| AC-5.7 | Event | WHEN `skip_if` is specified, the system SHALL render it as a Jinja2 template with the recipe's variables, then search for the rendered string in the target file. If found, the system SHALL skip the injection and report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with a reason | TEST-5.7 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.8 | Unwanted | IF the regex pattern matches zero lines in the target file, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the pattern, the file path, and a hint | TEST-5.8 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.9 | Unwanted | IF the target file for injection does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the missing file path | TEST-5.9 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.10 | Event | WHEN an inject operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with the path, location description, and line count (number of lines inserted) | TEST-5.10 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.11 | Event | WHEN the inject path contains Jinja2 expressions, the system SHALL render the path before resolving it | TEST-5.11 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.12 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL ignore the `at` field when `prepend` or `append` is specified | TEST-5.12 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.13 | Unwanted | IF `after` or `before` is specified without a regex pattern, the system SHALL exit with code 1 | TEST-5.13 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.14 | Unwanted | IF an inject operation's `after` or `before` pattern fails to compile as a valid regex, the system SHALL exit with code 1 during recipe validation, reporting the invalid pattern and the compilation error | TEST-5.14 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.15 | Unwanted | IF an inject operation specifies more than one of after/before/prepend/append, the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the conflicting fields | TEST-5.15 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-5.16 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not apply `--force` to inject operations — the `--force` flag only affects create operations (`skip_if_exists` override) | TEST-5.16 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### FR-6: Output Formatting\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nReport operation results as JSON (stdout, for LLM callers) or human-readable text (stderr, for terminal users). Auto-detect the appropriate mode.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\n| AC-6.1 | State | WHILE stdout is a TTY, the system SHALL output human-readable colored text to stderr and produce no stdout output | TEST-6.1 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-6.2 | State | WHILE stdout is piped (not a TTY), the system SHALL output JSON to stdout and produce no stderr output except errors | TEST-6.2 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-6.3 | Event | WHEN `--json` is specified, the system SHALL force JSON output to stdout regardless of TTY detection | TEST-6.3 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-6.4 | Event | WHEN `--quiet` is specified, the system SHALL suppress all stderr output. Stdout behavior is determined by `--json` independently | TEST-6.4 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-6.5 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include an `operations` array with action, path, lines (for success), reason (for skip), or error details (for error) for each operation | TEST-6.5 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-6.6 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include `files_written` and `files_skipped` summary arrays containing unique file paths. A path appears in `files_written` if any operation wrote to it. A path appears in `files_skipped` only if all operations targeting it were skipped. In dry-run mode, `files_written` lists paths that would have been written. Paths SHALL appear in the order they were first encountered during operation execution. | TEST-6.6 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-6.7 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified, the system SHALL include rendered template content in the output | TEST-6.7 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-6.8 | Event | WHEN `--dry-run` is specified, the system SHALL produce identical output format but write no files to disk. Create operations record their output paths and rendered content in a virtual file state. Inject operations targeting a path created earlier in the same dry-run SHALL behave as if the file exists with the rendered content from the create operation. Inject operations SHALL update the virtual file state with post-injection content so subsequent operations in the same dry-run see the cumulative result. | TEST-6.8 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-6.9 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL resolve flag interactions as: `--quiet` suppresses stderr only and has no effect on stdout or JSON content; `--verbose` adds rendered content to both human stderr and JSON stdout independently; in human mode with `--quiet`, `--verbose` has no visible effect (stderr is suppressed); in JSON mode with `--quiet`, `--verbose` still adds rendered content to JSON stdout | TEST-6.9 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-6.10 | Event | WHEN a file operation fails, the system SHALL stop execution immediately and not execute subsequent operations. The `operations` array SHALL contain results for executed operations only | TEST-6.10 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### FR-7: CLI Interface\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nProvide four subcommands for v0.1: `validate`, `vars`, `render`, and `run`. Accept global options for variable sources, output control, and execution modes.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\n| AC-7.1 | Event | WHEN `jig validate <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL parse the recipe and report whether it is valid, listing variables and operations | TEST-7.1 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-7.2 | Event | WHEN `jig vars <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL output the expected variables as a JSON object with type, required, default, and description fields | TEST-7.2 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-7.3 | Event | WHEN `jig render <template> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL render the template with the given variables and output the result to stdout. Note: `jig render` operates without recipe context — variable type validation is not available, but \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" hints work against the provided variable keys. | TEST-7.3 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-7.4 | Event | WHEN `jig render` is invoked with `--to <path>`, the system SHALL write the rendered output to the specified file instead of stdout | TEST-7.4 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-7.5 | Event | WHEN `jig run <recipe> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL execute all file operations in the recipe in declaration order | TEST-7.5 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-7.6 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accept global options: `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose`, `--version` | TEST-7.6 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-7.7 | Event | WHEN `--version` is specified, the system SHALL print the version string and exit with code 0 | TEST-7.7 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Non-Functional Requirements\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### NFR-1: Deterministic Output\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSame inputs must always produce the same outputs, with no randomness or environment-dependent behavior in rendered content.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\n| AC-N1.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL produce byte-identical output when given the same recipe, variables, and existing files across multiple runs | TEST-N1.1 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-N1.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not include timestamps, random values, or machine-specific identifiers in rendered output | TEST-N1.2 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### NFR-2: Idempotent Operations\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nRunning the same recipe twice with the same variables must produce no changes on the second run.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\n| AC-N2.1 | Event | WHEN a recipe designed for idempotent execution (all creates use `skip_if_exists: true` and all injects use `skip_if`) is run a second time with the same variables and the same existing files, the system SHALL report all operations as `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with reasons | TEST-N2.1 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-N2.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not produce duplicate content when create uses `skip_if_exists: true` or inject uses `skip_if` | TEST-N2.2 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### NFR-3: Single Static Binary\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNo runtime dependencies beyond the system C library. One binary that runs on macOS and Linux without Python, Node, JVM, or additional shared libraries.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\n| AC-N3.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL compile to a single static binary with no runtime dependencies beyond the system C library | TEST-N3.1 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-N3.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries to run | TEST-N3.2 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### NFR-4: Structured Errors with Rendered Content\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nErrors must include what/where/why/hint. File operation errors must include the rendered content so callers can fall back.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\n| AC-N4.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL include what, where, why, and hint fields in every error message | TEST-N4.1 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-N4.2 | Event | WHEN a file operation fails (exit code 3), the system SHALL include the rendered template content in the error output so the caller can fall back to manual editing. This is independent of `--verbose` — rendered content in errors is always present | TEST-N4.2 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-N4.3 | Event | WHEN a template rendering error occurs, the system SHALL report the template file path and the line number of the error | TEST-N4.3 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-N4.4 | Event | WHEN a variable validation error occurs, the system SHALL report the variable name, expected type, and actual value provided | TEST-N4.4 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### NFR-5: Stable Exit Codes\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExit codes are API. They must be deterministic and match the defined mapping.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\n| AC-N5.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL exit with code 0 on success, 1 for recipe validation errors, 2 for template rendering errors, 3 for file operation errors, 4 for variable validation errors | TEST-N5.1 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-N5.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL use the exit code corresponding to the first pipeline stage that fails: recipe validation (1) before variable validation (4) before template rendering (2) before file operations (3) | TEST-N5.2 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### NFR-6: Ordered Execution\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nFile operations must execute in declaration order so later operations can depend on files created by earlier ones.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\n| AC-N6.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL execute file operations in the order they appear in the recipe's `files` array | TEST-N6.1 |\\\\\\\\n| AC-N6.2 | Event | WHEN an inject operation targets a file created by an earlier create operation in the same recipe, the system SHALL find and operate on the newly created file | TEST-N6.2 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Interfaces\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Public API (CLI)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\njig validate <recipe>\\\\\\\\njig vars <recipe>\\\\\\\\njig render <template> [--to <path>] --vars '<json>'\\\\\\\\njig run <recipe> --vars '<json>' [options]\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOptions:\\\\\\\\n  --vars <json>        Inline variables as JSON string\\\\\\\\n  --vars-file <path>   Variables from a JSON file\\\\\\\\n  --vars-stdin         Read variables from stdin\\\\\\\\n  --dry-run            Preview without writing\\\\\\\\n  --json               Force JSON output\\\\\\\\n  --quiet              Suppress non-error output\\\\\\\\n  --force              Overwrite existing files\\\\\\\\n  --base-dir <path>    Resolve output paths from this directory\\\\\\\\n  --verbose            Include rendered content in output\\\\\\\\n  --version            Print version and exit\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### JSON Output Schema\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```json\\\\\\\\n{\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"dry_run\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": false,\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"operations\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": [\\\\\\\\n    {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"lines\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 42},\\\\\\\\n    {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"location\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"after:^# fixtures\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"lines\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 3},\\\\\\\\n    {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"reason\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip_if matched: BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\"}}\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-180424.md:22:{\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item_9\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sed -n '121,320p' docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase 4: Inject Operation\\\\\\\\nStatus: Planned\\\\\\\\nTraces to: FR-5, NFR-2 (complete), NFR-6\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nContent injection into existing files via regex anchoring. All injection modes: after, before, prepend, append, with at:first/last match selection and skip_if idempotency. This completes the v0.1 operation set.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\\\\\n- [ ] 4.1: `src/operations/inject.rs` — read target file (or from virtual_files if target created in same dry-run); skip_if string search; regex anchor matching (after/before); at:first/at:last match selection; prepend/append modes; render inject path as template; write modified content; inject ops update virtual_files with post-injection content for subsequent operations\\\\\\\\n- [ ] 4.2: Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\\\\\\\n- [ ] 4.3: Unit tests for every injection mode — after (first match), after (last match), before, prepend, append, skip_if, missing target file error, regex no-match error, templated inject path\\\\\\\\n- [ ] 4.4: Integration test: recipe with create + inject in same run (create file, then inject into it — tests ordered execution AC-N6.2)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\\\\\n- after: content on line after first match (AC-5.1)\\\\\\\\n- before: content on line before first match (AC-5.2)\\\\\\\\n- prepend: content at start of file (AC-5.3)\\\\\\\\n- append: content at end of file (AC-5.4)\\\\\\\\n- at:last uses last match (AC-5.5), at:first (default) uses first (AC-5.6)\\\\\\\\n- skip_if: skips when string found in file, reports action:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" (AC-5.7)\\\\\\\\n- Regex no-match exits 3 with pattern, file path, hint (AC-5.8)\\\\\\\\n- Missing target file exits 3 (AC-5.9)\\\\\\\\n- Inject path renders as template (AC-5.11)\\\\\\\\n- Create-then-inject in same recipe works (AC-N6.2)\\\\\\\\n- Second run with skip_if shows all skips — no duplicate content (AC-N2.2)\\\\\\\\n- Inject success reports action:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" with path, location, line count (AC-5.10)\\\\\\\\n- at field ignored when prepend/append specified; after/before without regex exits 1 (AC-5.12, AC-5.13)\\\\\\\\n- Invalid regex pattern in after/before exits 1 (AC-5.14)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### Key Files\\\\\\\\n- `src/operations/inject.rs`\\\\\\\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### Dependencies\\\\\\\\n- regex crate\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase 5: Integration Test Framework\\\\\\\\nStatus: Planned\\\\\\\\nTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nFixture-based integration test harness that makes adding new test cases a matter of adding a directory. Snapshot tests for all output formats. This phase validates the entire v0.1 pipeline end-to-end.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\\\\\n- [ ] 5.1: Test harness in `tests/integration.rs` — fixture discovery; copy existing/ to temp dir; run jig as subprocess; diff output against expected/; assert JSON output against expected_output.json; assert exit code against expected_exit_code\\\\\\\\n- [ ] 5.2: Fixtures for create operations — simple create, templated path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, directory creation\\\\\\\\n- [ ] 5.3: Fixtures for inject operations — after/before/prepend/append, at:first/at:last, skip_if\\\\\\\\n- [ ] 5.4: Fixtures for error cases — missing vars, bad type, missing template, missing target file, regex no-match, malformed YAML, file exists without force\\\\\\\\n- [ ] 5.5: Fixtures for combined operations — create + inject in one recipe, multi-file recipe, idempotency (run twice)\\\\\\\\n- [ ] 5.6: insta snapshot tests for JSON output format, human output format, error message format\\\\\\\\n- [ ] 5.7: Determinism test — run same recipe twice, assert byte-identical output (AC-N1.1)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\\\\\n- `cargo test` runs all unit + integration + snapshot tests green\\\\\\\\n- Every operation mode has at least one fixture\\\\\\\\n- Every error exit code (1-4) has at least one fixture\\\\\\\\n- Adding a new test case requires only a new directory, no code changes\\\\\\\\n- Idempotency fixture: second run produces all skips, no file changes\\\\\\\\n- Binary has no dynamic dependencies beyond system libc (verified via `otool -L` on macOS, `ldd` on Linux)\\\\\\\\n- Every `TEST-*` ID in SPEC.md has a corresponding test function named `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}` or `spec::nfr{N}::ac_n{N}_{M}`\\\\\\\\n- Error fixtures assert that error JSON contains `what`, `where`, `why`, and `hint` fields (at least one fixture per exit code)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### Key Files\\\\\\\\n- `tests/integration.rs`\\\\\\\\n- `tests/fixtures/` (directory tree)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### Dependencies\\\\\\\\n- insta crate for snapshot testing\\\\\\\\n- assert_cmd or similar for subprocess testing (or raw std::process::Command)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Dependencies\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\\\\\\\n- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Key Decisions\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\\\\\\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\\\\\\\n| Operations as enum, not trait objects | `FileOp` enum with match dispatch | Only 2 variants in v0.1 (create, inject). Trait abstraction adds complexity for no benefit until user-defined operations exist, which isn't planned. (ARCHITECTURE D-4) |\\\\\\\\n| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages (ARCHITECTURE D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\\\\\\\n| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable (ARCHITECTURE D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\\\\\\\n| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism (ARCHITECTURE D-5, I-1) |\\\\\\\\n| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate, good Display/Error trait impls |\\\\\\\\n| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\\\\\\\n| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\\\\\\\n| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling. Acceptable dependency for correctness. |\\\\\\\\n| \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) against declared variable names | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Hand-rolling edit distance serves I-10 (graceful degradation) — the error tells the caller exactly what to fix. |\\\\\\\\n| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves, even for ones minijinja provides (replace, indent, join) | We control exact behavior. No surprises from upstream minijinja changes. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\\\\\\\n| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. Templates reference variables not created files. (D-1) |\\\\\\\\n| Extra variables pass through | Don't error or warn on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness here breaks real usage. |\\\\\\\\n| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later operations may depend on earlier ones (e.g., inject targets a prior create). (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Risks / Open Questions\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **TTY detection on all platforms**: Need to verify `std::io::IsTerminal` works correctly on macOS + Linux. Rust 1.70+ has `IsTerminal` in std — prefer that over external crate.\\\\\\\\n- **stdin variable reading conflicts**: If --vars-stdin is used, stdin is consumed for variables. This is fine since jig is non-interactive (I-3), but document that stdin can only be used for one purpose per invocation.\\\\\\\\n- **Large file handling for inject**: The current design reads the entire target file into memory for injection. This is fine for source files (typically <10K lines) but should be documented as a known limitation.\\\\\\\\n- **pluralizer crate coverage**: Verify `pluralizer` handles irregular plurals (person→people, child→children). Fallback: `inflector` or hand-rolled rules for common irregulars.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Resolved Questions\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\\\\\\\n- **\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names. minijinja doesn't provide this. Serves I-10.\\\\\\\\n- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. We own the behavior, upstream changes don't affect us. Serves I-1.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Execution Order\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\nPhase 1 ──► Phase 2 ──► Phase 3 ──► Phase 4 ──► Phase 5\\\\\\\\nskeleton     vars+render  create+output inject     integration\\\\\\\\n                                                    tests\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nEach phase produces a working, testable artifact. No phase depends on \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"wiring up later.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" The CLI grows incrementally:\\\\\\\\n- After Phase 1: `jig validate`, `jig vars`\\\\\\\\n- After Phase 2: + `jig render`\\\\\\\\n- After Phase 3: + `jig run` (create only)\\\\\\\\n- After Phase 4: + `jig run` (create + inject) — **v0.1 complete**\\\\\\\\n- After Phase 5: Full test coverage validates the release\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\"}}\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-180424.md:29:{\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item_13\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sed -n '260,620p' docs/ARCHITECTURE.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"### `src/filters.rs` — Custom Jinja2 Filters\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOwns: Filter function implementations and registration.\\\\\\\\nDepends on: heck (case conversions), minijinja (filter API).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nFilters are pure functions: `fn(value: &str, args...) -> String`. No side effects, no I/O. This module is the thinnest — most filters are one-liners wrapping `heck` or standard string operations.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### `src/output.rs` — Output Formatting\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOwns: Dual-stream output (I-8), TTY detection.\\\\\\\\nDepends on: serde_json, owo-colors.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nResponsibilities:\\\\\\\\n- Collect `Vec<OpResult>` from operation execution\\\\\\\\n- JSON mode: serialize results to stdout\\\\\\\\n- Human mode: colored, summarized output to stderr\\\\\\\\n- Auto-detect: if stdout is a TTY, use human mode; if piped, use JSON\\\\\\\\n- `--json`: force JSON\\\\\\\\n- `--quiet`: suppress all output except errors\\\\\\\\n- `--verbose`: include rendered content in output\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### `src/error.rs` — Structured Error Types\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOwns: Error types, exit code mapping, error formatting.\\\\\\\\nDepends on: nothing (leaf module).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\nstruct StructuredError {\\\\\\\\n    what: String,\\\\\\\\n    where_: String,\\\\\\\\n    why: String,\\\\\\\\n    hint: String,\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nenum JigError {\\\\\\\\n    RecipeValidation(StructuredError),     // exit 1\\\\\\\\n    TemplateRendering(StructuredError),    // exit 2\\\\\\\\n    FileOperation(StructuredError),        // exit 3\\\\\\\\n    VariableValidation(StructuredError),   // exit 4\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nEvery error includes: what, where, why, hint (I-4). File operation errors include the rendered content so the caller can fall back to manual editing (I-10).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### `src/workflow.rs` — Multi-Recipe Orchestration (v0.3+)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOwns: Workflow definition, step execution, conditional logic, variable mapping.\\\\\\\\nDepends on: recipe, variables, renderer, operations.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNot built until v0.3. Included here for architectural completeness.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### `src/library/` — Library Management (v0.4+)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nOwns: Library manifest parsing, installation, discovery, convention mapping.\\\\\\\\nDepends on: recipe, filesystem, git (for remote installs).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNot built until v0.4. Included here for architectural completeness.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Dependency Map\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n                     main.rs\\\\\\\\n                    /   |   \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                   /    |    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n              recipe  variables  output\\\\\\\\n                |       |         |\\\\\\\\n                v       v         v\\\\\\\\n             renderer  (merges)  error\\\\\\\\n                |\\\\\\\\n                v\\\\\\\\n           operations/mod\\\\\\\\n           /    |    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n       create inject replace patch\\\\\\\\n                              |\\\\\\\\n                              v\\\\\\\\n                          scope/mod\\\\\\\\n                         /    |    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    indent  delim  position\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExternal crate usage:\\\\\\\\n- clap: main.rs only\\\\\\\\n- serde + serde_yaml: recipe.rs\\\\\\\\n- serde_json: variables.rs, output.rs\\\\\\\\n- minijinja: renderer.rs\\\\\\\\n- regex: inject.rs, replace.rs, patch.rs, scope/*.rs\\\\\\\\n- heck: filters.rs\\\\\\\\n- owo-colors: output.rs\\\\\\\\n- thiserror: error.rs\\\\\\\\n- indexmap: recipe.rs, variables.rs\\\\\\\\n- pluralizer: filters.rs\\\\\\\\n- strsim: renderer.rs (did-you-mean hints)\\\\\\\\n- insta: tests (snapshot testing)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Key Design Decisions\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### D-1: Render-Then-Execute\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAll templates are rendered before any operation executes. The pipeline renders every template in the recipe upfront, then executes operations sequentially. This means:\\\\\\\\n- A rendering error in any template prevents all file writes\\\\\\\\n- The rendered content is always available for error messages (I-4)\\\\\\\\n- Operations are simpler — they just deal with strings and files\\\\\\\\n- Note: execution failures (e.g., inject target missing) can still produce partial writes if earlier operations succeeded. Recovery: re-run with `--force` or use rendered content from error output for manual editing.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### D-2: Recipe-Relative Template Resolution\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nTemplate paths in recipes are relative to the recipe file, not the working directory. This keeps recipes self-contained (I-7). The `--base-dir` flag only affects output paths (where files are written), not template paths (where templates are read from).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### D-3: Regex for Anchoring, Not Parsing\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAnchors use regex, not AST parsing. This is deliberate:\\\\\\\\n- Language-agnostic (same tool works for Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust)\\\\\\\\n- No parser dependencies (keeps binary small, I-6)\\\\\\\\n- Failure is explicit (regex didn't match = clear error, not a silent wrong parse)\\\\\\\\n- Scope detection adds just enough structure without full parsing\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### D-4: Operations Are Values, Not Traits (Initially)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nFor v0.1, operations can be a simple enum with match dispatch rather than a trait-object pattern. The trait abstraction is only needed if we add user-defined operations, which isn't planned. Keep it simple — an enum with four variants covers all cases through v0.4.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### D-5: No Global State\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nNo singletons, no global config objects, no lazy_static. Every function takes its inputs as arguments. This makes testing trivial and ensures determinism (I-1).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Iterative Build Strategy\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nEach phase produces a working, testable, demoable artifact. Nothing is \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"wired up later.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" Each phase can be evaluated independently.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase A: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Build:**\\\\\\\\n- `Cargo.toml` with dependencies: serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap\\\\\\\\n- `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands\\\\\\\\n- `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe struct, YAML deserialization, structural validation\\\\\\\\n- `src/error.rs` — error types and exit codes\\\\\\\\n- `src/variables.rs` — VariableDecl types (no validation logic yet, just the types)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Demo:**\\\\\\\\n```bash\\\\\\\\njig validate recipe.yaml          # prints validation result, exits 0 or 1\\\\\\\\njig vars recipe.yaml              # prints expected variables as JSON\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\n- Can parse the example recipe from the PRD\\\\\\\\n- Rejects invalid YAML with exit code 1 and a clear message\\\\\\\\n- Template file existence is checked relative to recipe location\\\\\\\\n- `jig vars` output matches the PRD's expected format\\\\\\\\n- Unit tests: valid recipe, missing fields, bad types, missing templates\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase B: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Build:**\\\\\\\\n- `src/variables.rs` — full validation: type checking, required fields, defaults, merging\\\\\\\\n- `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja environment, template loading\\\\\\\\n- `src/filters.rs` — all built-in filters\\\\\\\\n- Add `render` subcommand to CLI\\\\\\\\n- Add `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin` to CLI\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Demo:**\\\\\\\\n```bash\\\\\\\\njig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"class_name\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Foo\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}'     # rendered output to stdout\\\\\\\\njig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"bad\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": 123}' 2>&1         # type error, exit 4\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\n- All 13 built-in filters produce correct output\\\\\\\\n- Variable type checking catches mismatches (string vs number, missing required, bad enum value)\\\\\\\\n- Merge precedence works: defaults < file < stdin < inline\\\\\\\\n- Template syntax errors produce exit code 2 with line number\\\\\\\\n- Undefined variables produce a helpful error with \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" hint\\\\\\\\n- Snapshot tests (insta) for rendered output across filter combinations\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase C: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Build:**\\\\\\\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` — operation dispatch (create only)\\\\\\\\n- `src/operations/create.rs` — file creation with directory creation, skip_if_exists\\\\\\\\n- `src/output.rs` — JSON and human-readable output, TTY detection\\\\\\\\n- Add `run` subcommand to CLI\\\\\\\\n- Add `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Demo:**\\\\\\\\n```bash\\\\\\\\njig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'                 # creates files\\\\\\\\njig run recipe.yaml --vars '...' --dry-run       # preview without writing\\\\\\\\njig run recipe.yaml --vars '...' --json | jq .   # machine-readable output\\\\\\\\njig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'                 # second run: skip_if_exists works\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\n- Template paths in `to` field render correctly (`tests/{{ module | replace('.', '/') }}/...`)\\\\\\\\n- Parent directories are created automatically\\\\\\\\n- `skip_if_exists` prevents overwriting\\\\\\\\n- `--force` overrides skip_if_exists\\\\\\\\n- JSON output matches the format in the PRD\\\\\\\\n- Human output is colored and readable\\\\\\\\n- Dry-run produces identical JSON output but writes no files\\\\\\\\n- Second run with same vars shows all skips (I-2)\\\\\\\\n- Integration test fixtures: recipe + vars + expected output directory\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase D: Inject Operation\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Build:**\\\\\\\\n- `src/operations/inject.rs` — all injection modes\\\\\\\\n- Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\\\\\\\n- regex dependency added\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Demo:**\\\\\\\\n```bash\\\\\\\\n# Recipe with create + inject operations\\\\\\\\njig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'         # creates file, injects into existing\\\\\\\\njig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'         # second run: skip_if prevents duplication\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\n- `after` regex: content appears on the line after the match\\\\\\\\n- `before` regex: content appears on the line before the match\\\\\\\\n- `prepend`: content at start of file\\\\\\\\n- `append`: content at end of file\\\\\\\\n- `at: first` vs `at: last`: correct match selection when regex matches multiple lines\\\\\\\\n- `skip_if`: string search prevents duplicate injection (I-2)\\\\\\\\n- Regex match failure produces exit code 3 with the pattern and file path in the error\\\\\\\\n- Integration test fixtures for each injection mode\\\\\\\\n- **This completes the v0.1 feature set**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase E: Integration Test Framework\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Build:**\\\\\\\\n- `tests/` directory with fixture-based integration test harness\\\\\\\\n- Fixture format: `recipe.yaml` + `vars.json` + `templates/` + `existing/` + `expected/`\\\\\\\\n- Test runner: copy existing/ to temp, run jig, diff against expected/\\\\\\\\n- Snapshot tests for all output formats\\\\\\\\n- Error case fixtures (missing vars, bad regex, missing template, missing target file)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\n- Every operation mode has at least one fixture\\\\\\\\n- Every error case has a fixture that asserts exit code + error message content\\\\\\\\n- `cargo test` runs all unit tests + integration tests + snapshot tests\\\\\\\\n- Adding a new test case is just adding a directory (no code changes)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase F: Replace Operation (v0.2 start)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Build:**\\\\\\\\n- `src/operations/replace.rs` — between, pattern, fallback modes\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\n- `between` start/end: content between markers is replaced\\\\\\\\n- `pattern`: matched lines are replaced\\\\\\\\n- `fallback: append/prepend/skip/error` each work correctly\\\\\\\\n- Markers themselves are preserved (only content between them changes)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase G: Patch Operation + Scope Detection (v0.2 completion)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Build:**\\\\\\\\n- `src/scope/mod.rs` — scope type dispatch\\\\\\\\n- `src/scope/indent.rs` — indentation-based scope detection\\\\\\\\n- `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — delimiter-based scope detection\\\\\\\\n- `src/scope/position.rs` — semantic position heuristics\\\\\\\\n- `src/operations/patch.rs` — anchor + scope + position + find\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\n- Indentation scope: correctly finds class/function body boundaries in Python code\\\\\\\\n- Delimiter scope: correctly finds brace/bracket/paren boundaries in TypeScript/Rust\\\\\\\\n- Nested delimiters handled correctly\\\\\\\\n- String literals don't confuse delimiter counting\\\\\\\\n- Each semantic position (after_last_field, after_last_method, etc.) tested with real code snippets\\\\\\\\n- `find` narrowing works within a scope\\\\\\\\n- `--verbose` shows scope boundaries and insertion point\\\\\\\\n- Full patch recipe from the PRD (add-model-field) works against a Django-style fixture\\\\\\\\n- Scope parse failure produces structured error with rendered content (I-4, I-10)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase H: Workflows (v0.3)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Build:**\\\\\\\\n- `src/workflow.rs` — workflow definition, step execution\\\\\\\\n- `workflow` subcommand in CLI\\\\\\\\n- Conditional steps, variable mapping, error handling modes\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\n- Multi-recipe workflows execute in order\\\\\\\\n- `when` expressions skip/include steps correctly\\\\\\\\n- `vars_map` renames variables between steps\\\\\\\\n- `on_error: stop/continue/report` behaves correctly\\\\\\\\n- Per-step results in JSON output\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase I: Libraries (v0.4)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Build:**\\\\\\\\n- `src/library/mod.rs` — manifest parsing\\\\\\\\n- `src/library/install.rs` — add/remove/update\\\\\\\\n- `src/library/discover.rs` — recipe listing\\\\\\\\n- `src/library/conventions.rs` — convention mapping and overrides\\\\\\\\n- `library` subcommand in CLI\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Evaluate:**\\\\\\\\n- Install from local directory works\\\\\\\\n- Install from git URL works\\\\\\\\n- Convention overrides in .jigrc.yaml apply correctly\\\\\\\\n- `jig library recipes <name>` lists all recipes\\\\\\\\n- Project-local extensions and template overrides work\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Testing Strategy\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThree layers, matching the pipeline architecture:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Layer | What | Tool | Location |\\\\\\\\n|-------|------|------|----------|\\\\\\\\n| **Unit** | Individual functions: parsing, validation, filter output, scope detection | `#[cfg(test)]` inline | Each `src/*.rs` |\\\\\\\\n| **Integration** | Full pipeline: recipe + vars in, files out, diff against expected | Fixture directories | `tests/fixtures/` |\\\\\\\\n| **Snapshot** | Rendered template output, JSON output format, error messages | `insta` crate | `tests/snapshots/` |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Fixture Directory Convention\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\ntests/fixtures/<test-name>/\\\\\\\\n  recipe.yaml              # input recipe\\\\\\\\n  vars.json                # input variables\\\\\\\\n  templates/               # template files referenced by recipe\\\\\\\\n    *.j2\\\\\\\\n  existing/                # files that exist before jig runs (for inject/replace/patch)\\\\\\\\n    *.py, *.ts, etc.\\\\\\\\n  expected/                # what the output directory should look like after\\\\\\\\n    *.py, *.ts, etc.\\\\\\\\n  expected_output.json     # optional: assert on jig's JSON stdout\\\\\\\\n  expected_exit_code       # optional: file containing just \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", etc.\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nTest runner:\\\\\\\\n1. Copy `existing/` to a temp directory\\\\\\\\n2. Run `jig run recipe.yaml --vars-file vars.json --base-dir $tmp --json`\\\\\\\\n3. Diff temp directory against `expected/`\\\\\\\\n4. If `expected_output.json` exists, assert JSON output matches\\\\\\\\n5. If `expected_exit_code` exists, assert exit code matches\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## What This Architecture Does NOT Cover\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThese are explicitly out of scope for the architecture and will be addressed in later workstreams:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Scan, Infer, Check** (v0.7-v0.8) — reverse operations, pattern learning, conformance\\\\\\\\n- **Schema-first generation** (v0.9) — OpenAPI/SQL/proto/GraphQL input\\\\\\\\n- **Observation engine** (post-1.0) — Claude Code hook for pattern discovery\\\\\\\\n- **Custom filters via shell commands** — .jigrc.yaml `filters:` block\\\\\\\\n- **Distribution** (v0.5) — CI, Homebrew, npm wrapper, Nix flake\\\\\\\\n- **Claude Code plugin** (v0.6) — /jig:init, /jig:doctor skills\\\\\\\\n- **Tree-sitter integration** (post-1.0) — optional AST-aware scoping\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\"}}\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-180424.md:38:{\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item_18\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"awk -F'|' '/\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\| AC-/{id=\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"'$2;gsub(/ /,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",id);type=$3;gsub(/''^ +| +$/,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",type);crit=$4;gsub(/''^ +| +$/,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",crit);trace=$5;gsub(/''^ +| +$/,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",trace); print id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"crit\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"trace}' docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md | sed 's/  */ /g' | head -n 140\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"AC-1.1\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a valid recipe YAML is provided, the system SHALL parse it into a Recipe struct containing name, description, variables, and files\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.1\\\\\\\\nAC-1.2\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a recipe declares variables with type, required, default, description, values (enum), and items (array), the system SHALL parse all fields into the VariableDecl struct\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.2\\\\\\\\nAC-1.3\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a recipe declares `create` file operations with template, to, and skip_if_exists fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Create\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.3\\\\\\\\nAC-1.4\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a recipe declares `inject` file operations with template, inject, after/before/prepend/append, at, and skip_if fields, the system SHALL parse them as FileOp::Inject\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.4\\\\\\\\nAC-1.5\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF the recipe YAML is malformed (invalid YAML syntax), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and an error message identifying the parse failure location\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.5\\\\\\\\nAC-1.6\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF a required field is missing from the recipe (e.g., files with no template), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and name the missing field\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.6\\\\\\\\nAC-1.7\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a recipe references template files, the system SHALL resolve those paths relative to the recipe file location, not the working directory\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.7\\\\\\\\nAC-1.8\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF a referenced template file does not exist at the resolved path, the system SHALL exit with code 1 and report which template is missing and where it looked\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.8\\\\\\\\nAC-1.9\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN the recipe has optional metadata fields (name, description), the system SHALL accept recipes with or without them\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.9\\\\\\\\nAC-1.10\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF the recipe contains an unknown operation type (e.g., `patch`, `replace`), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and report \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"unknown operation type '<name>' — this operation is not supported in v0.1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.10\\\\\\\\nAC-1.11\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN the recipe has an empty `files: []` array, the system SHALL exit 0 with an empty operations array\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.11\\\\\\\\nAC-1.12\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN the recipe has no `variables` key or an empty `variables` map, the system SHALL accept the recipe as valid\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.12\\\\\\\\nAC-1.13\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF the recipe file does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 1 naming the missing path\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.13\\\\\\\\nAC-1.14\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF a file operation contains both `to` and `inject` fields, the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the ambiguous operation type\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.14\\\\\\\\nAC-1.15\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF a file operation contains neither `to` nor `inject` fields, the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the missing field\\\\\\\\tTEST-1.15\\\\\\\\nAC-2.1\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `--vars` is provided with a JSON string, the system SHALL parse it as the variable input\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.1\\\\\\\\nAC-2.2\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `--vars-file` is provided with a path to a JSON file, the system SHALL read and parse the file as variable input\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.2\\\\\\\\nAC-2.3\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `--vars-stdin` is provided, the system SHALL read JSON from stdin as variable input\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.3\\\\\\\\nAC-2.4\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN multiple variable sources are provided, the system SHALL merge with precedence: recipe defaults < vars-file < vars-stdin < inline --vars\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.4\\\\\\\\nAC-2.5\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a variable is declared as `required: true` and no value is provided (after merging), the system SHALL exit with code 4 naming the missing variable and providing a hint\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.5\\\\\\\\nAC-2.6\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a variable is declared with a `default` and no value is provided, the system SHALL use the default value\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.6\\\\\\\\nAC-2.7\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF a provided variable value does not match its declared type (e.g., string given for number, object given for array), the system SHALL exit with code 4 with expected vs actual type\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.7\\\\\\\\nAC-2.8\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a variable is declared as `type: enum` with `values: [a, b, c]`, the system SHALL reject values not in the allowed set with exit code 4\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.8\\\\\\\\nAC-2.9\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a variable is declared as `type: array` with `items: string`, the system SHALL validate that each array element matches the item type\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.9\\\\\\\\nAC-2.10\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL accept all six variable types: string, number, boolean, array, object, enum\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.10\\\\\\\\nAC-2.11\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL accumulate all variable validation errors and report them together, using the exit code of the first error encountered\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.11\\\\\\\\nAC-2.12\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL accept variable input containing keys not declared in the recipe's variables section without error or warning\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.12\\\\\\\\nAC-2.13\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF `--vars` contains invalid JSON, the system SHALL exit with code 4 with a parse error identifying the location\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.13\\\\\\\\nAC-2.14\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF `--vars-file` points to a nonexistent file, the system SHALL exit with code 4 naming the missing path\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.14\\\\\\\\nAC-2.15\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF `--vars-file` points to a file containing invalid JSON, the system SHALL exit with code 4 with a parse error identifying the file path and the JSON error location\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.15\\\\\\\\nAC-2.16\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN no variable sources are provided (no `--vars`, `--vars-file`, or `--vars-stdin`), the system SHALL use an empty object as input and apply recipe defaults\\\\\\\\tTEST-2.16\\\\\\\\nAC-3.1\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a template is rendered with a variables context, the system SHALL substitute all `{{ variable }}` expressions with their values\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.1\\\\\\\\nAC-3.2\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a template contains `{% if %}` / `{% else %}` / `{% endif %}` blocks, the system SHALL evaluate them correctly against the variable values\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.2\\\\\\\\nAC-3.3\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a template contains `{% for item in list %}` loops, the system SHALL iterate and render the body for each element\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.3\\\\\\\\nAC-3.4\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL register and support all 13 built-in filters: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.4\\\\\\\\nAC-3.5\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `snakecase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.5\\\\\\\\nAC-3.6\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `camelcase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"bookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.6\\\\\\\\nAC-3.7\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `pascalcase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking_service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.7\\\\\\\\nAC-3.8\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `kebabcase` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"booking-service\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.8\\\\\\\\nAC-3.9\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `replace` is applied as `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a.b.c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\treplace('.', '/')`, the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a/b/c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nAC-3.10\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `pluralize` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotel\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.10\\\\\\\\nAC-3.11\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `singularize` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hotel\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.11\\\\\\\\nAC-3.12\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `quote` is applied to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", the system SHALL produce `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` (with literal quotes)\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.12\\\\\\\\nAC-3.13\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `indent(4)` is applied to a multiline string, the system SHALL indent each line by 4 spaces, including the first line. Use `indent(4, first=false)` to skip the first line. Note: this diverges from Jinja2 convention where indent() skips the first line by default\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.13\\\\\\\\nAC-3.14\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `join(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")` is applied to `[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"]`, the system SHALL produce \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a, b, c\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.14\\\\\\\\nAC-3.15\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `{# comment #}` appears in a template, the system SHALL strip it from the output\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.15\\\\\\\\nAC-3.16\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `{% raw %}...{% endraw %}` appears in a template, the system SHALL output the content literally without interpreting Jinja2 syntax\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.16\\\\\\\\nAC-3.17\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF a template references an undefined variable, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and include a \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" hint when a close match exists among the keys in the provided variable context (Levenshtein distance ≤ 3)\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.17\\\\\\\\nAC-3.18\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF a template has a Jinja2 syntax error, the system SHALL exit with code 2 and report the template file path and line number\\\\\\\\tTEST-3.18\\\\\\\\nAC-4.1\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a create operation executes, the system SHALL render the template and write the result to the path specified in `to`\\\\\\\\tTEST-4.1\\\\\\\\nAC-4.2\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN the `to` path contains Jinja2 expressions (e.g., `tests/{{ module \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\treplace('.', '/') }}/test_{{ class_name \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nAC-4.3\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN parent directories in the `to` path do not exist, the system SHALL create them automatically\\\\\\\\tTEST-4.3\\\\\\\\nAC-4.4\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `skip_if_exists: true` and the target file already exists, the system SHALL skip the operation and report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with a reason\\\\\\\\tTEST-4.4\\\\\\\\nAC-4.5\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF `skip_if_exists: false` (default) and the target file already exists and `--force` is not set, the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting the conflict\\\\\\\\tTEST-4.5\\\\\\\\nAC-4.6\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `--force` is set and the target file already exists, the system SHALL overwrite the file regardless of skip_if_exists\\\\\\\\tTEST-4.6\\\\\\\\nAC-4.7\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `--base-dir` is set, the system SHALL resolve `to` paths relative to the base directory instead of the working directory\\\\\\\\tTEST-4.7\\\\\\\\nAC-4.8\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a create operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with the path and line count (number of lines in the written file)\\\\\\\\tTEST-4.8\\\\\\\\nAC-4.9\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF a filesystem write fails due to permissions, the system SHALL exit with code 3 with the path and permission error\\\\\\\\tTEST-4.9\\\\\\\\nAC-4.10\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF `--base-dir` specifies a directory that does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 naming the missing directory\\\\\\\\tTEST-4.10\\\\\\\\nAC-5.1\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `after: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line after the first matching line\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.1\\\\\\\\nAC-5.2\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `before: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"regex\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content on the line before the first matching line\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.2\\\\\\\\nAC-5.3\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `prepend: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very beginning of the file\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.3\\\\\\\\nAC-5.4\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `append: true` is specified, the system SHALL insert rendered content at the very end of the file\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.4\\\\\\\\nAC-5.5\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `at: last` is specified with `after` or `before`, the system SHALL use the last regex match instead of the first\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.5\\\\\\\\nAC-5.6\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `at: first` (default) is specified, the system SHALL use the first regex match\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.6\\\\\\\\nAC-5.7\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `skip_if` is specified, the system SHALL render it as a Jinja2 template with the recipe's variables, then search for the rendered string in the target file. If found, the system SHALL skip the injection and report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with a reason\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.7\\\\\\\\nAC-5.8\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF the regex pattern matches zero lines in the target file, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the pattern, the file path, and a hint\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.8\\\\\\\\nAC-5.9\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF the target file for injection does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the missing file path\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.9\\\\\\\\nAC-5.10\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN an inject operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with the path, location description, and line count (number of lines inserted)\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.10\\\\\\\\nAC-5.11\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN the inject path contains Jinja2 expressions, the system SHALL render the path before resolving it\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.11\\\\\\\\nAC-5.12\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL ignore the `at` field when `prepend` or `append` is specified\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.12\\\\\\\\nAC-5.13\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF `after` or `before` is specified without a regex pattern, the system SHALL exit with code 1\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.13\\\\\\\\nAC-5.14\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF an inject operation's `after` or `before` pattern fails to compile as a valid regex, the system SHALL exit with code 1 during recipe validation, reporting the invalid pattern and the compilation error\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.14\\\\\\\\nAC-5.15\\\\\\\\tUnwanted\\\\\\\\tIF an inject operation specifies more than one of after/before/prepend/append, the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the conflicting fields\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.15\\\\\\\\nAC-5.16\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL not apply `--force` to inject operations — the `--force` flag only affects create operations (`skip_if_exists` override)\\\\\\\\tTEST-5.16\\\\\\\\nAC-6.1\\\\\\\\tState\\\\\\\\tWHILE stdout is a TTY, the system SHALL output human-readable colored text to stderr and produce no stdout output\\\\\\\\tTEST-6.1\\\\\\\\nAC-6.2\\\\\\\\tState\\\\\\\\tWHILE stdout is piped (not a TTY), the system SHALL output JSON to stdout and produce no stderr output except errors\\\\\\\\tTEST-6.2\\\\\\\\nAC-6.3\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `--json` is specified, the system SHALL force JSON output to stdout regardless of TTY detection\\\\\\\\tTEST-6.3\\\\\\\\nAC-6.4\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `--quiet` is specified, the system SHALL suppress all stderr output. Stdout behavior is determined by `--json` independently\\\\\\\\tTEST-6.4\\\\\\\\nAC-6.5\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include an `operations` array with action, path, lines (for success), reason (for skip), or error details (for error) for each operation\\\\\\\\tTEST-6.5\\\\\\\\nAC-6.6\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include `files_written` and `files_skipped` summary arrays containing unique file paths. A path appears in `files_written` if any operation wrote to it. A path appears in `files_skipped` only if all operations targeting it were skipped. In dry-run mode, `files_written` lists paths that would have been written. Paths SHALL appear in the order they were first encountered during operation execution.\\\\\\\\tTEST-6.6\\\\\\\\nAC-6.7\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `--verbose` is specified, the system SHALL include rendered template content in the output\\\\\\\\tTEST-6.7\\\\\\\\nAC-6.8\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `--dry-run` is specified, the system SHALL produce identical output format but write no files to disk. Create operations record their output paths and rendered content in a virtual file state. Inject operations targeting a path created earlier in the same dry-run SHALL behave as if the file exists with the rendered content from the create operation. Inject operations SHALL update the virtual file state with post-injection content so subsequent operations in the same dry-run see the cumulative result.\\\\\\\\tTEST-6.8\\\\\\\\nAC-6.9\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL resolve flag interactions as: `--quiet` suppresses stderr only and has no effect on stdout or JSON content; `--verbose` adds rendered content to both human stderr and JSON stdout independently; in human mode with `--quiet`, `--verbose` has no visible effect (stderr is suppressed); in JSON mode with `--quiet`, `--verbose` still adds rendered content to JSON stdout\\\\\\\\tTEST-6.9\\\\\\\\nAC-6.10\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a file operation fails, the system SHALL stop execution immediately and not execute subsequent operations. The `operations` array SHALL contain results for executed operations only\\\\\\\\tTEST-6.10\\\\\\\\nAC-7.1\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `jig validate <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL parse the recipe and report whether it is valid, listing variables and operations\\\\\\\\tTEST-7.1\\\\\\\\nAC-7.2\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `jig vars <recipe>` is invoked, the system SHALL output the expected variables as a JSON object with type, required, default, and description fields\\\\\\\\tTEST-7.2\\\\\\\\nAC-7.3\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `jig render <template> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL render the template with the given variables and output the result to stdout. Note: `jig render` operates without recipe context — variable type validation is not available, but \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" hints work against the provided variable keys.\\\\\\\\tTEST-7.3\\\\\\\\nAC-7.4\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `jig render` is invoked with `--to <path>`, the system SHALL write the rendered output to the specified file instead of stdout\\\\\\\\tTEST-7.4\\\\\\\\nAC-7.5\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `jig run <recipe> --vars '<json>'` is invoked, the system SHALL execute all file operations in the recipe in declaration order\\\\\\\\tTEST-7.5\\\\\\\\nAC-7.6\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL accept global options: `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose`, `--version`\\\\\\\\tTEST-7.6\\\\\\\\nAC-7.7\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN `--version` is specified, the system SHALL print the version string and exit with code 0\\\\\\\\tTEST-7.7\\\\\\\\nAC-N1.1\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL produce byte-identical output when given the same recipe, variables, and existing files across multiple runs\\\\\\\\tTEST-N1.1\\\\\\\\nAC-N1.2\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL not include timestamps, random values, or machine-specific identifiers in rendered output\\\\\\\\tTEST-N1.2\\\\\\\\nAC-N2.1\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a recipe designed for idempotent execution (all creates use `skip_if_exists: true` and all injects use `skip_if`) is run a second time with the same variables and the same existing files, the system SHALL report all operations as `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with reasons\\\\\\\\tTEST-N2.1\\\\\\\\nAC-N2.2\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL not produce duplicate content when create uses `skip_if_exists: true` or inject uses `skip_if`\\\\\\\\tTEST-N2.2\\\\\\\\nAC-N3.1\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL compile to a single static binary with no runtime dependencies beyond the system C library\\\\\\\\tTEST-N3.1\\\\\\\\nAC-N3.2\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries to run\\\\\\\\tTEST-N3.2\\\\\\\\nAC-N4.1\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL include what, where, why, and hint fields in every error message\\\\\\\\tTEST-N4.1\\\\\\\\nAC-N4.2\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a file operation fails (exit code 3), the system SHALL include the rendered template content in the error output so the caller can fall back to manual editing. This is independent of `--verbose` — rendered content in errors is always present\\\\\\\\tTEST-N4.2\\\\\\\\nAC-N4.3\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a template rendering error occurs, the system SHALL report the template file path and the line number of the error\\\\\\\\tTEST-N4.3\\\\\\\\nAC-N4.4\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN a variable validation error occurs, the system SHALL report the variable name, expected type, and actual value provided\\\\\\\\tTEST-N4.4\\\\\\\\nAC-N5.1\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL exit with code 0 on success, 1 for recipe validation errors, 2 for template rendering errors, 3 for file operation errors, 4 for variable validation errors\\\\\\\\tTEST-N5.1\\\\\\\\nAC-N5.2\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL use the exit code corresponding to the first pipeline stage that fails: recipe validation (1) before variable validation (4) before template rendering (2) before file operations (3)\\\\\\\\tTEST-N5.2\\\\\\\\nAC-N6.1\\\\\\\\tUbiquitous\\\\\\\\tThe system SHALL execute file operations in the order they appear in the recipe's `files` array\\\\\\\\tTEST-N6.1\\\\\\\\nAC-N6.2\\\\\\\\tEvent\\\\\\\\tWHEN an inject operation targets a file created by an earlier create operation in the same recipe, the system SHALL find and operate on the newly created file\\\\\\\\tTEST-N6.2\\\\\\\\nFR-1\\\\\\\\tAC-1.1 through AC-1.15\\\\\\\\tspec::fr1::*\\\\\\\\tPENDING\\\\\\\\nFR-2\\\\\\\\tAC-2.1 through AC-2.16\\\\\\\\tspec::fr2::*\\\\\\\\tPENDING\\\\\\\\nFR-3\\\\\\\\tAC-3.1 through AC-3.18\\\\\\\\tspec::fr3::*\\\\\\\\tPENDING\\\\\\\\nFR-4\\\\\\\\tAC-4.1 through AC-4.10\\\\\\\\tspec::fr4::*\\\\\\\\tPENDING\\\\\\\\nFR-5\\\\\\\\tAC-5.1 through AC-5.16\\\\\\\\tspec::fr5::*\\\\\\\\tPENDING\\\\\\\\nFR-6\\\\\\\\tAC-6.1 through One binary that runs on macOS and Linux without Python, Node, JVM, or additional shared libraries.\\\\\\\\n   190\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   191\\\\\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\\\\\n   192\\\\\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\\\\\n   193\\\\\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\\\\\n   194\\\\\\\\t| AC-N3.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL compile to a single static binary with no runtime dependencies beyond the system C library | TEST-N3.1 |\\\\\\\\n   195\\\\\\\\t| AC-N3.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not require any external programs, interpreters, or shared libraries to run | TEST-N3.2 |\\\\\\\\n   196\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   197\\\\\\\\t#### NFR-4: Structured Errors with Rendered Content\\\\\\\\n   198\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\"}}\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-180424.md:107:{\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item_53\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"nl -ba docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md | sed -n '192,197p'\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"   192\\\\\\\\t- assert_cmd or similar for subprocess testing (or raw std::process::Command)\\\\\\\\n   193\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   194\\\\\\\\t## Dependencies\\\\\\\\n   195\\\\\\\\t\\\\\\\\n   196\\\\\\\\t- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\\\\\\\n   197\\\\\\\\t- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\"}}\\\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-codex-20260403-180424.md:108:{\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item_54\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"agent_message\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"**Critical** (blocks implementation):\\\\\\\\n- `--quiet` behavior is contradictory across canonical docs, so one implementation cannot satisfy all specs simultaneously: “suppresses stderr only” vs “suppress all stderr output” vs “suppress non-error output” vs “suppress all output except errors”. ([INVARIANTS.md:43](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/INVARIANTS.md:43), [SPEC.md:142](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:142), [SPEC.md:147](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:147), [SPEC.md:245](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:245), [ARCHITECTURE.md:278](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:278), [PLAN.md:109](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:109))\\\\\\\\n- `--verbose` cannot be fully implemented with the current internal interface/data model: spec requires rendered content in output, but `format_output` only gets `OpResult`, and `OpResult` lacks rendered content for success/skip. ([SPEC.md:145](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:145), [SPEC.md:147](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:147), [SPEC.md:281](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:281), [SPEC.md:317](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:317), [PLAN.md:93](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:93), [PLAN.md:95](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:95))\\\\\\\\n- Variable-source optionality is inconsistent: AC-2.16 requires no vars sources to be valid, while the public CLI interface declares `--vars` as mandatory for `run` and `render`. ([SPEC.md:62](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:62), [SPEC.md:236](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:236), [SPEC.md:237](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:237))\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Major** (should fix before implementation):\\\\\\\\n- AC-1.11 is mis-scoped: it sits under recipe parsing but asserts runtime `operations array` output, and PLAN already defers it to Phase 3. ([SPEC.md:34](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:34), [PLAN.md:34](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:34))\\\\\\\\n- Plan validation under-specifies invariant I-4 / AC-N4.2: it checks `what/where/why/hint` but not required `rendered_content` on file-op errors. ([SPEC.md:205](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:205), [PLAN.md:184](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:184))\\\\\\\\n- Inject error handling has a coverage gap: create explicitly covers permission-denied writes, inject does not. ([SPEC.md:146](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:146), [SPEC.md:147](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/SPEC.md:147))\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Strengths** (what’s done well):\\\\\\\\n- EARS syntax is strong across the spec; acceptance criteria are consistently structured.\\\\\\\\n- No orphaned AC rows found: all 106 AC rows include `Traces To` test IDs.\\\\\\\\n- All FR/NFR sections have explicit AC tables, and every PLAN phase has validation criteria.\\\\\\\\n- Invariant alignment is generally solid on determinism, ordered execution, recipe-relative templates, and non-interactive JSON workflow.\\\\\\\\n- Workstream dependency boundaries are clearly documented.
